text: ma et al tried a bottomup linearization but they obtained worse results than topdown approaches liu and zhang kept the topdown strategy but using transitions of the topdown transition system of dyer et al instead of a bracketed linearization achieving a higher performance in transitionbased constituent parsing an inorder algorithm has recently proved superior to the bottomup and topdown approaches but we know of no applications of this approach in seqseq parsing contributions in this paper we advance the understanding of linearizations for seqseq parsing and improve the state of the art as follows we show that the superiority of a transitionbased topdown linearization over a bracketingbased one observed by liu and zhang does not hold when both are tested under the same framework in fact we show that the additional information provided by the larger vocabulary in the linearization of vinyals et al is beneficial to seqseq predictions we implement a novel inorder transitionbased linearization based on the inorder transition system by liu and zhang and manage to notably increase parsing accuracy with respect to previous approaches we enhance the inorder representation of parse trees by adding extra information following the shiftreduce version of the linearization obtaining stateoftheart accuracy among seqseq parsers we also tested empirically that a bottomup linearization is not suitable for seqseq parsing and discarded that option and on par with some wellknown transitionbased approaches we bridge the remaining gap with transitionbased parsers parsing speed by applying a new variant of deterministic attention to restrict the hidden states used to compute the attention vector doubling the system’s speed the result is a seqseq parser that for the first time matches the speed and accuracy of transitionbased parsers implemented under the same neural framework using the neural framework of dyer et al as testing ground we perform a homogeneous comparison among different seqseq linearizations and widelyknown transitionbased parsers enriched linearizations to cast constituent parsing as seqseq prediction each parse tree needs to be represented as a sequence of symbols that can be predicted from an input sentence

let m denote the set of latent variables the expected log likelihood function for observing the masked tokens x pi conditioned on the unmasked tokens x pi and marginalized over all possible configurations of m is defined as mathcal l text pmlm x pi mid x theta mathbb e m mid x left sum m log p x pi mid x pi theta p m mid x quad the term log p x pi mid x pi theta corresponds to the objective function presented in equation for a deterministic mask m in standard masked language modeling mlm it is assumed that the mask variables m are independent and identically distributed i i d across positions and independent of the input sequence x specifically p m mid x p m r k r n k quad where r represents the masking ratio and k is the number of masked tokens while most existing mlm architectures such as bert utilize a fixed value for r the proposed probabilistic masked language model pmlm treats r as a random variable drawn from a prior distribution p r consequently the marginal distribution of the mask m is formulated as p m int p m mid r p r dr int r k r n k p r dr quad incorporating these derivations the expected log likelihood function for pmlm is expressed as mathcal l text pmlm x pi mid x theta sum m left p m quad equation is optimized by sampling m according to the specified prior distribution over the training dataset by modulating this prior distribution the model can encompass a broader spectrum of sequence prediction tasks during training thereby potentially enhancing the representational capacity of the pretrained model for instance in a left to right autoregressive setting employing a masking ratio that is uniformly distributed across positions enables the model to learn next token generation conditioned on preceding contexts of varying lengths

the findings of this study indicate that the implementation of a specialized introductory course can offer prospective students essential guidance thereby better preparing them for advanced study in computer science cs and electrical engineering ee currently a significant number of first year students select their coursework based on immediate enjoyment or adherence to a default curriculum rather than on long term educational or professional objectives largely due to a deficit in prior knowledge and accessible advising this challenge is particularly acute within large academic programs at heavily enrolled public institutions where the rapid expansion of major enrollments has outpaced the capacity for academic support resulting in advising wait times extending to several weeks without early exposure the relevance of foundational disciplines—such as circuits device physics computer architecture and theoretical computer science—often remains obscure to novices this issue is compounded by resource constraints that generate institutional pressures frequently steering students toward popular courses while discouraging the exploration of alternative yet valuable specializations to address these challenges this work proposes a low overhead first semester survey course led by undergraduate students this course is designed to provide a comprehensive overview of ee cs topics enabling students to make more informed decisions regarding their academic trajectories and career paths the pedagogical model relies on the premise that experienced undergraduates can serve as effective instructors and mentors upon completion of the course students are expected to possess a high level understanding of how departmental offerings interconnect to form coherent areas of specialization furthermore the curriculum aims to expose students to a diverse array of subjects demonstrating the value of less frequently selected courses to facilitate the recruitment of undergraduate instructors the initiative collaborated with an institutional honor society representing ee cs students many members of this society possess prior experience as teaching or research assistants all instructors for this course served as volunteers selecting topics aligned with their specific areas of passion the involvement of the honor society ensures the program s sustainability by providing a pipeline of qualified staff as senior members graduate beyond student learning outcomes the initiative establishes abstract learning goals for the teaching staff focusing on benefits analogous to interteaching models including enhanced exposure to faculty graduate researchers and active research environments this approach aligns with a broader institutional trend observed at the university of california berkeley where the escalating demand for instructional support has led to the increased utilization of undergraduate students as teaching assistants in this evolving model undergraduates frequently contribute to course content delivery and lead recitation sessions thereby addressing staffing shortages while fostering a collaborative academic environment

threats to validity and study limitations to address potential threats to internal validity arising from research design a systematic methodology was established and documented utilizing the goal question metric gqm approach concerns regarding data quality and standardization were mitigated by employing a uniform assessment protocol specifically all evaluations were conducted automatically using the codemaster ui design–app inventor rubric thereby ensuring consistency across the dataset regarding the aggregation of data from diverse contexts the study utilizes a dataset comprising applications from a global cohort of creators while demographic information regarding the creators is unavailable within the app inventor gallery this limitation does not compromise the study s primary objective which is to evaluate the validity of the proposed rubric rather than to analyze creator specific variables in terms of external validity potential threats to the generalizability of the findings relate to sample size and data diversity the analysis is based on a sample of applications sourced from the app inventor gallery representing a worldwide community this sample size is deemed sufficient to yield statistically significant results for an initial assessment of the instrument to ensure reliability and minimize dependence on specific researchers the study adhered to a rigorous systematic methodology that explicitly defined the research objectives data collection procedures and statistical analysis techniques furthermore to address the validity of the statistical methods employed the analysis followed devellis s framework for constructing measurement scales this approach aligns with established procedures for assessing the internal consistency and construct validity of measurement instruments conclusion and future directions this study presents a rubric and an automated tool designed to assess the visual design of app inventor applications within the context of k– computing education statistical evaluation indicates that the rubric demonstrates acceptable levels of reliability and construct validity while discriminant validity could not be established this is consistent with the theoretical premise that all criteria measure a single underlying construct the quality of visual design however the analysis also identified specific areas requiring refinement criteria related to images exhibited the lowest inter item correlations additionally two items within this dimension demonstrated low item total correlations suggesting that their definitions require revision to improve the instrument s overall psychometric properties

methodological considerations and statistical analysis it should be noted that participant responses were not mutually exclusive as some responses were categorized under multiple themes the reported percentages do not sum to regarding the authors positioning on internships the first author completed an internship during their computer science graduate studies and has since accumulated several years of experience in the software industry the second author completed four internships throughout their undergraduate and graduate computer science programs both authors maintain that internships are instrumental in securing employment and that obtaining such opportunities requires proactive engagement beyond standard coursework while this perspective may have influenced the coding process the study employed rigorous quantitative methods to address research questions and rq and rq the quantitative analysis was restricted to multiple choice items and was conducted using ibm spss version the dataset was stratified into two distinct groups students who had not completed an internship and students who had completed an internship or were scheduled to intern during the summer following the study the latter two subgroups were consolidated to facilitate an analysis of the factors distinguishing students who successfully secure industry internships from those who do not to assess statistical significance two types of statistical tests were selected based on the variable types with practical significance evaluated using appropriate effect size measures a significance level of alpha p leq was established for rejecting the null hypotheses during analysis extreme groups with insufficient representation e g other gender categories n were excluded to ensure the validity of the nominal variable levels the specific statistical procedures employed were as follows chi square test of independence utilized for associations between two nominal variables e g internship participation and gender the null hypothesis posits no association between the variables for statistically significant results the strength of the association was quantified using cramer s v coefficient range – effect size magnitudes were interpreted according to cohen s guidelines small medium and large two sample mann–whitney u test two tailed employed to compare an ordinal or interval variable across the two nominal groups this non parametric test assessed whether the samples originated from similar or different populations

to quantify the metric a we calculate the discrepancy between the predicted label f x and the user s requirement phi v according to the following formulation a x v text err quad where text err denotes an error function with a range of refer to section joint optimization we propose two novel strategies designed to jointly optimize both user centric and system centric objectives combined sampling the first strategy denoted as s text comb v integrates uncertainty sampling with user oriented sampling it prioritizes instances suitable for a specific user v analogous to s text usr and subsequently selects the instance among them regarding which the system exhibits the highest degree of uncertainty analogous to s text unc s text comb v arg max x in x u x a x v quad tradeoff sampling the second strategy aggregates both objectives into a unified function s text tos v defined as a weighted sum of user oriented and uncertainty sampling metrics s text tos v arg max x in x left quad in this formulation the weighting parameter lambda in serves to modulate the optimization focus allowing for adjustment between the system objective and the user objective instantiation we posit that the proposed jointly optimized active learning framework is particularly advantageous for educational applications this is predicated on two primary factors users of such systems may fail to achieve their learning objectives if presented with inappropriate exercises and the acquisition of large scale difficulty annotated datasets for training is challenging as realistic data generation requires actual user interaction and existing learner datasets are often restricted from sharing due to privacy concerns consequently we instantiate our approach within a language learning platform designed to predict exercise difficulty and deliver appropriately calibrated content neither excessively easy nor difficult to users c tests for our experimental evaluation we utilize the setup of the c test difficulty prediction task as established by beinborn c tests are gap filling exercises originally proposed by klein braley and raatz in this specific gap scheme every second word is converted into a gap by removing the latter half of its characters unlike traditional cloze tests c tests do not require distractors as the remaining first half of the word serves as a contextual hint

the amount of waste people create each day is surprisingly high with each individual generating several kilograms of trash daily making it challenging to handle this has led some to suggest that there should be a cap on the amount of rubbish each household can produce though opinions differ on whether this is the best solution setting a limit on household waste seems like a reasonable answer to a serious issue especially considering that the u s alone produced over million tons of waste in imposing taxes on households that exceed these limits could encourage people to cut back on waste and pay more attention to recycling or composting however forcing people to reduce their waste may not be practical households generate waste based on their needs and the products they buy which they are likely to continue purchasing adding extra taxes could cause frustration especially for those already struggling with their bills moreover if people produce more waste than allowed they might resort to littering or illegal dumping to avoid penalties which would harm the environment even more while managing waste production is important there may be better ways to encourage people to reduce their output personally i think governments should promote composting and recycling as these methods also help cut down on waste raising awareness about waste production and offering education on how to reduce it might be more effective in decreasing rubbish than simply imposing limits

their cs graduate school and has worked for multiple years in the software industry after graduation the second author did four internships during their cs degree programs both authors believe that participating in internships have value in gaining employment and to secure internships one needs to take active steps outside of coursework this position might have influenced the coding process we used descriptive and inferential statistics to answer rq and rq the quantitative analysis was limited to the multiplechoice questions and was conducted in ibm spss we divided the data set into two groups students who did not intern and students who interned or were interning the summer following the study for the first time the students who interned or were interning the summer following the study were merged into one group as we are trying to understand students’ ability to secure an industry internship and what makes them different than those who do not secure an internship we ran two types of statistical tests based on the type of variable to assess statistical significance and we also report practical significance through the appropriate effect size measure we used p α to reject our corresponding null hypothesis also when conducting tests we excluded extreme groups e g other genders n as we did not have adequate representation for that level of the nominal variable we used the following tests chisquare test of independence both nominal variables to determine if there is an association between our nominal variable participation in internships and another nominal variable the null hypothesis for the test assumes there is no association between the two variables for example to understand if participation in internships is associated with gender we conducted this test we further describe the strength of our associations by reporting cramer’s v coefficient range for our statistically significant results cohen suggested that the magnitude of effect size for cramer’s v can belong to three categories small medium and large two samples mann–whitney u twotailed test one nominal one ordinal interval to assess if the samples of an ordinal interval variable of interest for our two groups came from similar or different populations

the source document details the primary election victory of conservative member of parliament zac goldsmith for the london mayoral candidacy however automated summaries frequently generate hallucinations fabricating entities e g nigel goldsmith or zac goldwin and asserting unsupported or factually incorrect information e g misidentifying party affiliations election outcomes or the roles of other political figures notably despite these factual inaccuracies the generated summaries often exhibit high topical coherence fluency and strong performance on rouge metrics lin and hovy to address this discrepancy we conducted a large scale human evaluation of hallucinated content across systems utilizing recurrent neural networks rnn see et al convolutional neural networks cnn narayan et al a and transformer architectures radford et al rothe et al these were compared against human written summaries within the context of the extreme summarization xsum task narayan et al a this study aims to answer two primary questions i what is the frequency of hallucination in abstractive summarization models and ii how do these models compare to human performance regarding factual fidelity

furthermore for every noun phrase np antecedent n identified in a we augment the answer set with semantic variations of n to capture its core meaning these variations include the head noun of n e g earthquake from last week s earthquake a modified form n derived by removing all post modifiers from n e g the preliminary conclusion from the preliminary conclusion from a survey of downtown highrises a modified form n derived by removing both post modifiers and the determiner from n e g total potential claims from the total potential claims from the disaster it should be noted that if the context c a contains no antecedent for the bridging anaphor a —a scenario that may arise when utilizing a restricted context window—the label no answer is included in the set a inference unlike standard squad style question answering which imposes no constraints on the position of the predicted span relative to the query bridging anaphora resolution requires that the antecedent precede the anaphor consequently during the inference phase the position of the bridging anaphor a within its context c a is first identified the model is then constrained to predict only text spans occurring prior to a subsequently the candidate list is pruned to retain only the top k spans where k that contain no more than l words where l additionally predictions consisting solely of function words e g a over standard bert embeddings on the squad dataset following initialization the model undergoes further training employing various pretraining and fine tuning strategies a detailed exposition of these the primary objective of this approach is to minimize the number of iterations required to identify a near optimal hyperparameter configuration evaluation datasets this study employs pretrained uncased bert base and large models utilizing the whole word masking variant across five distinct paired input tasks these tasks encompass three question answering qa benchmarks and two sentence pair classification tasks the inclusion of the latter two tasks serves to demonstrate the broader applicability of the proposed decomposition method in various information seeking scenarios the specific datasets utilized are as follows squad v rajpurkar et al an extractive question answering dataset comprising over question answer pairs generated by crowd workers based on wikipedia articles race lai et al a reading comprehension dataset derived from english examinations designed to assess the reading and reasoning capabilities of middle and high school students in china it contains over passages and more than questions boolq clark et al a a collection of naturally occurring yes no questions gathered from unconstrained settings mnli multi genre natural language inference williams et al a crowdsourced corpus consisting of sentence pairs annotated with textual entailment labels qqp quora question pairs iyer et al a dataset containing over potential duplicate question pairs sourced from quora for all five tasks the standard dataset splits were utilized additionally the original training data was further partitioned to create a subset designated for hyperparameter tuning the original development split was reserved for reporting efficiency metrics flops and memory usage and effectiveness metrics accuracy or f score depending on the specific task these tasks are analogous to qa then we use the cmu twitter tokeniser and partsofspeech tagger to tokenise the tweets and assign a partsofspeech tag to each token apart from nouns verbs adjectives and adverbs this tagger is also able to tag injunctions and microbloggingspecific tokens such as emoticons hashtags and urls using the tokens in a tweet and the output of the tagger we are able to find all the tokens that represent emoticons in the tweet since people often repeat certain punctuations to emphasise emoticons we remove all repeated characters from every emoticon string to obtain the bagofemoticons present in the tweet we match emoticons in the bagofemoticons of the tweet to a manually created list of positive or negative emoticons and count the number of positive and the number of negative emoticons present in the tweet the emoticon rules applied are if a tweet contains one or more positive emoticons and no negative emoticons it is labeled as positive if a tweet contains one or more negative emoticons and no positive emoticons it is labeled as negative if neither of the two rules above apply the tweet is labeled as unknown if these emoticonbased rules label a tweet as positive or negative we consider that label to be the final label outputted by our system however all tweets labelled as unknown by these rules are passed into the next stage in our sentiment analysis pipeline for supervised learning we represent each tweet as a feature vector of casesensitive ngrams unigrams bigrams and trigrams these ngrams are frequencies of sequences of or contiguous tokens in a tweet the tfidf weighting scheme from information retrieval is applied to the frequency counts and l regularisation is used for feature selection and dimensionality reduction finally a support vector machine is trained using the liblinear library to account for negation we append the string neg to all negated tokens in a tweet all tokens between certain negation words and the next punctuation mark are considered to be negated as long as they are either nouns adjectives adverbs or verbs typical supervised learning methods based on ngrams perform badly on sentences containing special partsofspeech such as conjunctions and conditionals due to their peculiar linguistic characteristics

the definitions of empathy and distress employed in this study are grounded in established psychological literature empathy is defined as a warm tender and compassionate affective response toward a suffering entity whereas distress is characterized as a self focused negative emotional state arising from witnessing another s suffering or need regarding solidarity this work adopts a definition comprising a mutual attachment between individuals or groups operationalized across two dimensions a factual level reflecting actual common ground and a normative level entailing mutual obligations to provide aid when necessary in contrast the definition of non supportive content lacks a direct psychological foundation instead it is derived from empirical observations by annotators within the specific context of this study non supportive content was identified as primarily exhibiting one or more of the following characteristics politically motivated hate speech warmongering attitudes expressions of schadenfreude references to politically contentious issues or irrelevant content such as product promotion this research utilizes two existing resources which are briefly described below first the hope speech detection task involves identifying social media content that conveys a unifying message encouraging peace discouraging war and highlighting the economic social and human costs of conflict specifically within the context of the india pakistan conflict a comprehensive definition of hope speech accompanied by illustrative examples is provided in the relevant literature second a classifier was trained using the empathy distress dataset which is itself rooted in prior psychological research on these constructs the original dataset comprises news article excerpts from prominent platforms and corresponding responses from annotators five articles per annotator yielding a total of responses following the exclusion of responses that deviated from the task instructions the final pruned dataset consists of responses evenly distributed between empathy instances and distress instances

drawing upon existing literature this study reviews the anticipated trajectory of artificial intelligence ai development in the near term and projects the timeline for the realization of advanced capabilities furthermore it provides a comprehensive overview of current ai driven healthcare initiatives undertaken by for profit enterprises of varying scales including ibm watson health google calico and deepmind health alongside relevant research conducted by academic institutions and non profit organizations the analysis demonstrates that the exponential growth of ai capabilities enhances the potential to counteract the exponential increase in age related mortality risk consequently ai may facilitate the attainment of longevity escape velocity prior to the emergence of superintelligence thereby enabling millions or potentially billions of individuals to extend their lifespans significantly and mitigate the inevitability of premature death this perspective is further elaborated in the authors prior work fighting aging as an effective altruism case the model of impact finally the study underscores the critical necessity of establishing patient advocacy organizations to promote awareness regarding life extension facilitate voluntary data collection encourage the early adoption of medical ai technologies and prepare for the eventual integration of a neuroweb as advanced ai systems mature

for example at the second turn the value of the target slot attractionarea is informed by a referring expression “ in the same area as the restaurant ” thus the system needs to retrieve the value of its source slot restaurantarea the last turn shows an obscurer utterance with multiple slot connections in which target slots taxideparture and taxidestination are implicitly connected to their source slots attractionname and restaurantname respectively for those slots that need connections existing methods attempt to find their values out from the lengthy dialogue history which usually fail because of high learning complexity in this paper we formally consider the above challenge as relatedslot problem and propose a novel model dstsc dialogue state tracking with slot connections to address it we follow previous work to build a copyaugmented encoderdecoder model specially dstsc is designed with a slot connecting mechanism to establish the connection between the target slot and its source slot explicitly thus it can take advantage of the source slot value directly instead of reasoning from preceding turns the contributions of this work are twofold • to the best of our knowledge this work is the first one to discuss the relatedslot problem in multidomain dst and address it by explicitly modeling slot connections across domains • we demonstrate that dstsc is more effective for handling the relatedslot problem and outperforms stateoftheart baselines in this section we will describe dstsc model in detail dstsc is an open vocabulary model based on the encoderdecoder architecture as shown in figure there are three components that contribute to obtain the target slot value word generation from the vocabulary word copying from the dialogue history value copying from the source slot to reduce the burden on the decoder dstsc also equips with a slot gate to predict for slot values of none and dontcare encoder our model uses a bidirectional gru to encode the dialogue history x w w · · · w m where m is the number of tokens in the dialogue history

the crescendo platform structures its learning activities into three distinct task categories use modify and create in use tasks learners conclude the activity by answering a brief multiple choice question regarding the provided code e g identifying which code block governs the quantity of geometric shapes rendered students may attempt these questions repeatedly until a correct response is achieved modify tasks present learners with incomplete or erroneous code requiring them to refine the implementation to meet specific objectives create tasks demand independent code design with minimal scaffolding progression from modify to create tasks or advancement to subsequent challenges is contingent upon the successful completion of all task objectives to monitor progress learners may utilize the check my work feature at any stage this interface employs a star based visualization system where filled golden stars denote achieved objectives while hollow stars indicate pending goals upon clicking the button a dropdown menu provides a comprehensive overview of the learner s status relative to the task requirements once all objectives are satisfied all stars become active and a continue button appears enabling the learner to proceed to the next module although crescendo is designed to support arbitrary programming exercises the current implementation focuses on a curated set of practice tasks targeting two foundational concepts loops and conditionals these tasks are sequenced to build upon prerequisite knowledge for instance the loops task requires an understanding of code sequences within loops and iteration counts which serves as a prerequisite for loops where learners analyze code execution both inside and outside loop structures instructors retain the flexibility to define the order of challenges during course planning furthermore drawing on prior research indicating the efficacy of interleaved practice over blocked practice it is recommended that challenges involving multiple concepts be interleaved to optimize learning outcomes

generally the demonstration of prior programming experience and a genuine interest in the field proved sufficient for initial engagement beyond this baseline student participation was largely driven by self selection when presented with concrete research tasks however this dynamic presents a potential challenge while the program has yielded numerous successful high school internships as detailed in section a notable number of participants have discontinued contact after a brief period consequently identifying effective strategies to sustain engagement among students who initially express interest represents a critical area for future inquiry key lessons from this experience indicate that student projects should be positioned as non critical components of the broader research agenda while still delivering significant value this approach fosters a robust sense of computing identity among participants furthermore granting students autonomy to define their project scope and determine its integration into the larger research vision reinforces their identity as leaders and local experts within their specific domains the benefits of this mentorship model extend beyond the mentees particularly within a cascading mentorship framework for graduate students the process of advising peers and delineating project scope offers invaluable professional development unlike undergraduate research which is often highly regulated through formal coursework summer internships or campus employment collaboration with high school students affords graduate mentors the flexibility to assume greater risks this flexibility generates enhanced learning opportunities particularly for graduate students preparing for the academic job market where experience in managing a microlab environment is highly advantageous the primary guideline governing these research programs is the provision of academic support to early stage students with a specific emphasis on cultivating the ability to ask pertinent questions observations suggest that students frequently underutilize mentorship resources spending excessive time on technical hurdles that could be resolved quickly with guidance this tendency poses a risk to the collaboration potentially leading to disengagement therefore it is imperative that mentors cultivate an environment where students feel comfortable seeking assistance conversely mentors must also discern when it is pedagogically beneficial for students to resolve challenges independently such autonomy is essential for developing a strong computing identity as it builds the confidence necessary for students to solve problems autonomously

here is my attempt to reconstruct the recent evolution of pretraining corpus sizes bert uploaded to arxiv in october was trained on billion words xlnet was trained on gb of text or approximately billion words roberta was trained on gb of text or around billion words and t was trained on gb of text or approximately billion words when we rely on a single leaderboard to compare systems trained on corpora with such a large range of sizes we are not comparing architectures but rather interactions of architectures corpus sizes and computational resources available for training while this may be a useful comparison for an engineer who seeks to plug an existing trained model into a larger pipeline this approach is unlikely to advance us towards the goal advocated in this article the billion word corpus that t was trained on is much larger than the corpus that a human can expect to be exposed to before adulthood fewer than million words see section but a leaderboard that evaluates only bottomline transfer learning accuracy inherently disadvantages a sampleefficient model pretrained on a few dozen million words compared to a model such as t for all we know it is possible that architectures corpus sizes reported in massivecorpus pretraining papers are often specified in gigabytes or number of modelspecific subword units instead of measures such as number of words that are easier to compare across articles my estimates are based on an average english word length of characters and a space or punctuation mark after each word rewarded by paid such as massive transformers only work well when given an amount of data that is orders of magnitude greater than that available to humans

evaluation protocol to ensure the robustness of relevance assessment each query–response pair underwent cross evaluation by three independent annotators this process adhered to the labeling criteria established in prior literature xing et al wang et al comprehensive details regarding data sampling and annotation procedures are provided in the supplementary material results and analysis performance on the douban corpus we first present the evaluation results on the douban corpus table summarizes the performance across automatic evaluation metrics values presented in bold indicate statistically significant improvements over competing methods p leq the analysis reveals that bleu scores across various models are relatively low and exhibit minimal variance this observation is attributed to the high semantic diversity of potential responses for a given query which vary significantly in speaking style and topic consequently lexical overlap between responses is often limited to high frequency words a phenomenon consistent with findings by mou et al and liu et al however user enhanced models demonstrate superior bleu performance likely due to their capacity to incorporate user preferences regarding embedding based metrics all models achieved satisfactory scores though no single model demonstrated a statistically significant advantage over the others this trend aligns with previous studies serban et al wang et al suggesting that most models generate responses semantically similar to the ground truth nevertheless the proposed pagenerator attained the highest average score indicating that its generated responses possess greater semantic relevance to the ground truth while standard metrics show comparable performance across models results on persona specific metrics reveal distinct differences all persona aware neural response generation nrg models outperformed the ssa and vae baselines which lack user information on the urank metric notably the two variational models incorporating user information significantly surpassed the remaining models these findings suggest that persona aware generators particularly those leveraging user embeddings to construct latent variables are more effective at identifying and adapting to individual user language styles among all models utilizing user modeling pagenerator achieved the highest urank furthermore the uppl metric corroborates the efficacy of integrating persona information into nrg frameworks

intermediate tasks probing tasks we use wellestablished datasets for our probing tasks including the edgeprobing suite from tenney et al function word oriented tasks from kim et al and sentencelevel probing datasets acceptability judgment tasks this set of binary classifications tasks was designed to investigate if a model can judge the grammatical acceptability of a sentence we use the following five datasets ajcola is a task that tests for a model’s understanding of general grammaticality using the corpus of linguistic acceptability cola which is drawn from theoretical linguistics publications the other tasks concern the behaviors of specific classes of function words using the dataset by kim et al ajwh is a task that tests a model’s ability to detect if a whword in a sentence has been swapped with another whword which tests a model’s ability to identify the antecedent associated with the whword ajdef is a task that tests a model’s ability to detect if the definite indefinite articles in a given sentence have been swapped ajcoord is a task that tests a model’s ability to detect if a coordinating conjunction has been swapped which tests a model’s ability to understand how ideas in the various clauses relate to each other ajeos is a task that tests a model’s ability to identify grammatical sentences without indicators such as punctuation marks and capitalization and consists of grammatical text that are removed of punctuation edgeprobing tasks the edge probing ep tasks are a set of core nlp labeling tasks collected by tenney et al and cast into boolean classification these tasks focus on the syntactic and semantic relations between spans in a sentence the first five tasks use the ontonotes corpus partofspeech tagging eppos is a task that tests a model’s ability to predict the syntactic category noun verb adjective etc for each word in the sentence

analysis of attention head patterns notably the electra large model exhibits distinct patterns across the majority of probing tasks specifically high performing attention heads are predominantly situated in the lower layers with the exception of the oddmanout task conversely attention heads in the lower layers of other configurations appear to process minimal information relevant to these probing tasks while electra small and electra base demonstrate similar heat map distributions see appendix b the electra large model diverges significantly from these counterparts these observations suggest that learning behaviors associated with attention heads are not strictly uniform across models even when they share identical architectures and pretraining objectives selection of influential attention heads given the observation that specific attention heads across various layers contribute differently to distinct probing tasks we developed a method to generate novel embeddings as detailed in section we subsequently conducted an experiment comparing this approach against two baselines across ten probing tasks table presents a comparative analysis of three embedding types constructed using the bert family the final layer output z i l the optimal layer output z best and the reconstructed sentence embedding h n h for each task and pretrained encoder comprehensive results are provided in appendix b a comparison of accuracy between the final layer and the optimal layer reveals that the final layer does not outperform the optimal layer for any of the probing tasks this indicates that certain linguistic features are primarily processed in earlier layers and are not further refined in subsequent layers furthermore the performance comparison between the optimal layer output and the proposed reconstructed sentence embedding demonstrates a significant increase in classification accuracy with a median improvement of across nearly all tasks these findings strongly support the efficacy of the proposed method in identifying superior attention heads to construct final representations tailored for specific linguistic information it is important to note that these newly constructed sentence embeddings consist exclusively of attention head outputs our results imply that these embeddings retain substantial information comparable to the hidden states generated by the full multi head attention and feed forward network layers enhancement of downstream tasks downstream tasks from glue to evaluate whether the proposed embedding construction method extracts not only simple linguistic features but also rich sentence level representations suitable for complex applications we assessed its performance on downstream tasks derived from the glue benchmark

advancements in automated authorship obfuscation in the pursuit of fully automated text obfuscation the digital text forensics community has developed a range of methodologies including rule based search based and model based approaches early rule based systems such as those proposed by mansoorizadeh et al karadzhov et al and castro et al aim to alter textual style through deterministic algorithms specifically karadzhov et al introduced a method to align text with the average style of a target corpus while castro et al focused on stylistic simplification subsequent research has expanded into search based and model driven techniques mahmood et al employed a genetic algorithm to identify word substitutions that maximize the disruption of authorship attribution while preserving sentiment utilizing sentiment preserving word embeddings similarly bevendorff et al developed a heuristic search algorithm to select synonyms or hypernyms that increase the stylistic distance between the modified text and the author s original corpus in the domain of deep learning shetty et al utilized generative adversarial networks gans to transfer the style of an input text to a target style furthermore emmery et al applied autoencoders equipped with gradient reversal layers to achieve style invariance effectively destyling input text obfuscation detection prior research has demonstrated the efficacy of stylometric analysis in detecting manual authorship obfuscation juola afroz et al operating on the premise that human attempts to disguise authorship often inadvertently retain consistent stylistic markers in a related context shahid et al applied stylometric techniques to identify text processed by automated spinners however as will be demonstrated these traditional stylometric methods exhibit limited accuracy when applied to advanced automated obfuscation techniques concurrently there is growing interest in distinguishing synthetic text generated by deep learning based language models such as bert and gpt from human authored content leveraging contextual word likelihoods estimated via pretrained language models gehrmann et al improved human detection accuracy of synthetic text from to additionally zellers et al established that classifiers based on language models can accurately detect synthetic text generated by the same model architecture nevertheless detection performance significantly degrades when the language models used for generation and detection differ

beats empire is a simulation game centered on music studio management wherein players gather data regarding listener preferences to inform strategic decisions concerning artist recruitment and song production while the game permits players to establish self directed objectives—such as maximizing chart topping releases or cultivating a studio specializing in a specific genre—it fundamentally requires the thoughtful selection interpretation and application of data to drive gameplay as a title within the management genre the game inherently engages players in rigorous mathematical practices including the analysis of extensive datasets and the utilization of advanced representations to navigate complex interconnected systems this characteristic renders the game particularly well suited for assessment purposes furthermore by situating the gameplay within a musical context and maintaining open ended goals the game facilitates interdisciplinary integration appealing specifically to educators in fields such as art and music who seek to incorporate computer science concepts dc to ensure alignment with identified foundational knowledge and skills assessments fksas the game design prioritizes two core competencies the ability to identify relevant data for addressing specific inquiries dvti and the capacity to interpret visualizations to formulate predictions or draw conclusions dvti consequently the design framework necessitates providing students with agency in selecting data representations alongside incentives for accurate interpretation in selecting appropriate data visualization methods the potential for adoption by non computer science educators dc influenced the exclusion of complex formats such as query based databases to address the targeted fksas in accordance with design principle ag the game presents multiple representations of song popularity—including line graphs bar charts and heat maps the pedagogical focus is placed on the student s ability to select the most appropriate representation for decision making rather than on the creation of such representations through these mechanisms students utilize visual data to make informed decisions regarding artist hiring and song recording as well as to generate predictions within the game environment

abstract in contrast to prior methodologies that address model limitations through architectural modifications the proposed approach leverages dynamically computed corpus level statistics to identify optimal conversational participants for data acquisition the resulting diversity informed data collection didc framework yields datasets with significantly higher diversity compared to baseline collection methods empirical evaluation demonstrates that models trained on didc data achieve superior performance on two downstream tasks emotion classification and dialogue generation furthermore this methodology is generalizable and adaptable to various corpus level metrics introduction it is well documented that neural dialogue models frequently struggle to generate engaging and contextually relevant responses li et al a often resorting to banal utterances such as yeah while such responses may suffice for casual chitchat maintaining human engagement necessitates a high degree of response diversity diverse models vary both linguistic structures and referenced content ensuring that generated utterances deviate from the most probable responses with a certain frequency conversely models restricted to a narrow set of generic responses e g yeah no i don t know lack diversity and fail to sustain engaging interactions previous research has sought to enhance model diversity through innovations in model architectures and decoding strategies li et al a baheti et al li et al shao et al cao and clark serban et al zhao et al building upon this foundation we propose a novel methodology for collecting and curating more diverse training data this approach is designed to be complementary to existing generation specific model innovations while some prior work on data collection has prioritized diversity—for instance rashkin et al instructed crowdworkers to generate dialogue for underrepresented emotion classes—such methods often overlook the variability in individual contributors capabilities specifically they do not account for the likelihood that certain crowdworkers are more proficient at producing specific types of data to address this gap this paper introduces diversity informed data collection didc a strategic framework for constructing conversational datasets by selectively including data from specific participants the core mechanism involves progressively constructing a diverse subcorpus from a larger existing collection this is achieved by sequentially adding conversations and evaluating the marginal contribution of each new participant s utterances to the overall diversity of the subcorpus additionally this strategy is applicable to real time data collection scenarios such as crowdworking initiatives facilitating the on the fly creation of high quality diverse datasets

it is possible to use information aggregation as part of a toolkit that centers human discretionary judgment and uses technology to promote consistency reconciling our desire for a humanled decisionmaking process with the reality that human discretion introduces inconsistencies and systemic and serves the purposes of conducting reconnaissance at the systemic level and creating an opportunity for reconsideration of individual cases perhaps the most prominent objection to the recon approach is analogous to the “dual use” argument for sentencing while we have developed information aggregation tools for a review use case what is there to stop someone turning that around and using these exact same features and for a codified justice use case in the california parole context employing technology for a predictive rulebased system requires legislative parole reform and an overhaul of california’s approach to criminal data record keeping as it is currently constructed the board of parole hearings operates with great discretion parole hearings are based only in part on data that is available before the hearing for example parole hearings often discuss mitigating precommitment factors such as the living circumstances of an individual at the time that the crime was committed touching on topics such as childhood abuse gang membership or neighborhood crime these data are often not even available in sentencing transcripts even for factors that are available in records before the hearing such as a candidate’s disciplinary conduct in prison the data often only exists in archived handwritten reports that prison staff aggregate prior to the hearing the data are read out in semistructured form for the first time by the commissioner during the hearing it is therefore not possible to extract a meaningful number of the features that are currently considered for a parole decision in california without first conducting a hearing a second ethical question is whether features extracted from hearing dialogue can be used as the input to a risk assessment algorithm before a decision is reached while constructing a such a risk assessment algorithm is possible in theory we believe that such an algorithm would be hard to construct and virtually meaningless in the context of parole unlike applications to sentencing the outcome variable for parole is unclear

this chapter examines the applicability of engineering principles to both consulting firms and municipal entities offering a broader perspective on the challenges inherent in the engineering profession the discussion draws upon the experience of rich kumpfbeck director of the antenna design laboratory at geomarconi hazeltine with over three decades of tenure at hazeltine mr kumpfbeck has observed the organization s strategic evolution from a high quality electronics manufacturer focused exclusively on defense contracts to a diversified corporation serving both defense and commercial sectors this transformation necessitated a fundamental shift in engineering mindset anticipating a decline in future defense contracting corporate leadership—comprising predominantly engineers—conducted strategic assessments of the firm s human capital and technical assets the objective was to identify areas of specialized expertise that could be effectively leveraged within the commercial marketplace a critical distinction exists between the constraints governing defense and commercial sectors government contracts typically prioritize maximum performance and the highest possible reliability often rendering cost a secondary consideration conversely the commercial sector is driven primarily by cost competitiveness in this environment superior functionality alone is insufficient products must demonstrate both enhanced performance and cost effectiveness relative to existing solutions to secure market share hazeltine s defense background provided a unique competitive advantage the rigorous standards required for military components necessitated manufacturing processes with extremely tight tolerances resulting in low variability and highly consistent product quality furthermore the firm possessed specialized expertise in developing antennas capable of operating in hostile environments including high frequency systems for a broad spectrum of communications such as cellular networks an analysis of the cellular telecommunications infrastructure revealed a bifurcated market the handsets which require extreme cost minimization due to mass production and the base station tower antennas the latter category characterized by larger size greater complexity and higher unit costs represented a viable market opportunity where hazeltine s high precision engineering and reliability standards could be successfully applied

the spatial configuration of seating arrangements is frequently cited as a primary determinant of peer accessibility as one student observed the proximity of tables facilitates interaction not only within immediate groups but also across adjacent tables in the traditional lecture setting of qualitative responses similarly highlighted the ease of peer interaction it is hypothesized that this perception may stem from a lack of comparative reference points given that the prerequisite course cs was exclusively delivered in traditional environments conversely the traditional section exhibited a higher incidence of reported social isolation with of respondents indicating such experiences while less prevalent of students in the active learning classroom alc also reported feelings of isolation noting that some peers either collaborated exclusively with familiar individuals or worked entirely independently these observations suggest that even within environments explicitly designed for collaboration certain students remain reluctant to engage with unfamiliar peers or do not perceive the value in shared ideation furthermore while instructors in both settings endeavored to engage all students regardless of seating position the traditional lecture hall architecture inadvertently facilitates self isolation particularly for students seated in the rear in contrast the alc layout precludes the option of hiding in the back additionally a distinct theme emerged exclusively within the traditional section of respondents identified the room layout as a barrier to collaboration citing restrictive row seating and static chairs as limiting factors for discussion this study builds upon prior research regarding the impact of active learning spaces on academic performance corroborating findings of no significant difference in student grades within the context of a cs course employing a quasi experimental design the study compared four alc sections varying by instructor and time of day against a single traditional lecture section all sections utilized an identical active learning pedagogy flipped classroom shared lecture materials and equivalent teaching assistant support statistical analysis revealed no significant differences among the sections regarding prior academic experience as measured by cs grades attrition rates exam failure rates or overall success metrics

the techniques used in rnng address some of these biases individually but none of the techniques addresses all three together instead of using a collection of techniques for each bias we replace them all with a single solution in the form of a global unnormalized model trained with beamsearch optimization that minimises all margin violations in the beam simultaneously this single technique addresses all of the mentioned biases and gives results that outperform all previous incremental parsing models even with a relatively small beam this is not to say that all unwanted biases are removed—for instance beam search is still a biased search however the biases that remain do not have the drastic effect on performance of the three identified above baseline model the parser of stanojevi´c and steedman already offers a fully incremental transition system with a nonincremental probabilistic model that gives state of the art accuracy in recovering predicateargument dependencies the parser encodes words using elmo and bilstm subtrees with tree encoders and the stack with stacklstm this provides the encoding of the whole configuration together with the buffer because the buffer is implicitly encoded via elmo and bilstm which look at the whole sentence given the hidden vector representation of the configuration the parser uses a feedforward network to determine the probability of the next action there are three main types of transitions • parsing actions shift and reduce x where x is a unary or binary combinatory rule • supertagging actions tag x where x is one of the lexical supertags from english ccgbank • rightadjunction actions adjoin x where x is one of the nodes to which the adjunct can be adjoined we refer the reader to for more detail on the original neural model and transition system which are not of particular relevance here what matters is only that the number of tagging actions is much bigger than the number of possible parsing actions and that the buffer is implicitly encoded with elmo and bilstm to make the parsing model fully incremental first we modify elmo embeddings instead of using full elmo embeddings we use only the forward lstm part of elmo

contribution and methodological context the second primary contribution of this study is an enhanced understanding of the referential capabilities inherent in contemporary language models alongside a comparative analysis of transformer and long short term memory lstm architectures empirical results indicate that the transformer architecture consistently outperforms the lstm across all evaluated metrics while both models exhibit similar behavioral patterns regarding morphosyntactic features—differing primarily in performance magnitude—they demonstrate distinct behaviors when processing semantic and referential information related work coreference and anaphora resolution mitkov poesio et al represent foundational topics in computational linguistics that have sustained significant research interest over the past decade as evidenced by numerous shared tasks pradhan et al poesio et al this analysis utilizes the ontonotes dataset hovy et al pradhan et al a standard resource developed within the coreference resolution community although the proposed probe tasks are intrinsically linked to coreference resolution the objective is not the development of a coreference system per se but rather the investigation of whether language models can extract features pertinent to reference without explicit supervision recent scholarship has focused on demonstrating that neural networks trained on language modeling objectives absent of linguistic annotation acquire syntactic properties and relations such as agreement and filler gap dependencies linzen et al gulordava et al kuncoro et al wilcox et al futrell et al this is typically achieved by analyzing model predictions on controlled datasets a subset of this research employs probe models also termed diagnostic models to examine the information encoded within hidden representations adi et al conneau et al hupkes et al lakretz et al giulianelli et al the present study adopts this methodology to specifically target referential information notably research concerning referential information remains less extensive than that addressing syntactic properties such as subject verb agreement regarding anaphoric reference peters et al conducted a limited evaluation using sentences from ontonotes their findings suggest that language models possess a degree of capability for unsupervised coreference resolution the first probe task proposed herein serves as an extension of their work designed to yield more granular insights into these capabilities

students and instructors were convened to identify and prioritize the development of features deemed most critical by end users integrated feedback mechanisms the incorporation of feedback and survey instruments directly within the system enabled the collection of insights from a broader user base including those experiencing difficulties behavioral analytics the implementation of google analytics allowed for the tracking of user interactions and behaviors revealing underutilized features furthermore this data indicated a substantial volume of traffic originating from mobile devices necessitating a strategic investment in optimizing the user experience for smartphones and tablets flexibility in response to the growing demand for educational tools capable of integrating with learning tools interoperability lti standard for integration into learning management systems lms this approach facilitated the seamless integration of ripple into blackboard at our institution thereby streamlining adoption additionally it enabled the invitation of instructors from external universities to pilot the system without requiring involvement from their respective central it departments scalability to ensure the system can meet real time demands it is essential that the architecture supports a user base ranging from hundreds to millions of concurrent students depending on education this paper details the experiences gained during the development and piloting of ripple an adaptive learning system

in the context of numerical reasoning over text nrot the target answer typically manifests in one of two forms a a text span or a sequence of spans extracted directly from the provided context or question or b a numerical value derived through computation two primary yet contrasting methodological frameworks have been proposed to address this task the first is the symbolic approach wherein a model parses the input to generate a formal numerical expression which is subsequently evaluated by an external symbolic calculator this methodology a specific instance of semantic parsing was prevalent in early nrot datasets however it presents significant limitations first the discrete and combinatorially expansive nature of numerical expressions necessitates the use of non differentiable search operations which are inherently difficult to optimize second this approach is restricted to generating numerical outputs whereas many tasks such as those in the drop dataset require numerical reasoning to arrive at a final answer that is a text span the second is the distributed approach which involves a model directly generating the answer from the input pair while this method facilitates straightforward training and allows for the extraction of text spans or the generation of novel numbers it requires the model to learn complex numerical computations from relatively limited training data empirical evidence suggests that this often results in suboptimal performance consequently the prevailing strategy in contemporary nrot research adopts a hybrid approach this architecture augments standard extractive question answering models with specialized modules designed to handle a constrained set of numerical operations this hybrid framework serves as the foundational architecture for the model proposed herein

in conversational interactions each participant is uniquely influenced by the others consequently we hypothesize that explicitly modeling these relational dependencies within a graph structure enhances the capture of inter speaker and intra speaker dynamics thereby facilitating robust speaker level context encoding the proposed methodology employs a two stage graph convolution process to transform initially speaker independent vertex feature vectors into speaker dependent representations both stages are conceptualized as specific instances of a differentiable message passing framework in the initial stage a new feature vector is generated for each vertex by aggregating local neighborhood information—specifically neighboring utterances defined by past and future context windows—via a relation specific transformation the subsequent stage applies a further local neighborhood transformation to the output of the first step this stacked architecture effectively accumulates a normalized sum of local neighborhood features thereby integrating contextual speaker information for each utterance self connections are incorporated to ensure the preservation of self dependent feature transformations following this the contextually encoded feature vectors derived from the sequential encoder and the speaker level encoder are concatenated a similarity based attention mechanism is then applied to generate the final utterance representation which is subsequently classified using a fully connected network the model is trained using categorical cross entropy loss augmented with l regularization optimized via the adam stochastic gradient descent algorithm hyperparameter tuning was conducted through grid search the proposed dialoguegcn model was evaluated on three benchmark multimodal datasets iemocap avec and meld while these datasets contain textual visual and acoustic modalities this study focuses exclusively on conversational emotion recognition utilizing textual information multimodal analysis is reserved for future work the iemocap dataset comprises dyadic dialogues involving ten unique speakers with the training set restricted to the first eight speakers from sessions one through four utterances within these dialogues are annotated with one of six emotion categories happy sad neutral angry excited and frustrated the avec dataset derived from the semaine database features interactions between humans and artificial agents with utterances annotated using four continuous affective attributes valence arousal expectancy and power

in the domain of neural machine translation nmt it is standard practice to define the conditional distribution via an autoregressive factorization sutskever et al bahdanau et al vaswani et al expressed as log p theta y mid x sum t t log p theta y t mid y t x this formulation can be interpreted as an energy based model lecun et al by defining the energy function as e theta x y log p theta y mid x given a set of trained parameters theta inference at test time aims to identify the translation hat y for a source sentence x that minimizes this energy hat y arg min y e theta x y identifying the translation that minimizes the energy necessitates a combinatorial search in this work we employ inference networks to approximate this search process the core premise of this approach is to substitute the combinatorial search typically utilized in structured prediction with the output of a network trained to generate approximately optimal predictions tu and gimpel formally we define an inference network a psi that maps an input x to a translation y trained such that a psi x approx arg min y e theta x y specifically assuming theta is pre trained and fixed the parameters psi of the inference network are optimized as follows psi arg min psi mathbb e langle x y rangle in mathcal d left quad where mathcal d denotes a training set of sentence pairs the architecture of the inference network a psi may differ from that of the energy function in this study we integrate an autoregressive energy function with a non autoregressive inference network this combination aims to leverage the representational effectiveness of the autoregressive energy model while capitalizing on the computational efficiency of non autoregressive inference energy functions for inference network training to facilitate gradient based optimization of the inference network parameters psi we subsequently define a generalized family of energy functions applicable to nmt

uncertainty than between pairs of american annotators they attribute these results to sociocultural differences between india and the united states they conclude that sarcasm annotation expands beyond linguistic expertise and is dependent on considering such factors labels provided by thirdparty annotators might therefore not reflect the sarcastic intention of the authors of the texts that are being labelled making this labelling method suboptimal for capturing intended sarcasm to investigate this further we looked at the riloff dataset which is published as a list of labelled tweet ids we could only retrieve tweets the others being removed from twitter we looked at the agreement between the presence of tags and manual annotation table shows the results we notice that of the tweets that contained the predefined hashtags were labeled nonsarcastic this disagreement between distant supervision and manual annotation provides further evidence to doubt the ability of the latter to capture intended sarcasm at least not the flavor that distant supervision might capture we could not perform the same analysis on the semeval dataset because only the text of the tweets is provided hashtags are filtered out and tweet ids are not available as we have shown both labelling methods use a proxy for labelling sarcasm in the form of predefined tags predefined sources or thirdparty annotators as such they are unable to capture the sarcastic intention of the authors of the texts they label generating both false positives and false negatives

in the domain of text classification park et al propose a data augmentation strategy designed to mitigate bias by swapping gender implying identity terms e g replacing he with she or actor with actress within training sentences the objective of this intervention is to sever the statistical correlation between the sensitive attribute z and the target label y however this gender swapping approach implicitly relies on the assumption that the underlying non discriminatory distribution denoted as tilde d satisfies the following conditions q x setminus z mid z q x setminus z q y mid x setminus z z q y mid x setminus z where x setminus z represents the semantic content of the sentence excluding identity specific information we contend that these assumptions are not universally valid for instance the first assumption can lead to the generation of semantically incoherent sentences e g he gives birth as noted by sun et al furthermore this methodology lacks scalability and practicality in scenarios involving a large number of demographic groups proposed instance weighting method this section introduces a novel instance weighting framework aimed at mitigating discrimination in text classification tasks we begin by establishing a set of assumptions regarding the generative process by which the biased distribution d is derived from the non discriminatory distribution tilde d subsequently we demonstrate that it is possible to estimate the unbiased loss with respect to tilde d using only samples drawn from d this capability enables models to align with the non discriminatory distribution without necessitating additional computational resources or manual annotations assumptions regarding the generative process adopting the perspective that the discriminatory distribution arises from the non discriminatory distribution tilde d we define a selection indicator variable s in this variable determines whether a specific sample is included in the biased dataset specifically we assume that each sample tuple x z y s is drawn independently from tilde d according to the following rule if s the sample is retained in the dataset otherwise it is discarded consequently we posit the following assumption the observed distribution p cdot is equivalent to the conditional distribution of the underlying population given selection i e p cdot q cdot mid s assumption as defined in section the non discriminatory distribution tilde d satisfies the condition of independence between the label and the sensitive attribute such that q y mid z q y ideally if the selection variable s operates entirely at random the resulting dataset would accurately reflect the original non discriminatory distribution tilde d thereby eliminating discriminatory bias
