for each experimental iteration of deebert across the evaluated datasets three entropy thresholds s were selected based on quality–efficiency trade offs observed on the development set these selections were designed to illustrate two distinct operational regimes maximal runtime reduction with negligible performance degradation less than and substantial runtime reduction accompanied by a moderate performance decline ranging from to the optimal threshold values were determined individually for each dataset the resulting trade off dynamics are visualized in figure where each curve represents an interpolation of data points corresponding to varying threshold settings as this analysis focuses on comparative performance within the deebert framework runtime metrics were derived exclusively from the development set an examination of table and figure reveals the following trends consistent performance trajectories despite variations in baseline performance both model architectures exhibit analogous behavior across all datasets accuracy and f scores remain largely stable until a specific inflection point in runtime savings is reached after which performance begins to decline gradually this inflection point typically occurs earlier for bert than for roberta however once this threshold is surpassed roberta demonstrates a steeper rate of performance degradation compared to bert the underlying mechanisms for this divergence are analyzed in section anomalous performance spikes in certain instances such as roberta on the sst dataset and both architectures on the rte dataset the curves exhibit localized performance spikes these anomalies are attributed to a regularization effect induced by early exiting which effectively reduces the model size this suggests that in specific contexts utilizing a subset of transformer layers may yield superior results compared to employing the full network depth note all reported runtime figures encompass both cpu and gpu execution times

abstract while recurrent knowledge interaction effectively addresses the challenge of selecting appropriate knowledge for generating informative responses the integration of such knowledge into conversational generation remains problematic specifically descriptive terms derived from external knowledge frequently constitute out of vocabulary oov tokens a persistent issue in natural language processing although neural generative models incorporating pointer networks have demonstrated efficacy in mitigating oov challenges vinyals et al gu et al existing research on copyable generative models has largely overlooked the specific requirements of knowledge driven dialogue where external knowledge terms are often critical components of the response to address this gap we propose a knowledge aware pointer network integrated with a recurrent knowledge interactive decoder this architecture combines a sequence to sequence seqseq framework with a dual pointer mechanism that attends to both utterance and knowledge distributions our approach demonstrates that leveraging knowledge copying effectively resolves oov issues and mitigates knowledge incompleteness our primary contributions are as follows recurrent knowledge interaction we introduce a mechanism for dynamic knowledge selection across decoding steps enabling the coherent integration of multiple knowledge sources into the response knowledge aware pointer network we employ a pointer network to facilitate knowledge copying thereby addressing oov limitations and preserving knowledge integrity particularly for long text inputs enhanced response quality the synergy between recurrent knowledge interaction and knowledge copying yields responses that are more informative coherent and fluent generalizability and efficiency comprehensive experiments indicate that our model is robust across diverse knowledge formats textual and structured and languages english and chinese furthermore the proposed model significantly outperforms competitive baselines while utilizing fewer parameters model description let the dataset be denoted as mathcal d x i y i k i i n where n represents the total number of samples in this framework a dialog response y y y dots y n is generated based on the conversation history x x x dots x m and a set of relevant knowledge candidates k k k dots k s here m and n denote the number of tokens in the conversation history and the generated response respectively while s represents the cardinality of the relevant knowledge set k

factbased content weighting for evaluating abstractive summarisation xinnuo xu ondˇrej duˇsek jingyi li verena rieser and ioannis konstas the interaction lab macs heriotwatt university edinburgh uk charles university faculty of mathematics and physics prague czechia xx jl v t rieser abstractive summarisation is notoriously hard to evaluate since standard wordoverlapbased metrics are biased towards specific words in the human reference we introduce a new evaluation metric which abstracts away from the wordlevel and instead is based on factlevel content weighting i e relating the facts of the document to the facts of the summary we follow the assumption that a good summary will reflect all relevant facts i e the ones present in the ground truth humangenerated reference summary we confirm this hypothesis by showing that our weightings are highly correlated to human perception and compare favourably to the recent manual highlightbased metric of hardy et al introduction text summarisation compresses long textual documents into short summaries while retaining the most important information from the source in contrast to extractive summarisation which directly copies the most relevant fragments abstractive summarization retains the most important facts and expresses them via paraphrasing aggregating and even inferring new facts recent advances in neural decoders led to a number of singledocument summarisation systems that exhibit some level of abstraction in their outputs usually in the simplest form of paraphrasing narayan et al liu and lapata inter alia evaluating abstractive summarisation remains an open challenge first decoders are amenable to pathogeniessuch as hallucination and or omission of important information which are hard to capture using existing evaluation metrics second most datasets used for abstractive summarisation only contain a single reference summary e g which most existing automatic metrics evaluate against e g rouge using exact ngram overlap and thus tend to downvote paraphrases

sen university china ² alibaba group china contact wangk shenwzh yangyy mail sysu edu cn abstract aspect based sentiment analysis absa aims to determine the sentiment polarity directed toward specific aspects within online reviews while recent approaches predominantly utilize attention based neural networks to implicitly associate aspects with opinion words these models frequently struggle to distinguish connections due to linguistic complexity and the presence of multiple aspects within a single sentence this paper addresses these challenges through the effective encoding of syntactic information first we define a unified aspect oriented dependency tree structure rooted at a target aspect by reshaping and pruning standard dependency parse trees subsequently we propose a relational graph attention network rgat to encode this novel tree structure for sentiment prediction extensive experiments conducted on the semeval and twitter datasets demonstrate that our approach more effectively establishes connections between aspects and opinion words resulting in a significant improvement in the performance of graph attention networks gat introduction aspect based sentiment analysis absa is dedicated to the fine grained analysis of sentiment in online affective texts such as product reviews

the analysis identified several instances where solutions comprised consecutive plans targeting distinct objectives despite the requirement for simultaneous goal addressing through plan merging in assignments involving multiple distinct goals the most prevalent error was the complete omission of a required plan this deficiency may be attributed to hasty interpretation or cognitive overload resulting from the simultaneous processing and integration of numerous goals and plans notably even in scenarios characterized by low complexity solutions frequently exhibited unnecessary complexity likely stemming from a trial and error approach enhancing students awareness of planning strategies could foster more constructive and directed debugging capabilities it is important to note that the current sample may not be fully representative of the broader population due to specific selection criteria typically only high performing students pass the examination in the initial session furthermore the cohort was restricted to those who successfully completed a preliminary filtering exercise thereby introducing a selection bias regarding student preparation and skill levels future research will extend this analysis to subsequent examination sessions to determine if these findings hold for students with lower average grades additionally the current evaluation framework did not account for the correctness of the output future iterations will incorporate this metric with a fixed weighting regarding faculty adoption existing literature predominantly focuses on the awareness and trial stages of innovation specifically how instructors discover new teaching practices and the motivations for initial experimentation while this knowledge is valuable sustainable reform is unlikely if innovations remain merely experimental therefore understanding the factors that drive sustained adoption is critical to address this the present study utilizes data from a two phase project conducted in the united states to investigate why computer science cs faculty continue or discontinue the use of specific practices following an initial trial the first phase involved interviews and observations of cs faculty members across diverse institutional settings to explore theory driven themes based on these qualitative findings the second phase collected survey data from faculty members at institutions this paper briefly summarizes the qualitative results and subsequently employs quantitative data to model the impact of several factors on sustained usage achieved benefits regarding student performance and satisfaction in cs perceived usefulness to the instructor student feedback and ease of use the results indicate that benefits to student performance are the paramount predictor of continued practice adoption

the morphological models examined herein operate on the premise that exposure to canonical forms alone is sufficient for acquiring speaker like behavior however corkery et al observe that training exclusively on single target forms yields highly skewed error distribution ed model scores characterized by excessive probability mass on the top ranked form and instability in lower rankings while they argue that training on a distribution may lack cognitive plausibility it remains possible that speakers regularly encounter variable realizations of plural forms for instance köpcke notes significant regional variation in german plural inflection where northern dialects may utilize the s suffix e g die mädels in contrast to the e n suffix preferred in southern dialects e g die mädeln consequently integrating dialect informed variability into training protocols may facilitate more speaker like generalization in neural models ¹³ parallel challenges arise regarding model evaluation specifically concerning how to assess production models when the target output constitutes a distribution rather than a single form in simplified tasks such as classification hahn and nakisa the output distribution is constrained within a finite space of plausible forms conversely sequence to sequence models operate within an open ended domain encompassing all possible strings for encoder decoder architectures likelihood scores generated during beam search decoding offer an intuitive evaluation metric indeed k c utilized these scores to evaluate their model against albright and hayes wug data nevertheless corkery et al demonstrate that such scores are unsuitable for this specific comparison furthermore recent scholarship has highlighted broader limitations inherent to both beam search and global model scores in neural sequence to sequence frameworks stahlberg and byrne our findings provide additional evidence that lower ranked ed predictions fail to reflect cognitively plausible distributions as they frequently contain ill formed outputs and omit inflectional classes such as e n which are prevalent in human speaker productions ¹³ note as in previous studies utilizing these stimuli study did not collect data regarding participants dialect backgrounds this limitation is being addressed in follow up research it is noted that study commenced with an onboarding task prompting speakers to inflect existing nouns in modern high german a procedure intended to prime the use of the standard variety for subsequent tasks

a text plot specification tpspec for a plot of type t is formally defined as tp t s v s v dots mid s i in s t v i in v t i consequently a tpspec constitutes a sequence of tokens serving as a structured textual representation of a plot this representation is invertible allowing for the deterministic reconstruction of the original slot value pairs from the tpspec the architectural design of tpspecs parallels the utilization of structured representations in dialog state tracking kan et al the proposed data collection pipeline leverages tpspecs and comprises two distinct phases phase plot generation this phase involves the synthesis of a dataset of matplotlib plots given the bijective mapping between text plot specifications and their corresponding plot images the generation process focuses exclusively on creating tpspecs for each plot type t and its associated set of slots s i in s t a value pool p t i subseteq v t i is established tpspec samples are then generated by randomly sampling slot values from these respective pools phase dialog collection the second phase entails the acquisition of dialogues pertaining to the plots generated in phase this process employs the wizard of oz woz methodology kelley a widely adopted framework in which one participant assumes the role of the user while another simulates the system this approach has been instrumental in constructing successful dialog corpora including the air travel information system atis hemphill et al and subsequent datasets budzianowski et al rojas barahona et al asri et al in this study woz tasks were implemented on the amazon mechanical turk mturk platform assigning two distinct roles the describer acting as the user this participant has access to a target plot which serves as the objective for the operator but remains invisible to them the operator acting as the plotting agent this participant utilizes an operation panel containing editable fields for each slot through this interface the operator executes plot functions on a server both participants share access to a working plot which reflects the current state of the visualization generated by the operator in response to the describer s instructions

this bytepair encoding was taken from the pretrained gpt model trained on a much larger corpus the results reported for these models thus conflate a choice of model class a deep transformer architecture and preprocessing standard subword tokenization computed on a larger corpus some preliminary work suggests that subword tokenization is indeed responsible for much of the larger gpt models’ success we find that gpt models trained on wordlevel representations of blliplg and bllipmd achieve good perplexity measures but degrade sharply in sg score peculiarities of the gpt training regime may be responsible for its particularly bad performance on the smaller corpora its subword vocabulary was held constant across training corpora meaning that the model vocabulary size also remained constant across corpora unlike the other models tested the poor performance of gpt models trained on smaller corpora may thus be due to overparameterization and not due to fundamental problems with the model architecture at small data scales we leave a thorough investigation of the role of subword tokenization to future work discussion this work addresses multiple open questions about syntactic evaluations and their relationship to other language model assessments our results dissociate model perplexity and performance in syntactic generalization tests suggesting that the two metrics capture complementary features of language model knowledge in a controlled evaluation of different model classes and datasets we find model architecture plays a more important role than training data scale in yielding correct syntactic generalizations our circuitlevel analysis reveals consistent failure on licensing but inconsistent behavior on other circuits suggesting that different syntactic circuits make use of different underlying processing capacities in addition to the insight these results provide about neural nlp systems they also bear on questions central to cognitive science and linguistics putting lower bounds on what syntactic knowledge can be acquired from string input alone targeted syntactic evaluation is just one in a series of complementary methods being developed to assess the learning outcomes of neural language processing models other methods include classifying sentences as grammatical or ungrammatical decoding syntactic features from a model’s internal state or transfer learning to a strictly syntactic task such as parsing or pos tagging

the code org advanced placement computer science principles ap csp curriculum employs a structured self paced instructional model that combines fixed lesson sequences with opportunities for students to develop visual interactive programs this approach is augmented by automated feedback mechanisms designed to provide real time support the crescendo platform extends upon these foundational environments by incorporating additional pedagogical supports specifically the use modify create umc framework and parsons problems the umc framework operates on a scaffolded progression initiating with highly supported activities utilizing pre existing code and gradually reducing instructional support until students engage in open ended creation tasks this methodology aligns with established educational theories such as the zone of proximal development zpd which posits that learners benefit most from carefully designed instructional support that facilitates skill acquisition until independence is achieved by guiding students through a sequence of using existing code to grasp underlying concepts modifying similar structures to solve new problems and finally creating original solutions from scratch the umc framework maintains learners within their zpd consequently this framework fosters the development of computational thinking by transitioning students from the consumption of existing programs to the authorship of their own empirical evaluation of the umc framework conducted by lytle et al through a quasi experimental study involving middle school students demonstrated its efficacy the study compared a umc based curriculum against a traditional approach revealing that students in the umc condition completed tasks more rapidly exhibited higher levels of engagement and developed a stronger sense of ownership over their programming artifacts furthermore educators reported that the umc curriculum was straightforward to implement these findings are consistent with observations from block based environments such as scratch where social sharing features facilitate remixing behaviors users who extensively modify prior projects demonstrate interaction with a broader array of coding blocks and structural patterns in addition to the umc progression the crescendo platform integrates parsons problems into each task to enhance programming we term the former property stability and the latter fidelity give intuition for both in this section and provide a formal instantiation in section stability for an encoding function α and some distribution over inputs p x the stability stab α measures how often α maps sentences x ∼ p x to the same encoding as all of their perturbations fidelity an encoding function α has high fidelity if models that use α can still achieve high standard accuracy unfortunately while we want to make task agnostic encoding functions standard accuracy is inherently task dependent different tasks have different expected distributions over inputs and labels to emphasize this challenge consider two tasks for an integer n predict n mod and n mod the information we need encodings to preserve varies significantly between these tasks for the former and can be identically encoded while for the latter they must encoded separately to overcome this challenge we consider a single distribution over the inputs p x that we believe covers many taskdistributions p task since it is hard to model the distribution over the labels we take the more conservative approach of mapping we can set z ⊆x when g accepts sentences the different sentences sampled from p x to different encodings with high probability we call this fid α and give an example in section tradeoff stability and fidelity are inherently competing goals an encoding function that maps every sentence to the same encoding trivially maximizes stability but is useless for any nontrivial classification task conversely fidelity is maximized when every input is mapped to itself which has very low stability in the following section we construct an instantiation of roben that balances stability and fidelity when the attack surface consists of typos robust encodings for typos in this section we focus on adversarial typos where an adversary can add typos to each token in a sentence see figure since this attack surface is defined at the level of tokens we restrict attention to encoding functions that encode each token independently such an encoding does not use contextual information we find that even such robust encodings achieve greater attack accuracy and robust accuracy in practice than previous work

certifiably robust training methods jia et al huang et al shi et al address existing challenges by optimizing over a convex outer approximation of the perturbation set thereby providing a lower bound on true robust accuracy however the tightness of these bounds degrades significantly as network size increases rendering them vacuous for state of the art architectures such as bert furthermore these approaches necessitate separate computationally expensive training procedures for each specific task even when defending against identical perturbation types ideally a modular robustness component should be reusable across multiple tasks requiring robustness optimization only during its initial construction this paradigm of reusable components has been instrumental in recent advancements in natural language processing nlp for instance word embeddings are constructed once and subsequently applied to diverse tasks the question arises whether a similarly reusable robust defense can be developed to integrate seamlessly with complex state of the art architectures while recent work by pruthi et al proposes a reusable defense utilizing a typo corrector against adversarial typos empirical evidence suggests that current typo correctors exhibit poor performance even against heuristic attacks thus limiting their practical utility our primary contribution is robust encodings roben a framework designed to construct encodings that confer robustness to systems employing any underlying model the core mechanism of roben is an encoding function that maps sentences to a compact discrete space of encodings which are subsequently utilized for prediction we define two essential criteria for a robust encoding function stability and fidelity first to ensure consistent predictions across perturbations the function must map all perturbations of a given sentence to a limited set of encodings stability simultaneously these encodings must remain sufficiently expressive to ensure that models trained on them maintain high performance on unperturbed inputs fidelity because systems utilizing roben are encoding based it is possible to compute exact robust accuracy tractably thereby avoiding the reliance on lower bounds inherent to certifiably robust training moreover these encodings can render any downstream model robust including state of the art transformers like bert and can be effectively reused across various tasks in section we demonstrate the application of roben in mitigating adversarial typos

this paper addresses the challenge of low adoption rates in digital health technologies a phenomenon often attributed to the absence of human interaction inherent in traditional clinical encounters to mitigate this limitation we introduce marie bot wellbeing a conversational agent designed to emulate key aspects of in person doctor patient interactions central to this design is the integration of a human avatar to facilitate grounded question answering mirroring the non verbal and spatial communication—such as pointing to specific anatomical regions—common in physical consultations furthermore the agent incorporates multiple interaction modes expanding its utility beyond strictly medical inquiries to include discussions on general topics and current events we posit that these features particularly the avatar and the versatility of interaction will enhance user engagement and adherence this manuscript provides a high level overview of the agent s design architecture details the implementation of an early prototype and presents preliminary evaluation results natural language processing nlp offers a pathway to scalable cost effective solutions for promoting public wellbeing such technologies are particularly vital for underserved populations including individuals in medically deprived regions those with limited mobility and patients engaged in self monitoring while evidence suggests the efficacy of these tools their widespread implementation remains hindered by adoption barriers by restoring elements of human presence and interaction this work aims to bridge the gap between technological potential and practical utility supporting individuals in understanding complex physiological conditions to facilitate effective monitoring and prevention

sentiment analysis encompasses a diverse range of applications including the assessment of film popularity based on online reviews and the identification of preferred consumer electronics or musical compositions fundamentally sentiment classification involves assigning textual documents such as product reviews to polarity categories—positive negative or neutral—based on their subjective content effective sentiment analysis relies heavily on commonsense knowledge defined as the foundational understanding acquired through human experience while automatic commonsense reasoning typically employs ontologies and algorithms ranging from predicate logic to machine learning the integration of such knowledge into sentiment analysis remains underexplored commonsense reasoning is particularly critical for resolving context dependent ambiguities for instance the descriptor small conveys a negative sentiment when applied to a hotel room but a positive one regarding a queue at a post office similarly the phrase go read the book implies a positive recommendation in a literary review but a negative critique in a film review to address these challenges we propose a commonsense knowledge based approach capable of detecting implicit sentiment this method analyzes concepts that do not explicitly convey polarity but are implicitly linked to sentiment bearing concepts by operating at the concept level this approach preserves the semantic integrity of multi word expressions thereby outperforming existing models the methodology utilizes a concept parser to decompose text into clauses and subsequently into discrete concepts which are then mapped onto a vector space of commonsense knowledge furthermore the efficacy of machine learning models is significantly influenced by feature selection a feature is deemed relevant if its removal results in a degradation of classification performance conversely irrelevant or redundant features can impair model accuracy in high dimensional datasets containing thousands of features it is common to encounter a substantial proportion of non informative attributes the inclusion of such irrelevant and noisy features often leads to performance deterioration consequently the identification and elimination of redundant features—detectable through correlation measures—are essential for optimizing classification performance

consider a scenario in which an incremental parser has processed a prefix x k i this prefix may be inherently ambiguous resulting in two competing derivations within the search beam one in state a and another in state b both possessing equal scores up to that point upon processing subsequent tokens the parser may encounter a word that resolves this ambiguity providing evidence that state a is the correct path an effective incremental parser should consequently assign higher scores to all derivations originating from state a and lower scores to those from state b however a locally normalized model is incapable of performing this adjustment due to the constraint of local normalization the probabilities of all transitions emanating from any given state must sum to unity consequently even if all transitions originate from an incorrect state they cannot be collectively penalized as the probability mass must still be distributed among them this limitation implies that the parser cannot recover from garden path errors regardless of the beam size this deficiency stems from the probabilistic model s independence assumption which posits that the parsing action depends solely on the processed prefix as a result the model effectively disregards its input history in specific contexts it is important to note that label bias exerts no influence during greedy search as no competing states with divergent histories exist in that mode label bias is detrimental exclusively in the context of beam search exposure bias the standard methodology for training sequence prediction models involves predicting the next action based on the ground truth gold history present in the training data however at inference time the model operates based on its own predicted history rather than the gold history in instances where the predicted history deviates from the ground truth the model may fail to assign appropriate probabilities to future actions as it has not been exposed to such erroneous histories during training this phenomenon is widely known as exposure bias this issue is particularly pertinent to incremental parsing for instance if the parser enters a garden path state defined here broadly as any transition path that ultimately proves incorrect and words remain in the suffix the model s performance may degrade due to this lack of exposure to error recovery scenarios

enhanced reliability in personality prediction would facilitate the widespread integration of automated personality detection systems across diverse human machine interaction platforms including voice assistants robotics and autonomous vehicles current research is evolving from text based analysis toward visual and multimodal approaches demonstrating significant practical potential it is anticipated that the deep learning architectures discussed herein will transition into industrial applications within the near future over the past five years the adoption of deep learning models for automated personality detection has surged with deep neural networks achieving state of the art performance particularly in visual modalities this trajectory is expected to continue driven by the development of increasingly sophisticated and robust models given the breadth of potential industrial applications this domain represents a rapidly expanding field of study however the efficacy of machine learning models remains contingent upon the quality and quantity of training data a critical challenge currently facing the field is the scarcity of sufficiently large labeled datasets required to train extensive neural networks there is an urgent need for datasets that are larger more accurate and more diverse presently the majority of available data focuses on the big five personality model with limited representation of alternative frameworks such as the myers briggs type indicator mbti or the pen model furthermore the validity of current labeling methodologies warrants scrutiny traditional personality assessment relies on self reported survey responses which are subject to issues of respondent honesty and self perception bias consequently more accurate and efficient methods for labeling personality traits must be developed currently dataset creation predominantly relies on manual annotation via crowdsourcing platforms such as amazon mechanical turk while recent multimodal deep learning techniques have demonstrated promising reliability in personality prediction the field must address these data limitations to fully leverage the computational power and data availability that deep learning offers thereby minimizing the need for extensive manual engineering

an intersectional approach becomes necessary when seeking to understand underrepresented or marginalized populations who are often relegated to the outer fringes of society because of the historical and political contexts of racism gender discrimination identity politics and white privilege prevalent in the united states collins bilge define intersectionality as a way of understanding and analyzing the complexity in the world in people and in human experiences conditions of social and political life and the self can seldom be understood as shaped by one factor they are generally shaped by many factors in diverse and mutually influencing ways in essence intersectionality takes a multidimensional approach to understanding how social constructs such as race gender class physical ability sexual orientation etc contribute to one s sense of self who am i one s day to day reality how do i see the world and how others relate to self how do others see me before it was first called intersectionality the origins of intersectionality can be traced to the coalitions of black women chicanos native american women afrobrazilian women and other women of color who fought social injustices that denied their humanity and civil rights as far back as the th century the term intersectionality appears in crenshaw’s work in critical race theory demonstrating the practice of first identifying and then criticizing the power structures that have a negative impact on black women’s well being in society before engaging in acts of resistance against those same power structures collins bilge refer to this practice as critical praxis intersectionality is not a new concept having been embraced by the social sciences and humanities as an analytical framework to study indigenous populations in north america europe and the global south countries located in asia africa latin america and the caribbean for hundreds of years likewise because of the acute underrepresentation of women of color blacks hispanics latinx native americans etc in the computing ecosystem intersectionality has recently surfaced as a critical framework to acquire a deeper understanding of the lived experiences of women of color as they navigate the computing ecosystem and how these experiences influence their persistence in computing

caskurlu et al conducted a study on faculty views on the alignment between cs programs and industry needs they found many faculty believe cs programs prepare students well for industry jobs citing the fact that the majority of their graduates have no problems finding high paying jobs however faculty also mentioned that soft skills are undertaught and that some topics such as software maintenance are hard if not impossible to teach a study by lewis surveyed faculty and undergraduates on their beliefs and attitudes about a variety of csrelated topics and then compared how students and faculty differed in their views topics included things like the importance of group work and the role of aptitude for success they found that although students and faculty disagree on many topics in general in some cases students later in their cs degree began to better align with faculty views than those earlier in their career but in other cases the disconnect persisted one such statement rejected by faculty but more accepted by the students was “in the real world computer scientists spend a lot of time working alone ” this finding is relevant to our study as it shows how faculty and students may disagree on basic assumptions about applications of cs even after interacting with these faculty during their undergraduate education these findings have been replicated and further explored focusing on areas where faculty and students disagree while the motivation for our study is primarily the gap between academia and industry we framed our interviews more broadly in particular we included interview questions concerning the goals of an undergraduate cs education in general and the preparation of students for careers in academia there are two reasons for this choice the first is that we wanted our interviews to allow us to identify other important aspects of a cs education besides preparation for industry the second is that we wanted to avoid potentially offending faculty by focusing solely on industryspecific interview questions that could cause them to take a defensive position compromising our data collection process our research questions are what are faculty views on the goals of an undergraduate cs education what are faculty views on the role of an undergraduate cs education in preparing students for industry what are faculty views on better preparing students for careers in industry

experimental setup and methodology the experimental evaluation is conducted using the german to english iwslt dataset alongside two additional corpora previously utilized by müller et al to assess domain robustness a curated selection of german to english data from opus lison and tiedemann and a low resource german to romansh scenario the study employs minimum risk training mrt och shen et al a training objective designed to inherently mitigate exposure bias empirical results indicate that mrt yields superior translation quality in out of domain settings compared to baseline approaches while simultaneously reducing the incidence of hallucination furthermore an analysis of translation uncertainty reveals that the maximum likelihood estimation mle baseline tends to overestimate the probability of random translations across all time steps except the initial ones mrt effectively alleviates this discrepancy finally the findings demonstrate that mrt mitigates issues associated with beam search decoding minimum risk training the prevailing training objective in neural machine translation nmt is the minimization of the negative log likelihood l theta over the training dataset mathcal d l theta sum x y in mathcal d sum t t log p y t mid x y t theta quad where x and y represent the source and target sequences respectively y t denotes the t th token in y and y t signifies the sequence of all preceding tokens mle is conventionally implemented using teacher forcing wherein y t consists of ground truth labels during training this creates a distributional mismatch relative to the inference phase where y t comprises model generated predictions minimum risk training mrt addresses this discrepancy through a sequence level objective specifically the mrt objective function minimizes the expected loss risk with respect to the posterior distribution r theta sum x y in mathcal d sum tilde y in mathcal y x p tilde y mid x theta delta tilde y y quad in this formulation the loss function delta tilde y y quantifies the discrepancy between the reference translation y and the model s predicted sequence tilde y

knowledge distillation knowledge distillation has emerged as a prominent technique for enhancing the performance of compact models by leveraging the guidance of larger more complex counterparts this methodology has been extensively applied across diverse domains including natural language processing kim rush kuncoro et al tan et al clark et al sun et al computer vision ba caruana and speech recognition huang et al in the context of simple classification tasks significant research has been conducted on applications such as sentiment analysis clark et al image recognition hinton et al and cross lingual text classification xu yang regarding structured prediction problems existing literature addresses neural machine translation kim rush tan et al connectionist temporal classification within speech recognition huang et al and dependency parsing kuncoro et al liu et al recent scholarship has increasingly focused on distilling large bert architectures into smaller variants for instance tsai et al successfully distilled a large multilingual bert mbert model into a three layer counterpart for sequence labeling achieving competitive accuracy alongside substantial speed improvements similarly jiao et al introduced tinybert for natural language understanding tasks furthermore sanh et al proposed a distilled bert variant that operates faster while retaining of the performance of the original large scale model discussion on flair mbert fine tuning prior research has empirically investigated two primary strategies for adapting monolingual pre trained embedding models to downstream tasks utilizing the models as fixed feature extractors or fine tuning them directly on the target tasks peters et al these studies generally indicate that both approaches yield comparable performance in most scenarios however wu and dredze observed that fine tuning mbert with the bottom layers fixed can yield additional performance gains in multilingual settings in the present study we adopt the first approach utilizing pre trained embeddings as fixed feature extractors this decision is driven by the computational inefficiency of fine tuning flair mbert models which poses a significant bottleneck for the large scale experimental design required for our multilingual knowledge distillation framework

gustav kirchhoff formulated two fundamental laws in the mid th century that remain essential for the analysis of electric circuits among the applications of these principles in purely resistive networks are the variable voltage divider commonly known as a potentiometer and the wheatstone bridge a potentiometer functions as a rheostat a device capable of varying electrical resistance in a typical configuration one terminal of the resistive element is grounded while the wiper and the opposing terminal are connected to a voltage source when integrated into a circuit such as one driving a loudspeaker the potentiometer s wiper adjusts the voltage supplied to the load by varying the resistance the potential difference across the speaker is modulated thereby controlling the audio volume in this context the lowercase variable v denotes the time varying voltage signal derived from the amplifier the total voltage is applied across the full resistance of the potentiometer with the load connected between the wiper and ground the position of the wiper determines the proportion of the voltage drop received by the load for instance a specific setting may deliver only percent of the total voltage resulting in low volume whereas an alternative setting increases this proportion to raise the volume the wheatstone bridge represents a specialized circuit configuration involving series and parallel resistor connections designed to measure minute resistance values such as those exhibited by strain gauges strain gauges are critical instruments in materials testing utilized to quantify the mechanical load applied to a material and the resulting strain a standard wheatstone bridge circuit comprises four resistive arms and a central meter with resistance r m in practical applications the resistance of one arm is unknown and the bridge is balanced to determine this value often corresponding to the strain measurement when the bridge is in a balanced state the current through the central meter i m is zero consequently the currents in the opposing branches are equal i i and i i and the potential difference across the meter is nullified the central meter typically a galvanometer within stem studies have shown that females and those from low socioeconomic status ses backgrounds have lower spatial abilities and not surprisingly they are underrepresented in many stem disciplines fortunately students can be taught how to improve their spatial skills which has been linked to improved retention within many stem majors there have been several studies within computer science classrooms suggesting that there is a link between a student’s spatial abilities and the student’s ability to learn to program this paper discuses our attempt to replicate one of those studies by cooper et al we pose the following research questions to frame our work is there a correlation between a student’s spatial skills and their success in learning to program can spatial skills successfully be taught to students taking introductory computing classes and do students who learn spatial skills also demonstrate improved performance in computing do students who have higher enjoyment with computing demonstrate higher performance in computing and spatial skills spatial skills have been shown to be important in success in many engineering disciplines and in science barker found that well developed spatial skills are essential to understand basic and structural chemistry carter et al ran a study with students enrolled in the first semester of a collegelevel general chemistry course where they found that spatial score is most strongly correlated to unit conversion calculations sorby found that a person’s spatial skills have a correlation between their ability to learn to use computer aided design software shea et al ran a twenty year long longitudinal study with thirteen year old children that scored at the top on the scholastic assessment test mathematics and scholastic assessment test verbal finding that the students’ spatial ability was also a good indicator in addition to their satm and satv in predicting educational outcomes over the years it makes sense that spatial skills would be important to engineering and chemistry students engineering and chemistry students typically deal with d and d objects and shapes regularly in their schooling it is less clear that there should be any correlation between spatial skills and students’ programming achievement

previous course iterations that allocated of time to coding furthermore students instructed via the spiral model demonstrated superior performance in subsequent courses compared to their peers taught through traditional methods these findings suggest that the spiral approach to computer science education may enhance topic retention thereby enabling curricula to either expand the scope of concepts covered or deepen the exploration of existing material while introductory computer science courses cs are ubiquitous across higher education institutions their specific objectives vary at colorado state university the primary aim of cs is to foster student success and retention by providing a gradual introduction to the discipline however state regulations currently restrict computer science coursework within the curriculum to majors in computer science related fields such as mathematics and physics or as electives feedback from students who enrolled in the course as an elective frequently indicated a preference for earlier enrollment this delay is largely attributed to the structure of the all university core curriculum aucc which comprises extensive lists of general education requirements students often utilize these early years to explore various majors through the aucc deferring elective selection until the final years of their academic careers consequently it not currently include computer science to align with these regulatory constraints the course curriculum was expanded to incorporate topics traditionally associated with the liberal arts such as philosophy and history simultaneously it remained imperative to preserve the course s accessibility and its efficacy in promoting student retention and success in advanced coursework to address the need for increased content without compromising student outcomes the course design was grounded in the principles of spaced retrieval from learning psychology

based on the inherent characteristics of these languages alpha is interpreted as an axis representing the spectrum of syntactic analysis and synthesis while the normalized sum of beta and gamma serves as the corresponding axis for morphological variation a high value of alpha indicates the presence of a small set of extremely frequent lexical items within the corpus empirical observations suggest that languages exhibiting a relatively large alpha such as those within the romance and germanic families typically rely on an abundance of prepositions particles and determiners to delineate syntactic roles conversely languages characterized by a smaller alpha including the slavic and uralic families tend to encode syntactic information through complex internal inflectional systems such as declension and conjugation notably bulgarian bg functioning as a highly analytic slavic language demonstrates a higher alpha value compared to other members of the slavic family in a separate dimension a large normalized beta gamma value signifies a substantial reduction in the frequency of low frequency vocabulary

in addition to the application data and technology architecture models it architects create specific views to communicate and ensure that the system meets the needs of various stakeholders an architecture is typically conveyed through one or more architecture models that collectively offer a clear description of the system’s structure a singular allencompassing model is often too intricate to be easily grasped displaying every intricate relationship among diverse business and technical elements just as an architect designs different aspects of a building such as wiring diagrams for electricians floor plans for owners and elevations for planners to address their unique needs the architecture of an information system is similarly broken down into various views for effective communication among its stakeholders in the it world for example an architect might create specific views of the system’s physical layout and security measures these tailored views ensure that stakeholders each with their own concerns and areas of focus have a clear understanding of the system’s components relevant to their interests after identifying business and technical characteristics through the diagrams discussed in the previous section solution models can be developed and implementations can be created this involves constructing new components as necessary and combining them with reusable design components obtained from a pattern catalog if implementations of these reusable components already exist and can be customized the implementation of the solution becomes much faster this approach helps avoid reinventing the wheel and developing software components or systems that already exist focusing instead on assembling existing components for efficiency illustrates the key dimensions for representing and categorizing architecture models accompanying diagrams and related patterns these architecture domains align with the togaf standard as discussed earlier the diagram also illustrates the levels of abstraction to characterize various architectural models additionally it introduces the architecture scope as another dimension classifying the models’ breadth of applicability at the enterprise portfolio or project level the architecture scope is the extent and boundaries within which architectural considerations decisions and solutions apply portfolioor domainlevel architectures usually concentrate on collections of solutions and projects associated with a specific business unit like marketing or sales in a large organization in contrast projector systemlevel architecture is geared toward individual solutions and projects within those business units defining the scope of a model is crucial because there exists a direct relationship between the scope and the level of detail achievable in a blueprint

scholarly efforts to analyze narrative construction have historically centered on several key dimensions the sequencing of events schank abelson chambers jurafsky the delineation of plot units mcintyre lapata goyal et al finlayson and the examination of structural frameworks lehnert rumelhart furthermore significant attention has been devoted to the roles of characters or personas within narratives black wilensky propp bamman et al valls vargas et al and the dynamics of their interrelationships elson et al agarwal et al srivastava et al despite this breadth of inquiry research into narrative summarization has remained relatively limited a constraint likely attributable to the scarcity of annotated datasets required for robust modeling and evaluation notable exceptions include kazantseva and szpakowicz who developed a method for summarizing short stories based on importance criteria—such as the presence of protagonist or location details—to assist readers in assessing interest without disclosing the plot similarly mihalcea and ceylan employed an unsupervised graph based approach to summarize books by operating over topical segments their algorithm generates individual segment summaries before synthesizing an overall summary through sentence aggregation in the domain of screenplays gorinski and lapata utilized a graph based methodology centered on principal characters to extract an optimal chain of scenes adopting a comparable strategy tsoneva et al generated video summaries for television episodes by ranking subscenes based on importance utilizing features derived from character graphs and textual cues found in subtitles and scripts more recently vicol et al introduced the moviegraphs dataset which leverages character centric graphs to characterize the content of movie video clips the present study synthesizes diverse strands of research concerning narrative structure analysis cutting hauge screenplay summarization gorinski lapata and neural network modeling dong specifically this work focuses on extractive summarization with the objective of identifying an optimal sequence of pivotal events within a narrative the ultimate aim is to produce concise summaries that effectively retell the plot of a story

abstract this paper presents a novel retrieval and rewriting framework designed to enhance the initialization of unsupervised machine translation umt models the proposed methodology involves retrieving semantically analogous sentences from monolingual corpora of two distinct languages followed by the application of a specialized rewriting model to minimize the semantic divergence between source sentences and their retrieved counterparts these refined sentence pairs serve as the foundation for initializing statistical machine translation smt models which subsequently generate pseudo parallel data to train neural machine translation nmt systems this process is integrated with iterative back translation empirical evaluations demonstrate that the proposed approach yields superior initial umt models resulting in a final translation performance improvement exceeding bleu points introduction recent advancements have established the efficacy of unsupervised machine translation umt artetxe et al lample et al artetxe et al b marie and fujita ren et al lample and conneau the prevailing paradigm entails the construction of two initial translation models source to target and target to source followed by iterative back translation sennrich et al a zhang et al utilizing pseudo data generated reciprocally by these models the initialization phase is critical suboptimal initialization may unduly constrain the search space while excessive noise introduced at this stage can significantly degrade final performance prior umt methodologies lample et al artetxe et al b marie and fujita ren et al typically rely on initialization strategies based on n gram embeddings these approaches first construct phrase translation tables leveraging unsupervised cross lingual n gram embeddings conneau et al artetxe et al a to initialize phrase based statistical machine translation pbsmt models koehn et al augmented by language models however this conventional approach presents two primary limitations complex syntactic structures inherent in the original training data are often difficult to reconstruct using n gram translation tables and the initial translation tables inevitably contain substantial noise which tends to be amplified during subsequent processing stages to address these challenges this study proposes a novel retrieval and rewriting initialization method for umt specifically the approach utilizes unsupervised cross lingual sentence embeddings to retrieve semantically similar sentence pairs from the monolingual corpora of the target languages

school administrators select educators who demonstrate a commitment to mastering new subject matter and a dedication to instruction these educators subsequently serve as primary advocates for raising awareness regarding computer science cs within their respective institutions the selected faculty members typically hold certifications in career and technical education mathematics or science with the majority possessing no prior experience in teaching cs student enrollment has been robust characterized by significant participation from underrepresented minorities urms consequently the final enrollment figures for the – academic year are projected to exceed the preliminary data provided the ecs professional development pd model is designed to cultivate specific instructional practices grounded in inquiry based learning and culturally relevant pedagogy throughout all pd sessions educators are organized into collaborative groups to foster team cohesion and community building while receiving preparation to deliver the six units of the ecs curriculum during these sessions teachers assume the role of learners engaging in ecs lessons within clusters while facilitators observe and provide targeted feedback the inquiry based processes experienced by teachers during pd mirror the pedagogical approaches they are expected to implement in their own classrooms the ecs pd framework is structured across multiple phases educators receive initial training for units and during a five day summer institute after which they begin instruction on these units in the fall quarterly meetings are conducted throughout the academic year to prepare teachers for the remaining four units in the subsequent summer teachers return for a second five day institute to revisit the inquiry and equity based frameworks of ecs as well as the foundational concepts of the first two units which serve as critical scaffolding for the course s problem solving focus the initial summer institute within the yearlong pd cycle functions as a pivotal mechanism for establishing community among educators an outcome highly valued by participants given that the ecsalabama initiative trains only one teacher per school participants do not have access to traditional in school peer support networks

methodology and data analysis of student discussion networks in this study a transaction t j where j in dots is defined as a specific message type posted by a student within a discussion forum d i these transactions are classified into seven distinct categories the dataset comprises discussion records capturing metadata including the source s j destination s k and relevant keywords for each interaction student discussion data encompassing identifiers names academic grades gender and message content were extracted from the canvas learning management system to facilitate analysis a network model was constructed using neoj a high performance scalable graph database within this framework nodes represent network entities students discussion forums or instructors while edges denote the relationships between these nodes the nature of each relationship is determined by the transaction type associated with the posted message the network topology was queried and analyzed using the cypher query language with results subsequently visualized concurrently forum content was subjected to supervised learning algorithms for classification to identify trending discussion topics these topics were further ranked by difficulty through sentiment analysis social network analysis sna metrics were applied to the resulting unweighted directed graph to derive the following inferences betweenness centrality cb this metric quantifies the frequency with which a student s i acts as an intermediary between two other students s j and s k thereby indicating the volume of information flow facilitated by that student within the network clustering coefficient cc this metric assesses the centrality of a node reflecting the degree of collaborative problem solving and teamwork among students degree centrality cd in the context of this social network this metric indicates node activity higher participation levels correlate with increased knowledge sharing and learning outcomes which are associated with higher academic grades furthermore the aggregation of commonly discussed keywords established a repository of trending topics sentiment analysis applied to messages containing these topics provides instructors with critical insights regarding student perspectives on course material the empirical results indicate a significant correlation between engagement and academic performance specifically students achieving top final scores grades of a a and a demonstrated on average a higher volume of discussion forum posts compared to their peers

the standard claim verification pipeline initiates with the retrieval of relevant evidence which is subsequently processed by a textual entailment model to assess the claim s veracity thorne et al however established fact checking platforms such as snopes extend beyond binary veracity determinations by investigating the contextual factors that contribute to a claim s perceived authenticity or falsity for instance such platforms may trace a claim s origin to a specific source such as nationalreport net revealing that the original assertion—while related to the query—carries a distinct semantic meaning in the cited example the original statement posited that facebook could cease to exist if they don t do something about their rising costs this initial assertion subsequently triggered the propagation of an inaccurate derivative claim across various websites and social media channels a process that may inadvertently enhance the perceived credibility of the misinformation among readers the concepts of data origins and causal derivations described above are formally modeled within database theory cheney et al and scientific workflow systems davidson and freire under the term data provenance we posit that modeling and analyzing the provenance of natural language claims is equally critical beyond simple attribution provenance analysis elucidates the background and context of claim generation the synthesis of various claim components and the temporal evolution of claims through the actions of different agents furthermore provenance offers explanatory power regarding public perception clarifying why specific claims are deemed credible or spurious based on their historical trajectory consequently determining a stance on a claim necessitates an examination of its entire evolutionary history and the contributing sources rather than solely its current iteration similarly understanding the influence networks surrounding a claim or its author requires a comprehensive analysis of the claim s origin and subsequent development

moreover these methods still exploit the hierarchy in a shallow manner thus ignoring the finegrained label correlation information that has proved to be more fruitful in our work in this paper we formulate the hierarchy as a directed graph and utilize prior probabilities of label dependencies to aggregate node information a hierarchyaware global model hiagm is proposed to enhance textual information with the label structural features it comprises a traditional text encoder for extracting textual information and a hierarchyaware structure encoder for modeling hierarchical label relations the hierarchyaware structure encoder could be either a treelstm or a hierarchygcn where hierarchical prior knowledge is integrated moreover these two structure encoders are bidirectionally calculated allowing them to capture label correlation information in both topdown and bottomup manners as a result hiagm is more robust than previous topdown models and is able to alleviate the problems caused by exposure bias and imbalanced data to aggregate text features and label structural features we present two variants of hiagm a multilabel attention model hiagmla and a text feature propagation model hiagmtp both variants extract hierarchyaware text features based on the structure encoders hiagmla extracts the inductive labelwise text features while hiagmtp generates hybrid information in a deductive manner specifically hiagmla updates the label embedding across the holistic hierarchy and then employs node outputs as the hierarchyaware label representations finally it conducts multilabel attention for labelaware text features on the other hand hiagmtp directly utilizes text features as the input of the structure encoder in a serial dataflow hence it propagates textual information throughout the overall hierarchy the hidden state of each node in the entire hierarchy represents the classspecific textual information the major contributions of this paper are • with the prior hierarchy knowledge we adopt typical structure encoders for modeling label dependencies in both topdown and bottomup manners which has not been investigated for hierarchical text classification • we propose a novel endtoend hierarchyaware global model hiagm we further present two variants for labelwise text features a hierarchyaware multilabel attention model hiagmla and a hierarchyaware text feature propagation model hiagmtp • we empirically demonstrate that both variants of hiagm achieve consistent improvements on various datasets when using different structure encoders

on the brink is a robot needing instructions to navigate a series of rooms each with colored tiles arrayed on a sixby six grid each color of tile is associated with a sequence of actions that the player specifies by placing cards on a control panel for instance the instructions associated with a blue tile may be to move forward and then move forward again while the instructions for a red tile may be to move forward only once the movement options involve moving forward turning degrees to the right or left or doing nothing all actions in a sequence must be executed before the next tile’s instructions are executed in more complex levels the additional actions are available including moving forward two steps moving backward making a long turn left or making a long turn right the last of which involved moving forward turning degrees and moving forward again the game has forty levels of increasing difficulty as advertised on the game box the game is intended to teach procedures and other computational ideas we built a scratch instantiation in which a sprite moved to various colored tiles and performed similar actions to the robot to move the sprite the player assigns moves and turns to custom procedures associated with specific colors on the board the board was designed using a colored backdrop in the scratch interface to check what color tile was being occupied a series of conditional statements was embedded in a foreverloop to continuously check what color the sprite was currently touching replicas of each board game level and a template for custom level creation were created the main challenge for students using the scratch shells was to populate and build procedures and to place procedures in conditionals three fifthgrade teachers from the same ruralserving school implemented the unit concurrently with each teacher doing their lesson and their class’s library visits on a different day of the week

however this approach proves suboptimal for sequence to sequence language generation tasks such tasks typically necessitate substantial task specific training data consequently fine tuning a masked language model mlm on large datasets often degrades its pre trained representations resulting in catastrophic forgetting zhu et al mccloskey cohen alternatively the fuse method utilizes pre trained mlm representations as auxiliary features during the training of a task specific model zhu et al while this strategy preserves the knowledge acquired during pre training when applied to grammatical error correction gec it fails to adapt the mlm to the specific requirements of the gec task or the distribution of erroneous inputs found in learner corpora this limitation may impede the gec model from fully leveraging the potential of the mlm given the limitations inherent in these two prevalent methodologies effectively harnessing the advantages of mlms for gec is more complex than anticipated this context motivates an investigation into the optimal integration of mlms within an encoder decoder encdec gec framework to the best of our knowledge this specific research question has not yet been addressed in this study we employ bert a widely adopted mlm qiu et al to evaluate three distinct integration strategies bertinit initializing an encdec gec model with pre trained bert weights following the methodology of lample and conneau bertfuse incorporating the output of a pre trained bert model as additional features within the encdec gec architecture zhu et al hybrid approach a novel method combining the strengths of the aforementioned strategies this involves fine tuning bert on the gec corpus prior to utilizing its outputs as auxiliary features in the gec model to implement this hybrid approach we examine two specific variants bertfuse mask further training the pre trained bert model directly on gec corpora bertfuse ged fine tuning the pre trained bert model via a grammatical error detection ged task in the bertfuse ged variant the objective is to enable the gec model to leverage both the robust representations derived from large scale general corpora via pre trained bert and the task specific information pertinent to gec which is induced through the gec training data

fine grained analysis of contextual embeddings probing for information regarding surrounding lexical units josef klafka department of psychology carnegie mellon university allyson ettinger department of linguistics university of chicago abstract while models utilizing contextual word embeddings have achieved state of the art performance across a diverse array of natural language processing nlp tasks the specific nature of the information these embeddings encode regarding their surrounding context remains insufficiently understood to address this gap we introduce a comprehensive suite of probing tasks designed to facilitate fine grained evaluation of contextual embeddings with respect to their encoding of information about neighboring words we apply these tasks to examine prominent contextual encoders including bert elmo and gpt our findings indicate that the tested information types are indeed encoded as contextual information across tokens often with near perfect recoverability however significant variation exists among encoders regarding the distribution of specific features to particular tokens the nuance of these distributions and the robustness of feature encoding relative to distance we discuss the implications of these results for understanding how different model architectures prioritize and process word level contextual information during the construction of token embeddings as well as the mechanisms underlying their potential failure modes introduction the field of natural language processing has recently witnessed substantial performance improvements attributable to the adoption of contextual word embeddings these high dimensional vectors incorporate information derived from the linguistic contexts of the words they represent models leveraging such embeddings have demonstrated state of the art efficacy in a wide spectrum of nlp applications ranging from question answering to natural language inference at the time of writing the vast majority of models featured on the superglue leaderboard wang et al incorporate contextual embeddings within their architectures most notably those based on bert devlin et al and transformer xl dai et al despite the demonstrable efficacy of integrating context into word representations there remains a paucity of knowledge concerning the precise information these embeddings encode regarding adjacent lexical items for instance in a sentence such as the lawyer questioned the judge it is unclear whether the contextual representation for the verb questioned reflects properties of the subject lawyer the object judge or both furthermore the determinants governing the specific information absorbed by a contextual embedding from its surrounding words remain undefined this paper addresses these inquiries by designing and implementing a suite of probing tasks to systematically test contextual embeddings for their retention of syntactic and semantic features pertaining to words within their immediate context

as computational resources and ambitions scale it would be interesting to add more tasks as well while retaining the twelve we have chosen here in order to continue to evaluate their success whilst extending the scope of the entire system all the subtasks in the collection we use here already exist other research projects have also built such collectionbased tasks before as well in particular the nlp decathlon from which the name of this paper is inspired collects together a diverse set of nlp tasks – from sentiment detection to parsing talmor and berant collect a set of qa datasets and build m ulti qa recently also similarly multitasked a large set of nlp tasks on an even bigger scale our work differs from these in that it is focused on dialogue tasks which naturally group together to form a conversational agent bert baseline we implement a generative baseline using bert via adapting the model using a standard autoregressive loss we concatenate both the context and current generation and provide these as input to the model using bert’s sentence embeddings to distinguish the roles in the network although bert is trained to predict masked tokens we find that finetuning can easily adjust its behavior to predicting the next token our bert baseline is roughly equivalent to the model of wolf et al but does not have a classification loss term the implementation relies on huggingface transformers we thus finetune this model for each of our tasks except image chat and igc which require images as input image seqseq we use a modification of a transformer seqseq architecture additionally adding image features to the encoder our model is a layer encoder layer decoder with dimensional embeddings and attention heads and is based on the parlai implementation we use bpe following humeau et al via lowercased wikipedia toronto books and open subtitles with k merges giving terms reported perplexities are computed with this dictionary

we note that that evolving teaching methods with agile or xp is out of this paper’s scope instead we examine methods for teaching agile xp the focus of our study is an approach that allows instructors to make informed design choices that benefit students and emphasize industrial practices we focus on projectbased learning as this is a widely used and effective teaching method for software development we inform our selection of agile processes using team process theories moe et al used a team process theory to observe a team transitioning to the scrum methodology they were able to connect observed barriers to success with the theory showing that team process theories could explain the failure and success of agile teams similarly lindsjørn et al borrowed ideas from teamwork theories to examine the success factors in agile software development they designed a survey based on a teamwork theory to measure teamwork quality and team success based on the survey data they showed that teamwork is a significant predictor of team performance the two studies showed that team theories could provide useful insights and measurements for agile teams in this study we choose marks et al ’s team process taxonomy we recognize that there are other successful team process theories but they do not contradict marks et al ’s taxonomy instead they present alternative perspectives that confirm and extend the taxonomy thus this taxonomy can serve our goal of selecting processes that are complete for working teams since the format of taxonomy gives us a straightforward way to define measurements for completeness pivotal is a usbased company that builds software for clients using the xp works with clients to transition their organizations to xp and provides services and tools to support xp the company has collaborated with other software engineering researchers we observed pivotal teams of different maturity levels our goal was to observe processes that teams used in an effective workflow and classify those processes with a taxonomy the eventual goal of doing so was to identify a subset of processes for incorporating into our xp course according to the following patterns and a manual annotation of replies to check if they are responses that counter the original hate content thus all the material collected is made of natural real occurrences of hscn pairs crowdsourcing crowd qian et al propose that once a list of hs is collected from smps and manually annotated we can briefly instruct crowdworkers nonexpert to write possible responses to such hate
