foundation prior work on novice programmers’ selfregulation have shown it to be inconsistent and shallow but trainable through direct instruction however prior work has primarily studied selfregulation retrospectively which relies on students to remember how they regulated their process or in laboratory settings limiting the ecological validity of findings to address these limitations we investigated novice programmers’ selfregulation in situ over weeks we had them to keep journals about their work and later had them to reflect on their journaling through a series of qualitative analyses of journals and survey responses we found that all participants monitored their process and evaluated their work that few interpreted the problems they were solving or adapted prior solutions we also found that some students selfregulated their programming in many ways while others in almost none students reported many difficulties integrating reflection into their work some were completely unaware of their process some struggled to integrate reflection into their process and others found reflection conflicted with their work these results suggest that selfregulation during programming is highly variable in practice and that teaching selfregulation skills to improve programming outcomes may require differentiated instruction based on students selfawareness and existing programming practices while selfregulation is important for programming among novices it is infrequent and shallow prior work has used retrospective survey data to identify selfregulated learning behaviors such as time management goal setting and planning showing that novices rarely engage in them and when they do they often do so in shallow unsuccessful ways other studies have used interviews finding that low performing students use few metacognitive or resource management strategies overall and that this is often half of cs students

this subtask has been proposed in a recent work where they develop a targetfused sequence tagging method however the opinion detection heavily depends on the extracted aspect accuracy which suffers from error propagation our framework is the first to joint perform the two subtasks into an endtoend model moreover our method does not need any external lexicons or parsers and can effectively deal with multiple relations joint entity and relation extraction joint entity and relation extraction jere which aims to detect entity mentions and their semantic relations simultaneously in text is an important task in information extraction the earliest works mostly depend on feature engineering approaches in recent studies neural models for jere have shown superior performance moreover neural multitask learning has been shown effective in enhancing the interaction between entities and relations in this paper we adopt a jere paradigm to solve the paote task and develop a multitask framework by extending previous unified setups and endtoend spanbased models spanbased multitask framework problem definition given an input sentence s w w w n of n words the paote task is to extract a set of all the aspect terms at at at at i a set of all the opinion terms ot ot ot ot j and a set of all the at ot pairs p at m ot n from the sentence note that the at m ∈ at and the ot n ∈ ot could be a single word or a phrase inspired by jere methods we process the task in a spanbased termrelation joint extraction scheme rather than as a sequence tagging problem

al guo et al zhu and gupta this work adopts the implementation described by zhu and gupta wherein parameters within each layer are sorted by magnitude and an increasing proportion of weights is pruned experimental setup data preparation and metrics for the sexist tweets dataset the intersectional protected test sets iptts were generated in accordance with the methodology outlined by park et al conversely for the toxicity comments and jigsaw toxicity datasets we utilized the iptts released by dixon et al comprehensive details regarding the iptts generation process are provided in appendix c to evaluate model discrimination we employed the false positive error difference fped and false negative error difference fned metrics within the iptts framework where lower scores indicate superior equality however as noted in section these metrics alone are insufficient as models may achieve low fped and fned scores through trivial predictions consequently we also report the area under the curve auc for both the original test sets and the iptts to assess the trade off between debiasing efficacy and predictive accuracy furthermore statistical significance tests were conducted at a confidence level for the sexist tweets and jigsaw toxicity datasets baseline comparisons baseline comparisons were tailored to the specific demographic structures of each dataset for the sexist tweets dataset which comprises only two demographic groups male and female we compared our approach against the gender swapping method proposed by park et al a technique well suited for binary demographic swapping for the toxicity comments and jigsaw toxicity datasets which encompass distinct demographic groups we compared our results against the data supplementation strategy introduced by dixon et al weight generation and feature engineering to generate the requisite weights we utilized random forest classifiers to estimate p y z following algorithm the prior probability q y was set equal to p y to isolate the influence of the prior distribution of y these weights were subsequently applied as sample weights to the loss functions during both training and validation phases the construction of the sensitive attribute vector z varied by dataset sexist tweets we extracted gender identity words as defined by zhao et al from each sentence to serve as z toxicity comments we constructed z by concatenating the demographic group identity words as defined by dixon et al present in each sentence with the sentence length mirroring the feature selection used by dixon et al for data supplementation jigsaw toxicity we utilized the provided identity attributes for each sentence concatenated with the sentence length to form z

the lack of segmentation coupled with the realtime processing constraint makes it computationally prohibitive to discover alignments between speech and its automatic transcription for these reasons we cannot utilize standard approaches to multimodal learning which typically rely on nearperfect crossmodal alignments between short and welldefined segments context and relevance learning to label sequences of text is one of the more thoroughly explored topics in natural language processing in recent times neural networks are applied not only to sequential labeling like partofspeech tagging or named entity recognition but also to cast into a labeling framework otherwise nonsequential tasks such as syntactic parsing by contrast assigning labels to audio sequences of human speech is comparatively less charted out when addressed speech labeling typically adopts a solution by proxy which is to automatically transcribe speech into text and then apply a textonly model the challenge then becomes not to natively label speech but to adapt the model to adverse conditions of speech recognition error rates such models typically feature in endtoend applications such as dialogue state tracking recent advances in endtoend neural network learning offer promise to directly label linguistic categories from speech alone from another viewpoint multimodal learning is successfully applied to multimedia processing where the modalities such as text speech and video are closely aligned however contributions there typically feature classification tasks such as sentiment analysis and not finergrained multimedia sequence labeling our contributions we propose a novel neural architecture to incrementally label questions in speech by learning from its two modalities or views the native audio signal itself and its transcription into noisy text via asr our model utilizes the online temporal alignment between the input audio signal and its raw asr transcription

a comparative analysis of success rate statistics against those reported by taipalus and perälä as well as ahadi et al reveals that the current study demonstrates higher overall success rates and reduced variance this outcome may be attributed to the untimed nature of the exercises which permitted students to complete tasks at their own pace consistent with findings by ahadi et al certain concepts exhibited relatively low attempt rates ranging between and regarding methodology students were permitted to view expected outputs throughout the sql query construction process while this aligns with protocols in similar studies it diverges from real world scenarios where final query results are typically unknown consequently this feature may have artificially simplified specific tasks the analysis aggregates data based on a single primary concept identified for each exercise it is acknowledged that this approach may overlook difficulties stemming from foundational concepts or unique challenges arising from specific combinations of sql concepts the study was conducted within a ten week quarter long course utilizing mysql with limited coverage of database design topics such as the entity relationship model and normalization therefore the generalizability of these results to courses employing different relational database management systems rdbms or semester long curricula with expanded design topics remains uncertain furthermore while instructional materials and laboratory exercises were standardized across two instructors the study did not comprehensively account for potential variances in student learning attributable to differing pedagogical styles this research underscores the necessity of a multifaceted approach to assessing sql concept difficulty and student learning in in vivo settings the results indicate significantly lower success rate variance compared to prior in vitro studies to identify concepts that are difficult to master the success rate metric was augmented with attempt rates a measure previously utilized in the literature additionally this study introduces the analysis of attempt counts session counts and time spent per exercise metrics not previously examined in this context where applicable the difficult sql concepts identified herein align with previous findings specifically corroborating the results of taipalus et al and ahadi et al self joins correlated subqueries and equi joins were identified as particularly challenging concepts for students

different from previous works we are interested in the calibration of nmt models in inference given that the training and inference are discrepant for standard nmt models definitions of calibration neural machine translation training in machine translation task an nmt model f x → y maximizes the probability of a target sequence y y y t given a source sentence x x x s p y x θ t p y t y t x θ where θ is a set of model parameters and y t is a partial translation at each time step the model generates an output token of the highest probability based on the source sentence x and the partial translation y t the training objective is to minimize the negative loglikelihood loss on the training corpus inference nmt models are trained on the groundtruth data distribution teaching forcing while in inference the models generate target tokens based on previous model predictions which can be erroneous the traininginference discrepancy caused by teacher forcing in maximum likelihood estimation training equation is often referred to as exposure bias in this work we aim to investigate the calibration of nmt in inference which we believe can better reflect the generation capacity of nmt models calibration of nmt calibration requires that the probability a model assigns to a prediction i e confidence equals to the true correctness measure of the prediction i e accuracy modern neural networks have been found to be miscalibrated in the direction of overestimation in this study we revisit the calibration problem in nmt if an nmt model is wellcalibrated the gap between the confidence of the generated tokens and the accuracy of them will be small expected calibration error ece ece is a commonlyused metric to evaluate the miscalibration which measures the difference in expectation between confidence and accuracy

various metrics have been developed to assess specific dimensions of summarization quality including content selection nenkova and passonneau and relevance prediction daume iii and marcu the concept of evaluating summaries based on their capacity to answer a set of questions is also well established mani et al similar to the present study eyal et al and scialom et al advance this line of inquiry by integrating neural network modules however the current work diverges from these studies in two significant respects first while the aforementioned works rely on cloze style questions generated by masking entities within the source document or reference summary this study employs a generative model to produce questions thereby enabling a substantially broader range of inquiries second questions are conditioned on the generated summary rather than the reference summary or source article this approach is more suitable for verifying the accuracy of the generated text whereas reliance on reference or source materials primarily measures content selection recently there has been a renewed interest in leveraging natural language understanding nlu models to evaluate the factuality of generated text goodrich et al utilize information extraction models to measure factual overlap however their approach is constrained by predefined schemas falke et al investigated the application of natural language inference nli models to assess the factual correctness of cnn dm summaries concluding that existing nli models are currently too brittle for reliable deployment in this context kryscinski et al b trained an nli based fact checking model using a dataset of factual inconsistencies constructed via noise heuristics in contrast the proposed question answering approach facilitates a finer grained analysis while nli operates at the sentence level the qags framework can generate multiple distinct questions regarding a single sentence conclusion this paper introduces a framework for the automatic detection of factual inconsistencies in conditionally generated texts and utilizes this framework to develop qags a metric designed to measure inconsistencies in abstractive summarization qags demonstrates a significantly stronger correlation with human judgments of factuality compared to standard automatic evaluation metrics for summarization and outperforms related nli based approaches to factual consistency checking furthermore qags offers inherent interpretability the questions and answers generated during the computation process explicitly identify which tokens within a summary are inconsistent and provide the rationale for such determinations the proposed framework is general in nature and its extension to other conditional text generation tasks such as image captioning and machine translation represents a promising avenue for future research

we use pretrained word embeddings from common crawl for the weights of this layer and we do not finetune them during training time encoder it is a bidirectional gru unit that takes as input a sequence of word vectors and computes a hidden vector h i per each i time step of the sequence attention layer we employ the attention variant of bahdanau et al and generate a context vector a i for each encoder output h i decoder to use the context a i and hidden h i vectors to predict the c l j l jk class of the hierarchy where j ∈ m m is the number of levels in the class taxonomy l j represents the jth layer of the hierarchy and l jk is the k th class in level l j similar to the encoder we use a bidirectional gru auxiliary task for an input sequence of words the model predicts a sequence of classes given the nature of recurrent neural networks iterating over a sequence stores historical information therefore for the last output computation we could take the previous inputs into consideration previous work in htc usually starts by predicting the most general category parent node and continues to a more specific class child nodes each time however by following the common approach the prediction of the most specific classes will have a smaller impact than the more general ones when the error propagates in this way it could be harder to learn the relationship of the last target class with the upper ones inspired by reversing the order of words in the input sequence we propose an auxiliary synthetic task that changes the order of the target class levels in the output sequence in other words we go upward from the child nodes to the parent with the proposed task the parent and child nodes will have a similar impact on the error propagation and the network could learn more robust representations classdefinition embeddings for external knowledge integration we analyze the potential of using textual definitions of classes for external knowledge integration

over the food to help keep the moisture from settling to the bottom of the food being dried the airflow can occur naturally due to density variations in the air caused by heating or induced by a fan the process of dehydration involves simultaneous mass and heat transfer as energy must be provided to the water in food to evaporate the water the process is often viewed as an adiabatic one in that energy just evaporates the water and does not heat the remaining food structure above ambient conditions in thinking about dehydration the importance of airflow led me to want to incorporate a fan mechanism of some kind into the design based on pictures of industrial models i selected three different rectangular devices all lined with mylar paper the first would be an open box approximately in high and in square the fruit was supported by in wire mesh that was suspended in from the bottom air was gently blown over the fruit bottom to top to prevent moisture accumulation on the bottom of fruit slices the samesize base was then covered with a pyramid plexiglas top in order to trap the sun s energy vent holes were drilled in the plexiglas to allow the air to escape last the base was covered with a mylar top to which the sides were open in the latter design very little direct sunlight would hit the fruit to discover which of these three designs for the dehydration of food is most effective all three were tested simultaneously initial testing indicated that little dehydration occurs without airflow so a in diameter fan used for room ventilation was employed the three boxes were training sachan and xing introduced a curriculum learning strategy enabling the qg model to progressively generate increasingly difficult questions for the qa model furthermore dhingra et al proposed a cloze style qag method for pretraining qa models while zhang and bansal suggested filtering low quality synthetic questions based on answer likelihood although the present study focuses on the answerable setting recent works have also addressed the unanswerable scenario zhu et al employed neural networks to transform answerable questions into unanswerable ones for semi supervised qa similarly alberti et al and dong et al converted generated questions into unanswerable forms using heuristics subsequently filtering or replacing corresponding answers based on expectation maximization em or f scores in the broader context of natural language understanding variational autoencoders vaes kingma welling have emerged as effective probabilistic generative models they have to facilitate an initial exploration of the parameter space the n gram order q was fixed at bigrams with inverse document frequency idf weighting applied all remaining parameters were initialized at their neutral baseline values alpha text bias p alpha text sim and text bias text sim preliminary analysis indicated that each individual parameter defines a performance function characterized by smoothness and a unique global maximum consequently it was hypothesized that the joint performance function across the five dimensional parameter space exhibits analogous properties optimal parameter configurations for each dataset were determined using a standard hill climbing algorithm while various n gram and spectral configurations were evaluated manually the results demonstrate consistent parameter behavior across paired training and test datasets this consistency is particularly pronounced in the msrvid and msrpar datasets though less distinct in the smteuroparl dataset furthermore the optimal parameters derived from the training sets for msrvid and msrpar closely aligned with those required for their respective test sets these findings suggest that the proposed parameter set effectively characterizes specific datasets for text similarity tasks regarding the asymmetry in referent selection posited by tversky the hypothesis was supported only at the text level within the msrvid datasets conversely the remaining datasets exhibited an opposing trend annotators tended to select the most salient document i e the longer text as the referent during pairwise comparisons notably the optimal parameter values for all datasets deviated significantly from the neutral baseline combination this confirms the utility of the proposed parameter set in tailoring the similarity function to specific datasets and tasks in summary this study introduces a novel parameterized similarity function for text comparison and a methodology for identifying optimal parameter values when training data is available while the parameter alpha grounded in tversky s similarity model proved effective in enhancing performance the empirical results did not substantiate tversky s hypothesis that humans preferentially select the object with lower stimulus salience shorter text length as the referent

defines the products services it offers the business model includes the organization location and process model as described in the following sections it may include additional models such as the financial model which will not be covered here to illustrate what a business model entails practically we will focus on a typical financial instruments trading capability which could be one of the capabilities in the overall business model of an organization operating in the global markets industry the organization location and process models are typically represented at a logical level of abstraction which is the most detailed representation for these types of models note again that layering and componentization heuristics play an important role in structuring these models the organizational model is the structure and design of an organization outlining how roles responsibilities and relationships are defined it provides a framework for understanding how various components within the organization interact and collaborate to support its functions in addition it offers clear definitions of roles and a matrix mapping of processes to roles role definitions encapsulate a set of functional capabilities essential for an individual to manage one or several distinct business processes as shown in it is common for an individual to fulfill multiple roles the process role matrix delineates which business roles are accountable for particular business processes typically structured at the elementary business process level this matrix can also be extended to more overarching levels when beneficial in general the role matrix is a visual representation or chart that outlines the various roles within an organization and their respective responsibilities it helps in clarifying and communicating the distribution of tasks and authorities among different positions or departments in a financial instruments trading business model various roles contribute to the overall functioning of the organization as shown in in addition to these titles there are two primary management layers upper management and systems management upper management is tasked with the supervision of various sectors within the organization encompassing numerous business units operations departments and other key areas

in the ct and networking topics improved as a result of the intervention this provides a contrast between our testbased assessments and student selfassessment for networking two questions specifically related to networking i understand what computer networking is and i use networked applications on a daily basis saw significant positive gains in selfefficacy p another question that asked if students recognized that they use network messaging applications daily also saw significant gains p this does not provide clarity between the disparities of what was taught and what was tested however it does point to a simplistic student understanding of networking concepts while further indicating a need for more applicable knowledge other key areas of growth included recognition of computer scientists and the importance of their professional work as well as distributed computing skills gained from working specifically on related projects in contrast we saw no growth among students for the following statements i am interested in computer science and i am interested in a career that involves computer programming these issues will have to be probed in greater detail in future work limitations of current study many topics that were covered during the camp were not tested due to the limited time that was allocated for these tests lists variables custom blocks events message passing in a more explicit way different types of blocks reporter command etc programming efficiency unicode binary and replay attacks are all areas that were not included in the tests this summer but could potentially be included in the future in addition we can include formative assessments with our units to facilitate selfassessment by the students thereby providing mechanisms for them to overcome their difficulties with certain concepts and become better learners for networking improvements could be made by introducing a visual representation of how the commands are passed from for example user to robot along with a better theoretical framing of the subject as opposed to the more implicit current structure key networking concepts such as message latency delivery failures types of networks and networking protocols could be introduced to students in an interesting way with the help of our robotics platform

theoretically the frequency of part of speech pos tagging errors tends to increase as an author s demographic characteristics diverge from the average profile of authors in the wall street journal wsj corpus from the s and s this phenomenon arises because many pos taggers were trained on this specific dataset introducing a selection bias in a distinct context sap et al identified error disparities in hate speech detection on social media observing that annotators commit more errors when evaluating posts by black individuals unlike the pos tagging scenario this disparity is not primarily attributable to a demographic mismatch between the author and the annotator populations rather it stems from annotators failure to adequately account for the authors racial backgrounds and associated sociolinguistic norms a fundamental assumption of the proposed framework is that disparity is contingent upon the population to which the model is applied this necessitates a distinction between the source population on which the model is trained and the target population for which the model is intended while cross validation over random folds typically involves training and testing on the same population practical deployment often involves applying models to novel data originating from different demographic groups consequently a disparity may manifest as an inherent property of a model in one application context but remain absent in another to quantify these disparities we propose utilizing established measures of distributional divergence or deviance specifically the log likelihood ratio is recommended as the primary metric d y hat y a log p y a log p hat y a in this formulation p y a represents the specified ideal distribution derived either empirically or theoretically while p hat y a denotes the distribution observed within the data for error disparity the ideal distribution is defined as uniform with hat y representing the error rate the kullback leibler kl divergence d kl serves as a secondary more scalable alternative this proposed metric synthesizes approaches utilized in prior literature addressing specific biases for instance the definition of outcome disparity presented here is analogous to metrics employed in the study of semantic bias

within scientific publications experiment describing sentences the annotation task is conceptualized as the identification of semantic frames fillmore representing sofc related experiments the scope of annotation encompasses four categories the introduction of novel content descriptions of specific prior work general knowledge typically found in textbooks or surveys and proposals for future research we posit that a semantic frame is introduced into the discourse by specific frame evoking elements although any part of speech may theoretically serve this function empirical observation indicates that annotators predominantly identified verbs—such as test perform and report —as frame evokers assigning them the label experiment consequently for the purposes of this study any sentence containing at least one such annotation is classified as an experiment describing sentence entity mention types a secondary annotation layer involves the marking of text spans with specific entity material which is utilized to annotate text spans referring to specific materials or chemical elements

unlikelihood training a generalized framework for improving dialogue generation unlikelihood training is a technique originally developed to mitigate repetitive patterns in language model completions by incorporating an auxiliary term into the optimization objective this method penalizes the probability of repetitive sequences thereby addressing the degenerative behaviors identified by holtzman et al beyond its initial application unlikelihood training represents a versatile framework capable of addressing broader challenges in generative modeling this work extends the unlikelihood paradigm to the domain of dialogue generation we first characterize the statistical properties of the training distribution by analyzing contextual repetitions intra utterance redundancies and vocabulary utilization subsequently we formulate specific loss functions designed to regulate these statistics demonstrating improved performance across multiple evaluation metrics furthermore we illustrate how the introductory computer science course cs enrolls approximately students during the fall semester and an additional in the spring while the majority of enrollees intend to major or minor in computer science the remaining population consists of students enrolling due to general interest or to satisfy general education requirements the course curriculum comprises eleven interactive codelab exercises twelve programming assignments automatically graded via autolab two written assignments two written midterms and a final written examination codelab assignments consist of short interactive programming tasks that allow unlimited attempts until the deadline in contrast autolab graded assignments require the development of more complex programs to meet specific problem specifications with a limited number of submission attempts permitted instructional support is provided through small group recitations led by learning assistants which focus on problem solving and interactive engagement additionally students have access to a community learning space staffed by lab assistants available to address inquiries observations from interviews with fourteen participants revealed that students engage with a diverse array of information sources while completing homework assignments these sources range from formal course materials such as lecture notes and assignment prompts to contextual cues within the programming environment including hint systems and color coding features although participants were not permitted to seek external assistance during the think aloud sessions they were queried regarding the resources they would utilize for various information needs and the appropriateness of such resources within the context of specific problems in response students identified learning assistants peers family members with programming experience and online forums as potential sources of support it is noted that the ability to navigate multiple information sources with varying degrees of credibility and convenience remains underdeveloped at this stage of the students academic careers the subsequent sections detail the information seeking behaviors observed during these interviews

an intersectional framework is essential for comprehending the experiences of underrepresented and marginalized populations who are frequently marginalized due to the historical and political legacies of racism gender discrimination identity politics and systemic privilege within the united states collins and bilge define intersectionality as a way of understanding and analyzing the complexity in the world in people and in human experiences noting that social and political conditions as well as individual identity are rarely shaped by a single factor but rather by multiple diverse and mutually influencing elements fundamentally this approach employs a multidimensional lens to examine how social constructs—including race gender socioeconomic class physical ability and sexual orientation—collectively shape an individual s self conception their perception of reality and their interactions with others although the specific terminology is relatively recent the conceptual origins of intersectionality are rooted in the th century coalitions of black women chicanas native american women afro brazilian women and other women of color who resisted social injustices that denied their humanity and civil rights the term intersectionality was formally introduced in kimberlé crenshaw s work within critical race theory to describe the practice of identifying and critiquing power structures that adversely affect black women followed by active resistance against those structures collins and bilge characterize this methodology as critical praxis while not a novel concept intersectionality has long been utilized across the social sciences and humanities as an analytical framework for studying indigenous populations in north america europe and the global south more recently given the significant underrepresentation of women of color including black hispanic latinx and native american individuals within the computing sector intersectionality has emerged as a critical tool for analyzing the lived experiences of these groups this framework facilitates a deeper understanding of how systemic factors influence their navigation of the computing ecosystem and impact their retention and persistence in the field

research to practice partnerships rpps are defined as long term mutualistic collaborations between practitioners and researchers intentionally structured to investigate practice related challenges and develop solutions aimed at improving district level outcomes these partnerships commence with the identification of problems pertinent to practice followed by a collaborative effort to formulate and implement solutions this process is predicated on a commitment to mutualism wherein all stakeholders share ownership in both the definition and execution of the work in the context of curriculum development and innovation it is imperative to acknowledge that educators often operate under constraints of limited resources and competing priorities dictated by their institutional environments consequently the mere provision of new curricula and professional development opportunities is insufficient sustained and substantial support is required effective collaboration must extend beyond isolated consulting agreements or single grant cycles furthermore successful interventions necessitate engagement across multiple tiers of the school system for instance while an intervention may be designed for classroom teachers and students its scalability depends critically on the buy in and active participation of instructional coaches and administrators to address these complexities this rpp employs a design based implementation research dbir approach to facilitate the implementation scaling and sustainability of stem ecosystems within the school dbir is an emerging methodology designed to build the capacity of entire systems to adopt and maintain innovations the stem ecosystem serves as a conceptual framework for analyzing the barriers and support structures influencing student learning trajectories in stem fields including computer science cs and computational thinking ct this model advocates for a holistic consideration of all elements within the school and the broader community that contribute to student engagement in these domains as illustrated in the associated graphical representation the model posits that effective cs ct learning is the product of the entire academic enterprise key components include school leadership educators students cs ct resources and available learning opportunities alongside prior experiences encouragement and specialized training for both teachers and students to flourish and realize their potential these ecosystem elements must function in coordination toward shared cs ct objectives given that all middle school students participate in mathematics science and language arts these core disciplines constitute the central foundation of the proposed stem ecosystem

this report outlines a methodological framework for assessing cognitive load and instructional efficacy in educational settings with a specific focus on integrating computational thinking into elementary curricula to evaluate the mental effort required by assessment items aligned with primary learning objectives the study employs paas point likert scale this instrument provides a reliable minimally intrusive mechanism for students to self report the cognitive resources expended during problem solving the scale ranges from very very low mental effort to very very high mental effort ideally data collection utilizing this metric should occur both prior to and following the implementation of revised course models to facilitate comparative analysis future research directions include a deeper investigation into knowledge transfer and variability with particular emphasis on subgoal structuring preliminary results indicated suboptimal performance on final examination items requiring far transfer despite prior exposure to similar constructs through midterms and worked examples consequently further inquiry is warranted regarding the correlation between student confidence and actual performance evidence of overconfidence suggests a need to enhance metacognitive instruction to better align self assessment with competency the core of this experience report details an approach for integrating computational thinking and coding within elementary schools by leveraging existing infrastructure specifically the school library and media center to complement classroom instruction this model utilizes unplugged pedagogical strategies employing tabletop board games as a familiar and motivating source domain by capitalizing on students prior knowledge of gaming mechanics the approach cues relevant concepts for core computational principles the proposed instructional model spans both classroom and library environments utilizing an expansive framing strategy to establish explicit linkages between the source domain board games and the target domain specially designed scratch project shells with partially completed code blocks this design ensures that reasoning activities across different contexts are perceived as instantiations of a unified conceptual framework the report presents case studies of three elementary school teachers implementing this unit in collaboration with school librarians additionally initial findings regarding the unit s impact on student interest n as measured by pre and post surveys are discussed the report concludes with lessons learned and recommendations for refining the unit for future implementation

our results provide a new justification for methods that reduce exposure bias even if they do not increase performance on indomain test sets they can increase model robustness to domain shift introduction neural machine translation nmt has advanced the state of the art in mt but is susceptible to domain shift koehn and knowles consider outofdomain translation one of the key challenges in nmt such translations may be fluent but completely unrelated to the input hallucinations and their misleading nature makes them particularly problematic we hypothesise that exposure bias a discrepancy between training and inference makes this problem worse specifically training with teacher forcing only exposes the model to gold history while previous predictions during inference may be erroneous thus the model trained with teacher forcing may overrely on previously predicted words which would exacerbate error propagation previous work has sought to reduce exposure bias in training however the relevance of error propagation is under debate wu et al argue that its role is overstated in literature and that linguistic features explain some of the accuracy drop at higher time steps previous work has established a link between domain shift and hallucination in nmt in this paper we will aim to also establish an empirical link between hallucination and exposure bias such a link will deepen our understanding of the hallucination problem but also has practical relevance e g to help predicting in which settings the use of sequencelevel objectives is likely to be helpful we further empirically confirm the link between exposure bias and the ‘beam search problem’ i e the fact that translation quality does not increase consistently with beam size

to summarize and clarify what educational strategies have been studied toward defining research needs moving forward we conducted a systematic literature review of research papers focusing on teaching accessibility and analyzed them for a number of factors including what kinds of courses accessibility is taught in what approaches were used to integrate accessibility what pedagogies were used which accessibilityrelated topics were covered and how learning objectives were assessed our goals were to understand the extent to which computing education research addresses how accessibility is included in current curricula and what opportunities exist for future research our findings suggest that computing education research has covered a specific category of topics across similar curricular integration strategies we also found that topics were reported to vary based on how accessibility was integrated into a given computing course we also found that the research reported that accessibility topics were more likely to be dropped when covered as an added topic rather than a core component further we found evidence in the literature that accessibility is disproportionately covered in a small number of elective courses as opposed to core courses and is not often evaluated for learning effectiveness thus our findings suggest that the research indicates that few have investigated the effectiveness of accessibility as part of core computing curriculum requirements based on these results computing education research should focus on developing effective strategies and materials to center accessibility in core curriculum given such a lack of inclusion as a core computing competency if computing faculty with limited accessibility expertise are inclined at all to include accessibility they may do so as an addition to a course thus there is a need to focus on ensuring that all topics particularly the commonly dropped topics can easily be integrated into a course and that there are good resources for instructors to learn and integrate accessibility into their courses we did a systematic literature review slr to learn how accessibility is taught in computing programs our goal was to analyze existing research that investigated the effectiveness of accessibility topics in computing courses to understand the direction of the field and determine future research opportunities we asked the following research questions rqs what courses cover accessibility topics what kind of accessibility concepts were covered and what were the accessibility learning objectives in these computing courses what pedagogies were used in teaching accessibility

the computer attitudes survey cas is a psychometric instrument designed to assess student attitudes toward knowledge acquisition and problem solving within the discipline of computer science the instrument is characterized by four primary design principles it elicits attitudes regarding the cs discipline broadly rather than focusing on specific courses pedagogical approaches or programming languages its items are formulated to ensure clarity conciseness and unambiguous interpretation and its categorical structure and subscales are derived empirically from student response data rather than being predetermined by researcher interests the development of the cas involved four iterative revisions between and the initial version adapted the classroom survey of student engagement class to a computing context cas version casv developed following think aloud interviews with faculty and students and piloted in fall with introductory students comprised likert scale items exploratory factor analysis efa of this version identified eight factors problem solving transfer problem solving strategies problem solving fixed mindset real world connections problem solving confidence problem solving sense making abstraction and personal interest enjoyment cas version casv was subsequently administered in a pre post format across three semesters at two universities yielding responses efa on this dataset revealed seven latent factors following confirmatory factor analysis cfa on these factors the instrument was further refined to produce the final iteration cas version casv developed by dorn and tew casv consists of items utilizing a point likert scale and measures five distinct factors problem solving transfer personal interest problem solving strategies real world connections and problem solving fixed mindset in the present study the casv was augmented with two additional sets of items first a subset of nine questions from wiebe et al was incorporated to assess attitudes related to gender issues it is noted that these items may introduce the potential for stereotype threat second five additional questions were added to probe student attitudes regarding the utility of computing these modifications were informed by findings from cooper et al who observed that standard attitude surveys often failed to fully capture insights from individual interviews—particularly among latina students—regarding the significance of gender equality and the perceived utility of computing

our weaklysupervised framework uses bootstrapping to update itself as new tweets are posted our experiments show that the system adapts to new topics in a social movement as well as new hijacking strategies maintaining strong performance over time social media has changed the way we live trade share news and engage in social activities twitter is one of the most popular social networks where users post short textual messages called “tweets ” a hashtag before a particular keyword or phrase in a tweet is used to categorize the tweet helping users find topics that are of interest to them one of the achievements of social media is reshaping and rescaling engagement in social movements via hashtag activism yang defines hashtag activism as large numbers of social media posts using a common hashtagged phrase with a social or political claim some popular hashtag activism movements include “ metoo ” a movement against sexual harassment and assault and “ blacklivesmatter ” which campaigns against violence and systemic racism towards african americans these hashtags help engage people in social movements by raising awareness on a larger scale and by giving opportunities for those with access limitations like the physically challenged to participate unfortunately hashtag activism is also a good target for spammers hashtag hijacking occurs when users “ a trending hashtag to promote topics that are substantially different from its recent context” or “to promote one’s own social media agenda” while the detection of spam tweets in general is an important issue the detection of spam related to social movements is of even greater importance because it targets excluded or marginalized groups we present a weaklysupervised bootstrapping framework to detect tweetlevel hashtag hijacking targeting specific social movements using a combination of features based on the tweet text use of other hashtags replies and user profile our experiments focus on metoo but our methodology can be applied to any hashtag prior work on hashtag hijacking has focused on general trending hashtags like job or android and could not adapt over time to attacker strategies these approaches were unable to account for changes in hashtag use over time ours is the first selfupdating approach to be developed for detecting hashtag hijacking at the tweet level our main contributions are as follows a new dataset of metoo tweets from october through may

perform better on both collaborative and cooperative type assessment than they do on individual type assessments also the results show that students perform similarly on collaborative and cooperative students prefer partner assessment to individual assessment computer science courses especially at the introductory level tend to weigh programming assignments as a significant portion of the final metrics established by budzianowski et al specifically measuring the frequency of correct entity provision inform rate the completeness of requested information fulfillment success rate and response fluency bleu score additionally a composite quality metric is reported calculated as text inform text success times text bleu following the methodology suggested by mehri et al implementation configuration the context encoder is implemented as a single layer bidirectional gated recurrent unit gru while the action and paraphrase decoders utilize standard gru architectures embedding and hidden layer dimensions are set to for the camrest dataset and for multiwoz the architecture incorporates both a copy mechanism and attention connections as illustrated in figure for response generation state of the art models specific to each dataset are employed the two stage copy net tscp lei et al for camrest and the domain aware multi decoder damd zhang et al for multiwoz model structures strictly follow the default configurations provided in the open source implementations of tscp and damd optimization is performed using the adam algorithm kingma ba with learning rates of for camrest and for multiwoz a learning rate decay strategy is applied halving the rate if the total loss on the development set fails to decrease over three consecutive epochs training terminates if to address inquiries regarding concepts and terminology the majority of instructors implemented a two stage examination protocol in this approach students were required to complete and submit a conceptual section on paper before receiving the subsequent programming instructions although this procedure introduced an additional administrative step no significant delays were observed and students demonstrated a seamless transition between exam sections following a year long investigation into the impact of examination formats on student performance within a cs course several recommendations and areas of concern are presented for instructional consideration comparative grade analysis indicated that the sole significant disparity between the two formats was likely attributable to lower completion rates on the initial exam utilizing the integrated development environment ide notably this reduction in completion rates did not persist in subsequent assessments consequently it is recommended that instructors utilizing ides for programming assessments carefully calibrate the difficulty level relative to student experience particularly at the beginning of the semester to mitigate potential overload the quantity or complexity of programming questions should be reduced early in the term with cognitive load incrementally increased as the semester progresses a primary concern entering the study was the potential impact of automated feedback on student performance the data revealed a significant reduction in syntax errors when using the ide however given the absence of a statistically significant difference in overall points earned syntax errors do not appear to be the primary determinant of performance even for novice students in conclusion there is no overwhelming evidence to suggest a superior outcome between ide based and paper based examinations absent contradictory findings from future longitudinal analyses instructors are advised to select the format that best aligns with their pedagogical preferences nevertheless several issues identified during this study warrant further investigation most significantly future research should aim to measure student learning outcomes beyond immediate exam performance while exam scores are a standard metric the alteration of exam formats may introduce confounding variables furthermore it remains possible that the act of taking an ide based exam potentially through real time feedback mechanisms may foster improved learning outcomes independent of the assessment results themselves

next to the noun this latter factor is measured by pmi this interpretation matches that of hahn et al who found separate contributions from pmi and a modelbased operationalization of subjectivity our interpretation is supported by the following points from the analysis above first among predictors based solely on wordforms the best accuracy is obtained by a combination of subjectivity and pmi second when we turn to estimates based on clusters two things happen the accuracy of pmi drops and the accuracy of information gain increases while the accuracy of subjectivity stays about the same this pattern of results suggests that pmi is measuring a factor that has more to do with specific wordforms while ig and subjectivity are measuring a factor that has more to do with semantic uncertainty about the noun or about the relationship between the adjective and the noun conclusion we examined a number of theoreticallymotivated predictors of adjective order in dependency treebank corpora of english we found that the predictors have comparable accuracy but that it is possible to identify two broad factors a semantic factor variously captured by subjectivity scores and information gain based on word clusters and a wordformbased factor captured by pmi this study provides a framework for evaluating further theories of adjective order and for evaluating the theories given here against new data from dependency treebanks generalizing to larger datasets of english is straightforward more excitingly we now have the opportunity to bring new languages into the fold the future research will extend the investigation of how instructional orientations influence academic performance beyond introductory computer science courses specifically examining advanced curricula such as cs and data structures while the impostor phenomenon ip is frequently cited as a significant challenge within the computer science community empirical data regarding its prevalence among computer science students remains limited although a survey conducted by the platform blind indicated that a substantial proportion of software engineers at major technology firms self reported feelings of impostor syndrome self reporting alone does not constitute a validated diagnostic method for identifying the impostor phenomenon to address this gap the present study employed the established clance impostor phenomenon scale to assess the prevalence of ip among undergraduate and graduate computer science students at a large research intensive north american institution within this cohort of over students were found to experience frequent feelings of the impostor phenomenon notably a higher proportion of female students reported frequent symptoms compared to their male counterparts furthermore the prevalence of ip within this computer science population was found to be higher than that observed in comparable studies of other student demographics given the detrimental effects associated with the impostor phenomenon it is imperative that computer science education initiatives prioritize enhancing student awareness and providing support mechanisms to help students manage these feelings originally defined as the experience of intellectual phoniness perceived by high achieving individuals ip is characterized by a persistent fear of being exposed as incompetent despite external evidence of success individuals experiencing ip often attribute their achievements to external factors such as luck networking timing or personal charm rather than their own ability while there is a prevailing belief that the impostor phenomenon is more prevalent in computer science than in other disciplines this hypothesis has not yet been confirmed through carefully controlled studies utilizing validated instruments although historical perspectives suggest that ip disproportionately affects underrepresented populations emerging evidence indicates that both underrepresented and represented groups are susceptible to these feelings

major international sporting events like the olympics and the world cup often capture global attention when they take place while many people believe that hosting these events offers significant benefits others argue that the preparations involved are not worth the rewards this essay will discuss both perspectives on one hand hosting an international sports tournament can result in a sudden boost to the local economy as it draws large crowds of sports fans from around the world with so many visitors hotels in the city and nearby areas are often fully booked and local businesses experience a surge in demand as they cater to tourists for instance the olympics typically fill up all available accommodations near the venues and cities can even profit by providing temporary lodging options however these economic opportunities often come with substantial costs to achieve these benefits the host country must invest significant resources into preparing for the event this includes building maintaining and upgrading venues and various related facilities to accommodate the influx of visitors since a high standard of readiness is expected failing to meet these expectations can damage the country‚äôs reputation additionally the host nation must manage various risks that could affect the event for example unforeseen events like a pandemic could lead to postponement or even cancellation in conclusion hosting international sports events can bring major developments to a country but these come at the cost of considerable investment and risk therefore governments need to carefully weigh the pros and cons before deciding to host such events

prior research on retention in cs has mostly examined factors such as prior exposure to programming and students’ personality characteristics which are outside the control of undergraduate instructors this study focuses on factors within the control of instructors namely instructional practices that directly impact students’ classroom experiences participants were recruited from sections of different courses over semesters a multilevel model tested the effects of individual and classaverage perceptions of cooperative learning and teacher directedness on the probability of subsequent enrollment in a cs course while controlling for students’ mastery of cs concepts and status as a cs major results indicated that students’ individual perceptions of instructional practices were not associated with retention but the average rating of cooperative learning within a course section was negatively associated with retention consistent with prior research greater mastery of cs concepts and considering or having declared a cs major were associated with a higher probability of taking a future cs courses implications for findings are discussed in recent years in the united states there has been an influx of interest and energy in growing computer science cs education across all educational levels from the csforall consortium in the united states to the increase in european countries that have added coding to the elementary and secondary school curriculum there is growing consensus that knowledge of cs concepts computational thinking skills and familiarity with coding will play an important role in future workforce readiness in order for the united states to meet future workforce demands for cs professionals it is important for tertiary cs programs to attract and retain students however at the collegiate level college stem disciplines have historically seen large proportions of students not completing the degree they initially pursue and cs is no exception a history of low retention and degree completion rates in cs have prompted research into factors contributing to retention because understanding these factors can help educators develop strategies to attract and retain students in cs programs for example researchers have found prior exposure to cs and programming personality characteristics motivation for a given course the learning environment and achievement in introductory cs courses are factors that predict students’ continued enrollment in cs courses

raising the price of car fuel is believed by some people to be an excellent method to solve the growing number of environmental issues however i strongly disagree with this idea as doing so would make life difficult for lower income households while making fuel more expensive wouldn‚äôt change the habits of wealthy families switching to fossil fuel alternatives could prove to be much more beneficial for the environment the most compelling reason against raising the cost of fuel is that the upper classes of society will hardly be affected by rising fuel costs as this only makes up a minor proportion of their expenditure they will continue to use their personal vehicles as they wish without a thought for the environment or the higher fuel prices even if their country has state of the art public transportation those who will feel the crunch of this decision are the middle class and lower income households since travelling is a major part of most people‚äôs routine be it to work school or shopping driving is almost unavoidable in many instances therefore raising the price of petrol would only serve to widen the gap between the rich and poor and serve as an inadvertent form of discrimination another reason why increasing the cost of fuel is far from the best way to deal with environmental pollution is that now there are much more viable alternatives due to the rapid advances in technology scientists and engineers have been able to develop several alternatives to fossil fuel such as cars powered by electricity or naturally produced biofuel the move to adopt clean energy sources instead of fossil fuels is a much more promising alternative compared to simply making fuel more expensive in conclusion i believe that governments of the world should invest more time and money in finding alternatives to fossil fuels that are more environmentally friendly rather than simply raising the cost of petrol

this study examined how four factors students’ perceptions of instructional practices classlevel indicators of instructional practices students’ status as a cs major and students’ mastery of course content are associated with the likelihood students will continue enrolling in cs courses through multilevel modeling these relationships were tested simultaneously across a large number of course sections that included courses from cs to seniorlevel courses findings from this study indicate that after controlling for students’ individual plans related to majoring in cs and their mastery of cs concepts the general instructional practices used in the course section do not predict retention additional research on other csspe cific instructional practices might help cs educators identify those practices that can encourage students to keep taking cs courses one of the contributions from this study is the use of multilevel modeling without multilevel modeling it would not be possible to analyze the relationships of courselevel variables across a set of courses and personlevel estimates would not be accurate had only the personlevel variables been used in this study the courselevel effect of cl would not have been detected in addition to investigating the impact of other instructional practices on retention future research should further study the role of content mastery and students’ majors on retention with content mastery more closely examining the aspects of core cs content that predicts retention and success in later classes could provide insight into how course topics should be timed reviewed and paired with other interventions designed to increase retention with students’ major considering the possibility of differential impacts of instructional practices on cs majors and noncs majors could provide insights into which practices are best suited for which groups of students with respect to retention if the relationships between instructional practices and retention are different for majors and nonmajors it would suggest that some practices are more suited for cs courses and others for later courses overall there is still much to be learned about how instructional practices are related to retention in cs this work was partially supported by the national science foundation active learning environments have only recently started to be analyzed in the cs discipline in terms of their effect on student performance

statistical analysis of pretest and posttest performance analysis of pretest scores revealed no statistically significant differences across the three semesters although the comparison between spring and fall approached significance p conversely posttest scores demonstrated significant variation specifically scores in both fall p and spring p were significantly higher than those in spring to evaluate individual learning gains dependent samples t tests were conducted for each semester results indicated a statistically significant increase from pretest to posttest across all three cohorts while the magnitude of improvement was greatest in spring the gains observed in the other two semesters were comparable with spring exhibiting the smallest yet still substantial increase impact of partnering and ability based grouping the data suggest that while baseline performance pretest remained consistent across semesters the implementation of partnering strategies in fall and spring yielded significantly higher posttest outcomes compared to the non partnered spring cohort these findings indicate that collaborative and cooperative learning models benefit students irrespective of their initial ability levels this conclusion is reinforced by the observation that students performed better on collaborative assessments even when controlling for grade point average gpa in this study partnerships were formed based on similar academic ability stratified by gpa given that gpa emerged as the strongest predictor of success across all four analytical models the results support the efficacy of ability based pairing over random assignment furthermore observational data suggest that collaborative assessments foster more engaging and active discussions compared to other formats learning styles and pedagogical implications the results indicate that students perform better in partnership settings regardless of their preferred active learning style however nuanced interactions were observed regarding learning styles there is preliminary evidence suggesting that active partners may be slightly less beneficial to neutral or reflective partners model conversely reflective partners appear to contribute most significantly to group outcomes models and notably when assessing individual performance no significant differences were found among reflective neutral and active learners pedagogically cooperative assessment may be particularly advantageous early in the semester when students have limited experience with partnering and are still establishing rapport with peers contribution to existing research this study contributes to the existing body of research on pair programming and collaborative learning by empirically validating the benefits of ability matched partnerships it extends current literature by demonstrating that collaborative structures enhance performance across diverse learning styles and by highlighting the specific dynamics between different learner types within a paired environment

others have been interested in the level of rigor and reporting in computing education research with efforts to document how research is conducted in computing education and uncovering the areas the field still needs to grow in addition there is a general lack of evaluation instruments with evidence of validity and reliability available for use within research in k computing education previous research has shown that studies and interventions are not well described missing key variables for understanding the populations studied and the intervention its researchers have also discovered weaknesses in the way statistics are reported and used such as underreporting of effect size considering statistical analysis and reporting of results the american psychology association apa what works clearinghouse wwc american education research association aera and consolidated standards of reporting trials consort among others have set recommendations and standards for reporting statistical data these standards bodies however go further than offering recommendations they actively promote and even require these standards for articles submitted to publication venues that they oversee other journals and publication venues have also required that these standards be met for publication they recognize that an adequate level of reporting of data is needed to compare studies to replicate and reproduce studies and to use in metaanalysis there is little evidence on the state of statistical data reporting in computing education research with the most recent example focused on a single venue as the csforall movement continues to grow in the united states and around the world determining the baseline of statistical reporting in recent studies can enable the creation of recommendations to be used in the field and increase its level of rigor thus we designed a study to compare a subset of reporting standards for statistical data in the aforementioned entities apa aera wwc and consort against k computing education research the overarching research question for this study was what statistical data is reported in k computing education research and how does that align with best practices from this we developed two research questions that are the focus of this study what are commonly recommended standards for reporting statistical data that are important to computing education research

the unsupervised training procedure for the proposed model operates via an iterative process that alternates between inferring latent labels through a dynamic programming algorithm and updating the classifier to maximize the likelihood of these inferred labels to accommodate step regions with durations exceeding a single timestep the classifier is extended to predict a background class the scores of which are integrated into the dynamic programming framework regarding related work richard et al employ a generative model with a structural architecture analogous to the present approach however their methodology relies on dataset statistics—such as average video length and step count—to learn structural distributions rather than optimizing parameters to maximize data likelihood similar to the current model region durations are modeled using a class conditional poisson distribution furthermore the feature distribution is derived via bayesian inversion of a discriminative classifier specifically a multilayer perceptron incorporating an estimated label prior in the original formulation structural parameters durations and class priors are estimated based on video length and the cardinality of possible step types notably the original model assumes knowledge of step occurrences during training to ensure a fair comparison this work adapts the model to align with the supervision constraints established by zhukov et al specifically by enforcing canonical step ordering during both training and evaluation phases experimental setting model performance is evaluated across various supervision conditions using the crosstask dataset the primary evaluation metric is action segmentation while prior research on this dataset zhukov et al has predominantly focused on step recognition—defined as the identification of individual timesteps corresponding to specific steps—performance metrics for this task are also reported for all models to facilitate comprehensive comparison supervision conditions in all experimental configurations the task associated with a given video and consequently the set of possible steps is known the variations across settings pertain to the availability of additional supervisory signals specifically timestep level step labels and constraints derived from language and step ordering models are trained on a designated training set and evaluated on a separate held out test set comprising distinct videos from the same tasks the specific supervision regimes are defined as follows supervised full timestep labels l t are provided
