the monitor component collects students’ terminal input and output on all machines in the exercise and collates it into a single log file per student the analyzer processes students’ log files and produces individual and summary reports showing students’ progress on the exercise tasks and highlighting common mistakes many public handson exercises specify tasks that require students to interact with a remote testbed environment often accessing multiple machines simultaneously using ssh and a terminal we focus on monitoring student interaction in this setting but note that our solution could be easily modified to monitor interaction in a local lab or home environment the monitor component of acsle consists of a program which records student input to the terminal the output that they see terminal identifier username current working directory and the time of each interaction we wanted to record student input and output to evaluate their progress on the exercise we needed to record time so we could evaluate how long the student spent on each part of the exercise the current working directory username distributed experiment and terminal identifiers provide context for the interaction especially when the machine may be shared by multiple users or host multiple simultaneous terminal sessions we investigated several tools to record the needed information script history snoopy and ttylog of these only ttylog could log both the input and the output of a terminal our first monitor prototype which was used to collect data for this paper combined ttylog and snoopy ttylog captures everything that comes from a serial device dev tty using strace and thus it successfully logs all terminal input and output snoopy intercepts all exec and execve calls on a linux system and logs the time user group current directory terminal identifier and executed command to syslog during analysis we matched logs for ttylog and snoopy to arrive at the final set of records for each student and each exercise our monitoring approach had some challenges and limitations our first challenge was how to reliably and accurately record terminal input and output ttylog logs every keystroke and output character this presents two general challenges reconstructing which command the user actually executed dealing with large terminal outputs we reconstruct commands by interpreting escape sequences such as backspace arrow key press control key combinations etc in the ttylog records we also truncate long outputs to lines

feature importance and interaction analysis existing literature on model interpretability includes several prominent approaches for probing black box models for instance li et al introduced the leave one out method which assesses model behavior by observing probability shifts in predicted classes upon the removal of specific tokens similarly lime ribeiro et al estimates local feature contributions through linear approximations derived from perturbed data instances a significant body of related research employs shapley value variants shapley to quantify feature importance notable examples include sampleshapley kononenko et al kernelshap lundberg and lee and l cshapley chen et al while these methods primarily generate word level explanations their development has largely focused on mitigating the computational complexity inherent in calculating exact shapley values datta et al in this study we draw inspiration from extensions of shapley values owen grabisch fujimoto et al to design a function capable of detecting feature interactions this function facilitates the construction of hierarchical model agnostic explanations as detailed in subsection distinct from prior applications of shapley values for feature importance evaluation we propose a more efficient and streamlined methodology for this purpose subsection empirical results presented in subsection demonstrate that our approach outperforms existing shapley based methods in the selection of salient words for explanation generation hierarchical explanations the limitations of word level explanations as discussed in section have motivated the development of phrase level and hierarchical interpretability frameworks tsang et al generated hierarchical explanations by exhaustively searching for interactions among all feature combinations however this approach incurs prohibitive computational costs singh et al proposed agglomerative contextual decomposition acd which utilizes contextual decomposition cd scores murdoch et al godin et al to evaluate feature importance and employs hierarchical clustering to aggregate features into coherent explanations furthermore jin et al identified formal limitations in cd and acd regarding the calculation of phrase interactions and introduced two algorithms that quantify the context independent importance of words and phrases a core component of our proposed method for feature interaction detection is the shapley interaction index owen grabisch fujimoto et al this work extends the index to effectively capture interactions within a hierarchical structure thereby addressing the challenges identified in previous hierarchical explanation models

abusive language detection is an essential task in our modern times multiple studies have reported this task in various languages because it is essential to validate methods in many different languages in this paper we address the automatic detection of abusive language for tweets in the urdu language the study introduces the first dataset of tweets in the urdu language annotated for offensive expressions and evaluates it by comparing several machine learning methods the twitter dataset contains tweets all manually annotated by human experts this research uses three text representation techniques two countbased feature vectors and the pretrained fasttext word embeddings the countbased features contain the character and word ngram while the pretrained fasttext model comprises word embeddings extracted from the urdu tweets dataset moreover this study uses four nonneural network models svm lr rf adaboost and two neural networks cnn lstm the study finding reveals that svm outperforms other classifiers and obtains the best results for any text representation character trigrams perform well with svm and get an of f score the bestperforming words ngrams are unigrams with svm which obtain f score the fasttext word embeddingsbased representation yields insignificant results abusive language detection is an alluring concept people use language to highlight depict elicit instruct and urge to inform the nuances of themselves and their worlds some use it for a good cause and some use it for spite impacts of abusive language are detrimental ranging from shortterm emotional reactions outrage dread selffault etc to longterm mental health effects low confidence misery etc causing psychological and medical problems rest issues migraine dietary issues etc according to the guardian report abusive language can change human behavior although several prevention and intervention strategies were introduced usage of abusive language on social media increased in recent years the task of abusive language detection is widely investigated in languages other than the english language some studies discussed linguistic aspects and linguistic resources in different languages such as arabic german japanese indonesian danish and portuguese although automatic abusive language detection is still in its earliest stage no study to date investigated abusive language detection with automatic manners on twitter in urdu a local language of pakistan having over million worldwide native speakers

jumelet et al investigate the information sources utilized by lstm based language models lms in resolving within sentence anaphora their findings indicate a significant male bias encoded within the network s weights whereas input word embeddings primarily influence the resolution of feminine pronouns in contrast the present study extends the analysis of anaphora to longer contexts ranging from token spans to entire documents and incorporates transformer based architectures the aforementioned research suggests that lms largely capture morphosyntactic properties related to anaphora however there is limited evidence that these models effectively conceptualize entities as the referents of nominal elements or that they can track such entities across a discourse for instance parvez et al demonstrate that lstm based models yield poor performance on texts with a high density of entities while paperno shows their inability to predict final words in text fragments requiring context from an entire passage particularly when the data consists predominantly of nominal elements consequently several models henaff et al yang et al ji et al have been developed as augmentations to rnn based lms to improve entity handling operating under the implicit assumption that standard models perform inadequately in this regard although aina et al achieved strong results on an entity linking task they noted that the network failed to acquire robust entity representations regarding transformer based architectures recent studies suggest they provide contextualized representations that are comparable to or superior to those of lstm lms while also better encapsulating syntactic information goldberg wolf conversely van schijndel et al report that large transformer models perform on par with or even worse than smaller lstms on tasks involving number agreement and coordination furthermore similar to lstms transformers exhibit a decline in agreement accuracy as the distance between the subject and its verb increases finally the most recent scholarship analyzing linguistic phenomena in neural networks has predominantly focused on bert tenney et al clark et al reif et al broscheit

the replacement operator identifies a candidate vocabulary denoted as w t text replace by selecting the top k words based on the product of the language model s forward and backward probabilities surrounding the target position specifically this involves evaluating the likelihood of the candidate word w given the preceding context and the subsequent context a comparable procedure is employed to compute the vocabulary for word insertion w t text insert with minor adjustments to the positional indexing of w further elaboration on this similarity is omitted for brevity in the experimental setup the parameter k is fixed at copy mechanism empirical observations indicate that named entities and low frequency words are frequently deleted or substituted during stochastic sampling rendering them difficult to recover due to their inherently low probabilities within the language model to address this we introduce a copy mechanism for stochastic sampling drawing inspiration from sequence to sequence learning frameworks gu et al this mechanism permits the candidate sentence generator to incorporate words directly from the original input sentence x during replacement and insertion operations effectively this expands the top k sampling vocabulary to include the tokens present in x defined as tilde w t text op w t text op cup w dots w l quad where text op in text replace text insert consequently tilde w t text op constitutes the effective vocabulary from which the stochastic sampling process selects the word w although this approach constrains the proposal space it demonstrates robust empirical performance this is attributed to the fact that lower ranked candidate words outside this expanded set are typically either semantically irrelevant or result in disfluent sentences such candidates generally yield low objective scores and are likely to be rejected even if sampled overall optimization process the proposed upsa algorithm is summarized in algorithm given an input sentence x the algorithm explores the sentence space to maximize the objective function f x which balances semantic preservation expression diversity and linguistic fluency the process initializes with x at each iteration a search action—specifically word insertion deletion or replacement—is randomly selected at a specific position k line if the selected action is insertion or replacement a candidate word is proposed to construct a paraphrased sentence x line subsequently an acceptance probability p text accept is calculated based on the improvement in the objective function f and a temperature parameter t line

the unverified model error rate denotes the proportion of instances in which the model s prediction diverges from the annotator s target label yet the correctness of the example remains unconfirmed conversely the verified model error rate represents the proportion of model errors derived from instance pairs where the ground truth label has been corroborated by additional annotators error rate serves as a critical metric for assessing model performance assuming consistent annotator quality and contextual difficulty a lower error rate indicates superior model capability our analysis indicates a general decline in model error rates across successive rounds in round which incorporated a more diverse array of contexts from various domains the aggregate error rate exhibited a marginal increase relative to the preceding round however within the subset of wikipedia contexts the error rate decreased substantially while approximately one in five examples constituted a verified model error in the initial round this figure diminished rapidly over subsequent iterations resulting in an overall verified model error rate of less than one in ten this trajectory underscores significant progress achieved within three rounds simultaneously it highlights the remaining challenges as even untrained annotators can relatively easily generate adversarial examples that confound ensembles of state of the art models table further details the average number of attempts tries required per context to identify a model error or until the maximum attempt limit was reached and the corresponding average duration in seconds these metrics provide additional insight into model robustness the observed increase in both the average number of tries and the time required per verified error in later rounds suggests a progressive escalation in task difficulty additional dataset statistics and inter annotator agreement metrics are provided in appendix c table presents the primary experimental results in addition to bert devlin et al and roberta liu et al b we include xlnet yang et al to represent a distinct yet robust model architecture the reported performance encompasses test set results for the anli dataset per round the aggregate anli test set and the exclusive test subset comprising examples generated by dedicated test set workers furthermore accuracy scores on the snli test set and the mnli development set are included to facilitate comparisons across different model configurations the following section discusses these observations in detail

theoretically under optimal learning conditions for both primal and dual tasks a complete transformation cycle—mapping data from the original space to an intermediate representation and subsequently reconstructing it to the original space—should yield an output identical to the input this reconstruction fidelity represents the fundamental objective of the dual problem formulation in the specific context of this study generating sentences from semantic representations x via function f and subsequently recovering the original semantics perfectly via function g ensures that the generated sentences are strictly grounded in the source semantics this requirement is mathematically defined by the identity condition g f x equiv x consequently the primary objective is to achieve perfect cyclic reconstruction through the joint training of the dual models f and g algorithm description as depicted in figure the proposed framework comprises two distinct components the primal cycle and the dual cycle

we posit that while language modeling pretraining is theoretically orthogonal to multi task learning mtl swayamdipta et al their practical benefits frequently exhibit significant overlap to disentangle these factors our analysis proceeds in two stages first we utilize glove a non contextualized representation to investigate task relatedness subsequently we employ bert to evaluate the extent of performance gains achievable through mtl when leveraging strong contextualized representations as illustrated in table glove tasks demonstrate varying degrees of utility for one another specifically our findings indicate that for openie and orl mtl incorporating semantic role labeling srl yields substantial performance improvements whereas other auxiliary tasks result in marginal or negligible gains and dependency parsing and srl function as generic source tasks that confer benefits across the majority of target tasks the unified spanrel framework facilitates the implementation of mtl and aids in the identification of optimal source tasks furthermore our framework serves as a robust platform for analyzing the similarities and divergences among distinct tasks drawing upon the premise that attention coefficients reflect a model s internal focus li et al vig clark et al we hypothesize that the degree of similarity or dissimilarity in attention mechanisms correlates with task similarity and consequently with the efficacy of mtl

practical coursework frequently involves the development of code however it more commonly entails the utilization of existing tools and programs to comprehend configure and monitor applications hosts and network infrastructure as well as to diagnose and resolve issues these exercises serve to reinforce theoretical concepts presented in lectures while simultaneously familiarizing students with industry standard system utilities although the primary objective of such hands on activities is to demonstrate solidify and deepen conceptual understanding their successful completion is contingent upon a student s proficiency with the requisite tools and environments for instance students may be required to demonstrate competency in linux operations ssh and scp utilities bash scripting and process management these exercises may be hosted either within local laboratory environments or on public testbeds in both scenarios students must acquire specific knowledge regarding the hosting environment including access protocols and file storage conventions a deficiency in either domain—conceptual understanding or technical proficiency—can significantly impede progress and hinder the learning process furthermore as these exercises are often assigned as independent homework students may have limited opportunities to seek assistance additionally some students may be reluctant to request help due to the perception that they are uniquely struggling or that admitting difficulties reflects poorly on their capabilities consequently instructors often face challenges in assessing student progress prior to submission and grading rendering timely intervention impossible ideally educators should possess the capability to monitor student progress in real time such monitoring would facilitate a range of pedagogical interventions including encouraging early initiation of work identifying struggling students to provide targeted and timely support modifying or clarifying tasks that present widespread difficulties and identifying and addressing common error patterns while learning management systems lms and autograding platforms can address certain aspects of these goals none have been specifically adapted for hands on exercises requiring terminal based interaction to address this gap we have developed a system known as acsle as illustrated in its architectural design acsle automatically monitors and analyzes student progress within linux based environments during structured terminal centric exercises the system comprises two primary components the monitor and the analyzer

contributions the primary contributions of this study are threefold proposal of a novel machine translation mt paradigm we introduce a new mt task framework that relies exclusively on a ground truth bilingual dictionary and monolingual corpora thereby eliminating the dependency on parallel sentence pairs development of the anchor translation at methodology we propose at as a solution to the aforementioned task this approach leverages the bilingual dictionary to establish semantic anchors which serve to align the monolingual embedding spaces of both languages thereby facilitating the translation process comprehensive empirical evaluation extensive experiments across diverse language pairs demonstrate that at particularly the biview at variant significantly outperforms existing baselines including word by word translation unsupervised mt and cross lingual embedding transformation methods notably at and biview at exhibit superior performance on distant language pairs where conventional unsupervised mt approaches have proven ineffective related work in prior literature bilingual dictionaries have predominantly been utilized for bilingual lexicon induction bli the standard bli workflow involves independently learning word embeddings for each language using monolingual corpora followed by the derivation of a transformation matrix between the two embedding spaces this transformation is typically optimized by minimizing the squared euclidean distances between word pairs present in the dictionary mikolov et al artetxe et al subsequent research has sought to refine this transformation through the introduction of novel training objectives constraints and normalization techniques xing et al lazaridou et al zhang et al artetxe et al smith et al faruqui and dyer lu et al additionally bilingual dictionaries have been employed in supervised neural machine translation nmt a paradigm that necessitates large scale parallel corpora arthur et al zhang and zong to the best of our knowledge this work represents the first application of a bilingual dictionary to the mt task without the utilization of any parallel sentences our research is closely related to the field of unsupervised neural machine translation unmt artetxe et al c lample et al b yang et al sun et al which similarly operates without parallel data however a key distinction lies in the handling of lexical resources unmt approaches often rely on artificial dictionaries generated via unsupervised bli for initialization artetxe et al c lample et al a or dispense with dictionaries entirely by employing joint byte pair encoding bpe to share subword units across languages lample et al b in contrast our methodology utilizes a ground truth dictionary and integrates it throughout a novel training process

discussion although certain alternative approaches demonstrate superior accuracy on the hans benchmark particularly regarding generalization to constituent and subsequence cases see table a they are not directly comparable to the present study the primary objective of this work is to evaluate the impact of syntactically challenging examples within the training set on bert s natural language inference nli performance strictly without modifying the underlying model architecture or the training procedure our most effective strategy involved augmenting the mnli training set with a limited number of instances generated via subject object inversion this approach yielded significant generalization capabilities extending not only to the hans challenge set but also to distinct syntactic constructions such as relative clauses and prepositional phrases these findings support the missed connection hypothesis suggesting that minimal augmentation with a specific construction can induce abstract syntactic sensitivity rather than merely inoculating the model against failure on a specific challenge set by exposing it to a similar distribution liu et al conversely the inversion transformation did not fully mitigate reliance on heuristics notably model performance remained suboptimal on passive sentences this suggests that for such constructions bert s pretraining may not have established sufficiently robust syntactic representations to be activated by minor augmentation this observation aligns with the representational inadequacy hypothesis which posits that pretrained bert functioning primarily as a word prediction model struggles with passive structures and may require task specific learning of these properties addressing this deficiency would likely necessitate a substantially larger volume of augmentation examples furthermore the optimal augmentation strategy involved generating premise hypothesis pairs from single source sentences thereby eliminating dependency on existing nli corpora this methodology facilitates the generation of augmentation data from any corpus enabling the investigation of the efficacy of large scale augmentation sets however it is important to note that introducing augmentation data from disparate domains may adversely affect performance on the original mnli task ultimately the development of models possessing a strong inductive bias for syntactic processing across language understanding tasks remains a critical goal such models should maintain syntactic awareness even when heuristic overlaps yield high training accuracy reflecting the fundamental role of syntax in human language comprehension

query classification and complexity metrics query types are categorized based on their functional objectives binary inquiries e g isempty retrieval of item lists e g projection followed by done cardinality assessments e g count and aggregation operations e g sum avg the result type is determined by the entity types contained within the result set for example a query retrieving directors born in france yields a result type of person constraints function as filters applied to specific entity attributes such as restricting the birth place attribute to france entity types identify the specific entities involved in the query which are typically integrated via join operations e g combining movie and person entities through a cast table aggregation types encompass reduction operations including min max calculations set operations on relational groups and group by clauses to classify these query trees ots a complexity score is employed analogous to the methodology proposed by yu et al this metric is derived from the number of constituent components within the query tree specifically an increase in the frequency of join group by aggregation or filtering operations results in a higher complexity score consistent with yu et al queries are stratified into four distinct categories easy medium hard and extra hard corpus construction methodology the conventional approach to constructing a corpus comprising natural language nl questions and their corresponding ot queries involves a two stage process first collecting a set of nl questions and second generating the corresponding ot queries however this methodology is labor intensive and presents significant limitations primarily nl questions often possess a narrow scope failing to comprehensively cover the full spectrum of entity types attributes and relationships inherent in the database schema furthermore the translation of nl questions into ot queries necessitates advanced sql proficiency and a robust verification mechanism to ensure semantic alignment between the natural language query and the formal statement consequently this study adopts an inverted process to address these challenges

nevertheless the spearman correlation between morphological complexity and average accuracy is when german is included rising to when it is excluded given that the training data volume per language and the domain remain constant this discrepancy suggests the significance of explicit linguistic cues that facilitate structural learning by models for instance feature f is assigned a value of if the world atlas of language structures wals indicates the presence of negative morphemes in a language and otherwise while further evaluation across a broader range of language varieties is necessary to confirm the robustness of this trend the finding aligns with the results of ravfogel et al in their study which compared english to a synthetic variant augmented with case markers the addition of such markers was shown to enhance lstm accuracy in agreement prediction tasks conversely mbert exhibits an opposing trend table when calculating the average accuracy across all stimulus types for which scores are available for every language—specifically excluding long vp coordination and within an object relative clause—a correlation of rho is observed this indicates that accuracy tends to decline as morphological complexity increases this unexpected inverse correlation may be an artifact of mbert s limited vocabulary particularly regarding non latin scripts morphologically complex languages inherently possess a higher number of unique word types although bert mitigates this issue to some degree by segmenting words into subword units the efficacy of this strategy is likely constrained in languages featuring non concatenative morphology such as hebrew finally it is important to note that the exclusion of specific stimulus types and the variation in training data volume per language serve as confounding variables thereby complicating direct comparisons between mbert and lstm architectures conclusions in this study we introduced the clams dataset for the cross linguistic syntactic evaluation of word prediction models and utilized it to assess both monolingual and multilingual implementations of lstms and bert the experimental design adhering to the conditions established by marvin and linzen and our cross linguistic replications effectively rules out the possibility of models relying on training data memorization or statistical token collocations consequently our findings demonstrate that lstm language models can distinguish between grammatical and ungrammatical subject verb agreement dependencies with considerable overall accuracy across diverse languages although performance declines on specific constructions notably center embedded clauses

this study employs the twitter spam analysis framework developed by sedhai and sun to identify a set of hashtags frequently associated with spam content utilizing the twitter api we retrieved tweets containing the metoo hashtag alongside at least one of these identified spam indicators following the removal of duplicates—specifically tweets containing multiple spam related hashtags—the resulting dataset comprised tweets identified as likely instances of hashtag hijacking it is important to distinguish between general hashtag hijacking and spam based hijacking while hijacking broadly refers to the use of a hashtag to increase visibility for unrelated topics a spam tweet utilizing metoo is by definition a form of hijacking consequently while the collected spam tweets represent a probable subset of hijacked metoo content they may not constitute a fully representative sample of all hijacking instances from this cohort of tweets were randomly selected for the test set with the remainder allocated for training and validation purposes to establish a baseline of non hijacked content we collected metoo tweets for each month spanning october the inception of the online movement through november after excluding retweets and replies this process yielded a total of tweets given the prevalence of genuine usage compared to hijacking this collection is presumed to primarily consist of authentic metoo discourse reflecting the hashtag s evolution over its lifespan from this pool we randomly sampled tweets for training and validation and for testing ensuring an even distribution across the month period these samples were merged with the previously identified potentially hijacked tweets and duplicates were removed to form a seed dataset of tweets for training and validation and tweets for testing this composition is designed to approximate a distribution of hijacked and non hijacked instances finally the collected tweets were labeled via crowdsourcing on amazon mechanical turk amt although the metoo tag possessed a distinct meaning prior to the movement the profound cultural impact of the campaign post october leads to the assumption that all users employing the hashtag after this date were aware of its contemporary significance

established workflows of consumer protection agencies and to establish a continuous improvement pipeline for the model leveraging manual annotations and expert corrections this approach is intended to locally normalized parsers typically depend on either tree decompositions or linearization strategies from the perspective of decomposition the probability distribution over trees is factorized often at the level of individual spans for instance teng and zhang propose a model that predicts probabilities for each candidate span while this approach yields promising parsing results the reliance on simple local probability factorization suggests potential for further optimization conversely the linearization perspective involves transforming structured trees into shallow sequences recent work by shen et al exemplifies this by encoding a tree as a sequence of integers representing the syntactic distance between adjacent words specifically the height of their lowest common ancestor similar methodologies have been employed by vinyals et al choe and charniak and in various transition based systems cross and huang liu and zhang a although tree linearization can accelerate training to o n it often obscures the direct relationship with original tree spans thereby complicating the utilization of span based features and supervision signals in this work we introduce a novel linearization of constituent trees that is intrinsically linked to their span representations given a sentence w and its corresponding parse tree t we assign a parsing target d i to each split point following the word w i where d i i denotes the longest span ending at index i within t we demonstrate that for a binary parse tree the set d i i encompasses all left child spans in t rendering the linearization sufficient for the complete reconstruction of the parse tree distinct from prior approaches our linearization is directly grounded in tree spans which may facilitate more efficient parameter estimation furthermore we propose a refined local normalization scheme that diverges from the simple per span normalization utilized by teng and zhang specifically the probability p d i mid i is normalized over all candidate split points preceding index i this enhanced local model aims to improve parsing performance while maintaining rapid learning and inference speeds by employing a greedy heuristic to manage illegal sequences the proposed method achieves an average inference complexity of o n log n

model more laid back appella examples include “it’s true— back to and on to could position for more forward convoys without needing the rear fleet ” and “idk if it’s true just letting u know since were allies” tions e g “dude” “man” skew towards truthfulness as do words associated with reconnaissance e g “fyi” “useful” “information” and time e g “weekend” “morning” contested areas on the diplomacy map such as budapest and sevastopol are more likely to be associated with lies while more secure ones like berlin are more likely to be associated with truthful messages related work early computational deception work focuses on single utterances especially for product reviews but deception is intrinsically a discursive phenomenon and thus the context in which it appears is essential our platform provides an opportunity to observe deception in the context in which it arises goaloriented conversations around ingame objectives gathering data through an interactive game has a cheaper perlie cost than hiring workers to write deceptive statements other conversational datasets are mostly based on games that involve deception including werewolf box of lies and tailormade games however these games assign individuals roles that they maintain throughout the game i e in a role that is supposed to deceive or in a role that is deceived thus deception labels are coarse an individual always lies or always tells the truth in contrast our platform better captures a more multifaceted reality about human nature everyone can lie or be truthful with everyone else and they use both strategically hence players must think about every player lying at any moment “given the evidence do i think this person is lying to me now ” deception data with conversational labels is also available through interviews some of which allow for finergrained deception spans

methodological contributions our proposed methodology computes pre softmax scores as a weighted aggregation of synset specific scores efficiently realized through a dot product operation with a sparse adjacency matrix furthermore we generalize the sense vector dot product technique originally introduced in ewise demonstrating the efficacy of utilizing off the shelf pretrained embeddings we establish that the structured logits mechanism and the integration of sense embeddings are orthogonal components that can be effectively leveraged in conjunction the resulting framework is characterized by its simplicity and extensibility notably it operates without requiring the fine tuning of contextualized embeddings and maintains a minimal parameter footprint beyond the synset embeddings empirically ewiser establishes a new state of the art performance for all words english word sense disambiguation wsd additionally the model achieves state of the art results in cross lingual all words wsd evaluations accomplished without the utilization of non english training data related work supervised wsd supervised systems typically necessitate substantial quantities of expensive manually labeled data to achieve competitive performance pasini contemporary leading approaches predominantly rely on neural network architectures for instance raganato et al formulate the task as a token classification problem employing an lstm with an attention based classifier to generate a probability distribution over both words and senses subsequent research indicates that superior results can be attained by restricting score generation to senses or synsets alone vial et al moreover shallower and more streamlined network architectures have been shown to yield even higher performance uslu et al while contextualized vectors have been successfully integrated into token tagging architectures vial et al bevilacqua and navigli hadiwinoto et al purely supervised systems remain inherently dependent on their training distributions consequently these models struggle to accurately predict senses that are underrepresented in the training corpus lexical knowledge bases lkbs in supervised wsd more directly pertinent to our core contribution is the integration of lexical knowledge base lkb information such as natural language definitions into neural token tagging architectures for example glossbert huang et al utilizes a pretrained bert encoder to process both the context sentence and the gloss training the model to determine whether the gloss accurately describes the target word s usage similarly successful outcomes have been reported through the encoding of glosses into dense vector representations luo et al

to tackle this problem we intend to get some clues about the importance of words from the selfattention graph we propose a selfattention guided copy mechanism sagcopy that aims to encourage the summarizer to copy important source words selfattention layer in the transformer builds a directed graph whose vertices represent the source words and edges are defined in terms of the relevance score between each pair of source words by dotproduct attention between the query q and the key k we calculate the centrality of each source words based on the adjacency matrices a straightforward method is using textrank algorithm that assumes a word receiving more relevance score from others are more likely to be important this measure is known as the indegree centrality we also adopt another measure assuming that a word sends out more relevance score to others is likely to be more critical namely outdegree centrality to calculate the source word centrality we utilize the centrality score as guidance for copy distribution specifically we extend the dotproduct attention to a centralityaware function furthermore we introduce an auxiliary loss computed by the divergence between the copy distribution and the centrality distribution which aims to encourage the model to focus on important words our contribution is threefold • we present a guided copy mechanism based on source word centrality that is obtained by the indegree or outdegree centrality measures • we propose a centralityaware attention and a guidance loss to encourage the model to pay attention to important source words • we achieve stateoftheart on the public text summarization dataset related work neural network based models achieve promising results for the abstractive text summarization

table presents a statistical comparison of the matinf c dataset against other existing datasets matinf qa health domain question answering conventional question answering approaches typically require models to retrieve information from a predefined document corpus or query a manually constructed knowledge base for instance the msmarco dataset nguyen et al employs a search engine to pre filter ten documents from the internet to serve as the context set however the retrieval process itself presents significant challenges that can substantially impact overall system performance furthermore in real world scenarios it is impractical to define a static document set capable of encompassing all knowledge required to address arbitrary user queries to address these limitations the matinf qa training set is provided as a comprehensive source of potential documents this framework encourages the development and evaluation of diverse methodologies including retrieval based generative and hybrid models formally the task is defined as generating a natural language response to a given query q rightarrow a the substantial scale of this dataset ensures that models can effectively generalize and acquire sufficient domain knowledge to answer user questions accurately notably task descriptions are excluded from the input formulation as empirical observations indicate that their inclusion negatively impacts model generalization table provides a statistical overview of matinf qa alongside other commonly used datasets matinf summ summarization in the professional domain existing summarization datasets are predominantly confined to the domains of news and academic literature in extractive summarization tasks summary relevant content frequently appears at the beginning or end of articles due to standard reporting and academic writing conventions this structural bias prevents models from developing a comprehensive understanding of the text and often leads to artificially inflated evaluation metrics conversely current abstractive summarization datasets rely heavily on formal news sources lacking the necessary diversity to ensure model robustness when applied to complex real world scenarios in the matinf summ dataset the question description serves as an extended and specific elaboration of the core question providing detailed contextual background additionally the questions themselves are formulated as well structured interrogative sentences rather than extracted phrases the objective of this task is to generate the specific question based on the corresponding descriptive text d rightarrow q

recoversat predicts a token for each segment conditioned on all previously generated tokens at each generation step which can be formulated as p y x i p s i t s t · · · s k t x where s i t denotes the t th token in the i th segment s i t s i · · · s i t − denotes the translation history in the i th segment and l is segment length here two natural problems arise for the decoding process • how to determine the length of a segment • how to decide a segment should be deleted we address the two problems in a uniform way in this work suppose the original token vocabulary is v we extend it with two extra tokens eos and del then for the segment s i the most probable token ˆ s i t at time step t ˆ s i t arg max s i t ∈ v ∪ eos del p s i t s t · · · s k t x has three possibilities ˆ s i t ∈ v the segment s i is incomplete and the decoding process for it should continue ˆ s i t eos the segment s i is complete and the decoding process for it should terminate ˆ s i t del the segment s i is repetitive and should be deleted accordingly the decoding process for it should terminate the entire decoding process terminates when all the segments meet eos del or reach the maximum token number it should be noticed that we do not explicitly delete a segment when del is encountered but do it via postprocessing in other words the model is trained to ignore the segment to be deleted implicitly learning to recover from errors as there is little targetside information available in the early stage of the decoding process the errors caused by the multimodality problem is inevitable in this work instead of reducing such errors directly we propose two training mechanisms to teach our recoversat model to recover dynamically according to the sentence length

computer algorithms and models can simulate the human immune system responses to particular treatments within hours rather than years the first treatments can then be produced at a small scale using computerengineered cells in less than a month from the initial sequencing to ensure the treatments are safe and effective clinical trials are held at disease transmission “hot spots” predicted using data science methods drawing on data aggregated and monitored from across the world once a treatment is proven safe and effective it is massproduced with the help of computercontrolled robots and automated assembly lines algorithms manage the inventory supply and demand and control the transportation of treatments on trucks and planes guided by computer navigation systems web apps and services notify people throughout the process yet the use of computer technology throughout modern medicine is anything but politically neutral computers algorithms and mathematical models solve the problems that their creators wish to solve and encode the assumptions of their target populations supply and demand data for the data models are determined by various factors at least partly in response to the money and relationships between countries that control the technology the global north and countries that don’t the global south within local communities the uptake of medical treatments is often inequitable reflecting and reinforcing historical inequities and disparities in public health computer technology alone often doesn’t address these issues in fact without people thinking about these issues computer technologies can often amplify disparities consider datasets which can be biased if they overrepresent or underrepresent specific groups of people if decisions are made on the basis of biased data people in the groups that are not represented fairly may receive inequitable treatment for example if a local government agency is working with a biased dataset political leaders may make decisions that result in certain citizens receiving inadequate funding or services this is an example of why responsible computing which we will cover these problematic histories are not only aggravated in medicine and public health but also reflected in housing redlining refers to the inequitable access to basic public services based on residents’ neighborhoods and communities which includes the practice of withholding financial services from areas with a large underrepresented population in the united states these communities reflect the histories of racial segregation and racial wealth inequalities

given a queryresponseuser triple q r u a pretrained seqseq model s s and a model m to be evaluated we first generate n userirrelevant responses r ′ i i ∈ from s s using beam search for a desired personaaware model m it is expected to assign the ground truth response r with a higher probability than other userirrelevant ones r ′ i i ∈ thus taking s s as reference we set urank to be if m scores r a higher ranking position among r ′ i than s s specifically rank m i p m r ′ i p m r rank s s i p s s r ′ i p s s r if rank m rank s s otherwise where p m r and p s s r are the probabilities of q r u given by m and s s respectively x presents the cardinal number of a set x and the lower score of either rank m or rank s s indicates a better ranking position overall for model m its average urank for different queries denotes the rate of rankpromoted groundtruth replies language style imitation apart from perceiving users’ language styles an effective personaaware model should also be able to imitate language styles by generating responses satisfying users’ language behaviors userlanguageperplexity uppl is proposed to measure this property given a user u i to conduct such metric a statistical language model lm i is first trained using the user’s utterances after that for a generated response r ′ its corresponding uppl is defined as the perplexity of r ′ given by lm i uppl quantifies the power of a personaaware model on generating responses similar to users’ history utterances diversity between users finally yet importantly due to the introduction of user information given a query we expect that responses for different users from a personaaware model should be also diversified

the analysis of student performance revealed several distinct categories of error particularly regarding the construction and conceptualization of loops a primary source of confusion arose when multiple instructions were nested within a single loop structure in the actor condition a phenomenon was observed where participants correctly executed the loop logic alternating two instructions four times yet the corresponding builders erroneously constructed separate distinct loops for each instruction furthermore students frequently struggled to distinguish between instructions contained within the loop and those situated outside it some erroneously repeated instructions following the loop while others repeated only the initial instruction effectively excluding the subsequent one from the loop s scope a unique challenge specific to the physical building condition involved the modular nature of the lego repeat block unlike the integrated interface of scratch the lego system requires the placement of two separate physical components top and bottom to represent a single conceptual instruction consequently many students omitted the bottom piece or positioned it incorrectly such as placing it above the loop s initiation point these findings underscore the complexity of loop construction and suggest that instructional strategies should extend beyond debugging to include proactive planning and building protocols we propose the implementation of loop basics to address these specific misconceptions additionally significant misconceptions were identified regarding event handling specifically involving the when green flag clicked and when this sprite clicked blocks both actors and builders frequently omitted a starting event or selected an incorrect one these omissions likely indicate a fundamental lack of understanding regarding the necessity of event triggers to initiate program execution the original rules of the scratch charades activity did not adequately address this concept as the game mechanics required a user to perform the starting event rather than the sprite itself to clarify the role of events the gameplay rules have been revised finally informal feedback collected from observers outside the formal protocol highlighted a practical difficulty students encountered significant challenges in disassembling lego components this observation has informed further revisions to the scratch charades activity for the current academic year to better accommodate these physical constraints

seven consecutive offerings of the course we posit that using go in strategic conjunction with c not only achieves the student learning outcomes given above but also ameliorates the problematic issues associated with the traditional exclusive c approach we present our multisemester experience using this hybrid c go approach including desirable consequences of its use § and student feedback § using this approach for seven consecutive offerings of the course we have experienced the following desirable consequences of it reinforces the idea that linux system calls are provided by the linux kernel not c thus system calls to the kernel can be called from any programming language not just c contrasts linux pipes visà vis go channels this approach fosters a salient contrast between concurrent programming with one process and multiple threads of control see fig —left and multiple processes each with one thread of control see fig —right as employed in the linux filter style of programming this reinforces the idea that the concept of a linux pipe is an extension of a go channel applied to multiple processes and a precursor to a network socket across multiple computers no compromise in purity necessary this approach involves the use of go in conjunction with not in replacement of c thus students continue to gain experience with the traditional systems programming language there are benefits to learning multiple languages including increased versatility adaptability to change and improved alignment with industrial requirements for employee professional skills and knowledge moreover an environment where student experience concepts through multiple languages inhibits student over association of a concept with a particular language this approach facilitates student abstraction of the particular details of each individual language from the concept they are experiencing through that language lastly and perhaps most importantly it is widely believed that one’s labeled data for english but labeled data is extremely scarce in other languages at orders of magnitude less for a couple of languages and none for the rest in this paper we conduct a study aimed at answering the following question given a large annotated webscale dataset such as conceptual captions in one language and a baseline machine translation system what is the optimal way to scale a crossmodality language generation system to new languages at webscale we focus our study on the task of automatic image captioning as a representative for crossmodal language generation where backandforth consistency cannot be leveraged in a straightforward manner in this framework we proceed to test several possible solutions as follows a leverage existing english en image captioning datasets to train a model that generates en captions which are then translated into a target language x we call this approach traingeneratetranslate tgt b leverage existing en captioning datasets and translation capabilities to first translate the data into the target language x and then train a model that generates x language captions we call this approach translatetraingenerate ttg c stabilize the ttg approach by directly using the en gold data along with the translated training data in the x language silver data to train a model that first generates en captions conditioned on the image and then generates x language captions conditioned on the image and the generated en caption this approach has en acting as a pivot language between the input modality and the x language output text studied as it is amenable to exploiting backandforth consistency as a powerful learning signal

second with respect to the extraction of questionrelated rules it is difficult in the current approach to extract the most relevant text span to generate the next question for example the stateoftheart e model has only f for questionrelated span extraction to address these issues we propose a new framework of conversational machine reading with a novel e xplicit m emory t racker emt which explicitly tracks each rule sentence to make decisions and generate followup questions specifically emt first segments the rule text into several rule sentences and allocates them into its memory then the initial question user scenario and dialog history are fed into emt sequentially to update each memory module separately at each dialog turn emt predicts the entailment states satisfaction or not for every rule sentence and makes a decision based on the current memory status if the decision is inquire emt extracts a rule span to generate a followup question by adopting a coarsetofine reasoning strategy i e weighting tokenlevel span distributions with its sentencelevel entailment scores compared to previous methods which only consider entailmentoriented reasoning for decision making or followup question generation emt utilizes its updated memory modules to reason out these two tasks in a unified manner we compare emt with the existing approaches on the sharc dataset our results show that explicitly tracking rules with external memories boosts both the decision accuracy and the quality of generated followup questions in particular emt outperforms the previous best model e by in macroaveraged decision accuracy and in bleu for followup question generation in addition to the performance improvement emt yields interpretability by explicitly tracking rules which is visualized to show the entailmentoriented reasoning process of our model

however relying solely on the prediction of segment boundaries is insufficient for capturing the topical nuances required for informative labeling the dual tasks of document segmentation and labeling have been extensively investigated within the clinical domain where both processes are critical pomares quimbaya et al offer a comprehensive overview of recent advancements in clinical segmentation while ganesan and subotin employed a logistic regression model for clinical segmentation their work did not address segment labeling conversely tepper et al addressed both segmentation and labeling through a two stage pipeline that first segments the text and subsequently classifies the resulting segments in contrast the proposed model is trained jointly on both tasks concurrent research has explored document outline generation zhang et al which aims to segment text and generate potentially hierarchical headings for each segment the authors introduced histgen a hierarchical lstm model equipped with a sequence decoder this work presents an alternative perspective on the joint segmentation and labeling problem evaluating performance via exact match for segmentation and rouge lin for heading generation contingent upon correct segment prediction differing from this approach the current study utilizes the probabilistic segmentation metric pk beeferman et al which awards partial credit for imperfect segmentations furthermore an alignment technique is employed to assign partial credit to labels associated with incorrect segmentations during both training and evaluation additionally this work explicitly addresses model transferability by assessing pretrained models on external datasets the challenge of jointly learning segmentation and classification is also well established in natural language processing nlp primarily at the token level through inside outside beginning iob tagging ramshaw and marcus conditional random field crf decoding has long been utilized with iob tagging to simultaneously segment and label text such as in named entity recognition ner mccallum and li historically the most effective models for joint segmentation and classification tasks including ner and phrase chunking were iob based architectures typically comprising lstms with a crf decoder lample et al prior to the advent of bert devlin et al and elmo peters et al

need for separate interventions—warrants investigation given that existing literature identifies a gender disparity in spatial abilities further analysis is required to ascertain whether this gap contributes to the underrepresentation of women in computing fields the findings of the present study corroborate the results reported by cooper et al while establishing a critical foundation for understanding the role of spatial skills in introductory computing curricula consequently substantial avenues for future work remain this material is based upon work partially supported by the national science foundation under grant numbers and any opinions findings and conclusions or recommendations expressed in this material are those of the author s and do not necessarily reflect the views of the national science foundation the authors extend their gratitude to s roscoe and a jobbins for their insightful feedback and comments on earlier drafts of this manuscript furthermore this paper introduces a novel inverted two stage examination format designed to foster the anticipation of transfer problems we detail the design implementation and initial assessment of this format within the context of low stakes quizzes in an algorithms course unlike the traditional two stage model in which the group stage follows the individual stage to emphasize retrospective learning through reflection on solved problems the proposed inverted format prioritizes the group stage this structure incentivizes prospective learning preparing students to transfer their knowledge to novel problems encountered in the subsequent individual stage and future assessments teaching assistants reported that the new format yielded reliable student engagement survey data indicate that the majority of students preferred the inverted two stage quizzes over a combination of individual quizzes and teaching assistant walkthroughs students favoring this format cited the value of peer learning domain specific brainstorming and the collaborative resolution of ambiguities regarding both the subject matter and the problems themselves in summary this study describes a straightforward adoptable framework that provides a structured incentive for students to practice the transfer of learning

the proposed framework for transferring monolingual models to additional languages addresses a critical scenario the integration of new languages into an existing model architecture without access to the original training corpus this setting holds substantial practical significance given that pre trained models are frequently distributed without their corresponding training data consequently this work establishes a foundational baseline for multilingual lifelong learning related work unsupervised lexical multilingual representations a prevalent methodology for acquiring multilingual representations involves cross lingual word embedding mappings these techniques generate distinct monolingual word embeddings for each language and project them into a shared vector space via linear transformation contemporary approaches facilitate this mapping through unsupervised initialization strategies such as heuristic based methods artetxe et al or adversarial training zhang et al conneau et al a often refined further through self learning mechanisms artetxe et al this paradigm has subsequently been extended to accommodate contextual representations schuster et al unsupervised deep multilingual representations in contrast to lexical level approaches state of the art methods focus on learning deep representations utilizing transformer architectures predominantly based on multilingual bert mbert notable extensions to mbert include scaling model capacity and incorporating parallel data conneau and lample introducing auxiliary pre training tasks huang et al and enforcing similarity between representations of translated pairs cao et al concurrent with this study tran proposed a more complex methodology for transferring monolingual bert models to other languages achieving performance comparable to our results however tran observed that post hoc embedding learning from random initialization yields suboptimal outcomes conversely our findings demonstrate that monolingual representations possess strong generalization capabilities across languages enabling effective transfer to new languages through the learning of novel subword embeddings

we theorise that one such characteristic is that a certain part of the sentence either becomes irrelevant for sentiment analysis or possesses a semantic orientation that is opposite to the sentence s overall orientation we analyse tweets containing the conjunction but and the conditionals if unless until and in case and formulate rules that should enable removal of irrelevant or oppositely oriented ngrams from the tweet s feature vector before it is used for supervised learning from analysing tweet examples containing but we observe that the part of the sentence posterior to the word but is usually though not always a better indicator of the overall polarity of the tweet as compared to the anterior part in certain cases it is difficult to determine the most salient part of the tweet and some tweets require more complex linguistic analysis which is beyond the scope of this study to best handle tweets with multiple occurrences of but in the same sentence only the part posterior to the last occurrence is considered the most salient hence we propose the following strategy to modify ngrams for tweets containing the conjunction but we use the punkt sentence tokeniser to break a tweet into sentences in each sentence we find the location of the last occurrence of the word but we remove all tokens except the tokens posterior to that location so the modified sentence only contains tokens succeeding the last but once we have processed all the sentences in the tweet we merge the modified sentences together to obtain the modified tweet the conditional if has many more syntactic positions than but the most salient part of a tweet containing if is generally the part that occurs after then or after the comma following the conditional however handling tweets where the salient part is not preceded by a then or comma or where if occurs in the middle of the sentence requires more complex linguistic analysis which is beyond the scope of this study for the conditionals unless until and in case we consider the most salient part of the tweet to be the part that occurs after the first comma succeeding the conditional whereas for if we consider the part occurring after then as well as the comma

what are the impacts of annotation guidelines annotator assumptions and perceptions and annotation aggregation processes ⋄ evaluation how are nlp systems evaluated what are the impacts of evaluation metrics are any nonquantitative evaluations performed how do nlp systems reproduce or transform language ideologies which language varieties or practices come to be deemed good or bad might “good” language simply mean language that is easily handled by existing nlp systems for example linguistic phenomena arising from many language practices are described as “noisy text” and often viewed as a target for “normalization ” how do the language ideologies that are reproduced by nlp systems maintain social hierarchies which representational harms are being measured or mitigated are these the most normatively concerning harms or merely those that are well handled by existing algorithmic fairness techniques are there other representational harms that might be analyzed conceptualizations of “bias” turning now to r we argue that work analyzing “bias” in nlp systems should provide explicit statements of why the system behaviors that are described as “bias” are harmful in what ways and to whom as well as the normative reasoning underlying these statements in other words researchers and practitioners should articulate their conceptualizations of “bias ” as we described above papers often contain descriptions of system behaviors that are understood to be selfevident statements of “bias ” this use of imprecise terminology has led to papers all claiming to analyze “bias” in nlp systems sometimes even in systems developed for the same task but with different or even inconsistent conceptualizations of “bias ” and no explanations for these differences yet analyzing “bias” is an inherently normative process—in which some system behaviors are deemed good and others harmful—even if assumptions about what kinds of system behaviors are harmful in what ways for whom and why are not stated we therefore echo calls by bardzell and bardzell keyes et al and green for researchers and practitioners to make their normative reasoning explicit by articulating the social values that underpin their decisions to deem some system behaviors as harmful no matter how obvious such values appear to be

the historical integration of computer technologies within african american communities is exemplified by initiatives such as afronet an early social networking platform for black technologists and netnoir a digital initiative dedicated to promoting black culture these endeavors illustrate the early efforts of black technologists to develop computing solutions specifically tailored to the needs of black populations in the united states however this history also underscores significant political challenges regarding the systemic exclusion of black technologists from the broader technological landscape while black cultural expression on the internet has profoundly influenced mainstream media and culture in the united states the economic and attentional value generated by these cultural products frequently accrues to dominant global platforms—such as x and tiktok—rather than to the black creators entrepreneurs and communities that originate them consequently there is a risk that computer technologies may perpetuate social inequities through the uneven distribution of benefits and harms the digital divide has emerged as a critical societal issue particularly as access to computing computer science and connectivity becomes increasingly integral to education employment and social mobility this divide is characterized by disparities in technology access and distribution across various demographics including geography socioeconomic status race and ethnicity although technological access generally improves over time significant disparities persist both within the united states and globally regarding access to high speed internet cellular infrastructure and functional educational computing resources furthermore infrastructural reliability such as consistent electricity remains a fundamental determinant of effective technology usage beyond systemic infrastructure gaps disparities in access to specific products and services can create internal divisions within communities for instance if advanced artificial intelligence driven search and optimization tools are restricted to high cost subscription models specific populations may be systematically excluded from their benefits as with other cutting edge technologies computer systems alter the balance of power within society however access to these innovations is rarely equitable while computer science has enhanced the quality of life for those with access to and control over these technologies populations in the global south and other marginalized groups often require context sensitive designs to meet their specific needs this necessity is historically evident while consumer internet access in the s relied on dial up systems utilizing public telephone networks many regions of the world still lack basic telephone coverage let alone internet connectivity

while comprehensive initiatives such as those previously described have demonstrated efficacy their implementation necessitates substantial resources and the coordinated commitment of personnel across multiple institutional levels consequently in environments characterized by fiscal constraints or other impediments to large scale transformation smaller scale interventions executable by a limited number of dedicated individuals present a more viable alternative given the contemporary imperative for academic units to serve expanding student populations with diminishing financial resources retention strategies must increasingly prioritize resource efficiency over comprehensive models two primary avenues for enhancing retention with minimal resource expenditure include program level modifications and classroom level instructional adjustments empirical evidence suggests that specific interventions within these domains can effectively improve retention rates among computer science cs majors for instance ott et al documented a program level intervention wherein the implementation of mandatory placement examinations rather than voluntary opt in mechanisms ensured that students were enrolled in introductory cs courses aligned with their prior programming experience this procedural adjustment significantly increased the likelihood of students continuing their cs coursework similarly carver et al reported on a classroom level intervention involving pair programming during laboratory exercises in introductory courses this collaborative methodology wherein students alternate between the roles of driver and navigator was associated with higher persistence rates in computing majors compared to traditional instruction in both cited cases interventions deeply integrated into the cs curriculum successfully bolstered retention in contrast the present inquiry focuses on determining whether broader instructional practices influence students decisions to enroll in subsequent cs courses

resiliency denotes a combination of strength and adaptability whereas hardness implies strength coupled with rigidity visual cognition constitutes a fundamental component of the creative process a mechanism significantly enhanced by rapid sketching which facilitates the refinement of conceptual frameworks visual thinking is a ubiquitous human activity a fact reflected in the english lexicon through terms such as insight foresight and hindsight indeed the etymological origin of the word idea traces back to the greek idein meaning to see cognition is a pervasive yet elusive phenomenon as evidenced by cerebral electrical activity thinking persists even during sleep it is a complex systemic activity involving the entire organism physiologists have demonstrated the influence of muscle tone on thought neurologists correlate the nervous system with cognitive function and psychologists link affective states to the thinking process this activity manifests visually verbally and mathematically and is susceptible to any factor influencing physiological well being while not all cognitive activity is productive or purposeful it remains a constant occurrence visual thinking permeates all human endeavors ranging from the profound to the mundane examples include albert einstein s visualization of traveling at the speed of light during his formulation of the theory of relativity an athlete s mental rehearsal of victory and the routine navigation of a crowd interaction with this visual cognitive process relies on three interrelated forms of imagery perception seeing conception imagining and representation drawing these modalities function as overlapping mutually reinforcing systems in this context drawing refers not to formal artistic production but to informal sketching or doodling consequently the act of observing reality informs drawing while the act of drawing enhances perceptual clarity similarly drawing stimulates imagination and imagination fosters the motivation to draw the relationship between imagination and perception is more intricate imagination filters perceptual input while perception stimulates imaginative capacity these forms of imagery continuously merge and interact rapid sketching aids in problem formulation and resolution by enabling the examination of concepts from multiple perspectives by externalizing ideas sketching alleviates the cognitive load of retaining multiple mental images and actively stimulates the imagination this dynamic represents a flow of imagery from the perceptual seeing to the internal imagining a softmax function alpha t frac exp mathbf w top mathbf h t sum j n exp mathbf w top mathbf h j quad in these equations mathbf w represents a vector of learnable parameters and mathbf h t denotes the input or activation vector at temporal position t it is noted that while visually grounded speech models e g chrupała et al chrupała merkx et al employ similar mechanisms to aggregate final rnn layer activations this approach is utilized here as a general analytical method to pool any sequential representation of interest furthermore scalar weights alpha t and a linear learning model are adopted quantify the degree of correspondence between the activation similarity space and the phoneme or phoneme string similarity space diagnostic classifiers for diagnostic classifiers performance is of learning effects to evaluate the sensitivity of various methods to the impact of training on activation patterns it is essential to compare scores obtained from trained models against those from randomly initialized models consequently results for both conditions are presented jointly a desirable property of an analytical method is its sensitivity to the learning effect characterized by a clear separation between the scores of trained and randomly initialized models coefficient of partial determination correlations between the similarity structures of two representational spaces may in principle be partially attributable to their mutual correlation with a third space for instance a high global rsa value observed in the top layers of the rnnvgs model might reflect a correlation between the string similarities of phonemic transcriptions and the visual similarities of corresponding images rather than indicating that the layer explicitly encodes phoneme strings the coefficient
