our framework should enable its users to apply evolving standards and norms across nlp’s many application contexts a prototypical example of outcome disparity is gender disparity in image captions zhao et al and hendricks et al demonstrate a systematic difference with respect to gender in the outcome of the model ˆ y even when taking the source distribution as an ideal target distribution q ˆ y target gender ≁ q y target gender ∼ q y source gender as a result captions overpredict females in images with ovens and males in images with snowboards error disparity we say there is an error disparity when model predictions have larger error for individuals with a given user attribute or range of attributes in the case of continuouslyvalued attributes formally the error of a predicted distribution is ϵ d y d − ˆ y d if this difference ϵ d is not distributed uniformly with respect to a d then there is an error disparity q ϵ d a d ≁ uniform in other words the error for one group might systematically differ from the error for another group e g the error for green people differs from the error for blue people under unbiased conditions the difference would come from a uniform distribution this formulation allows us to capture both the discrete case arguably more common in nlp for example in pos tagging and the continuous case for example in age or income prediction we propose that if either of these two disparities exist in our target application then there is a predictive bias note that predictive bias is then a property of a model given a specific application rather than merely an intrinsic property of the model by itself this definition mirrors predictive bias in standardized testing “a cannot be called biased without reference to a specific prediction situation thus the same instrument may be biased in one application but unbiased in another a prototypical example of error disparity is the “wall street journal effect” – a systematic difference in error as a function of demographics first documented by hovy and søgaard

a fundamental imperative for engineers and scientists is to ensure that their models remain consistent with underlying mathematical assumptions for example the anisotropic nature of materials such as wood necessitates that directional variations in properties be accounted for assuming isotropy may yield inaccurate results furthermore the assignment of physical values to symbolic representations is critical as it directly influences mathematical validity while abstract arithmetic dictates that this logic does not universally apply to physical quantities without regard for unit compatibility the summation of homogeneous quantities such as two cups of coffee and three cups of coffee yields five cups however the addition of heterogeneous quantities such as two cups of sugar to three cups of coffee does not result in five cups of coffee in the latter instance the principle of volume conservation does not apply whereas the conservation of mass governs the interaction of the substances technology born of engineering design serves as a mechanism to modify the environment to better satisfy human needs this process yields both anticipated benefits and unforeseen costs or risks engineering synthesizes the practical knowledge of fabrication with the scientific understanding of operational principles to generate novel technologies historical figures such as alexander graham bell and thomas edison exemplify the integration of theoretical and practical knowledge in technological innovation science and technology are inextricably linked operating in a reciprocal relationship where each facilitates the advancement of the other the development of computing for instance has significantly expanded the capacity to study complex weather systems while thermodynamic principles were essential to the comprehension and optimization of the steam engine as technologies become increasingly sophisticated the distinction between engineering and science often becomes indistinct in fields such as solid state device design physics and engineering are deeply intertwined technological progress frequently necessitates new scientific understandings while scientific inquiry often relies on advanced technological tools unlike the abstract realms of science and mathematics the engineered world interacts directly and immediately with social and cultural values consequently the assessment of benefits and risks along with the management of trade offs and constraints is intrinsic to the engineering process certain constraints are dictated by immutable natural laws such as the conservation of energy for example the creation of an engine with efficiency is theoretically impossible underscoring the absolute limits imposed by physical reality

a primary advantage of distant supervision is its capacity to generate large scale labeled datasets without the need for manual annotation however as detailed in section the labels derived through this method are often subject to significant noise an alternative approach involves the collection of textual data followed by manual annotation by human experts for instance filatova tasked annotators with identifying pairs of amazon reviews one sarcastic and one non sarcasmic resulting in a dataset comprising positive and negative examples similarly abercrombie and hovy annotated twitter conversations yielding positive and negative labels riloff et al employed a hybrid methodology initially gathering tweets containing the hashtags sarcasm or sarcastic and a control set of tweets without these tags after removing the hashtags the texts were presented to human annotators for final classification this collection is referred to herein as the riloff dataset a comparable strategy was utilized by van hee et al who introduced a balanced dataset of tweets as part of a semeval shared task on sarcasm detection this collection is designated as the semeval dataset sarcasm detection models based on the information utilized to classify text as sarcastic or non sarcastic the existing literature identifies two primary categories of models text based models and contextual models

one significant challenge associated with this dataset is the prevalence of typographical errors since term frequency inverse document frequency tf idf weighting inherently favors low frequency terms these errors are disproportionately elevated in importance while manual filtering can mitigate this issue the resulting keyword extraction quality remains suboptimal consequently we employ a refined extraction strategy this approach begins with the definition of a stop word list utilizing the standard nltk corpus bird et al augmented by domain specific terms e g hotel that lack informational value within the context of hotel reviews for each sentence we identify non stop words with the highest global frequency typically two keywords are selected per sentence however this number is adjusted to one or zero when the pool of candidate words is insufficient following the removal of stop words and typos empirical observation indicates that keywords extracted via this methodology effectively capture the semantic essence of the majority of sentences regarding model architecture and hyperparameters the system comprises several distinct components the encoder e and the sentence level transformer t are configured with the same dimensions as bert base the decoder d matches the architecture of gpt m parameters when lexical constraints are applied the constraint feature encoder k is also sized equivalently to bert base during the decoding phase beam search is utilized with a beam width of experimental results sentence representation learning we first conduct a qualitative assessment of the smoothness of the latent space sentence embeddings learned through denoising autoencoding table presents two examples of sentence interpolation on the tripadvisor dataset in each instance the initial and final sentences are manually provided while the three intermediate sentences are generated via interpolation by our denoising autoencoder the results demonstrate that the interpolated sentences not only synthesize vocabulary from the input endpoints but also maintain readability semantic coherence and a smooth transition between the source and target meanings we posit that the capability to generate fluent and semantically consistent interpolations is derived from the underlying bert and gpt architectures inheriting properties from these large scale pretrained models the latent space embeddings exhibit the smoothness evidenced by our interpolation results automatic evaluation table reports the bleu nist meteor entropy distinct n distn scores and the average sentence length in word count for the generated outputs for the tripadvisor dataset we additionally present performance metrics under conditions involving keyword constraints

predicting one of the big five personality traits the resulting personality predictions serve as input to a gaussian process regression gpr model which generates a binary classification determining the candidate s suitability for a job interview while many multimodal architectures for personality recognition rely on bimodal fusion of audio and visual data this approach employs multi task learning to simultaneously recognize extraversion and leadership traits the system utilizes data extracted from meeting videos within the emergent leader elea corpus the prediction of extraversion is of particular significance in psychological research due to its capacity to explain diverse behavioral patterns forecast performance and assess individual risk to enhance model robustness the network architecture employs a transfer learning strategy initially training on the larger vlog corpus before fine tuning on the elea dataset additionally the application of maximum relevance minimum redundancy mrmr feature selection techniques yielded a marginal performance improvement of approximately the study further investigates the interpretability of deep residual networks drn used for perceived personality analysis this end to end deep architecture operates as a two stream network processing visual and audio modalities independently analysis of the model s decision making process reveals that high levels of personality traits in female faces are associated with increased colorfulness and contrast whereas low levels correspond to more uniform coloration and lower contrast furthermore an examination of unisex average faces indicates a bias wherein high trait levels are associated with female facial characteristics and low levels with male characteristics a trend particularly evident in annotation based averages specifically high trait levels correlate with brighter more colorful imagery and positive facial expressions finally the deep bimodal regression dbr framework which achieved the highest accuracy in the chalearn challenge for perceived personality analysis is highlighted the dbr framework comprises three primary components visual modality regression audio modality regression and an ensemble mechanism for fusing information from both modalities notably this network is trained end to end without reliance on external feature engineering pipelines

machine learning is defined as the development of algorithms that enable computational systems to derive insights from data and execute decisions without explicit programming the realization of such capabilities is often facilitated by integrating platform services from various public cloud providers thereby granting access to these transformative technologies contemporary solution models leverage these resources to support diverse applications including mobile health mhealth body area networks bans emotion monitoring and social media platforms furthermore adaptive design reuse methodologies can be employed to develop custom components within these hybrid architectures complementing the capabilities offered by major cloud infrastructures prominent examples of applications utilizing location based data include google maps and zillow in these systems global positioning system gps devices determine user coordinates which are transmitted through central networks subsequent applications present this data in an accessible format connecting users with real time geographic information or housing market analytics the integration of gps with broader internet of things iot ecosystems enables the seamless delivery of customized location specific content thereby enhancing the overall user experience the evolution toward web further empowers organizations to deliver personalized and predictive services by granting users greater control over their data this paradigm fosters increased trust and engagement for enterprises this shift presents new avenues for collaboration innovation and direct consumer interaction eliminating intermediaries and driving more efficient business models that generate value unattainable through earlier web technologies historically navigation relied on static paper based maps derived from non digital sources in contrast modern online mapping systems facilitate navigation in a rapidly changing environment this chapter examines the foundational structures and analytical methods employed by computer scientists to underpin these and other contemporary technologies data structures and algorithms serve as the bedrock not only for mapping applications but also for a vast array of other systems ranging from autonomous vehicles and inventory management to galactic simulations and inter computer data transfer these mechanisms are essential for the efficient organization and processing of large scale information ultimately computer science is the study of computational systems encompassing data representation and process automation given their historical origins as calculating devices computers are inherently suited to representing numerical data just as traditional calculators rely on algorithms to perform arithmetic operations modern computational systems utilize sophisticated algorithmic logic to solve complex problems practically and efficiently

it’s important to not only understand that diversity includes a number of different factors but also that understanding and appreciating all factors are critical for an inclusive environment one of the most critical and often uncomfortable components of cultural competence is accurately and exhaustively assessing one’s current beliefs against current practices one may value diversity and still display bias and discriminatory behavior given one’s perspective it may be difficult to identify beliefs and practices as biased or discriminatory unless attention is brought to understanding why these are problematic research notes how many individuals who consider themselves majority allies are often unaware of their own biases this work coins the term “allersary” to describe such individuals as those who consider themselves majority allies yet still exhibit toxic traits that are adversarial cross describes “dynamics of difference” as “ w hen a system of one culture interacts with a population from another both may misjudge the other’s actions based on learned expectations” having good intentions around dei doesn’t mean one can appropriately manage differences that exist in crosscultural communication not only is selfassessment of one’s own biases critical it is also important to recognize that interactions with individuals with different identities may not be received as intended in these instances it is important to understand the relationship history between one’s own identity culture and the other party’s especially for those in majority demographic s proper communication means understanding the historical impact of certain actions words and beliefs on people from diverse backgrounds acknowledging these and working actively to address and minimize misjudgements based on them learning more about various cultures helps to eliminate issues that stem from an ignorance of cultural differences including biases stereotypes and cultural appropriation however the acquisition of crosscultural knowledge is not a onetime event the transfer of knowledge after one conversation workshop or course does not equate to the institutionalization of that knowledge like any other knowledge or skill development it requires continued learning engagement and practice in order to develop proficiency institutionalization must occur across all organizational levels and provide research and demonstration projects to all stakeholders especially important is that “avenues to such knowledge are as important as the knowledge itself” this means that individuals from the respective cultures must be the primary contributors when addressing culturally related questions and concerns

rofl bridges the domain gap between dialog data and the rl environment by using the dialog transitions generated during training to update the expert’s weights adapting the previously learned knowledge to the learning environment our experiments show that rofl training improves demonstrations gathered from the employed experts giving a boost in rl performance and hastening convergence related work our work is closely related to research in using expert demonstrations to guide reinforcement learning dialog managers lipton et al “spike” the deep qnetwork replay buffer with a few successful demonstrations from a rulebased dialog manager gordonhall et al extend this approach and apply deep qlearning from demonstrations dqfd to dialog prefilling a portion of the buffer with expert transitions and encouraging the agent to imitate them by adding an auxiliary term to the dqn loss demonstrations are not the only way to incorporate external expertise into the dialog manager one alternative is to use supervised learning to train a neural network policy on an indomain dialog dataset and then finetune it with policygradient rl on a usersimulator liu et al finetune their rl policy on human rather than simulated users another parallel approach to rlbased dms aims to increase the frequency of meaningful rewards takanobu et al use inverse rl to learn a dense reward based on a dialog corpus while lu et al decompose the task into subgoals that can be regularly assessed weak demonstrations have been used outside of dialog system research to tackle rl environments with large state spaces and sparse rewards aytar et al train an expert to imitate youtube videos of people playing challenging atari games and exceed humanlevel performance salimans and chen beat their score on montezuma’s revenge using only a single human demonstration resetting the environment to different states from the expert trajectory however we believe our work is the first to explore the use of weak demonstrations for dqfd in a dialog environment

al employed as the text encoder rnn a recurrent neural network utilizing simple recurrent unit sru recurrence lei et al as the text encoder integrated with a fasttext word embedding layer bojanowski et al rnn self attention the aforementioned rnn architecture augmented with a multi head self attention layer lin et al vaswani et al the study further examines three interaction paradigms random interaction at each turn the system selects a question uniformly at random following t turns the final classification label is determined based on the posterior belief p y x t no initial query interaction questions are selected via the maximum information criterion without conditioning on the user s initial query this approach is equivalent to employing a static decision tree wherein the recent methods for bilingual lexicon induction are mostly based on unsupervised crosslingual word embeddings they follow the same procedure that is first building initial solutions a seed dictionary and then learning a mapping function between the two word embedding spaces during inference for a given source word they find the target word via the nearest neighbors search by calculating the distance of the mapped source embedding and all target word embeddings the main focus of the previous methods is how to find the initial solution which is the most important part their methods can be divided into three categories according to the way of finding the initial solution the first category is using heuristic rules such as treating identical words as the seed but this kind of method is restricted to languages sharing the vocabulary or at least the notation of numbers the second category is adversarial methods they train a generator to finish mapping between the two word embedding spaces and a discriminator to distinguish the mapped embeddings from the target embeddings however they suffer from the drawbacks of generative adversarial models i e the sensitivity of hyperparameters long training time and lack of interpretability the third category is structurebased methods which achieve the stateoftheart performance on bli they either leverage the intralinguistic word similarity information or principal components of monolingual word embeddings but their methods infer initial solutions just based on wordlevel information which is limited and prone to contain some noise due to the insufficient training of pretrained embeddings different from their methods ours leverages cliquelevel information which is richer and more accurate and uses a coarsetofine procedure to reduce the adverse effect of the noise mentioned above conclusion in this paper we propose a novel graphbased coarsetofine paradigm for unsupervised bilingual lexicon induction

studies have examined the effectiveness of different features for stance detection including textual features such as word n grams and hashtags network interactions such as retweeted accounts and mentions and profile information such as user location network interaction features were shown to yield better results compared to using textual features sridhar et al leveraged both user interactions and textual information when modeling stance and disagreement using a probabilistic programming system that allows models to be specified using a declarative language trabelsi and za ¨ ıane described an unsupervised stance detection method that determines the viewpoints of comments and of their authors it analyzes online forum discussion threads and therefore assumes a certain structure of the posts it also assumes that users tend to reply to each others’ comments when they are in disagreement whereas we assume the opposite in this paper their model leverages the posts’ contents whereas we only use the retweet behavior of users many methods involving supervised learning were proposed for stance detection such methods require the availability of an initial set of labeled users and they use some of the aforementioned features for classification such classification can label users with precision typically ranging between and label propagation is a semisupervised method that starts with a seed list of labeled users and propagates the labels to other users who are similar based on the accounts they follow or retweet while label propagation may label users with high precision often above it is biased towards users with more extreme views moreover careful choice of thresholds is often required and postchecks are needed to ensure quality

to generate n parallel reasoning chains the air algorithm is executed n times this process ensures diversity by initiating each chain with a distinct initial justification sentence specifically in practice a new chain is launched for each of the top n sentences retrieved during the first retrieval step upon obtaining these n parallel evidence chains the supporting evidence text for a given candidate answer is constructed by taking the union of all justification sentences across the individual chains experiments we evaluated the proposed approach on two datasets multi sentence reading comprehension multirc this dataset introduced by khashabi et al a frames reading comprehension as a multiple choice question answering task each question is grounded in a paragraph that contains the ground trust justification sentences in our experiments every sentence within the paragraph is treated as a potential candidate justification for the associated question implementation details multirc configuration we utilized the roberta base model with a maximum sequence length of a batch size of a learning rate of times and trained for epochs roberta base demonstrated consistent performance across multirc experiments conversely multiple runs with roberta large failed to converge or yielded near random performance a phenomenon previously noted by wolf et al additional configuration for other experiments we employed hyperparameters similar to those used for multirc but substituted the model with roberta large and reduced the maximum sequence length to observations the dataset creators have reported similar findings regarding model behavior khot et al a evaluation metrics the performance of the computational supervised method is assessed using the following metrics f m micro averaged f score f a macro averaged f score em exact match score evidence selection steps precision p recall r and f score for justification selection

table presents the experimental results across various evaluation metrics the analysis is structured around the impact of four key components the copy mechanism embedding factorization the table text embedding similarity loss and the optimal transport ot constraint loss first the efficacy of the copy mechanism is demonstrated by comparing the first and second rows of table the inclusion of this mechanism yields significant performance improvements across all automatic metrics with a particularly notable enhancement in faithfulness as measured by the parentt score second the impact of embedding factorization is assessed by comparing the model against a variant without this component rows two and three the results indicate that embedding factorization reduces the parameter count by approximately while maintaining comparable performance levels third the contribution of the table text embedding similarity loss is evaluated by removing this component from the model rows three and four while the removal of this loss leads to a marginal decrease in bleu and meteor scores it results in a degradation of parent and parentt scores conversely the inclusion of the loss improves these faithfulness metrics which aligns with the loss function s objective of enhancing the fidelity of generated text to the source data fourth the effectiveness of the ot constraint loss is examined through three configurations a without ot loss b with ot loss computed over the entire table and text and c with ot loss computed using only extracted nouns from both modalities these results are detailed in rows three five and six of table respectively the incorporation of the ot loss improves performance across nearly all metrics particularly the parentt score furthermore restricting the ot computation to extracted nouns yields superior results compared to using the full text these findings underscore the effectiveness of the proposed ot loss in enforcing faithfulness to the original regarding feedback mechanisms for student behavior educators expressed a dual need for real time data to monitor in class engagement and periodic summaries daily or weekly to provide detailed insights into student learning outcomes furthermore teachers emphasized that formative assessment data should not be utilized for performance evaluations drawing a distinction from the use of standardized testing metrics additional concerns were raised regarding the viability of commercial games such as plague inc citing potential risks to data security incompatibility with the limited network bandwidth prevalent in many educational institutions and lack of support for standard classroom hardware specifically chromebooks collectively the professional development pd activities addressed the study s second research question and yielded four primary design considerations dc dc the integration of data and analysis across diverse curricula presents a significant opportunity particularly within subjects such as art music and health dc while assessments of computer science cs concepts are necessary they must be concise and engaging to avoid the testing fatigue associated with traditional evaluation methods dc assessment feedback should encompass both just in time behavioral data and aggregated classroom learning outcomes that contextualize findings for cs instruction moreover feedback should avoid purely numerical formats to prevent its misuse as a metric for teacher performance dc digital tools must be developed with explicit consideration for existing technological infrastructure including hardware limitations network bandwidth constraints and student privacy protocols utilizing the formative knowledge structures fksas and assessment guidelines derived from an evidence centered design ecd approach alongside the design considerations established through the pd process the research team developed beats empire this game based formative assessment targets the middle school data and analysis strand of the k– cs framework games offer students the opportunity to encounter and apply concepts within authentic meaningful contexts and have been demonstrated to effectively assess cs understanding furthermore the game format aligns with the finding that short playful assessments are essential for cross curricular cs integration dc beats empire is designed as a lightweight web based standalone assessment tool that is subject matter agnostic and intended to complement regular classroom activities its flexible architecture allows for deployment in multiple short sessions or fewer extended sessions enabling educators to adapt the tool to their specific instructional needs

introduction the points of tangency between a linear combination line and the pareto front constitute pareto optimal solutions in the context of convex problems all pareto optimal points are attainable conversely in non convex scenarios those located within the concave regions of the pareto front remain inaccessible to standard linear scalarization methods consequently such approaches inherently exclude a significant subset of pareto optimal solutions from their search space to illustrate this limitation consider a two task learning scenario as depicted in figure the figure demonstrates that for non convex problems pareto points situated in the concave portions of the front are unachievable via linear methods given the uniform convergence properties of multitask learning mtl baxter the exclusion of these optimal solutions may degrade the generalization performance of multitask text classification systems to address the challenges posed by non convexity this paper proposes a novel tchebycheff based procedure designed to enhance multitask text classification performance the efficacy of the proposed method is validated through experiments on two classical text classification benchmarks sentiment analysis on reviews blitzer et al and topic classification on news articles lang experimental results indicate that the proposed method achieves convergence and outperforms several state of the art multitask text classification approaches related work methods grounded in pareto optimality including l metric approaches weighted sum methods maurer et al chen et al kendall et al and the multiple gradient descent algorithm mgda sener and koltun have emerged as prevalent strategies in multitask learning mtl within the domain of multitask text classification l metric methods are particularly widespread liu et al xiao et al yadav et al however in non convex problem settings l metric methods risk excluding the optimal hypothesis from the hypothesis class to mitigate this issue mgda utilizes karush kuhn tucker kkt conditions to identify pareto stationary points nevertheless these stationary points do not guarantee pareto optimality therefore there is a critical need for a novel mtl methodology capable of achieving true pareto optimality without relying on convexity assumptions thereby addressing the limitations inherent in both l metric and mgda approaches

abstract through a comprehensive series of experiments encompassing diverse classification and sequence to sequence tasks this study demonstrates that attention scores are highly susceptible to manipulation we propose a straightforward training methodology that enables models to ostensibly assign negligible attention to a designated set of impermissible tokens while simultaneously retaining reliance on those features for accurate prediction the ease with which attention mechanisms can be altered without compromising model performance implies that even if raw attention weights in standard models offer interpretive value—a proposition that remains an open and ill defined question—such insights are contingent upon knowledge of the specific training objectives these findings carry significant implications for the application of attention mechanisms in domains requiring fairness accountability and transparency specifically the proposed manipulation scheme could enable bad actors to mislead regulatory bodies by presenting attention weights as justification for model behavior for instance an analysis of manipulated attention based explanations as illustrated in table might erroneously suggest that a model does not utilize gender prefixes despite the model s actual dependence on them to quantify the extent of this potential deception we conducted studies involving human subjects evaluating biased occupation classification models similar to those audited by dearteaga et al our results indicate that the manipulation scheme successfully deceives human annotators into believing that the models are gender neutral whereas the models exhibit substantial bias against gender minorities see section furthermore practitioners frequently overlook the fact that attention is typically computed over final layer representations rather than individual words these representations inherently aggregate information from neighboring tokens we investigate the mechanisms by which manipulated models achieve low attention values observing that i recurrent connections facilitate the flow of information to neighboring representations ii in scenarios where such flow is restricted models tend to increase the magnitude of representations corresponding to impermissible tokens to compensate for reduced attention scores and iii models employ various alternative mechanisms that vary across random seeds see section related work recent scholarship has extensively debated the validity of attention mechanisms as explanatory tools notably jain et al identified alternative adversarial attention weights that can be applied post training to yield identical predictions thereby arguing that attention weights do not constitute valid explanations of model behavior

overview of methodological framework and data collection text classification also referred to as text categorization entails the assignment of textual data to predefined categories based on content analysis the primary objective is to develop classifiers through supervised learning algorithms utilizing labeled training datasets thereby enabling the automated classification of unlabeled test data complementary to this keyword extraction comprises four distinct processes tokenization filtering lemmatization and stemming furthermore sentiment analysis a subfield of natural language processing nlp focuses on identifying subjective information to gauge social sentiment within online discourse this technique is applicable across various domains including product and service reviews political opinion monitoring and the assessment of customer support performance collaboration is defined as the collective pursuit of a common goal through knowledge sharing and cooperative effort in the context of this study a student interaction network was analyzed to quantify collaboration and reveal underlying interaction patterns concurrently message content was mined to elucidate the nature of student conversations for pedagogical evaluation the following sections delineate the study s objectives data collection protocols and procedural framework specifically examining the impact of these methodologies on learning outcomes within an introductory computer science cs programming course the study contributes to the existing body of knowledge through the application of social network analysis sna metrics to student interaction data derived from graded discussion boards assessing the impact of this engagement strategy on both collaborative behaviors and academic performance the visualization of link dynamics within interaction networks to demonstrate the extent of student engagement data collection and preprocessing the preliminary analysis utilized data collected from seven online discussion boards across eight course sections encompassing a total of students enrolled in the cs course during the fall semester at a medium sized public university these discussion forums served as a platform for students to document learning experiences share insights pose and answer questions articulate challenges and provide feedback on peer contributions the resulting unstructured data was preprocessed into a transactional dataset within this formal representation students are denoted as s s s s dots s n while the instructor is denoted as t the study leveraged seven distinct discussion boards db d d dots d hosted on the learning management system lms

the following points outline specific pedagogical considerations and technical nuances associated with instruction in c first the declaration char p is frequently employed as a shorthand for defining both a character array and a pointer to it while syntactically valid in many contexts this practice can induce confusion among students when applied in scenarios where such a substitution is invalid second when p denotes a pointer to a character array the expression cout p outputs the string content rather than the memory address this behavior results from the overloading of the insertion operator for character pointers third the c str method of the c std string class returns a pointer to a character array residing in system memory this memory is transient and not guaranteed to remain valid indefinitely consequently passing this pointer directly to other functions is unsafe instead it is necessary to allocate new memory and transfer the data to a stable location before passing the pointer furthermore concurrent programming in c offers practical illustrations of theoretical concepts a primary example involves the interlacing of output when parent and child processes write to standard output cout without synchronization to address this students are introduced to two distinct strategies initially they learn a workaround utilizing existing constructs recognizing that cout a b is syntactic sugar for two separate operations cout a cout b which allows a process to be preempted mid line the solution involves using std ostringstream to construct a complete string prior to a single i o operation however output buffering may still disrupt the intended order of execution this can be mitigated by unbuffering the stream via cout std unitbuf prior to output generation or by utilizing std cerr which is unbuffered by default in advanced stages of the curriculum students are taught that employing a semaphore to protect access to cout provides a more robust and generalizable solution to the interlacing problem finally the instruction of c necessitates the introduction of alternative methods for tokenization and random number generation this is required because standard c functions such as strtok and rand are not thread safe and thus unsuitable for concurrent environments

algorithm st decoding with an adaptive policy input two integers k min and k max a set of nmt models m k and a sequence of thresholds ρ k for k min ≤ k ≤ k max while x x ̸ and y y ̸ do k ← x − y y top p top ← p k m k x y if k ≥ k max or k ≥ k min and p top ≥ ρ k y ← y ◦ y top ▷ write action else x ← x ◦ read ▷ read action while y y ̸ do y top p top ← p k max m k max x y y ← y ◦ y top ▷ write action return y ensemble of waitk models using the corresponding model m k with each waitk policies may not give us the best performance if we have a set of models trained independently with different waitk policies then we can apply ensemble of those models to improve the translation quality which is also used to improve the translation quality of fullsentence translation however there may be two issues to apply ensemble of all models the runtime for each prediction could be longer resulting in higher latency and the translation accuracy may be worse for the best model for one policy may give bad performance when doing inference with another policy to avoid these we propose to apply ensemble of the top models for each policy that is we first generate distribution with the top models independently with the same policy and then take the arithmetic average of the three distributions as the final token distribution at that step experiments datasets and models we conduct experiments on chinese → english zh → en and german → english de → en translation for zh → en we use nist corpus m sentence pairs as training set nist as dev set and nist as test set for de → en we use wmt parallel corpus for training newstest for validation and newstest for testing

the billion word corpus comprises complete articles thereby implicitly providing unaligned parallel data however such data may be unsuitable for unsupervised methodologies consequently we further trained sentvec embeddings using the twitter corpus established by pagliardini et al the hc title twitter configuration demonstrates superior performance compared to hc title and other competing models in group c we evaluate models against baselines where summaries constitute approximately of the input sentence length to ensure a fair comparison we set our target length to and included the leadp baseline while prior studies claim performance improvements over the leadn baseline table indicates that these models do not surpass the more appropriate leadp baseline notably our model is the sole unsupervised summarization system to outperform leadp on this dataset despite being trained exclusively on the article side it is observed that models trained on the title side hc title consistently outperform those trained on the article side hc article this outcome is expected as the former can generate headlines directly from the learned target distribution this finding underscores the significance of learning a summary language model even in the absence of supervised parallel source target data regarding the duc dataset table presents the corresponding results as this dataset is designated for testing only we directly transferred the hc article and hc title models from the headline generation corpus maintaining identical hyperparameters with the exception of length constraints the results indicate that our approach outperforms all previous methods and the leadc baseline these findings are consistent with those in table demonstrating the generalizability of our approach finally we conducted a human evaluation via pairwise comparison of system outputs following the methodology of west et al annotators were presented with the source sentence alongside headlines generated by our system and a competing method displayed in random order participants were asked to assess fidelity and fluency selecting from three options i the first headline is superior ii the second headline is superior or iii both headlines are of equal quality this procedure was repeated for instances with each instance evaluated by five annotators the final determination for each instance was established through majority voting

to address these limitations we model topics with dirichlet prior and propose a novel bidirectional adversarial topic model bat based on bidirectional adversarial training the proposed bat employs a generator network to learn the projection function from randomlysampled documenttopic distribution to documentword distribution moreover an encoder network is used to learn the inverse projection transforming a documentword distribution into a documenttopic distribution different from traditional models that often resort to analytic approximations bat employs a discriminator which aims to discriminate between real distribution pair and fake distribution pair thereby helps the networks generator and encoder to learn the twoway projections better during the adversarial training phase the supervision signal provided by the discriminator will guide the generator to construct a more realistic document and thus better capture the semantic patterns in text meanwhile the encoder network is also guided to generate a more reasonable topic distribution conditioned on specific documentword distributions finally to incorporate the word relatedness information captured by word embeddings we extend the bat by modeling each topic with a multivariate gaussian in the generator and propose the bidirectional adversarial topic model with gaussian gaussianbat the main contributions of the paper are • we propose a novel bidirectional adversarial topic bat model which is to our best knowledge the first attempt of using bidirectional adversarial training in neural topic modeling • we extend bat to incorporate the word relatedness information into the modeling process and propose the bidirectional adversarial topic model with gaussian gaussianbat • experimental results on three public datasets show that bat and gaussianbat outperform the stateoftheart approaches in terms of topic coherence measures the effectiveness of bat and gaussianbat is further verified in text clustering related work our work is related to two lines of research which are adversarial training and neural topic modeling adversarial training adversarial training first employed in generative adversarial network gan has been extensively studied from both theoretical and practical perspectives theoretically arjovsky and gulrajani proposed the wasserstein gan which employed the wasserstein distance between data distribution and generated distribution as the training objective

consequently given that human editors frequently modify both the lexical content and syntactic structure of sentences during the simplification process it is reasonable to anticipate that models designed for automatic sentence simplification should be capable of performing similar transformations evaluation data for sentence simplification the majority of existing datasets for sentence simplification ss zhu et al coster and kauchak hwang et al rely on automatic sentence alignments between corresponding articles in english wikipedia ew and simple english wikipedia sew in sew contributors are instructed to employ simplified language such as by shortening sentences or utilizing vocabulary from basic english ogden however yasseri et al observed that the syntactic complexity of sentences in sew remains comparable to that of ew furthermore xu et al noted that automatically aligned simple sentences often retain the complexity of their original counterparts typically involving only minor lexical substitutions or deletions while leaving the overall sentence structure intact a more diverse range of simplifications is available in the newsela corpus xu et al which comprises news articles manually simplified into up to five distinct levels of difficulty these parallel articles can be automatically aligned at the sentence level to facilitate the training and evaluation of simplification models alva manchego et al štajner et al nevertheless access to the newsela corpus is restricted by a licensing agreement that prohibits the public sharing of train test splits thereby hindering research reproducibility evaluating models based on automatically aligned sentences presents significant challenges particularly when only a single potentially noisy reference simplification is available for each source sentence to address these limitations xu et al compiled the turkcorpus a dataset containing original sentences from ew each accompanied by eight manually generated reference simplifications this dataset is partitioned into a validation set of sentences and a test set of sentences the availability of multiple references makes turkcorpus particularly suitable for automatic evaluation metrics that require such diversity such as bleu papineni et al and sari xu et al it should be noted however that the annotation guidelines for turkcorpus emphasized lexical paraphrasing annotators were instructed to reduce the frequency of difficult words or idioms without deleting content or splitting sentences

addressing pedagogical challenges prior to the completion of baccalaureate computing degrees is essential for cultivating a workforce that not only recognizes the critical importance of diversity equity and inclusion dei but also actively fosters inclusive environments and technologies furthermore such interventions are vital for improving retention rates among students from marginalized groups this study analyzes programming process data derived from two distinct introductory programming courses these contexts vary significantly across three dimensions the programming languages employed the pedagogical approaches utilized and the spoken languages of the participants in both settings non intrusive in situ keystroke data—comprising timestamps and specific key presses—were recorded as students engaged with programming assignments the research investigates variations in this data across the differing contexts and evaluates the generalizability of existing research regarding the prediction of course outcomes based on keystroke latencies the findings indicate notable differences in key usage frequency between the two contexts which can be partially attributed to variations in both spoken and programming languages while the results suggest that non intrusive programming process data can effectively predict course outcomes across multiple settings the predictive efficacy varies this variability likely stems from the linguistic and syntactic differences inherent to the specific contexts consequently context specific fine tuning of predictive models appears necessary to optimize accuracy learning and instruction occur within a diverse array of global contexts while traditional classroom settings with chalkboards may predominate in some regions other locales may prioritize small group collaboration or apprenticeship models the organization of teaching activities significantly influences learning outcomes this dynamic is equally pertinent to computing education where instruction is delivered globally across a multitude of spoken languages programming languages and pedagogical strategies resulting in varied learning outcomes beyond influencing outcomes contextual differences critically affect the transferability of research findings while results from one context may be immediately applicable to another certain approaches may possess limited utility in different settings such limitations may arise from the nature of the data and its production context the research methodology or the specific application of methodologies and libraries recently the analysis of student keystrokes has garnered significant attention within the computing education research community as a means to address these complexities

research in computing for global development seeks to enhance the quality of life worldwide by engineering technological solutions tailored to low income and underserved populations extending beyond the scope of affluent nations this field necessitates the design of systems that accommodate distinct resource constraints including unreliable power infrastructure limited or absent internet connectivity and varying levels of literacy consequently computer scientists frequently integrate methodologies from the social sciences to develop effective interventions however designing for diverse communities presents significant challenges particularly when designers lack direct experience with the target populations morgan ames in the charisma machine critiques the one laptop per child olpc initiative a nonprofit project launched in by the mit media lab the project aimed to distribute durable low cost hand cranked laptops to children in the global south drawing on fieldwork conducted in paraguay ames contends that the initiative failed to meet its objectives due to infrastructural deficits hardware reliability issues software limitations and a scarcity of curricular resources ames posits that charismatic technologies are deceptive as they obscure the complex political and choice laden nature of technological adoption and social change presenting them instead as straightforward processes furthermore even when the hardware functioned the project s educational vision often remained unrealized as users frequently prioritized entertainment over the intended pedagogical outcomes although ames s specific account of the olpc project has faced criticism for oversimplifying the narrative it remains a significant contribution to the discourse on the risks inherent in designing technology for global development it underscores that technology does not operate in a vacuum but is deeply embedded within complex social and historical contexts mitigating these risks requires more than mere humility or community inclusion in the design process many challenges are fundamentally sociopolitical or technopolitical rather than purely technical for instance pilot testing can inadvertently be perceived as favoritism toward specific groups these social tensions are particularly acute in the global south where historical legacies of imperialism and racial hierarchies continue to perpetuate and exacerbate social inequities and injustices

cs departments are an integral part of the pipeline for individuals to attain careers in computing as such cs departments have borne the brunt of burgeoning interest in computing careers and are facing record enrollments in computing majors and computing courses as enrollments increase it might be tempting for some cs faculty to rely on instructorcentered teaching methods that focus on oneway content delivery e g lecture use of slides etc that make managing an introductory course with hundreds of enrolled students seem easier however when students are passively learning such as listening to a lecture and taking notes their conceptual understanding of course content suffers studentcentered approaches which prioritize students’ experiences and development by increasing interactivity between and among students instructors and the course content are more effective in achieving student learning and professional skill development research suggests these approaches have a myriad of benefits for students in stem courses including enhancing student engagement and performance studentcentered approaches are also a cornerstone of efforts to broaden participation in computing because they are particularly beneficial for women who are vastly underrepresented in undergraduate computing majors given these positive outcomes the computer science education community has focused on promoting the adoption of studentcentered teaching methods among cs instructors for example the national center for women and information technology ncwit has developed the webresource engagecsedu which outlines a number of researchbased studentcentered teaching strategies for cs instructors and serves as an outlet for instructors to share peerreviewed lesson plans that utilize these practices although there is great momentum around studentcentered approaches to cs teaching a recent study suggests traditional teaching methods such as lecturing are used by most cs faculty and are used more frequently than studentcentered practices throughout cs higher education reasons cs faculty provide for not trying studentcentered practices include lack of time being satisfied with current teaching methods and logistical concerns such as lacking familiarity to implement the innovation

it is plausible that model performance may improve with the adoption of more robust classifiers however the current findings suggest that encoding context information at the granularity of specific word identities is neither practical nor necessary for contextual embeddings instead these models appear to prioritize the encoding of salient contextual features over word identities themselves discussion the results presented herein elucidate the mechanisms by which various contextual encoders distribute information across token embeddings within a sentence while definitive causal inferences regarding the relationship between model properties and observed patterns remain premature broad correlations can be established to guide future inquiry overall the deeper transformer based architectures of bert and gpt do not exhibit dramatic differences in information distribution relative to the shallower elmo model the primary distinction associated with elmo s recurrent architecture is a marginal attenuation of information—specifically regarding number and word identity—over longer distances whereas bert and gpt maintain robust encoding capabilities this outcome is consistent with the theoretical capacity of self attention mechanisms to capture long range dependencies conversely the minimal overall divergence observed in elmo is somewhat unexpected these patterns imply that deeper transformer architectures may not be essential for encoding and distributing such contextual information except perhaps over substantial distances furthermore bert and elmo which utilize bidirectional context demonstrate greater similarity to one another than to gpt this is particularly evident in their preferential encoding of number and animacy over gender as well as their strong encoding of number for nouns in contrast gpt exhibits a more uniform distribution of noun information at least regarding subject nouns this divergence suggests that the distinction between bidirectional and unidirectional context exerts a more significant influence on the distribution of contextual information than does model depth or architectural type additionally gpt s comparatively weaker encoding of object information relative to subject and verb information indicates that its left to right architecture may prioritize earlier linguistic elements over later ones regarding the two bidirectional models bert s specific architectural properties confer advantages beyond mere robustness to distance manifesting as distinct selectivity patterns specifically bert discards subject gender information earlier than elmo retains object animacy information over longer distances and attenuates verb tense information more rapidly

this algorithm is used for classification and regression problems that is based on multiple decision trees each decision tree is constructed by using features of a dataset that are some randomly selected random forest consists of multiple decision trees which are used for model output generation each tree is used for classification task and the desired output is performed by aggregating the majority votes of all the trees constructed with the input samples support vector machine svm is a machine learning classifier used for linear nonlinear classification and regression problems this algorithm is used to find out decision boundary by the support vectors optimal hyperplane to separate output into different classes this task is done using the kernel trick to separate data points into classes by drawing ndimensional hyper planes svm has been used in threatening language detection however it is not recommended to use svm especially with sparse datasets due to high memory and poor intractability previous studies reported that svm outperformed other classifiers to detect threatening speech in tweets adaptive boosting algorithm known as adaboost classifier is an iterative ensemble method which is widely used to solve classification and regression tasks adaboost provides better results because it concatenates multiple machine learning classifiers and reassigns all the weights to the input samples particularly this algorithm assigns higher weights to misclassified examples to obtain a high accuracy strong classifier adaboost quickly overfits while dealing with highly noisy datasets in contrast it provides better results in case a dataset contains less noise patterns finally if the irrelevant features are used to train the model this algorithm provides insignificant results a multilayer perceptron mlp is a feedforward artificial neural network which is used for solving regression and classification tasks this algorithm is mostly used for supervised learning tasks and generates a set of outputs from a set of inputs the algorithm uses backpropagation for training the model which contains three layers i an input layer ii a hidden layer and iii a fully connected output layer all the data instances are fed as inputs then the dot product of the input instances with the weights are fed to the hidden layer and passed through an activation function finally the output of the activation function is further multiplied dot product with weights which are pushed forward to the output layer that provides the label for the classification task

related work in regularization and augmentation recent literature has explored various regularization strategies to enhance model robustness in natural language processing gururangan et al chen et al and yang et al employed variational autoencoders vaes within sequence to sequence frameworks for tasks such as text classification and sequential labeling in the realm of adversarial training miyato et al applied virtual adversarial training to the text domain by introducing perturbations to word embeddings hierarchical supervision has also been utilized specifically yang et al a leveraged hierarchical structures to propagate supervision from higher level to lower level labels furthermore consistency regularization has been applied to unlabeled data through techniques such as back translation and tf idf based word replacement xie et al clark et al introduced cross view training for unlabeled data utilizing auxiliary prediction modules that process restricted views of the input e g partial sentences to align with the predictions of a full model processing the complete input interpolation based regularizers interpolation based regularizers such as mixup have recently gained prominence in both supervised zhang et al verma et al a and semi supervised berthelot et al verma et al b learning contexts particularly for image data these methods generate virtual training samples by overlaying input images and linearly combining their corresponding labels achieving state of the art performance across diverse image classification tasks and network architectures various extensions of these mixing strategies have been developed including interpolation within the input space zhang et al hybrid approaches combining interpolation with cutout operations yun et al and interpolation within hidden space representations verma et al a c despite their success in computer vision these techniques remain underexplored in natural language processing nlp this limitation arises primarily from the discrete nature of textual input spaces which typically rely on one hot encodings rather than the continuous rgb values found in images as well as the inherent structural complexity of language data augmentation for text in scenarios where labeled data is scarce data augmentation serves as a critical technique for expanding training datasets in computer vision standard augmentation practices include geometric transformations shifting zooming rotation flipping and photometric adjustments distortion hue shifting perez and wang however applying analogous augmentation strategies to text presents significant challenges due to the intricate syntactic and semantic structures inherent in natural language which render simple perturbations often ineffective or semantically invalid

the key idea behind our proposed interactive mrc imrc is to restrict the document context that a model observes at one time concretely we split a supporting document into its component sentences and withhold these sentences from the model given a question the model must issue commands to observe sentences in the withheld set we equip models with actions such as ctrl f to search for matches to a query within partially observed documents a model searches iteratively conditioning each command on the input question and the sentences it has observed previously thus our task requires models to ‘feed themselves’ rather than spoonfeeding them with information this casts mrc as a sequential decisionmaking problem amenable to reinforcement learning rl our proposed approach lies outside of traditional qa work the idea can be applied to almost all existing mrc datasets and models to study interactive informationseeking behavior as a case study in this paper we repurpose two well known related corpora with different difficulty levels for our imrc task squad and newsqa table shows an example of a model performing interactive mrc on these datasets naturally our reframing makes the mrc problem harder however we believe the added demands of imrc more closely match weblevel qa and may lead to deeper comprehension of documents’ content the main contributions of this work are as follows we describe a method to make mrc datasets interactive and formulate the new task as an rl problem we develop a baseline agent that combines a top performing mrc model and two stateoftheart rl optimization algorithms and test it on imrc tasks we conduct experiments on several variants of imrc and discuss the significant challenges posed by our setting related works skipreading is an existing setting in which mrc models read partial documents concretely these methods assume that not all tokens in the input sequence are equally useful and therefore learn to skip irrelevant tokens since skipping decisions are discrete the models are often optimized by the reinforce algorithm

beside the chart parser approach there is also a long tradition of transitionbased parsers recently however with the advent of powerful neural encoders such as lstms the focus has been switched more towards effective modeling of correlations in the input’s latent space as the output structures are nothing but a function of the input various neural network models have been proposed to effectively encode the dense input representations and correlations and have achieved stateoftheart parsing results to enforce the structural consistency existing neural parsing methods either employ a transitionbased algorithm or a globally optimized chartparsing algorithm meanwhile researchers also attempt to convert the constituency parsing problem into tasks that can be solved in alternative ways for instance fern´andezgonz´alez and martins transform the phrase structure into a special form of dependency structure such a dependency structure however requires certain corrections while converting back to the corresponding constituency tree g´omez and vilares and shen et al propose to map the constituency tree for a sentence of n tokens into a sequence of n − labels or scalars based on the depth or height of the lowest common ancestors between pairs of consecutive tokens in addition methods like apply the sequencetosequence framework to “translate” a sentence into the linearized form of its constituency tree while being trivial and simple parsers of this type do not guarantee structural correctness because the syntax of the linearized form is not constrained during tree decoding our approach differs from previous work in that it represents the constituency structure as a series of pointing representations and has a relatively simpler cross entropy based learning objective the pointing representations can be computed in parallel and can be efficiently converted into a full constituency tree using a topdown algorithm

end we introduce h urricane e mo a dataset of disasterrelated tweets in english streamed during hurricanes harvey irma and maria which were devastating tropical storms occurring in the atlantic hurricane season our samples are annotated with finegrained emotions derived from the plutchik wheel of emotions a welldefined ontology of emotion classes commonly used in computational social science to measure interannotator agreement on finegrained emotion labels we conceptualize the p lutchik e motion a greement pea metric pea is intuitively grounded our human evaluation shows workers agree culminates in averaged absolute accuracy

abstract this paper investigates the nature of homework engagement in introductory computer science cs courses contrasting it with practices in other stem disciplines to determine how these distinctions influence student expectations and experiences while think aloud protocols may offer limited data for addressing these broad research questions alternative methodologies—including longitudinal interviews recitation observations homework diaries and focus groups—are proposed as viable avenues for future inquiry the current study presents observations derived from think aloud interviews with fourteen cs students engaged in programming assignments the analysis specifically targets information seeking behaviors to elucidate how students navigate homework tasks and interpret available support structures and information sources future research will aim to quantify the prevalence of these behavioral patterns across the broader cs student population furthermore this work addresses the challenges inherent in collaborative programming such as the absence of real time collaboration features development environment setup latency and hardware or operating system heterogeneity while existing platforms like google docs and google colaboratory effectively mitigate these issues for text processing and data analysis respectively they lack the capacity to support the development of interactive applications to explore this gap a web based literate programming system was deployed over a three month period within an introductory application development course data collected from weekly questionnaires focus groups involving students and teaching assistants and keystroke level logs were analyzed to examine the nuances of collaborative programming within computational notebooks the findings indicate the existence of distinct collaboration patterns which vary both between student pairs and within pairs over time recognizing these dynamic patterns is essential for the design of future computational notebooks tailored to collaborative programming assignments marcel borowski johannes zagermann clemens n klokmose harald reiterer and roman rädle

research within the augmentative and alternative communication aac domain has over recent decades remained largely confined to a narrow scope of natural language processing nlp tasks while significant efforts have been undertaken to enhance device usability and communication efficiency by integrating geographic temporal and contextual data into word prediction systems these advancements have not yielded substantial improvements in communication rates or device retention among aac users consequently future research must prioritize experimental aac designs that leverage a broader spectrum of nlp applications to more effectively address the needs of this population a critical prerequisite for advancing nlp applications in aac is the acquisition or aggregation of representative training datasets currently the most comprehensive resource containing conversational data from aac users is the aac and nonaac workplace corpus which comprises transcripts of over hours of interactions involving eight participants utilizing aac devices in workplace settings however a single corpus of conversational transcripts is insufficient for developing statistical models capable of delivering significant benefits in contrast to the scarcity of transcribed conversational data the landscape of audio datasets has expanded considerably to include speech samples representing a diverse array of language disorders several corpora are available featuring speech from adults with parkinson s disease while others focus on the dysarthric speech associated with cerebral palsy and amyotrophic lateral sclerosis als furthermore the corpus developed by little et al encompasses speech samples from individuals with a heterogeneous mix of language disorders these audio datasets also demonstrate demographic diversity across age groups for instance the child s pathological speech database includes data from children diagnosed with autism spectrum disorder or other language impairments similarly the cslu autism speech corpus contains audio data derived from speech pathology evaluations of children conducted between and totaling hours of audio and utterances notably among the datasets cited the aac and nonaac workplace corpus remains the sole resource that specifically includes individuals communicating via aac devices

this study presents a quantitative comparison grounded in the reproducibility of methods based on information publicly available in the literature rather than on proprietary implementations accessible only to the original authors by adhering to a uniform implementation framework we were able to assess the practical utility of published descriptions while simultaneously evaluating qualitative attributes such as implementation simplicity and extensibility we acknowledge that evaluating diverse approaches on a common dataset may introduce bias against methods specifically optimized for distinct application domains for instance the system proposed by shi and li originally designed for hotel reviews demonstrated significantly lower performance in our experiments compared to the authors reported results likely due to this domain mismatch to address these challenges we evaluated a range of existing multilingual sentiment analysis approaches across two widely recognized datasets representing key application domains the cornell movie review dataset comprising positive and negative reviews and the blitzer dataset specifically the amazon reviews for books and dvds these publicly available datasets are standard benchmarks in the field and offer sufficient diversity to test methodological robustness our implementation of existing approaches utilized a variety of tools and programming languages including libsvm weka java and python the results reveal a notable discrepancy between the accuracy figures reported by original authors and those obtained in our experiments we attribute this divergence primarily to insufficient methodological detail in the original publications which hindered exact reproduction furthermore direct comparability is sometimes limited by differences in experimental settings toolchains and linguistic resources while some evaluated approaches were developed for non english languages the state of the art methods in the literature employed diverse machine learning classifiers e g svm light weka libsvm whereas our experiments were standardized using libsvm and weka

within a code paragraph using javascript and arbitrary data storage is managed by a data paragraph utilizing json notably body paragraphs can be configured for fullscreen display thereby functioning as standalone web applications furthermore sections facilitate the logical grouping of paragraphs to organize the overall document architecture similar to google colaboratory codestrates supports real time collaboration extending beyond the editing of computational code to include the reprogramming and extension of the notebook environment itself the application of computational notebooks in educational settings has been examined across various disciplines including artificial intelligence and chemistry for instance o hara et al leveraged jupyter to teach artificial intelligence through accessible interfaces while srnec et al utilized notebooks to elucidate the concept of reciprocal space for undergraduates by converting real space vectors both studies highlighted the pedagogical value of integrating theoretical lecture materials with executable code and equations additionally other researchers have investigated the use of notebooks in computing education specifically regarding the creation and automated grading of assignments despite these advancements the utilization of notebooks for collaborative application development remains largely underexplored while the dynamics of student groups working on programming assignments have been frequently analyzed within the context of pair programming existing literature often focuses on constrained role based interactions in their comprehensive meta analysis umapathy and ritzhaupt concluded that properly implemented pair programming can positively influence students assignment grades examination scores and persistence in computer programming courses however whereas prior research has predominantly examined pair programming—characterized by distinct roles such as the driver and the navigator —this work investigates collaboration within an unconstrained symmetric setting where students autonomously determine their collaborative strategies and workload distribution to gain insights into the deployment of a literate computing platform during the development of interactive applications the system was introduced in an introductory human computer interaction hci course the curriculum was divided into two components the first consisted of weekly lectures covering fundamental hci topics such as human visual perception and design principles like affordances and constraints the second comprised practical weekly tutorials and assignments designed to complement the theoretical instruction

specific word type was assigned a value of if the word was present in the document and otherwise latent dirichlet allocation lda since lda produces feature vectors containing continuous probability values representing topic membership these values were converted to binary format a threshold was calculated by dividing the sum of all topic probabilities by the total number of established topics any probability value equal to or exceeding this threshold was mapped to values below the threshold were mapped to linguistic inquiry and word count liwc this method generates feature vectors
