crossmodal language generation tasks such as automatic image captioning are significantly impeded by the current scarcity of multilingual resources while extensive labeled datasets exist for english—including flickr young et al a mscoco lin et al and conceptual captions sharma et al —annotated data for other languages remains critically limited elliott et al specifically data availability for a few non english languages is approximately two orders of magnitude lower than that for english while many other languages lack labeled data entirely this paper investigates the optimal methodology for scaling crossmodal language generation systems to new languages at web scale given a large annotated monolingual dataset e g conceptual captions and a baseline machine translation system we focus on automatic image captioning as a representative task for crossmodal generation a domain where bidirectional consistency cannot be readily exploited as a learning signal unlike in text only modalities where parallel data is more abundant and the problem is more extensively studied artetxe et al within this framework we evaluate three distinct approaches train generate translate tgt a model is trained on existing english image captioning datasets to generate english captions which are subsequently translated into the target language translate train generate ttg existing english captioning data is first translated into the target language using machine translation capabilities a model is then trained on this translated data to generate captions directly in the target language stabilized ttg with english pivot this approach refines the ttg method by training a model on a combination of original english gold data and translated target language silver data the model generates english captions conditioned on the image followed by target language captions conditioned on both the image and the generated english caption in this configuration english serves as a pivot language between the input modality and the target output thereby stabilizing the training process and mitigating error propagation

although the task formulation shares certain similarities with existing approaches our work offers a distinct perspective characterized by four key differentiations first while the premises in tabfact are derived from wikipedia tables and exhibit homogeneity—where columns possess structural redundancy and uniform entry types allowing for inference based on column wide attributes—our dataset features heterogeneous premises second whereas tabfact is limited to binary classification entailment and contradiction we posit that inference is inherently non binary necessitating a third undetermined neutral category third in contrast to the predominantly numerical or comparative reasoning required by tabfact hypotheses our dataset demands multifaceted reasoning capabilities finally the inclusion of the alpha and alpha subsets enables a rigorous examination of annotation and domain specific artifacts regarding artifacts recent advancements in pretrained transformer based models have demonstrated performance surpassing human benchmarks on various natural language inference nli tasks however extensive research indicates that these models often achieve high scores by exploiting spurious patterns or artifacts within the data rather than through genuine reasoning consequently the development of datasets that facilitate the controlled study of such artifacts is imperative while current strategies frequently rely on adversarial annotation or input rewriting we argue for the systematic construction of test sets designed to evaluate model robustness across specific artifact dimensions in conclusion we introduce info tabs a high quality nli dataset comprising heterogeneous semi structured premises and natural language hypotheses our analysis confirms that the dataset encompasses a diverse spectrum of inference types furthermore info tabs includes multiple test sets specifically engineered to challenge models that rely on superficial input label correlations thereby necessitating genuine reasoning extensive experimental results demonstrate that derivatives of several prominent model classes find this new inference task significantly challenging

experimental results show that upsa achieves a new stateoftheart unsupervised performance in terms of both automatic metrics and human evaluation in summary our contributions are as follows • we propose the novel upsa framework that addresses unsupervised paraphrasing by simulated annealing • we design a searching objective function for paraphrasing that not only considers language fluency and semantic similarity but also explicitly models expression diversity between a paraphrase and the input • we propose a copy mechanism as one of our search actions of simulated annealing to address rare words • we achieve the stateoftheart performance on four benchmark datasets compared with previous unsupervised paraphrase generators largely reducing the performance gap between unsupervised and supervised paraphrasing we outperform most domainadapted paraphrase generators and even a supervised one on the wikianswers dataset related work in early years paraphrasing was typically accomplished by exploiting linguistic knowledge and statistical machine translation methods recently deep neural networks have become a prevailing approach to text generation where paraphrasing is often formulated as a supervised encodingdecoding problem for example using stacked residual lstm and the transformer model unsupervised paraphrasing is an emerging research direction in the field of nlp the encodes more local syntactic information at lower layers and more global semantic information at higher layers peters et al find that encoders differ in encoding strength for semantic features but all encode these features strongly where possible hewitt and manning provide evidence that contextual encoders capture sentencelevel hierarchical syntactic structures in their representations

abstract although block based programming environments have demonstrated efficacy in facilitating learning by scaffolding concepts through the abstraction of syntax they often fail to address the syntactic challenges learners encounter when transitioning to production grade text based languages dual modality environments which offer simultaneous representations of programming constructs in both block and text formats allow for seamless transitions between these modalities such environments have the potential to bridge the gap between block based conceptualization and text based implementation by presenting identical programs in both formats dual modality systems not only scaffold syntax acquisition but also foster the association between visual and textual representations thereby supporting cognitive chunking and abstraction this study investigates the efficacy of dual modality programming environments and instruction in supporting the acquisition of programming knowledge specifically in comparison to traditional text based pedagogical approaches within introductory computer science cs courses the research was conducted at a large public university over two consecutive week semesters involving a total of students under the instruction of a single faculty member the first semester n served as a control group utilizing traditional text based instruction the second semester n functioned as the intervention group employing dual modality instruction alongside a custom developed dual modality integrated development environment ide plugin learning outcomes were assessed using the scs a computer science inventory assessment and course examination items which were categorized as either definitional code reading or code writing tasks additionally student perceptions regarding block based text based and dual modality instruction were evaluated through weekly surveys administered at the beginning midpoint and conclusion of the course the central hypothesis posited that dual modality instruction and tools would enhance students ability to chunk and abstract code segments resulting in superior performance on examinations and the scs assessment for the intervention group relative to the control group context and rationale in higher education introductory computer science curricula instruction traditionally relies on text based languages prevalent in industry and research however even for languages with relatively simple structures syntax errors and semantic complexities present significant barriers to novice learners consequently block based programming environments have gained traction in k– introductory computing to mitigate these challenges despite their benefits empirical evidence suggests that students often struggle to transfer skills from block based environments to text based programming contexts this study aims to address this transition gap through the implementation of dual modality instructional strategies

recent scholarship has introduced several significant datasets aimed at addressing the challenge of fake news detection across diverse linguistic contexts notably a study presented bend the truth a dataset comprising urdu language news articles categorized as either real or fake this corpus encompasses five thematic domains sports entertainment business technology and health the dataset consists of authentic news articles with an equal distribution of articles per domain these articles were manually curated from news portals across the united states the united kingdom india and pakistan covering the period from january to december the study provides a comprehensive account of the data collection and annotation methodologies employed in parallel another study introduced banfakenews a dataset focused on the bengali language which serves as the official language of bangladesh and is spoken by over million native speakers across bangladesh and india this dataset classifies news into three categories clickbait satire and a combined category of real and fake news including their respective headlines the corpus covers topics which are further aggregated into broader news domains such as sports politics crime and technology it comprises authentic articles sourced from mainstream bangladeshi news websites with an average length of words per article in contrast fake news articles were collected from the fact checking platforms jaachai com and bdfactcheck com averaging words per article due to the complexity of the annotation task detailed metadata—including source publication time topic and the relationship between headline and article content—was provided for a subset of articles the labeling process was conducted by undergraduate students with backgrounds in computer science and engineering or software engineering who manually annotated the news sources and classified the headline article relationship as either related or non related furthermore a recent contribution to the field includes the first stance detection dataset in the persian language designed to facilitate article claim and headline claim stance classification tasks to support this initiative the researchers developed a web based tool for the systematic collection of claims and corresponding news articles

introduction of the emotyda dataset to advance research in multimodal dialogue act classification dac augmented by user emotion we present emotyda a novel dataset comprising short video recordings of dialogue conversations this corpus features manual annotations for dialogue acts da alongside pre existing emotional labels data collection in order to assemble a corpus of emotionally rich conversations suitable for investigating their impact on dac we conducted a comprehensive review of existing multimodal emotion recognition er datasets our initial survey identified several prominent resources including youtube morency et al moud pérez rosas et al iemocap busso et al ictmmmo wöllmer et al cmu mosi zadeh et al cmu mosei zadeh et al and meld poria et al following this review we selected the iemocap and meld datasets for further investigation this selection was necessitated by the nature of the alternative datasets which predominantly consist of monologues such as opinion pieces and product reviews in contrast our research objectives require task independent dyadic or multiparty conversations to fully assess the potential of emotion aware dac furthermore neither iemocap nor meld includes annotations for dialogue acts conversely established benchmark dac datasets present different limitations the switchboard swbd godfrey et al and icsi meeting recorder shriberg et al corpora contain text and audio based conversations but lack emotional annotations the trains dataset heeman and allen consists solely of text based dialogues without emotional tags while the hcrc map task corpus anderson et al includes both audio and transcripts it is restricted to task oriented interactions and lacks emotion annotations it is important to note that task oriented conversations typically constrain the diversity of dialogue acts and emotional expressions commonly found in task independent settings to the best of our knowledge at the time of this writing no sizable open access multimodal dialogue dataset existed that provided concurrent annotations for both dialogue acts and emotions

two primary hypotheses may account for the observed score advantage collaborative cheating and enhanced student confidence or preparation derived from the flexibility of asynchronous scheduling while further investigation is required the contribution of collaborative cheating to this advantage is not unexpected anecdotal evidence suggests that student communication regarding examinations is prevalent and attempts to entirely prevent such interaction may be impractical nevertheless it is reassuring that the impact of this communication appears modest within the context of this course indicating that asynchronous examinations can serve as a reasonably secure alternative to traditional synchronous formats the primary mechanism for mitigating this score advantage appears to be randomization extrapolation from our data suggests that if all problems were fully parameterized to generate unique versions for each student the aggregate score advantage would decrease from percentage points to percentage points furthermore the examinations analyzed utilized problem pools minimally in over of instances all students received the same question generator consequently future research should prioritize investigating the extent to which the implementation of problem pools reduces score advantages several limitations constrain the generalizability of these findings to other contexts the study relied on data from a single large highly selective research university with a student population that was predominantly male and domestic to the united states additionally the assessment format was limited to short answer and multiple choice questions replicating this research across diverse institutional environments would provide valuable insights into the efficacy of asynchronous examinations in varied settings these results hold potential implications for other contexts involving asynchronous assessment with the recent proliferation of commercial online proctoring services that offer similar scheduling flexibility it is plausible that increased randomization would similarly mitigate score advantages in those environments although the precise magnitude of such effects remains uncertain the authors acknowledge the invaluable assistance of david mussulman and carleen sacris in facilitating the synchronous examination schedule at the cbtf morris et al introduced a generalization of graph based neural networks termed k dimensional gnns k gnns capable of incorporating higher order graph structures across multiple scales similarly cao et al proposed a novel multichannel graph neural network designed to learn alignment oriented knowledge graph embeddings by robustly encoding two knowledge graphs through multiple channels more recently several studies have applied graph based neural networks to aspect sentiment classification asc for instance hou et al and zhang et al a constructed graph convolutional networks gcns over sentence dependency trees to leverage syntactic information and word dependencies thereby enhancing aspect specific sentence representations for asc distinct from the aforementioned approaches this paper proposes a novel cooperative graph attention networks cogan framework designed to capture document level sentiment preference information within the asc task to the best of our knowledge this represents the first attempt to integrate such information into asc cooperative graph attention networks cogan in this section we formally define the aspect sentiment classification asc task given a document d comprising a set of sentences s s dots s i the objective is to predict the sentiment polarity ell for a specific aspect a k where k in dots k within a given sentence s i where i in dots i this is achieved by automatically learning an aspect specific sentence representation r i here i denotes the total number of sentences in the document and k represents the number of aspects identified within the document to address this task we propose the cogan approach which utilizes two distinct types of graph attention networks gat to incorporate twofold preference information the overall architecture of cogan illustrated in figure comprises five primary components encoding block intra aspect consistency modeling block inter aspect tendency modeling block interaction block softmax decoding block following the methodology of pontiki et al all aspects within each sentence are unrolled at the document level consequently a sentence containing multiple aspects is represented multiple times in succession with each instance corresponding to a specific aspect

it is posited that the development of advanced models necessitates evaluation metrics capable of identifying subtle semantic discrepancies this study presents a generalized framework for assessing conditional text generation specifically engineered to detect factual inconsistencies between generated outputs and their corresponding inputs the proposed methodology comprises three sequential stages a question generation qg model formulates a set of inquiries based on the generated text question answering qa models address these inquiries utilizing both the original input and the generated text and a quality score is derived by calculating the similarity between the respective answers this approach capitalizes on recent advancements in qa and qg to facilitate the creation and resolution of human readable contextually relevant questions devlin et al song et al the framework requires only access to a question answering dataset for training the constituent models and is adaptable to any modality supporting qa capabilities including text images and knowledge graphs applying this framework we introduce qags question answering and generation for summarization a metric designed to evaluate the factual consistency of abstractive document summaries in comparison to conventional automatic metrics such as rouge lin qags demonstrates a significantly stronger correlation with human assessments of factuality for instance on the cnn dailymail summarization task qags achieves a pearson correlation coefficient of whereas rouge yields furthermore qags establishes new state of the art results in evaluating summary factuality surpassing recently proposed natural language inference nli models kryscinski et al b an ablation study was conducted to analyze the robustness of qags the results indicate that the metric remains resilient to variations in the quality of the underlying qg and qa models the domain of application and the volume of questions generated even under the most constrained ablation conditions qags maintains a superior correlation with human judgments relative to other automatic metrics in summary the primary contributions of this work are the introduction of qags an automatic model based evaluation metric for quantifying the factual consistency of model generated text the compilation of a novel dataset comprising human judgments regarding the factual consistency of model generated summaries across two distinct summarization datasets

the distinctions outlined above exemplify the broader phenomenon of construal a concept we posit has been insufficiently addressed within the field of natural language processing nlp we argue that a rigorous acknowledgment of construal would establish a unified framework for resolving a diverse array of issues pertaining to meaning and linguistic variation thereby facilitating the development of systems that more accurately emulate natural language this paper surveys the theoretical and empirical landscape concerning construal phenomena and advocates for their significance in nlp following a clarification of the terminology employed herein § we delineate key dimensions of construed meaning § and subsequently elaborate on the underlying mechanisms of construal § a series of case studies § demonstrates how various forms of construal pose challenges to current nlp systems the discussion concludes with a summary of findings and recommendations for addressing these challenges § meaning and construal our conceptualization of construal and its intrinsic relationship with meaning is grounded in both frame based and cognitive semantic traditions the premise that linguistic units evoke background scenes alongside specific perspectives on those scenes is encapsulated by fillmore s assertion that meanings are relativized to scenes this principle carries implications extending beyond the mere assignment of distinct semantic roles as illustrated in example a as langacker p observes any given situation can be viewed in multiple if not infinitely many ways starting from the same basic conceptual content we can form an endless variety of specific conceptions by making alternate choices in regard to the many dimensions of construal this perspective on linguistic meaning—which may be characterized as inherently multivalent—offers greater flexibility than many theoretical and computational approaches particularly truth conditional models that equate meaning with static database facts the visual domain provides a more apt analogy a photographic or artistic representation of a scene may vary significantly based on vantage point viewing distance focal elements color and lighting langacker talmy furthermore context plays a critical role for instance a painting displayed in a preschool may be interpreted differently than the same work exhibited in a museum just as there is no single objective context independent depiction of a visual scene there exist numerous valid modes of articulating ideas through language

to address the challenge of label discrepancy we propose a collapsed dependency transfer mechanism this approach facilitates the transfer of label dependency information from source to target domains by abstracting domain specific labels into domain independent representations and modeling the dependencies among these abstracted labels furthermore computing emission scores—specifically word label similarity in this context—presents significant difficulties conventional few shot models such as the prototypical network snell et al typically derive label representations by averaging the embeddings of support examples however these representations often cluster closely within the embedding space leading to misclassification to mitigate this yoon et al introduced tapnet which learns to project embeddings into a space where instances of different labels are distinctly separated building upon this foundation we adapt this methodology for slot tagging and further enhance label representation by leveraging the semantic information inherent in label names we posit that label names frequently share semantic relationships with slot words thereby aiding in the modeling of word label similarity e g the semantic correlation between the word rain and the label weather to exploit this semantic potential and ensure robust separation of label representations we introduce label enhanced tapnet ltapnet this model constructs an embedding projection space grounded in label name semantics ensuring that label representations are both well separated and aligned with the embeddings of both label names and slot words similarity calculations are subsequently performed within this projected space additionally we incorporate a pairwise embedding mechanism to represent words within their domain specific contexts extensive experiments conducted under one shot and five shot settings for both slot tagging and named entity recognition demonstrate that our model achieves significant performance improvements over strong few shot learning baselines ablation studies confirm that these gains are attributable to both the ltapnet module and the collapsed dependency transfer mechanism furthermore detailed analysis of label dependencies indicates that our approach captures non trivial information outperforming traditional rule based transition models

improvements row k in summary explicit feedback proves more effective for enhancing natural language generation nlg performance as reconstruction metrics and automatic scores directly correlate with generation quality in contrast implicit feedback offers greater utility for improving natural language understanding nlu where the made mechanism effectively captures salient information to construct more robust nlu models these findings are consistent with the observations reported by su et al qualitative analysis tables and present selected examples comparing the proposed model against the baseline within the primal and dual cycle frameworks as outlined in algorithm the primal cycle initiates with semantic frames x transforms the representation via the nlg model f and subsequently feeds the generated sentences into the nlu model g to compute loss by comparing the output with the original input the examples in table demonstrate that f g y theta y to x theta x to y successfully reconstructs x confirming the proposed method s ability to preserve original semantics conversely the dual cycle begins with natural language utterances the results in table indicate that the proposed method maintains semantic integrity throughout the training cycle ensuring that g y theta y to x remains equivalent to x collectively this qualitative analysis confirms that integrating duality into the objective function and employing joint training simultaneously improves the performance of both nlu and nlg tasks future work while the proposed framework is theoretically sound and empirically validated its formulation remains contingent upon specific data characteristics not all nlu datasets are suitable for adaptation as nlg tasks nor vice versa additionally although the framework theoretically permits fully unsupervised training of dual models experimental results indicate that this approach can be unstable and difficult to optimize consequently future research should focus on developing more robust dual learning algorithms and leveraging advanced techniques such as pretrained models and adversarial learning to enhance the stability and efficacy of the proposed framework

the following analysis evaluates the performance of various language models and baseline systems across four key metrics correctness sentence completeness grammaticality and alignment with the majority option the assessment focuses on responses to specific queries regarding historical events scientific principles and cryptographic protocols query the consequences of sadat s policies question what was the outcome of sadat s attempts to bring islamists into the political fold baseline performance the coqa baseline provided a factually incorrect response stating that sadat was rewarded by islamists the quac baseline offered a fragmented and incoherent response model performance pgnpre gpt and dgpt standard these models generated incomplete sentences e g sadat was rewarded piety and charity that lacked grammatical structure and factual accuracy dgpt with optimization o this variant produced the most accurate and complete response sadat was assassinated this answer correctly identifies the historical outcome forms a complete sentence and adheres to grammatical standards query safety protocols for liquid oxygen question from what type of materials must liquid oxygen be separated baseline performance the coqa and sts bert baselines correctly identified combustible materials but failed to form complete sentences the quac baseline provided an irrelevant description of nitrogen and oxygen s physical properties model performance pgnpre gpt and dgpt standard these models generated grammatically incomplete fragments e g it must be separated from combustible dgpt with optimization o this model generated a fully formed grammatically correct and factually accurate sentence liquid oxygen must be separated from combustible materials query cryptographic foundations of diffie hellman question on what type of exponentiation does the diffie–hellman key exchange depend baseline performance the coqa and sts bert baselines correctly identified modular exponentiation but failed to construct complete sentences the quac baseline provided a general description of public key cryptography without directly answering the specific question model performance pgnpre gpt and dgpt standard these models produced incomplete responses e g it depends on modular dgpt with optimization o this model successfully generated a complete and grammatically correct sentence it depends on modular exponentiation query sadat s political motivations question what was sadat seeking by releasing islamists from prison baseline performance the coqa baseline provided a complete and accurate response political support in his struggle against leftists the quac baseline data was incomplete in the provided text model performance the evaluation for this specific query was cut off in the source data however the trend indicates that the optimized dgpt model generally outperforms standard variants in generating complete grammatical and factually accurate sentences conclusion the analysis demonstrates that while baseline models and standard pre trained variants often struggle with sentence completeness and grammatical structure the optimized dgpt model denoted as dgpt with ss o consistently generates responses that are factually correct grammatically sound and syntactically complete this suggests that the optimization process significantly enhances the model s ability to adhere to formal linguistic standards while maintaining factual accuracy

this paper aims at exploring the explicit guidance to help the variational response generator exploit persona information hidden in the nonstructured contents produced by the users by utilizing intuitive characteristics of personalized conversations for model training the contributions of this paper can be summarized as follows • a personaaware variational response generator is proposed to exploit persona while modeling the conversations • based on the model two regularization terms are presented to guide the model in encoding user information into the latent variables and converging to userspecific responses • three discriminative metrics are further introduced to evaluate the capabilities of personaaware response generators approach based on the current progress on the development of latent variable models we propose a personaaware variational response generator to automatically exploit persona from conversations and utilize such personal information to model the future conversation besides given that personal information can be exploited as optimization guidance to better modeling persona we further introduce two regularization terms to guide the model learning in the following section we first describe the general structure of pagenerator and then explain the two additional regularization terms personaaware variational response generator utilizing latent variables in response generation has become a widely accepted methodology in nrg due to their bayesian essence it helps to deal with external knowledge efficiently e g persona therefore our proposed model is built based on the generation model with latent variables the overall architecture of the single turn personaaware variational response generator proposed in this paper is illustrated in figure let q r u stand for the query the reply and the corresponding user of r respectively and e u stands for the embedding of user u a bidirectional lstm is first employed to encode the query and reply into fixed size vectors h q and h r after that the prior network parametrized by θ takes u e h q as inputs to generate the distribution p θ z q u of latent variable z meanwhile h q h r are fed into a posterior network parameterized by φ to compute q φ z q r

besides recent work proposed to learn a separate network to predict sample weights data imbalance issue in computer vision the backgroundobject label imbalance issue is severe and thus well studied in the field of object detection the idea of hard negative mining hnm has gained much attention recently pang et al proposed a novel method called ioubalanced sampling and chen et al designed a ranking model to replace the conventional classification task with an averageprecision loss to alleviate the class imbalance issue the efforts made on object detection have greatly inspired us to solve the data imbalance issue in nlp sudre et al addressed the severe class imbalance issue for the image segmentation task they proposed to use the class rebalancing property of the generalized dice loss as the training objective for unbalanced tasks shen et al investigated the influence of dicebased loss for multiclass organ segmentation using a dataset of abdominal ct volumes kodym et al proposed to use the batch soft dice loss function to train the cnn network for the task of segmentation of organs at risk oar of medical images shamir et al extended the definition of the classical dice coefficient to facilitate the direct comparison of a ground truth binary image with a probabilistic map in this paper we introduce dice loss into nlp tasks as the training objective and propose a dynamic weight adjusting strategy to address the dominating influence of easynegative examples notation for illustration purposes we use the binary classification task to demonstrate how different losses work the mechanism can be easily extended to multiclass classification

the beam search optimization bso laso method as previously described is employed to train three distinct parameters corresponding to supertagging word generation and reduction actions these parameters serve to exponentiate the probabilities of their respective actions thereby normalizing them to a common scale while this approach when applied to a generative model effectively mitigates label bias and imbalanced probability bias it does not resolve exposure bias upon training the rescaled generative model evaluates each new transition sequence using the following scoring function text score p a cdot p t cdot p w where a t and w denote parsing tagging and word generation actions respectively and the exponents represent the learned scaling parameters proposed method ii bso all to address all identified biases simultaneously through a unified technique we modified existing margin based approaches to minimize all margin violations within the beam rather than focusing solely on the single most violated hypothesis in standard bsoearly and bsolaso implementations parameter updates are triggered only by the most violated hypothesis when the gold standard falls outside the beam however there is no theoretical justification for ignoring other violations present within the beam as argued by lecun et al a key advantage of conditional random field crf models is their capacity to simultaneously reduce the weights of all incorrect hypotheses the proposed bso all approach approximates this principle within a beam search framework this modification incurs no significant computational overhead during training as the forward pass for all additional hypotheses is already performed during beam search yet it yields substantial improvements in parsing accuracy experimental commonly utilized in ccg parsing research the first derived from clark et al aligns closely with the canonical ccg notion of dependencies the second from clark and curran offers a more formalism neutral representation albeit with reduced expressiveness

following a comprehensive analysis of prevailing issues and a strategic pivot away from exclusive reliance on the criminal justice system law enforcement agencies successfully mobilized public support to foster behavioral changes within the community this initiative enhanced informal social control thereby rendering target areas less susceptible to burglary concurrently home security packages were distributed to the most vulnerable residents these measures resulted in a significant reduction in burglary rates across the majority of problem oriented policing pop intervention zones contrasting sharply with a regional trend of increasing incidents beyond demonstrating cost effectiveness relative to traditional criminal justice responses the intervention achieved the critical outcome of substantially reducing the number of households subjected to violation the efficacy of the intervention was assessed through a comparative analysis of crime levels in intervention versus control areas utilizing pre and post response data this evaluation relied primarily on simple count data to determine whether pop initiatives had successfully reduced crime in target areas relative to controls while this methodology provided a baseline estimate of impact it possessed a notable limitation without more granular analysis it failed to elucidate the specific mechanisms of crime prevention or the potential evolution of offending patterns consequently the utility of this assessment for refining tactics or adapting them for broader application was constrained crucial information for effective pop is often embedded in textual data sources including police generated crime notes such as modus operandi descriptions witness statements forensic reports and community complaints the analysis of such qualitative data is predominantly conducted manually rendering the process time consuming and labor intensive given existing resource constraints this work is frequently executed on a selective basis enhancing access to and the processing of this information would empower analysts and officers to leverage a more comprehensive evidence base for implementing pop strategies as noted by scott and kirby in their pop guide successful implementation necessitates both the recruitment and training of appropriate personnel chapter and the provision of robust analytical support chapter while pop demands specific knowledge skills and experience to be delivered effectively these competencies are often absent within police agencies as they are not integral to the traditional policing model

the detection of contentious topics and viewpoints within political discourse presents significant challenges particularly regarding the risk of algorithmic bias that may result in the selective identification of specific perspectives while this study does not claim to resolve these complexities it aims to underscore their critical importance we posit that a primary objective for the societal impact of natural language processing nlp is the promotion of viewpoint diversity in news recommendation systems thereby fostering a robust democratic dialogue extending beyond prior research this work establishes a theoretical linkage between diversity in news curation and democratic theory while also incorporating findings from communication science regarding individual variations in the acceptance of diverse viewpoints we conclude that cultivating a healthy public sphere requires not only the detection of distinct viewpoints but also the assessment of individual users tolerance for diversity nlp is well positioned to facilitate these tasks specifically vector space models can map users and news articles within a multidimensional framework where diversity is quantified through distance and variance metrics this approach enables the personalization of diversity thresholds allowing for the delivery of varied recommendations that support democratic discourse while maintaining user satisfaction nevertheless we identify several technical conceptual and ethical impediments indicating that achieving this objective necessitates rigorous research and critical reflection rather than the mere application of off the shelf nlp solutions this research was supported by the open competition digitalization humanities and social science grant nr d awarded by the netherlands organization for scientific research nwo we extend our gratitude to the anonymous reviewers for their insightful comments which significantly enhanced the quality of this manuscript all opinions expressed and any remaining errors are solely the responsibility of the authors furthermore information extraction and question answering hold the potential to establish a new paradigm for the application of machine learning in criminal law current methodologies predominantly rely on tabular data for predictive metrics however an alternative approach is requisite for matters of equitable justice where adjudication occurs on a case by case basis through verbal or written deliberation and the interpretation of specific case factors although such deliberations are inherently individualized they remain grounded in underlying factual premises information extraction can play a pivotal role in surfacing these facts in this context we evaluate the efficacy of unsupervised weakly supervised and pretrained models in extracting factual information from the unstructured dialogue of california parole hearings with few exceptions the resulting f scores remain below consequently we utilize these findings to delineate specific avenues for future research in information extraction and question answering within legal domains

consequently users of traditional learning management systems lms such as moodle are often compelled to integrate disparate tools to execute necessary academic activities or forgo these activities entirely in contrast to monolithic lms architectures infrastructor is designed as a modular suite of independent tools that can be composed to accommodate diverse usage patterns new functionalities may be introduced as standalone utilities and integrated as required while several initiatives have explored academic workflows on the github platform such as teacher s pet—a ruby based suite for assignment creation starter file population issue management and submission collection—these solutions often suffer from platform lock in teacher s pet for instance was exclusively compatible with github and was subsequently deprecated in favor of github classroom although github classroom natively incorporates the functions of its predecessor it retains the inherent limitations of monolithic systems conversely infrastructor operates on github but remains adaptable to other platforms and workflows due to its fundamentally extensible design philosophy future development of infrastructor will prioritize the integration of automatic plagiarism detection e g via moss and automated feedback mechanisms including code linting and autograding furthermore support for alternative platforms such as gitlab is planned to accommodate institutions preferring local hosting solutions the project also aims to enhance scalability for multi instructor environments specifically addressing the challenge of synchronizing configuration files among faculty members managing large enrollments this paper introduces infrastructor as a flexible toolkit designed to significantly reduce administrative overhead for computer science instructors empirical evidence from its deployment across ten course sections indicates substantial time savings across various instructional styles notably infrastructor requires no dedicated on site infrastructure and can be deployed using commodity cloud based version control services the motivation for this work stems from the inadequacy of traditional pen and paper examinations for modern programming curricula which prioritize practical coding proficiency given that many institutions lack the resources or physical space to conduct assessments in dedicated computer laboratories bring your own device byod exam formats have emerged as a viable alternative while byod allows students to utilize environments consistent with their learning processes it introduces significant challenges regarding the prevention of plagiarism and academic dishonesty which infrastructor seeks to mitigate

this research outlines the development of supplementary classroom activities and instructional guides designed to facilitate educators in leveraging data derived from game based learning environments these resources aim to sustain discourse on data literacy enabling teachers to further investigate student competencies and challenges while gameplay data alone may not suffice for definitive conclusions regarding individual student abilities it serves as a critical diagnostic tool for adapting instructional strategies and initiating meaningful pedagogical discussions the efficacy of formative assessment is contingent upon the provision of robust support for educators in interpreting and acting upon feedback furthermore this study addresses the evaluation of practical programming skills within higher education contexts traditional practical examinations which require students to implement complete programs based on specific specifications often exhibit limitations that hinder comprehensive and accurate assessment this paper identifies these deficiencies and proposes a modular practical testing framework designed to circumvent common pitfalls central to this approach is the principle of modularity which ensures the independent and thorough verification of specific learning outcomes the proposed methodology has been successfully applied over several years to assess object oriented programming skills among undergraduate students demonstrating superior validity compared to conventional assessment techniques given that programming courses necessitate the evaluation of both theoretical knowledge and practical application the accurate assessment of these dual components is essential this work underscores the importance of rigorous verification of learning outcomes as a fundamental element of the educational process the authors acknowledge the national science foundation for their financial support of this research gratitude is also extended to the middle school educators who provided valuable insights the students who participated in the pilot studies and the project team members who facilitated the interviews and pilot implementations

a primary limitation of the proposed method is its reliance on an extensive dictionary nevertheless empirical evidence suggests that the method demonstrates considerable robustness even when utilizing dictionaries of suboptimal quality for instance in our experimental evaluation a french dictionary generated via automatic translation from an english source was employed when the algorithm was applied to a corpus of english documents alongside their manually translated french counterparts the resulting performance metrics exhibited negligible variance furthermore the algorithm successfully identified high semantic similarity between documents representing the same content in different languages while the experimental outcomes generally aligned with the assessments of human experts certain challenges were observed predominantly stemming from lexical ambiguity these issues include polysemy e g the noun versus adverb distinction for well and homonymy e g the multiple distinct meanings of bill as a noun to address these limitations future work will focus on integrating a part of speech tagger to resolve syntactic ambiguities and implementing a disambiguation algorithm that leverages manually annotated sense distinctions within the dictionary such an approach may be thesaurus based or statistical additionally a key avenue for algorithmic enhancement involves the incorporation of anaphoric resolution specifically this entails replacing pronouns and zero subjects as found in spanish with their corresponding antecedent nouns to improve contextual accuracy

this study employed a design based research methodology in collaboration with middle school educators to establish a comprehensive set of design considerations for integrating data and analysis concepts into curricula and developing corresponding formative assessment tools guided by these considerations and a principled framework for assessment design a game based formative assessment targeting data and analysis competencies in middle school was developed the research presents findings from a pilot study utilizing this instrument to examine students conceptual understanding and challenges regarding data related topics the investigation is structured around three primary research questions what assessment goals and guidelines are necessary to elicit evidence of competency in data and analysis as defined by the k– computer science framework what are the critical design considerations for developing data and analysis activities and assessments suitable for both computer science and non computer science middle school teachers how can these assessment goals guidelines and design considerations be effectively integrated into a game based formative assessment for data and analysis to formulate assessment goals for the computer science concept of data and analysis the study utilized evidence centered design ecd a principled framework that supports validity arguments by ensuring assessments accurately measure intended constructs ecd fosters coherence in the design of assessment tasks rubrics and the interpretation of student performance by explicitly linking claims regarding student learning evidence derived from student work products and the specific design features of tasks intended to elicit such evidence the ecd process commenced with a domain analysis involving the systematic gathering and organization of information pertaining to the data and analysis concept for grades through this was followed by domain modeling which entailed articulating a design specification for each construct of interest to inform the subsequent development of tasks and rubrics these design specifications delineate the focal knowledge skills and abilities fksas or assessment targets associated with each construct the requisite types of student evidence for measurement and the characteristics and variable features of tasks capable of eliciting said evidence while ecd has been extensively applied in computer science education including the development of principled assessments for computational thinking determining the scope of assessment also necessitates a careful consideration of the specific learning context

this research outlines a strategic roadmap for expanding the deployment of our system across diverse hardware platforms with a specific focus on enhancing accessibility within k– educational environments to facilitate broader adoption the framework is designed to integrate cost effective robotic units drone systems and simulation based alternatives the utilization of affordable hardware will enable the distribution of multiple units to student groups or individuals thereby fostering the development of cooperative problem solving algorithms concurrently the project aims to collaborate with educators to adapt the curriculum for middle and high school settings preliminary feedback from teachers indicates potential infrastructure limitations including insufficient wireless network capacity and restricted computer access which may hinder the execution of current curricular tasks to address these challenges the development of an offline executable environment is proposed this would allow students to design and validate algorithms prior to physical implementation complementing existing simulation options furthermore the integration of an automated grading and feedback generation system is recommended to alleviate instructor workload and identify detrimental programming practices serving as a robust supplement to current project rubrics the overarching objective of this work is to operationalize the k– computer science framework by translating its broad guidelines into explicit learning targets and aligned instructional tools specifically this paper details the design of a playful curriculum neutral assessment aligned with the data and analysis concept for grades – employing evidence centered design and participatory design methodologies we present a comprehensive set of assessment guidelines and design considerations for integrating data and analysis competencies across both computer science and non computer science middle school curricula this rather than organize elements in sorted order from left to right as in a binary search tree hash tables store and retrieve elements in an array indexed by hash value hash tables data structures apply hashing to implement abstract data types such as sets and maps but must handle collisions between elements that share the same hash value attribution copyright rice university openstax under cc by ncsa license hashing search algorithms are often preferred over binary search algorithms for their runtime benefits but they come with unique drawbacks ideally different objects would hash to different hash values but the combinatorial explosion of possibilities for unique strings or collections of complex data means that a collision or a situation in which multiple objects hash to the same integer index value is inevitable since integers in a computer can typically only represent a certain fixed range of integer numbers combinatorial explosion is not only a problem for the design of efficient algorithms but also the design of efficient data structures too while data structure problems focus primarily on storage and retrieval of elements in a collection graph problems include a wide variety of computing problems involving the graph data structure unlike other data structures graphs include not only elements vertices but also relationships between elements edges graph problems often ask algorithm designers to explore the graph in order to answer questions about elements and the relationships between elements traversal is the problem of exploring all the vertices in a graph graph data structures differ from tree data structures in that there is no explicit root node to begin the traversal and edges can connect back to other parts of the graph in general there is no guarantee of hierarchy in a graph graph traversal algorithms such as depthfirst search and breadthfirst search begin at an arbitrary start vertex and explore outwards from the start vertex while keeping track of a set of explored vertices is a graph traversal algorithm that recursively explores each neighbor continuing as far possible along each subproblem depthfirst explored vertices are in many countries the number of elderly people is steadily rising what challenges might this create for individuals and society what steps can be taken to lessen the effects of an ageing population it is true that people in developed countries now enjoy longer life expectancies than ever before while this trend does bring certain drawbacks there are ways for societies to address and reduce these issues as life expectancy increases and populations age several problems are likely to emerge the most significant challenge is that more people will reach retirement age and become eligible for pensions at the same time the proportion of younger working adults will decrease meaning governments will collect less tax revenue relative to the population size in short an ageing population will put a heavier tax burden on those who are still working additionally there will be increased demand for healthcare services and adults may find themselves responsible for caring for elderly family members there are a number of measures governments can take to address these challenges one straightforward solution is to raise the retirement age perhaps from to since many people remain healthy and capable of working at this age another approach is to encourage immigration to boost the number of working age taxpayers lastly governments may need to reallocate national budgets directing more funds towards essential healthcare housing and transportation for the growing elderly population in conclusion there are several effective strategies to deal with the issues that come with an ageing society this essay addresses the prompt though some points about the problems of an ageing population are only briefly mentioned the transitions in the second body paragraph are somewhat basic and could be improved but the overall structure and organisation are strong the vocabulary is varied and used fluently with few errors making this a solid example of a band essay that is concise yet effective

abstract and introduction initially each word within the pretrained embedding space determines its optimal code length in an end to end fashion by employing the gumbel softmax technique jang et al subsequent to this selection discrete codes are learned for each word via a binary constrained encoder decoder architecture to integrate task specific characteristics into the selection mechanism the proposed approach compresses word embeddings through the optimization of a downstream task thereby facilitating the natural acquisition of task relevant features in contrast to prior methodologies the proposed adacomp framework offers enhanced representational flexibility by leveraging multiple distinct codebooks allowing for a more granular encoding of word semantics to demonstrate the general applicability of adacomp we evaluate the model across four diverse natural language processing nlp tasks sentiment classification chunking natural language inference and language modeling comprehensive empirical results indicate that the proposed method achieves significant compression of original word embeddings without compromising task accuracy furthermore the analysis reveals that adacomp dynamically assigns words to specific codebooks based on their significance to the target task given that the architecture is network agnostic and designed for in place integration adacomp can be readily adapted to most existing nlp systems with minimal modification consequently we posit that current nlp frameworks stand to benefit substantially from this approach the remainder of this paper is structured as follows section reviews related literature section details the proposed methodology sections and present performance evaluations and a detailed methodological analysis respectively and section provides concluding remarks related work this section surveys existing literature concerning the compression of neural models with a specific focus on embedding layers neural network compression the predominant body of research in model compression targets the neural network architecture itself such as convolutional neural networks cnns and recurrent neural networks rnns with a primary emphasis on applications within computer vision these approaches generally encompass pruning quantization and low precision representation techniques regarding pruning several studies han et al li et al lee et al investigate the impact of individual connections i e weights on task performance subsequently eliminating redundant or insignificant connections from the network topology conversely other works han et al chen et al louizos et al employ quantization strategies to map connections into discrete bins thereby enforcing weight sharing to reduce model size

our sampling approach is to approximate the distribution of latent variables for a given responsetype using gaussians for example if we have a collection of labelled backchannel responses and their corresponding z encodings we can approximate the distribution of p z label backchannel using an isotropic gaussian by simply calculating µ backchannel and σ backchannel the maximum likelihood mean and standard deviations of each of the z dimensions these vectors can also be used to calculate directions in the latent space with different semantic characteristics and then interpolate between them input feature representations linguistic features we use the word annotations from the msstate transcriptions as linguistic features these annotations give us the timing for the starts and ends of all words in the corpus as our feature representation we use dimensional word embeddings that are initialized with glove vectors and then jointly optimized with the rest of the network in total there are unique words in the annotations we reduced the embedding number down to by merging embeddings that had low word counts with the closest neighbouring embedding calculated using cosine distance we also introduce four additional tokens that are specific to our task sil wait none and unspec sil is used whenever there is a silence wait and none are used at the start and end of all the system encodings respectively the use of unspec unspecified is shown in fig unspec was introduced to represent temporal information in the linguistic embeddings we approximate the processing delay in asr by delaying the annotation by ms after the ground truth frame where the user’s word ended this ms delay was proposed in skantze as a necessary assumption to modelling linguistic features in offline continuous systems however since voice activity detection vad can supply an estimate of when a word has started we propose that we can use this information to supply the network with the unspec embedding ms after the word has started acoustic features we combine logmel filterbanks and features from the gemaps feature set the gemaps features are the complete set excluding the mfccs e g pitch intensity spectral flux jitter etc

research indicates that while students demonstrate proficiency in grasping design concepts and creating solutions tailored specifically to either disabled or nondisabled populations they often encounter difficulties in developing universal designs intended for simultaneous use by both groups this paper presents a case study detailing the instructional experience of two concurrent accessibility courses hcc inclusive design and accessible technology and cpsc creative inquiry introduction to inclusive design and accessible technology both courses were designed to introduce students with backgrounds in computer science human computer interaction human centered computing or human factors psychology to the principles of accessibility through project based instruction although the courses were administered separately with distinct lecture schedules they maintained identical curricula including lecture content modules assignment deadlines and instruction hcc was a three credit graduate level human centered computing course with a final enrollment of six students cpsc was a three credit undergraduate computer science course with a final enrollment of five students the primary objective of both courses was to equip students with the skills to apply inclusive design practices for identifying user characteristics analyzing user needs ideating designing prototyping and evaluating accessible technologies specific learning outcomes are detailed in the accompanying documentation the majority of coursework was conducted collaboratively in small teams with the exception of discussion forums reflective exercises and the midterm examination the course grading structure was distributed as follows assignments discussions midterm examination project video project report final project prototype and prototype evaluation instruction took place on tuesdays and thursdays excluding holidays across two minute lecture sessions per week over the spring semester time slots were scheduled however classes convened times with two sessions cancelled to facilitate out of class working periods the curriculum was organized into ten modules most of which encompassed content for two to three lectures accompanied by readings and supplementary media module one served as an introduction providing a course overview syllabus review and clarification of expectations and structure within the canvas learning management system additionally students completed an introductory discussion exercise in which they described their professional backgrounds relative to design and accessibility and articulated their learning objectives for the course

students’ perception of their professional future is often limited to wellknown tech companies particularly those offering technologies they have used directly such as search engines social media and other consumerfocused companies teaching the concept of computing for social good helps students bridge the gap between theory and application in a realworld setting and consider the application of their skills in new ways it shows students how their computing skills can be used to provide solutions to realworld problems and benefit others however an individual student’s conception of social good can vary as their community and issues that their community faces can be very different from other students factors like culture can heavily influence one’s perception of community and the issues that a community may face in turn students would benefit from exploring and learning about computing in a national or global context beyond their personal use of technology an internationalization at home iah project is any oncampus postsecondary activity that incorporates an international element to a program area these projects allow students to explore their profession in an international context and come to understand how factors such as culture can influence perceptions ideas and practices in their field in this initiative we combine the concepts of iah with computing for social good in a single activity particularly we explore the outcomes of an iah initiative that exposes first year computer science students to the concept of computing for social good in an international context we explore how differences in culture can influence students’ perceptions and approaches to computing for social good more specifically our study aims to identify similarities and differences in the perception of technology as an instrument of social good between students at canadian and mexican universities the present study ties together the concepts of computing for social good and internationalization by making crosscultural comparisons of ideas and approaches to computing for social good computing for social good shows students how they can use their computer skills in the real world beyond their perceptions of typical computing professions internationalization initiatives are activities that demonstrate how culture can influence practices in a field of study taken together cross cultural comparisons of computing for social good allow for identification of differences in approaches to social good and how culture can contribute to these differences

title demographic and stylistic biases in commercial machine translation systems authors dirk hovy federico bianchi and tommaso fornaciari affiliation bocconi university milan italy contact dirk hovy f bianchi fornaciari tommaso unibocconi it abstract the primary objective of machine translation mt has historically been the accurate conveyance of semantic content with stylistic considerations treated as secondary this study demonstrates that this prioritization results in systematic stylistic distortions within commercial mt systems an analysis of three major platforms—bing deepl and google—reveals that translations of demographically diverse samples across five languages consistently exhibit a shift toward older and more masculine linguistic registers compared to the source texts these findings indicate that current translation models inadvertently reflect demographic biases inherent in their training data consequently this research highlights the necessity of integrating stylistic awareness into mt frameworks and suggests new avenues for investigation into bias mitigation and personalization introduction while the accurate transmission of propositional content remains the paramount goal of machine translation the manner in which information is expressed significantly influences the perception of the final output previous scholarship such as mirkin et al has established that demographic linguistic features play a critical role in translation quality and offer potential for system personalization despite this the prevailing focus on content fidelity has often overlooked the preservation of stylistic and demographic nuances leading to the homogenization of voice in translated texts this paper addresses this gap by examining the extent to which commercial systems alter the perceived age and gender of source material
