motivated by these works and our preliminary experimental results we propose to use the word vector norm as a criterion to determine the difficulty of a sentence specifically we first train a simple word embedding model on the training corpus and then obtain an embedding matrix e wv given a source sentence x x · · · x i · · · x i it can be mapped into distributed representations x · · · x i · · · x i through e wv the normbased sentence difficulty is calculated as i x i long sentences and sentences consisting of rare words or significant words tend to have a high sentence difficulty for cl the proposed normbased difficulty criterion has the following advantages it is easy to compute since the training of a simple word embedding model just need a little time and cpu resources linguistically motivated features such as word frequency and sentence length can be effectively modeled modelbased features such as learningdependent word significance can also be efficiently captured normbased model competence besides finding an optimal sentence difficulty criterion arranging the curriculum in a reasonable order is equally important as summarized by zhang et al there are two kinds of cl strategies deterministic and probabilistic from their observations probabilistic strategies are superior to deterministic ones in the field of nmt benefiting from the randomization during minibatch training without loss of generality we evaluate our proposed normbased sentence difficulty with a typical probabilistic cl framework that is competencebased cl in this framework a notion of model competence is defined which is a function that takes the training step t as input and outputs a competence value from to c t ∈ min t − c λ t c where c is the initial competence at the beginning of training and λ t is a hyperparameter determining the length of the curriculum

prior research indicates that long distance grammatical relations specifically advcl and csubj exhibit significant improvement following fine tuning on semantically related tasks whereas other relations do not however the absence of a control task and the lack of granular reporting on individual attention heads—limited instead to aggregate maximum performance metrics—leave several critical questions unresolved specifically it remains unclear whether the observed improvements in advcl and csubj exceed chance expectations whether the stagnation of other relations stems from irrelevance or whether non maximal attention heads experienced substantial but sub threshold gains the methodology proposed herein which incorporates control tasks and analyzes individual attention head dynamics is designed to address these uncertainties while existing literature has investigated the presence of specific linguistic knowledge in pre trained models few studies have examined how the encoding of such knowledge evolves during fine tuning for instance clark et al identified that certain syntactic relationships are encoded intuitively with dependents primarily attending to their grammatical heads we posit that verifying the persistence of such hypothesized encodings post fine tuning is essential for validating them as explanatory mechanisms for transformer predictions furthermore to our knowledge no prior work has assessed the cross dataset consistency of attention mechanisms we argue that measuring this consistency is vital for distinguishing between attention heads that have learned dataset specific artifacts and those that have captured genuine linguistic phenomena for example fine tuning might enhance sensitivity to negation across multiple datasets yet the specific heads responsible for this improvement may vary this lack of consistency is evident in our analysis of bert large on a control task where there is negligible overlap in the active attention heads across two distinct datasets finally several limitations of the current study suggest avenues for future research first our analysis focuses on a single interpretable encoding of negation scope however alternative hypotheses exist such as a hierarchical tree of attention linking in scope words to negation cues rather than direct attention second while we utilized a single non linguistic control task future investigations could explore attention head dynamics across a gradient of fine tuning tasks with varying degrees of relevance to the linguistic phenomenon under study

knowledge graph inspired by the work of document grounded conversation we provide a knowledge graph to support the annotation of more informative dialogs we build them by crawling data from baidu wiki and douban websites table presents the statistics of this knowledge graph multitype dialogs for multiple domains we expect that the dialog between the two taskworkers starts from a nonrecommendation scenario e g question answering or social chitchat and the recommender should proactively and naturally guide the dialog to a recommendation target an entity the targets usually fall into the seeker’s interests e g the movies of the star that the seeker likes moreover to be close to the setting in practical applications we ask each seeker to conduct multiple sequential dialogs with the recommender in the first dialog the recommender asks questions about seeker profile then in each of the remaining dialogs the recommender makes recommendations based on the seeker’s preferences collected so far and then the seeker profile is automatically updated at the end of each dialog we ask that the change of seeker profile should be reflected in later dialogs the difference between these dialogs lies in subdialog types and recommended entities rich variability of interaction how to iterate upon initial recommendation plays a key role in the interaction procedure for recommendation to provide better supervision for this capability we expect that the task workers can introduce diverse interaction behaviors in dialogs to better mimic the decisionmaking process of the seeker for example the seeker may reject the initial recommendation or mention a new topic or ask a question about an entity or simply accept the recommendation the recommender is required to respond appropriately and follow the seeker’s new topic task templates as annotation guidance due to the complexity of our task design it is very hard to conduct data annotation with only highlevel instructions mentioned above inspired by the work of multiwoz we provide a task template for each dialog to be annotated which guides the workers to annotate in the way we expect them to be

bow and dictionary methods have shown good performance however in the effort to improve results the context of words has been also taken into account for example by analyzing the syntactic relations between the words using dependency trees in general the use of syntactic relations has not shown an outstanding performance in the task of classifying deceptive text however combining this method with a bow approach can improve results in some cases not only information of words or syntactic structures in the text is available but also additional information supplied by the source from which the texts were extracted fornaciari and poesio collected fake and real opinions from the amazon web site the authors took into account for example information on who bought the book in question and who did not people who bought the book and wrote their opinion had higher credibility than people who did not buy the book this kind of extra information is rarely available so we did not focus on this kind of features in the present study pérezrosas and mihalcea collected features using different approaches such as partofspeech pos tags contextfree grammars cfg unigrams and liwc as well as combinations of these features the authors achieved accuracy between and predicting whether a person of feminine or masculine gender had written a deceptive text research findings showed that the use of pos tags and cfg did not significantly improve accuracy as compared with unigrams and liwc this suggests that bowand dictionarybased approaches give performance similar to that of linguistic style approaches combining different methods is a common method in generating accurate models used in deception detection this is done to exploit the advantages of each approach and thus improve the accuracy of classification however to our knowledge getting a universal domain deception detector has been scantily studied in this section we present our method for deception detection first in sect we present our model for deception detection using support vector networks next in sect we describe the different datasets we will use for evaluation finally in sect we detail the different feature sources we will use

observations on attention mechanisms and qualitative utility a notable finding is that the attention gain ratio agr reaches a plateau rapidly for messages receiving high attention weights this trend suggests that while high signal messages are relatively rare they possess exceptional predictive power consequently while the incorporation of any attention mechanism yields benefits message level attention demonstrates superior overall performance compared to word level attention qualitative value of identifying informative text the high signal text identified by attention based models offers significant qualitative value for researchers investigating the relationship between language and personality traditional bag of words approaches can isolate attribute relevant terms e g via word clouds however this method is inherently limited by its lack of contextual information in contrast personality researchers can leverage learned attention weights to identify entire messages that warrant further analysis thereby capturing how specific traits such as high extraversion manifest within a broader linguistic context table presents examples of messages assigned high and low attention weights by the sn attn model for users at the extreme ends of each personality dimension the results indicate that high attention messages are thematically aligned with the target personality dimension for instance messages associated with conscientiousness predominantly focus on work and academic responsibilities whereas those linked to extraversion center on social interactions furthermore the specific words receiving high attention highlighted in green are consistent with the respective dimensions for example openness to experience highlights philosophical terms e g weird nothingness trippy while agreeableness correlates with specific lexical choices e g shit conversely low attention messages exhibit minimal thematic relevance to empirically validate the qualitative utility of this high signal text two domain experts psychologists with established research backgrounds in personality were tasked with evaluating paired message sets sets per dimension for each pair experts selected the set they deemed more informative regarding the individual s personality each pair comprised five messages from the top third of attention weights and five from the bottom third for a specific user to control for message length variability only messages falling within characters of the user s average message length were selected the user sample was randomly drawn from individuals in the top or bottom th percentile of each personality dimension provided they had a minimum of total messages

limitations of tpcgcn and the proposal of disentangled tpcgcn as previously noted in section the primary limitation of the tpcgcn framework lies in its tendency to generate representations that are heavily biased toward topic specific information consequently the model exhibits limited generalizability rendering it more effective for intra topic detection tasks rather than inter topic detection scenarios disentangled tpcgcn dtpcgcn intuitively features independent of specific topics are more robust when evaluating posts from unseen topics inter topic detection conversely topic dependent features remain valuable when the unseen topics share semantic similarities with those present in the training set therefore both topic related and topic unrelated features are essential however their relative importance varies dynamically across individual samples this observation suggests that these feature types should be disentangled and subsequently fused through a dynamic mechanism based on this analysis we propose an extension to the original framework termed disentangled tpcgcn dtpcgcn specifically designed to enhance inter topic detection capabilities see figure b the dtpcgcn architecture comprises two core components a two branch multitask architecture for feature disentanglement and an attention mechanism for dynamic fusion two branch multitask architecture to simultaneously extract topic related and topic unrelated features we employ a dual branch multitask architecture these branches are denoted as r the topic related branch and u the topic unrelated branch in both branches an auxiliary topic classification task is introduced to guide the learning of topic oriented representations for each branch the initial layer of the graph convolutional network gcn is trained using the topic classification task the input to the topic classifier consists of fusion vectors derived from h generated via the same process used for f i in the standard tpcgcn the training objective utilizes the cross entropy loss function l t frac n sum i sum k y ik t log p ik t quad where y ik t is the ground truth label for the correct topic otherwise p ik t represents the predicted probability that the i th post belongs to the k th topic and n denotes the size of the training set the fundamental distinction between branches r and u lies in the optimization of l t branch r minimizes l t to learn topic distinctive features branch u maximizes l t to learn topic confusing topic invariant features subsequently the second layer of the gcn is incorporated and each branch is trained individually on two concurrent tasks topic classification and controversy classification this process ensures that both branches can effectively evaluate controversy albeit utilizing features with differing relationships to the underlying topics attention mechanism following the individual training phases branch u and branch r are expected to have successfully captured topic unrelated and topic related features respectively these disentangled representations are then integrated via an attention mechanism to facilitate dynamic fusion tailored to the specific characteristics of each input sample

task taxonomy and model performance analysis task definitions and categorization natural language processing tasks can be categorized based on the structural nature of the spans they identify and the relationships they model constituency parsing in this framework each node within a parse tree represents an individual text span associated with a specific constituent label these spans are characterized by a nested hierarchical structure part of speech pos tagging as a sequence labeling task pos tagging assigns a grammatical tag to every individual token within a text ratnaparkhi toutanova et al aspect based sentiment analysis absa this task involves the identification of specific text spans designated as aspect terms followed by the prediction of the sentiment polarity associated with each term pontiki et al relation oriented tasks relation oriented tasks focus on the interactions between entities predicates or semantic roles relation extraction re this task concerns the identification and classification of semantic relationships between pairs of entities hendrickx et al coreference resolution the objective is to link named nominal and pronominal mentions that refer to the same underlying concept whether within a single sentence or across a broader discourse pradhan et al semantic role labeling srl srl aims to identify the arguments of a predicate typically a verb or noun and classify them according to their semantic roles relative to that predicate gildea jurafsky open information extraction openie unlike traditional re which relies on a fixed set of relation types openie seeks to extract open domain predicates along with their corresponding arguments usually subjects and objects directly from sentences banko et al niklaus et al dependency parsing in this syntactic analysis spans consist of single word tokens the task involves establishing a directed relation between a word and its syntactic parent annotated with a specific dependency type kübler et al opinion role labeling orl this task detects spans representing opinion expressions as well as the associated opinion holders and targets yang cardie empirical results bert large it took a century before john harrison of england created the first chronometer an extremely accurate clock that permitted accurate longitude location for an interesting story of the chronometer s development and the obstacles this creative genius had to overcome read the book longitude by dava sobel in this instance the science and mathematics of accurately finding a position on the earth s surface were known but it took technological invention to solve the problem the steam engine associated with james watt who actually held patents on improvements in steam engines was originally designed by thomas savery and thomas newcomen watt created two significant improvements an external condenser for the steam so the steam cylinder would not be cooled down and a slide valve mechanism that allowed steam to automatically enter the cylinder as the piston was pushed by the steam in either direction these fundamental inventions were not enough to garner success in the marketplace in this era the mids in england the designer of the engine would have to manufacture and install it at a factory and then be compensated for the effort this took more capital than watt possessed and he sold onehalf of his patent to a partner john roebuck together they were able to build several engines but the financial return and roebuck s capital were limited so the business could not expand time was also running out on watt s patent fortunately matthew boulton with sound financial resources took an interest in the engine and purchased roebuck s share boulton also had the connections to get the patent extended for another years securing his investment because of boulton s deep pockets boultonwatt company created an interesting and profitable marketing tool it would provide an engine free to the purchaser in exchange for ouly percent of the savings that accrued for its use per year for as long as the engine was operating engines from other manufacturers often exhausting to the atmosphere were comparatively very inefficient so the manifold efficiency improvement of the watt engine provided the purchaser with significant savings ten percent of these funds also significant flowed to boulton and watt this story illustrates an important lesson inventiveness is important but seldom does one person have all the technical marketing and financial knowhow required to make a financially successful product a team effort is required

the internet is widely used around the globe and has become a vital means of communication however its widespread adoption has sparked significant debate some people believe that internet usage should be regulated while others argue that such control is unnecessary this essay will examine both perspectives and share my own view supporters of regulating internet use often base their stance on the prevalence of online crime which poses risks to both individuals and society examples include hackers stealing personal information fraudulent advertisements designed to scam people and terrorist groups spreading propaganda to manipulate the public if left unchecked these threats can have serious negative impacts therefore regulating online activities can be seen as a necessary measure to protect users on the other hand those who oppose internet regulation emphasize the benefits of unrestricted online activity such as increased efficiency in work and communication for instance companies can reach a larger audience more quickly by advertising online rather than distributing flyers in person however this same convenience can be exploited to spread false or misleading information which can deceive the public to address these risks some level of control over online activities becomes necessary from my perspective given the widespread dangers present on the internet it is important to intervene and filter harmful content to ensure online safety in summary while regulating online activities may slightly reduce work efficiency it plays a crucial role in protecting us from cybercrimes personally i believe that the importance of internet regulation should not be underestimated

algorithm outlines the comprehensive procedure for decomposing an input text x into a hierarchical structure of text spans and evaluating the contribution of each segment the process initiates with the entire text x which is initially partitioned into two segments in subsequent iterations the algorithm selects one existing segment and recursively subdivides it into smaller spans as illustrated in algorithm executing this top down procedure requires resolving two critical decisions at each timestep which text span to select for subdivision and the optimal location for the split point both decisions are addressed through the following optimization framework min s i s i in p min j in s i s i phi x s i j x j s i mid p quad here phi x s i j x j s i mid p denotes the interaction score between the sub segments x s i j and x j s i conditioned on the current partition p a detailed exposition of this scoring function is provided in subsection for a specific span s i s i in p the inner optimization problem identifies the point of weakest interaction thereby determining the optimal split location for that span a trivial instance of this inner optimization occurs when the span length is two as only a single division is possible conversely the outer optimization problem determines which span within the partition p should be selected for splitting this is resolved by enumerating all elements in p a special case arises at the initial iteration t where the partition p x n contains only the complete input text upon updating the partition the new structure is incorporated into the hierarchy h the final step of each iteration involves evaluating the contributions of the newly generated spans and updating the contribution set c as specified in line of algorithm

dataset and task definition for the political slant transfer task prabhumoye et al this study utilizes the dataset released by voigt et al methodology the task involves two sets of non parallel sentence samples x x dots x n and x x dots x m drawn from source style s and target style s respectively the objective is to efficiently generate a set of samples hat x hat x dots hat x n in the target style s conditioned on the input samples in x for any given style s v where v in the process begins by identifying a set of characteristic phrases denoted as gamma v which define the stylistic attributes of s v the presence of phrases from gamma v within a sentence x i serves as an indicator of its association with style s v for instance in sentiment transfer tasks phrases such as pretty good or worth every penny are indicative of a positive style we propose a two stage approach first a tagger model infers an intermediate sentence representation z x i from the input x i the primary function of the tagger is to ensure that z x i is agnostic to the original style s of the input subsequently conditioned on z x i a generator model produces the transferred sentence hat x i in the target style s the intermediate variable z x i is a common component in various style transfer methodologies shen et al prabhumoye et al yang et al hu et al in these prior works the input x v i is transformed into a latent representation z x i that ideally encodes the semantic content of the input while remaining invariant to the style s v however whereas previous approaches typically encode z x i within a continuous latent space our method manifests z x i in surface form this capability to generate observable intermediate outputs renders our pipeline more interpretable than methods relying on continuous latent representations

in the future we plan to continue to investigate the impacts different orientations have on class performance beyond cs such as cs and data structures the impostor phenomenon ip is often discussed as a problem in the field of computer science but there has yet to be an empirical study to establish its prevalence among cs students one survey by the blind app found that a high number of software engineers at some of the largest technology companies selfreported feelings of impostor syndrome however selfreporting of impostor syndrome is not the standard diagnostic for identifying whether an individual exhibits feelings of the impostor phenomenon in this work the established clance ip scale is used to identify the prevalence of ip among graduate and undergraduate computer science students at a large researchintensive north american institution among this population of over students were found to exhibit frequent feelings of the impostor phenomenon with a larger fraction of women experiencing frequent feelings of the imposter phenomenon than men additionally ip was found to have greater prevalence among computer science students than among students of other populations from comparable studies due to the negative impacts associated with feelings of the impostor phenomenon computer science education should work to improve student awareness and help student cope with these feelings the impostor phenomenon ip as originally defined is the experience of intellectual phoniness as perceived by high achieving individuals these individuals have a great fear that others might discover that they are not as competent as they appear attributing their successes to luck knowing the right people being in the right place at the right time or even their personal charm although there is a strong belief that the impostor phenomenon is more prevalent in computer science than in other fields there has not yet been a carefully controlled study using a validated instrument to confirm this belief in blind’s survey of the software engineers selfreported feelings of impostor syndrome however selfreporting is not considered a validated means for measuring ip historically the impostor phenomenon is understood to be prevalent among underrepresented populations in a particular field however there is evidence that both underrepresented and represented groups are affected

reverse self training is a methodology employed for strategy selection in both supervised and unsupervised learning contexts this approach posits that model performance can be enhanced by labeling samples where the classifier exhibits low certainty regarding polarity the procedure involves the classifier identifying unlabeled data inverting the data labels and subsequently augmenting the training set with the most confident unlabeled instances alongside the least confident inverted instances upon completion of this process the classifier achieves comprehensive coverage of the decision space without a heavy reliance on majority class samples in the evaluation of chinese microblogs the nlp and cc datasets were utilized these datasets encompass twenty distinct topics comprising subjective items of which are positive and are negative the analysis incorporated a sentiment lexicon derived from hownet containing positive and negative sentiment terms empirical results indicated that standard self training achieved a precision of a recall of and an f measure of in contrast reverse self training yielded improved metrics with a precision of a recall of and an f measure of a distinguishing feature of this approach relative to prior methodologies is its application to specific domains such as digital product reviews however microblog sentiment classification inherently involves multidomain information consequently models trained on a specific domain often exhibit significant performance degradation when applied to different domains separately mahyoub et al proposed a framework for sentiment determination in arabic text this study introduced a semi supervised approach to identify arabic sentiment by constructing a dedicated arabic sentiment lexicon capable of assigning polarity scores to arabic vocabulary the lexicon was developed using arabic wordnet employing a small seed list of positive and negative words as a training set to infer the polarity of the remaining vocabulary the proposed semi supervised algorithm leveraged semantic relations within arabic wordnet to propagate sentiment scores while the scoring mechanism mirrored that of sentiwordnet—categorizing words as positive negative or neutral—the scores in this study were not normalized to the – range the study processed a total of words identifying approximately as neutral as positive and as negative

title named entity recognition in tweets an experimental study conference emergence of syntax needs minimal supervision authors raphaël bailly samm ea fpm cnrs université paris panthéon sorbonne and kata gábor ertim ea inalco abstract this paper presents a theoretical contribution to the discourse regarding the learnability of syntax from corpora lacking explicit syntactic supervision our methodology leverages the observable structural properties of a corpus to delineate and isolate grammatical syntactic information from semantic and pragmatic data we delineate the formal characteristics of an autonomous syntax and demonstrate that syntax based lexical categories can be identified through a straightforward optimization process necessitating no prior hypotheses regarding the model s architecture introduction syntax constitutes the fundamental component of human linguistic capacity enabling the production and comprehension of a potentially infinite array of novel sentences the principle of compositionality frege posits that the meaning of a complex expression is entirely determined by the meanings of its constituents and their structural arrangement consequently the comprehension of previously unheard sentences relies on the ability to construct meaning from constituent parts while the inventory of constituents and their assigned meanings is necessarily finite syntax facilitates their creative combination it is widely accepted that syntax operates via algebraic compositional rules chomsky and a finite set of syntactic categories accordingly computational models of language are expected to possess or acquire this compositional capability the recent efficacy of neural network based language models across various natural language processing tasks coupled with their opaque black box nature has precipitated critical inquiry into two primary areas first when recurrent neural language models generalize to unseen data does this imply the acquisition of syntactic knowledge if so does this knowledge manifest as human like compositional capacities baroni lake and baroni linzen et al gulordava et al

initially many researchers used vector space model vsm to assess the semantic similarity between responses and prompts in recent years with the blooming of deep neural networks dnn in natural language processing nlp many dnnbased approaches were applied to detect offtopic responses malinin et al used the topic adapted recurrent neural network language model rnnlm to rank the topicconditional probabilities of a response sentence a limitation of this approach is that the model can not detect offtopic responses for new question prompt which was not seen in training data unseen prompt later offtopic response detection was considered as a binary classification task using endtoend dnn models malinin et al proposed the first endtoend dnn method attentionbased rnn attrnn model on offtopic response detection task they used a bilstm embedding of the prompt combined with an attention mechanism to attend over the response to model the relevance cnns may perform better than rnns in some nlp tasks which require keyphrase recognition as in some sentiment detection and questionanswer matching issues lee et al proposed a siamese cnn to learn semantic differences between ontopic responsequestions and offtopic responsequestions wang et al proposed an approach based on similarity grids and deep cnn however the coldstart problem of offtopic response detection has not been handled well by the aforementioned approaches it is not until enough training data of unseen prompts are accumulated that good performance could be achieved besides these methods draw little attention to the vital ontopic falsealarm problem for a production system i e extremely high recall of ontopic responses is also required to make realuserfacing systems applicable in this paper to address the issues mentioned above a novel approach named gated convolutional bidirectional attentionbased model gcbia and a negative sampling method to augment training data are proposed the key motivation behind our model gcbia is as follows convolution structure captures the key information like salient ngram features of the prompt and the response while the biattention mechanism provides complementary interaction information between prompts and responses

lau et al correlate acceptability scores generated from a lm to average human acceptability ratings suggesting that humanlike gradient syntactic knowledge can be captured by such models futrell and levy also look at gradient acceptability in both rnn lms and humans by focusing on alternations of syntactic constituency order e g heavy np shift dative alternation their results suggest that rnn lms acquire soft constraints on word ordering like humans however the alternations in futrell and levy while varying in their degree of acceptability maintain the same syntactic relations throughout the alternation e g gave a book to tom and gave tom a book both preserve the fact that tom is the indirect object our work expands this line of research by probing how rnn lms behave when multiple valid interpretations with crucially different syntactic relations are available within a single sentence we find that rnn lms do not resolve such ambiguity in a humanlike way there are of course a number of other modeling approaches that exist in the current literature the most notable of these being bert these transformer models have achieved high performance on a variety of natural language processing tasks however there are a number of properties that make them less suitable to this work one immediate consideration is that of training we are interested in the behavior of a class of models so we analyze the behavior of several randomly initialized models we do not know how representative bert is of models of its same class and training more bert variants is immensely time consuming and environmentally detrimental additionally we are interested in probability distributions over individual words given the preceding context something that is not part of bert’s training as it takes whole sentences as input finally the bidirectional nature of many of these models makes their representations difficult to compare to humans for these reasons we restrict our analyses to unidirectional rnn lms this necessarily reduces the generalizability of our claims however we still believe this work has broader implications for probing what aspects of linguistic representations neural networks can acquire using standard training data experimental stimuli in the present study we compare the attachment preferences of rnn lms to those established in fern ´ andez

previous research has typically quantified abstractiveness by measuring the overlap of text spans between a summary and its source document grusky et al zhang et al or by evaluating the performance of extractive baselines such as lead nallapati et al a while metrics like extractive fragment coverage and density grusky et al offer a continuous assessment of abstractiveness this study proposes a more granular categorization based on the construction of individual summary sentences the underlying premise is that highly abstractive sentences aggregate content from broader segments of the source text thereby necessitating a reduction in direct lexical copying to maintain conciseness consequently we define four distinct categories of abstractiveness based on the extent of textual copying sentence extraction the summary sentence is identical to a single source sentence span extraction the summary sentence constitutes a contiguous substring of a single source sentence e g extracting the plane was coming back from the ncaa final from a longer source sentence word extraction the summary sentence is formed by a non contiguous subset of tokens from a single source sentence achieved through the deletion of intervening words perfect fusion k the summary sentence is constructed by concatenating substrings from k k distinct source sentences while preserving their original relative order to quantify the degree of abstractiveness within a set of summaries each sentence is assigned the first applicable category from the list above which is ordered by increasing levels of abstraction the score for each category is calculated as the percentage of sentences classified under that type accordingly a summary exhibiting higher fusion scores and lower extraction scores is deemed more abstractive

we perform empirical evaluation and analysis of a variety of classification methods for the above task related work argument mining the starting point for the current work is a collection of pro and con arguments for a given topic as previously mentioned these arguments may be collected from a large audience by conducting a survey or mined automatically from text some of the previous work on argument mining focused on specific domains such as legal documents student essays and user comments on proposed regulations mining arguments and argument components for a given topic also known as context has been a prominent line of research in argument mining levy et al introduced the task of contextdependent claim detection in a collection of wikipedia articles and rinott et al did the same for contextdependent evidence detection more recently several works focused on topicrelated argument mining from the web or other massive corpora stance classification of extracted arguments can be performed as a separate step or jointly with argument detection as a threeway classification pro argument con argument none as done by stab et al argument clustering and summarization several works have focused on identifying pairs of similar arguments or clustering similar arguments together ajjour et al addressed the task of splitting a set of arguments into a set of nonoverlapping frames such as economics environment and politics reimers et al classified argument pairs as similar dissimilar misra et al aimed to detect argument pairs that are assumed to share the same argument facet which is similar to our notion of key points however they did not attempt to explicitly identify or generate these facets which remained implicit but rather focused on detecting similarity between argument pairs in contrast to these works we directly map arguments to key points

learning approaches to derive representations of student program trajectories facilitating the clustering of distinct learning styles similarly swidan et al analyzed multiple choice questionnaires derived from scratch environment examples to identify common misconceptions inherent to block based interfaces the present work extends this domain by introducing a novel methodology for clustering block based programs accompanied by a qualitative analysis of the relationship between these clusters and patterns of misunderstanding while cluster analysis has been extensively applied in disciplines beyond computer science its application in educational technology remains significant for instance min et al and akram et al leveraged clustering of student problem solving behaviors to inform stealth assessment models amershi and conati applied clustering to student behaviors within interactive laboratory environments to refine behavioral classification models furthermore kardan et al clustered users based on log data from complex simulation environments to enhance the understanding of student behaviors and misconceptions although these prior studies did not involve programming environments the current research builds upon their feature extraction and clustering techniques extending them to include post hoc cluster analysis the studies reported herein were conducted within the prime environment an adaptive block based programming system designed to support novices in acquiring introductory programming concepts prime comprises twenty sequential programming activities each building upon the skills acquired in preceding tasks these activities address fundamental computer science concepts including input output operations numeric data types mathematical expressions variables iterations both definite and indefinite abstraction functions parameters return values boolean data types conditionals and debugging within this environment students construct programs using a customized version of google s blockly block based programming plugin the empirical study was conducted at a large university in the southeastern united states targeting two online sections of an introductory course for undergraduate engineering majors a total of participants accessed the prime system however the analysis focused on the students who completed at least one activity the although stability maximizing clustering strategies often map multiple distinct words to a single token—thereby potentially compromising representational fidelity—empirical results indicate that such encodings maintain robust performance in practical applications see section agglomerative cluster encodings connected component encodings prioritize stability exclusively which can result in unnecessarily low fidelity for instance as illustrated in figure the words at and about are grouped within the same connected component despite lacking a direct typographical relationship given the high frequency of both terms assigning them distinct encoded tokens would significantly enhance fidelity with only a marginal reduction in stability notably only the infrequent word abet is susceptible to perturbation across multiple encoded tokens to address such scenarios we introduce agglomerative cluster encodings these are constructed by balancing stability stab against a formally defined fidelity objective fid we subsequently approximate the optimization of this combined objective function phi utilizing an agglomerative clustering algorithm fidelity objective as established in section an encoding is considered to possess high fidelity if it facilitates high standard accuracy across diverse tasks while this property is difficult to characterize precisely we aim to formulate an objective function that serves as a valid approximation it is important to note that distinct encoded tokens possess no inherent semantic relationship rather the model g learns to utilize these encodings during the training process revisiting the aforementioned example suppose at and abet are assigned to the same cluster and share an encoded token z during training every occurrence of at and abet is replaced by z however due to the significantly higher frequency of at classifiers tend to associate z primarily with the semantics of at to optimize overall performance consequently this results in largely uncompromised performance for sentences containing at at the expense of performance on sentences containing the less frequent abet this dynamic motivates the following definition let vec v i in mathbb r v denote the indicator vector corresponding to word i while vec v i could theoretically represent a word embedding we employ indicator vectors to avoid introducing additional assumptions the encoded token vec mu j associated with the words in cluster c j is defined as vec mu j frac sum w i in c j rho w i vec v i sum w i in c j rho w i quad here we weight the vectors by frequency rho to account for the influence of training dynamics on the encodings as described above under this formulation fidelity is maximized when each word is assigned a distinct encoded token

we produce a similar example for the topicalchat dataset and find that always outputting a concatenation of the ten most common tokens in the dataset “ i the that a to it is of” attains an f score of which is a improvement over the transformer presented by gopalakrishnan et al bleu is a wellknown word overlap metric that computes ngram precision between the generated sequence and the reference because precision favors shorter sentences bleu also adds a brevity penalty that punishes shorter sentences bleu has been found to correlate poorly with human judgment meteor was designed as an improvement on bleu using a harmonic mean of precision and recall as well as stemming and synonyms rougel identifies the longest common subsequence between the generated and reference sequence to better account for sentencelevel structure when computing word overlap greedy matching is an embeddingbased metric that greedily matches each word in the generated sequence to a reference word based on the cosine similarity of their embeddings the final score is then an average over all the words in the generated sequence embedding average computes a sentence embedding for both the generated sequence and the groundtruth response by taking an average of word embeddings the score is then a cosine similarity of the average embedding for both the generated and reference sequence vector extrema follows a similar setup to embedding average where the score is the cosine similarity between sentence embeddings rather than taking an average over word embeddings this method identifies the maximum value for each dimension of the word embedding taking the maximum is motivated by the idea that common words will be deemphasized as they will be closer to the origin vector extrema has been shown to perform better on dialog tasks than other metrics skipthought uses a recurrent neural network to produce a sentencelevel embedding for the generated and reference sequences

abstract this study demonstrates that a multitasking approach yields significant improvements over a bert based pretrained baseline these gains are primarily attributed to the integration of extensive dialogue datasets within a similar domain furthermore the proposed multitasking framework enhances performance across both text based and image based tasks as evidenced by multiple metrics in both fine tuning and task transfer scenarios the model achieves state of the art results on numerous tasks thereby establishing a robust baseline for this challenge introduction a primary objective of artificial intelligence is the development of multimodal agents capable of natural communication encompassing discussion reasoning empathy and advisory functions ideally such systems should emulate human interaction by adapting their persona to be knowledgeable personable expert or humorous as the context dictates these agents must be proficient in answering and posing questions responding to statements maintaining a consistent persona and grounding dialogue in external information and visual data while no single task currently exists to train or evaluate an agent across all these dimensions simultaneously several large scale datasets targeting specific subsets of these capabilities have recently become available consequently we have aggregated these disparate tasks into a unified benchmark the dodeca dialogue challenge comprising twelve distinct subtasks each subtask includes training data to cultivate specific agent skills alongside validation and test sets to evaluate performance the overarching goal is to develop a single agent capable of exhibiting the full spectrum of these competencies given that certain subtasks involve massive datasets e g billion utterances they possess the potential to facilitate learning across other skills to leverage this we have constructed a model capable of training and multitasking across all available sources we employ a transformer based architecture vaswani et al that accepts images external textual information and dialogue history as inputs to generate appropriate responses practically by pretraining on the largest subtasks and subsequently multitasking across the entire suite our approach achieves state of the art results compared to previously reported independent performances on ten of the twelve subtasks with comparable baselines this establishes a strong benchmark for the challenge while many existing methodologies rely on large scale pretraining on general text corpora we demonstrate that utilizing dialogue specific datasets—which are more directly aligned with the intended agent objectives—serves as a highly effective alternative

this paper introduces maads mixed methods approach for the analysis of debugging sequences a scalable and generalizable framework that integrates quantitative and qualitative methodologies by employing a state action representation coupled with visualization techniques maads facilitates a granular step by step examination of the debugging process to validate the efficacy of this approach the study analyzed the debugging behaviors of middle school students engaged in coding activities within may s journey an educational game designed to impart fundamental programming principles the application of maads proved highly effective in distinguishing variations in students debugging strategies and learning trajectories the instruction of programming has garnered significant scholarly attention in recent decades due to its inherent complexity a critical component of programming proficiency is debugging—the systematic identification and resolution of code errors debugging is widely recognized as a primary challenge for novice programmers begum et al delineate the requisite knowledge domains for successful debugging including an understanding of both intended and actual program behavior language syntax general programming expertise and specific knowledge regarding bugs debugging methodologies and the application domain existing literature has established high level frameworks and identified patterns that characterize the strategic elements of debugging although specific details vary these frameworks generally adhere to a cyclical structure the programmer first acquires sufficient system understanding to detect anomalies and formulate an initial hypothesis regarding the error subsequently the programmer engages in iterative hypothesis testing until the error is resolved or the process loops back to earlier stages for refinement mastery of debugging is essential as it not only enhances general problem solving capabilities but also reinforces broader learning outcomes consequently a detailed investigation of the debugging process is imperative such inquiry elucidates the specific factors contributing to the difficulties faced by introductory students and informs the development of systems aimed at refining problem solving techniques and mitigating steep learning curves

quoted and thus removed after removing nonenglish and heavily quoted forecasts forecasts with no text justifications or justifications less than tokens in addition users with fewer than predictions with text forecasts made by forecasters are selected for the final dataset the distribution of predictions made by each forecaster is heavily skewed of forecasters make over forecasts on average each forecaster makes forecasts excluding those who made over predictions in table we also provide breakdown statistics for top and bottom forecasters measuring ground truth in order to build a model that can accurately classify good forecasters based on features of their language we first need a metric to measure people’s forecasting skill for this purpose we use brier score a commonly used measure for evaluating probabilistic forecasts for questions in our dataset forecasters could even make over forecasts with justifications other possible scoring rules exist for example ranking forecasters by loglikelihood for a loglikelihood scoring rule however we need to adjust estimates of and which are not uncommon in the data to avoid zero probability events there are many ways this adjustment could be done and it is difficult to justify one choice over another with binary answers it is defined as forecaster’s brier score i f i − o i here f i is the forecaster’s estimated probability o i is a binary variable indicating the final outcome of the event and n is the total number of forecasts brier scores can be interpreted as the mean squared error between the forecast probability and true answer lower scores indicate better forecasts ranking forecasters directly comparing raw brier scores is problematic because users are free to choose questions they prefer and could achieve a lower brier score simply by selecting easier questions to address this issue we standardized brier scores by subtracting the mean brier scores and dividing by the standard deviation within questions

besides the detection of viewpoints we also seek to incorporate nudgelike personalization features the key insight from the nudging literature is that environments can be designed in a manner that takes heuristics and biases into account to steer behavior of the users of these environments such nudges could also be aimed at the individual person the most famous example is the ‘cafeteria example’ where the healthy food options are placed in an easier to reach spot than the unhealthy options as a result more people choose the healthier options without making the unhealthy options completely unavailable the same approach can be connected to the idea of a “healthy media diet” where “healthy” is connected to a healthy democracy and public debate nudging has previously been incorporated in recommender systems a systematic review by jesse and jannach reveals that of nudging mechanisms identified in the literature only a small subset was previously investigated in the context of recommender systems these include using visuals to increase item salience item reranking and setting defaults for news recommendation specifically gena et al found that nudges based on giving users the idea certain items were popular were not effective while negative framing nudging users to consume certain news items because of limited availability was the authors argue that future work should address which types of nudges are ethically acceptable in the area of persuasive technologies we define latitude of diversity as an individual user’s acceptance of diversity research shows that groups of individual users can differ considerably in the extent to which they are open towards and interested in diverse viewpoints we argue that considering individual users’ latitudes of diversity increases the likelihood that a given user engages with diverse recommendations and potentially prevents unwanted side effects if individual latitudes of diversity are not considered users who are not interested in or open towards diverse viewpoints might simply not select diverse recommendations moreover recommending news that are too diverse can backfire motivated reasoning literature suggests that people evaluate counterattitudinal information more critically than attitudecongruent information under some circumstances this might go as far as that exposure to counterattitudinal information causes people to actively counterargue resulting in even stronger attitudes and increased political polarisation

one potential explanation for the observed phenomena is that given a sufficiently large training set d the probability of encountering a test instance within an extrapolation regime may be negligible however this perspective overlooks the substantial costs associated with errors in extrapolation scenarios which can pose significant barriers to deployment in critical real world applications such as autonomous driving systems ¹ this paper focuses specifically on extrapolation within sequential data more precisely we address the challenge of generalizing sequence to sequence predictors to input sequences of length n n d where n d represents the maximum sequence length observed in the training set such extrapolation is fundamental to language acquisition given the unbounded nature of language human learners must process novel and potentially longer sentences despite having limited learning resources chomsky accommodating this unboundedness presents a significant challenge for neural networks recent investigations into simple artificial tasks have highlighted this limitation in sequence to sequence models lake and baroni liska et al weber et al specifically liska et al observed that while sequence to sequence architectures may converge to local minima capable of generalization such outcomes are rare this suggests that while neural networks possess the capacity to generalize they often lack the necessary inductive biases to favor extrapolatable behavior in the following sections we review the theoretical concepts of attention and extrapolation we subsequently argue that current attention mechanisms which have driven recent advancements in natural language processing nlp are unlikely to support extrapolation due to their reliance on the content of trained embeddings to address this we propose a novel location based attention mechanism loosely inspired by human visual attention to ensure that extrapolation capabilities are achieved without compromising model expressivity we introduce an attention mixer that integrates both content based and position based attention finally we demonstrate that recurrent models equipped with this proposed mechanism can successfully extrapolate to sequences longer than those encountered during training extrapolation although the term extrapolation is frequently employed in the literature it is rarely defined with formal rigor ebert et al note that when explicitly defined extrapolation typically refers to data points lying outside a geometric hull delimited by the training set such as a rectangular concave or convex hull ¹ note extrapolation remains prevalent in practical scenarios high dimensional problems typically require an exponentially large training set d to be representative and the underlying data distribution may shift over time hooker

to the best of our knowledge this study represents the first application of unknown intent detection within this specific domain we conducted extensive experiments across three real world datasets to validate the efficacy of the proposed seg model for unknown intent detection and its utility in generalized zero shot intent classification the remainder of this paper is structured as follows section reviews related literature on intent classification and open world classification section provides a detailed exposition of the proposed seg model section presents experimental results regarding unknown intent detection section demonstrates the application of seg to enhance generalized zero shot intent classification accompanied by a case study finally section offers concluding remarks related work intent classification user intent classification constitutes a critical component of dialogue systems significant research efforts have been dedicated to understanding user intent across diverse domains ranging from search engine queries hu et al to medical inquiries zhang et al deep learning architectures including convolutional neural networks cnns xu and sarikaya and attention based recurrent neural networks rnns ravuri and stolcke liu and lane are widely employed for this task cnn based approaches construct sentence embeddings by aggregating the embeddings of adjacent words whereas rnn based methods derive sentence embeddings through the sequential encoding of word embeddings both methodologies have demonstrated promising performance in practical applications yin et al traditional intent classification techniques typically require substantial amounts of labeled data for each class to train discriminative classifiers in contrast zero shot intent classification sappadla et al zhang et al addresses the challenge wherein not all intent categories are observed during the training phase this capability is vital for natural language understanding as novel intents may continuously emerge within dialogue systems liu and lane nam et al xu and sarikaya the objective of zero shot intent classification is to generalize knowledge and concepts acquired from seen intents to recognize unseen intents early approaches ferreira et al a b yazdani and henderson explored the relationship between seen and unseen intents by incorporating external resources such as manually defined attributes or label ontologies however the acquisition of such resources is often cost prohibitive

while topic related cues can facilitate the identification of posts within similar domains their efficacy in dissimilar contexts is contingent upon specific situational factors consequently a comprehensive evaluation of post controversy necessitates the systematic integration of information from both comments and related posts at both semantic and structural levels although existing social media controversy detection methods have leveraged the semantic attributes of target posts and their comments alongside structural features their performance is constrained by three primary limitations neglect of related posts current approaches often overlook the role of related posts within the same topic which can provide critical supporting or refuting evidence relying solely on comment data is therefore insufficient for a holistic analysis inadequate structural modeling these methods typically employ statistical structure based features that fail to capture complex reply structure relationships e g parent child or sibling comment interactions this limitation can lead to the misinterpretation of comment stances topic specific bias existing models tend to capture topic specific features that lack generalizability across different domains while such features may enhance performance when test posts align with training topics they can degrade detection accuracy when applied to dissimilar topics recently graph convolutional networks gcns have demonstrated significant success across various domains due to their capacity to encode both local graph structures and node features to address the aforementioned limitations we propose the topic post comment graph convolutional network tpcgcn this framework integrates information regarding the graph structure and content of topics posts and comments to facilitate post level controversy detection the methodology proceeds as follows graph construction we construct a tpc graph to delineate relationships among topics posts and comments to preserve reply structure information edges are established between each comment node and the specific post or comment it addresses furthermore to incorporate data from fluids possess viscous properties defined as a measure of internal friction that acts to retard motion within the medium when a fluid stream such as air flows past a solid surface like a sail the fluid particles immediately adjacent to the surface adhere to it and come to rest relative to the object these stationary particles do not move with the main airstream rather due to viscosity they exert a retarding force on their immediate neighbors reducing their velocity this deceleration propagates outward through successive layers of fluid until at a certain distance from the surface the fluid attains the full velocity of the mainstream this phenomenon is illustrated in figure the region between the solid surface and the mainstream characterized by fluid velocities lower than the free stream velocity is termed the boundary layer as fluid particles within this layer decelerate they experience a loss of kinetic energy this energy dissipation is transmitted as shear stress through the fluid layers and is ultimately communicated to the solid surface as frictional forces acting parallel to it consequently these frictional forces are proportional to the square of the velocity reflecting kinetic energy loss while the power required to overcome them is proportional to the cube of the velocity force multiplied by velocity the study of boundary layers constitutes a critical domain of fluid mechanics as the interaction between an object and the surrounding fluid dictates the boundary layer s characteristics which in turn significantly influence the drag forces acting upon the object it is notable that the power extractable from a wind stream also varies with the cube of the velocity thus the cubic relationship governing frictional power loss is consistent with fundamental fluid dynamics principles for instance increasing a vehicle s speed from mph to mph represents a increase in velocity yet results in a increase in the velocity cubed leading to a correspondingly dramatic rise in aerodynamic drag in the context of this instructional unit students were tasked with developing a design project necessitating a comprehensive understanding of the laws of conservation of mass and energy the subsequent project focuses on food dehydration specifically targeting the design of systems capable of reducing the mass of moist food products

objective of predicting whether en wikipedia org wiki help infobox the table belongs to the text or is a random table but did not find this to improve the performance on the end tasks this is aligned with liu et al that similarly did not benefit from a next sentence prediction task for pretraining to be efficient we restrict our word piece sequence length to a certain budget e g we use in our final experiments that is the combined length of tokenized text and table cells has to fit into this budget to achieve this we randomly select a snippet of to word pieces from the associated text to fit the table we start by only adding the first word of each column name and cell we then keep adding words turnwise until we reach the word piece budget for every table we generate different snippets in this way we follow the masking procedure introduced by bert we use whole word masking for the text and we find it beneficial to apply whole cell masking masking all the word pieces of the cell if any of its pieces is masked to the table as well we note that we additionally experimented with data augmentation which shares a similar goal to pretraining we generated synthetic pairs of questions and denotations over real tables via a grammar and augmented these to the end tasks training data as this did not improve end task performance significantly we omit these results finetuning overview we formally define table parsing in a weakly supervised setup as follows given a training set of n examples x i t i y i n i where x i is an utterance t i is a table and y i is a corresponding set of denotations our goal is to learn a model that maps a new utterance x to a program z such that when z is executed against the corresponding table t it yields the correct denotation y the program z comprises a subset of the table cells and an optional aggregation operator

tao et al introduced ruber an unsupervised framework for training automatic dialogue evaluators this approach optimizes a model to distinguish between ground truth responses and negatively sampled responses by minimizing a margin ranking loss the methodology parallels the next sentence prediction nsp task utilized in the pre training of bert devlin et al wherein the model predicts whether a given sentence is a genuine continuation of a preceding context in this paradigm positive samples consist of ground truth subsequent sentences while negative samples comprise unrelated text segments the nsp mechanism has proven beneficial not only for evaluation tasks tao et al but also for broader language understanding devlin et al and generation bruni fernandez wolf et al by leveraging large scale conversational datasets ruber has demonstrated superior performance compared to the adem baseline this study utilizes both adem and ruber as foundational baselines to identify their respective limitations and to propose solutions for developing more precise and robust evaluators the broader field of dialogue response evaluation continues to evolve through the refinement of automated metrics and improved approximations of response quality notable advancements include the utilization of multiple reference points for comparison gupta et al and the integration of human judgment with automated metrics hashimoto et al furthermore li et al highlighted the unreliability of single turn human assessments advocating instead for multi turn evaluation protocols ghandeharioun et al introduced novel automated metrics to approximate sentiment semantic similarity and engagement employing a hybrid metric within a multi turn setting additionally dziri et al demonstrated that entailment serves as a viable proxy for assessing dialogue coherence and quality background adem is a regression based model that accepts three inputs a dialogue context vector c a hypothesis response vector hat r and a reference response vector r the model s output is defined as the sum of a reference dependent metric and a reference independent metric formulated as follows text adem text ref r hat r r t n hat r quad text adem text unref c hat r c t m hat r quad in these equations the encoding vectors are generated by pre trained recurrent neural network rnn encoders while m and n represent trainable parameters

the final hidden state of a dedicated classification token undergoes a linear transformation to yield the sufficient statistics of a normal latent distribution from which a latent state is subsequently sampled this latent representation is prepended to the embedded input sequences of the conversational turns the resulting sequence is then decoded via a separate transformer architecture employing a left to right lr attention mask this approach was investigated given the established utility of latent variables in regulating stylistic attributes across diverse research domains no reference context baseline gccn rc this variant mirrors the gccd ec architecture but excludes reference materials during the decoding phase it effectively serves as a reimplementation of the model proposed by olabiyi and mueller with two primary modifications the introduction of token types to facilitate multi actor modeling and the omission of the random padding strategy the latter was deemed unnecessary as the substantial volume of training data mitigated overfitting concerns consequently gccn rc functions as the primary state of the art sota baseline for evaluating the efficacy of conditioning on prior conversational context experiments while validation perplexity computed via teacher forcing is recognized as an imperfect proxy for generative capability—despite its correlation with such performance—it remains a standard metric in language modeling due to its computational efficiency and parallelizability which obviate the need for autoregressive sampling accordingly the evaluation framework comprises two distinct phases an architecture search utilizing automatic evaluation based on validation perplexity and a qualitative assessment of generated samples conducted via amazon mechanical turk automatic evaluation all evaluations presented in this section were performed on the validation set comprising reddit comments from april using perplexity ppl calculated as follows text ppl theta d exp left frac n sum i n log p theta c i r i right quad all models were trained utilizing mixed precision arithmetic the optimization process employed the adam algorithm with default hyperparameters kingma and ba the learning rate schedule was configured to linearly increase from to times over the initial of training iterations followed by a decay to over the remaining iterations according to a cosine annealing schedule

smaller than its distance to the mean of any other class specifically this distance must not exceed a fraction m of the distance to the nearest competing class mean a central component of the proposed methodology is semantic enhancement via class descriptions this approach integrates semantic information into the gaussian mixture model by designating the embedding derived from the textual description d k of class k as the class centroid mu k the description d k may take the form of a single word label a sentence or a detailed paragraph formally this is expressed as mu k text feature extract d k quad where text feature extract cdot denotes the feature extraction module detailed in section generation loss and objective function in addition to the standard cross entropy loss the model aims to maximize the observed likelihood of the embeddings under the gaussian mixture distribution this is achieved by minimizing the negative log likelihood defined as mathcal l g sum i n log mathcal n z i mu y i sigma y i sum i n z i mu y i top sigma y i z i mu y i text const quad where const represents a constant term as indicated in equation the generation loss mathcal l g encourages the embedding z i to converge toward its class centroid mu y i this mechanism facilitates the learning of a more class concentrated embedding distribution which is advantageous for downstream outlier detection tasks the total objective function combines the cross entropy loss mathcal l mce and the generation loss mathcal l g as follows mathcal l mathcal l mce lambda mathcal l g quad where lambda serves as a trade off parameter balancing the two loss components outlier detection following the feature learning procedure described above each utterance x is encoded into an embedding z this embedding is subsequently processed by the local outlier factor lof algorithm breunig et al to identify novel or unknown intents outliers lof is a well established unsupervised density based anomaly detection method its underlying principle involves comparing the local density of a data point with that of its neighbors to identify regions of similar density data points exhibiting a substantially lower local density relative to their neighbors are classified as outliers

furthermore we conjecture that a portion of our analysis is affected by catastrophic forgetting of knowledge learned during pretraining experimental pipeline our experimental pipeline figure consists of two steps starting with a pretrained model intermediatetask training and finetuning on a target or probing task intermediate task training we finetune roberta on each intermediate task the training procedure follows the standard procedure of finetuning a pretrained model on a target task as described in devlin et al we opt for single intermediatetask training as opposed to multitask training to isolate the effect of skills learned from individual intermediate tasks target and probing task finetuning after intermediatetask training we finetune our models on each target and probing task individually target tasks are tasks of interest to the general community spanning various facets of natural language domains and sources probing tasks while potentially similar in data source to target tasks such as with cola are designed to isolate the presence of particular linguistic capabilities or skills for instance solving the target task boolq may require various skills including coreference and commonsense reasoning while probing tasks like the senteval probing suite target specific syntactic and metadatalevel phenomena such as subjectverb agreement and sentence length detection table presents an overview of the intermediate and target tasks intermediate tasks we curate a diverse set of tasks that either represent an especially large annotation effort or that have been shown to yield positive transfer in prior work the resulting set of tasks cover question answering commonsense reasoning and natural language inference qamr the question–answer meaning representations dataset is a crowdsourced qa task consisting of question–answer pairs that correspond to predicate–argument relationships it is derived from wikinews and wikipedia sentences for example if the sentence is “ ada lovelace was a computer scientist ” a potential question is “ what is ada’s last name ” with the answer being “ lovelace ” commonsenseqa commonsenseqa is a multiplechoice qa task derived from conceptnet with the help of crowdworkers that is designed to test a range of commonsense knowledge
