as illustrated in figure d the outputs generated by the parser encoder are extracted and integrated as supplementary inputs into the bilstm based encoder of the orl model the hidden states from each layer of the parser s bilstm serve as syntactic representations denoted as h dots h l n where h l n represents the output of the l th layer at word w n to derive a unified syntactic representation h text syn i for each word w i a weighted sum operation is applied h text syn i lambda sum j l alpha j h j i quad in this formulation l denotes the number of layers in the parser s bilstm encoder the parameters w alpha j and lambda are learnable model weights specifically alpha j represents softmax normalized weights assigned to the layer outputs h j while lambda functions as a scaling factor for the syntactic representations these derived syntactic vectors h text syn i are subsequently concatenated with the original orl input vectors to construct enriched word representations furthermore to leverage both implicit and explicit syntactic information simultaneously an edge weighted graph is extracted from the parser decoder as depicted in figure f a dependency graph convolutional network dep gcn is then applied over the orl encoder to generate syntax enhanced representations pipeline versus multi task learning the three methodologies illustrated in figures d through f can be implemented via either a pipeline architecture or a multi task learning mtl framework in the pipeline approach the dependency parser is trained independently and its parameters are subsequently frozen during the training of the orl model conversely the mtl approach entails the simultaneous training of both the parser and the orl model this subsection investigates the mtl strategy to further mitigate error propagation complementing the dep gcn approach as a baseline figure c depicts the standard mtl configuration which employs a shared encoder with multiple task specific output layers a method known as hard parameter sharing ruder marasović and frank however this approach is suboptimal for the current scenario where the auxiliary parsing task possesses a significantly larger volume of labeled data compared to the primary orl task in such cases the shared encoder is prone to biasing its optimization toward parsing performance thereby potentially compromising the main task xia et al a

entity masking notably masking entity mentions with their corresponding named entity and grammatical role information significantly degrades performance on entity type related tasks specifically typehead and typetail this decline suggests that masking effectively redirects the encoder s attention away from specific entity mentions a hypothesis corroborated by reduced performance in probing tasks assessing argument position and distance e g argdist argord and sentlen among the architectures evaluated cnn and bilstm encoders exhibit the most substantial performance drops indicating a pronounced tendency to overfit to specific entity mentions in the absence of masking in contrast the graph convolutional network gcn demonstrates a lower propensity for such overfitting conversely and somewhat unexpectedly the self attentive encoder sattn exhibits an increased focus on entity mentions and their immediate context when masking is applied as evidenced by improved performance on distance and argument related probing tasks word representations the integration of contextualized word representations such as those generated by elmo or bert yields a marked improvement in probing tasks centered on named entity and part of speech information this finding indicates that contextualized representations effectively encode syntactic and semantic features pertinent to relation extraction re aligning with prior research by peters et al and radford et al regarding the utility of deep language representations for downstream tasks the enhancement in syntactic and semantic capabilities is further reflected in an overall improvement in re task performance when compared to elmo encoders utilizing bert generally demonstrate superior and more balanced performance across probing tasks a trend that correlates with enhanced re outcomes this suggests that bidirectional language models encode the linguistic properties of input data more effectively interestingly the uncased version of bert performs equally well or better than its cased counterpart on probing tasks related to entity and part of speech information while this result is intuitive for the semeval dataset which emphasizes semantic relations between concepts it is counterintuitive for the tacred dataset where casing is typically critical for identifying proper entities e g person and company names probing tasks versus relation extraction a significant observation is that superior performance on probing tasks does not necessarily translate to improved performance on the downstream re task for instance while the cnn elmo configuration achieves the highest scores on the majority of probing tasks it records an f score points lower than the optimal model for this dataset which is the sattn bert cased configuration with masking

highlighting present in addition the heuristics that were used for session time calculation showed consistency across results and thus support these results further future work includes exploring the psychological factors behind users’ initiative to compile manually in bluej as part of a larger study examining users who transition from bluej to bluej with users who start programming for the first time in bluej this will provide insights about the role that prior exposure to a given programming environment affects experience in another we will also investigate the high percentage of success for manual compilations in bluej again this may be due to the users’ bluej experience our results support hypotheses that user behavior is substantially affected by the programming environment specifically by the compilation mechanism and error message presentation further exploration of user interaction with programming environments may yield important insights that could help pave the way toward developing tools that are evidencebased in their effectiveness research suggests using studentcentered practices in the classroom is a key component of attracting and retaining diverse students to better understand the link between attitudes toward students and learning and the usage of specific teaching strategies we analyze survey responses from faculty who teach introductory computer science cs courses from u s colleges and universities participating in braid using principal component analysis we scale responses to attitudinal questions into four dimensions rugged individualism “learning and success are the individual student’s responsibility” challenging work “the pace and workload in cs is hard” a developmental orientation to learning “students need individual attention in a noncompetitive environment” and capable students “all students can do well in my class” we then test these scales on four types of teaching three studentcentered approaches—collaborative learning approaches discourse activities and studentled learning—and one traditional approach lecturing results indicate that a developmental orientation predicts the use of discourse activities and studentled practices but not collaborative learning approaches rugged individualism is associated with frequent lecturing

this motivates the studies of crosstarget stance detection which infers the attitude of the destination target by leveraging a large amount of annotated data from the source target so far several previous studies have been conducted for crosstarget stance detection these methods leverage either common words or conceptlevel knowledge shared by different targets to bridge the knowledge gap across the different targets such models suffer from two issues when they are applied to crosstarget stance detection in practice first stance detection often involves analyzing the texts from social media that are short and informal making it difficult to extract domainindependent common words shared by different targets from the training data second users may express their stance towards a given target in an implicit way thus the existing conceptlevel based methods may fail to distinguish implicit stancecarrying terms and context information to alleviate the aforementioned issues we propose a semanticemotion knowledge transferring sekt model for crossdomain stance detection which leverages external knowledge as a bridge between source and destination targets the proposed model is motivated by the observation that the data with different targets usually shares certain common external knowledge that can be transferred from the source to destination targets first we build a semanticemotion graph segraph from semanticrelated and emotionrelated lexicons which incorporates external knowledge from both wordlevel and conceptlevel in segraph each node is either a word or an emotion tag and the edge between each node pair indicates the cooccurrences of the two nodes in the lexicons second a graph convolutional network gcn is employed to learn the graph representation that captures the multihop semantic connections between words or emotion tags rather than onehop connection third we extend the standard bidirectional lstm bilstm classifier to fully integrate the external knowledge segraph by adding an additional knowledgeaware memory unit kamu to the lstm cell kamu is capable of controlling the influence of the external knowledge in learning the hidden state of each word the main contributions of this paper can be summarized as follows • we construct a semanticemotion heterogeneous graph from external semantic and emotion lexicons and employ gcn to learn the semantic graph representation

business architecture modeling serves to articulate an organization s comprehensive business model by delineating its core capabilities and the underlying processes required to sustain them a business model functions as a strategic framework that defines the mechanisms through which an entity creates delivers and captures value encompassing revenue generation strategies target market identification and product or service definitions this framework typically integrates organizational locational and process models though it may also incorporate supplementary elements such as financial models which fall outside the scope of this discussion to illustrate the practical application of these concepts this analysis focuses on a representative capability financial instruments trading within a global markets organization the organizational locational and process models are generally depicted at a logical level of abstraction representing the most granular detail appropriate for such structures the effective structuring of these models relies heavily on the application of layering and componentization heuristics the organizational model defines the structural design of the entity specifying the allocation of roles responsibilities and interrelationships it establishes a framework for understanding how internal components interact to support organizational functions providing clear role definitions and a process to role mapping matrix role definitions encapsulate the functional capabilities necessary for an individual to manage one or more distinct business processes it is standard practice for a single individual to assume multiple roles the process role matrix explicitly delineates accountability identifying which business roles are responsible for specific processes while typically structured at the elementary business process level this matrix may be extended to higher level aggregations where beneficial as a visual representation the role matrix clarifies the distribution of tasks and authority across various positions and departments within the context of a financial instruments trading business model a diverse array of roles contributes to operational efficacy beyond specific functional titles the organizational hierarchy includes two primary management layers upper management and systems management upper management is charged with the oversight of various organizational sectors including multiple business units operations departments and other critical functional areas

the analysis of gender differences in responses yielded a cohen s d effect size of indicating a small to medium magnitude of effect where d represents a small effect and d represents a medium effect although the foundational study by blind utilized a binary yes no query rather than a validated diagnostic instrument the current findings corroborate their conclusion that a majority of individuals within the computer science cs domain exhibit impostor phenomenon ip specifically the data confirms that female students reported significantly higher levels of impostor feelings compared to their male counterparts this disparity may be attributed to the underrepresentation of women in computer science a subject frequently addressed at venues such as the grace hopper conferences regarding racial and ethnic demographics no statistically significant differences in ip were observed between students identifying as white or asian and those identifying as members of underrepresented groups however this null result may be confounded by the limited sample size of underrepresented students within the dataset particularly the scarcity of hispanic latino and african american participants to contextualize these findings the current results were compared with prior studies utilizing the same instrument across various disciplines based on the clance impostor phenomenon scale cips scores the prevalence of ip among the cs student sample was found to be statistically significantly higher than that observed in the following cohorts health professional students education college students in the midwestern united states educational psychology students from the southwestern united states who self identified as minorities and undergraduate students majoring in management economics business informatics law and program engineering from russia assessed via t tests while mean cips scores were reported in these comparative studies the specific percentages meeting diagnostic criteria were not consequently for the purposes of this discussion estimated percentages meeting diagnostic criteria were derived by assuming a normal distribution of the data reconstructed from the reported means and standard deviations this assumption of normality was supported by the current dataset as a kolmogorov smirnov test revealed no evidence of non normality the finding that ip is more prevalent among cs students than among students in other domains was unexpected this observation underscores the necessity for further investigation into the specific factors contributing to the elevated prevalence of impostor feelings within the computer science community several hypotheses have been proposed to explain why more than half of cs students may experience frequent impostor feelings

pointwise mutual information pmi has traditionally been employed to quantify the relatedness of word pairs based on search engine hit frequencies however pmi is not directly applicable as a similarity function within the framework of soft cardinality to address this limitation pmi is normalized by dividing it by the logarithm of the joint probability yielding a metric bounded within the interval in this normalized scale a value of indicates complete co occurrence signifies statistical independence and denotes the absence of co occurrence given that values within the interval are deemed irrelevant for this application a normalized and trimmed expression is utilized wherein negative values are clipped to zero the requisite probabilities for pmi are derived via maximum likelihood estimation using sentence frequencies from a large corpus the proposed methodology integrates three fundamental components—the strm resemblance coefficient soft cardinality and three word similarity measures—to construct three distinct text similarity functions sts qgrams sts csds and sts npmi the primary component the strm resemblance coefficient operates on pairs of texts represented as weighted bags of words this coefficient is enhanced by substituting classical cardinality with soft cardinality a mechanism that leverages both word importance weights and pairwise word comparisons while sts qgrams operates independently of external statistics both sts csds and sts npmi necessitate statistical data derived from a large corpus all training and test texts underwent a standardized preprocessing pipeline comprising the following steps tokenization of text strings conversion of uppercase characters to lowercase removal of stopwords and punctuation marks and stemming of words using porter s algorithm subsequently each stemmed word was assigned an inverse document frequency idf weight calculated across the entire text collection the sentence frequencies required for the csds and npmi word similarity functions were extracted from the ukwac corpus which comprises approximately billion words million sentences and million documents this corpus was processed iteratively sentence by sentence applying the aforementioned preprocessing steps to identify all occurrences of target words and word pairs from the training and test sets to optimize efficiency target words were stored in a trie data structure reducing the total corpus iteration time to approximately minutes on a standard laptop the three proposed text similarity functions incorporate several configurable parameters the exponent p for soft cardinality the parameters alpha beta and bias for the strm coefficient their subscripted variants for the qgrams and csds word similarity functions and the parameters q and q specific to the qgrams function

abstract this study introduces a cascaded analytical framework designed to determine the political stance of twitter users regarding polarizing topics through the examination of retweet behaviors the methodology employs an initial unsupervised learning phase to infer user stances followed by a supervised learning phase that utilizes these derived user labels to characterize the general political orientation of online media outlets and prominent twitter users as well as their specific positions on target issues model performance was validated against gold standard labels from the media bias fact check database yielding an accuracy rate of introduction online media entities and influential twitter users collectively referred to herein as influencers frequently articulate distinct political leanings observable through their positions on various political and cultural issues traditionally the determination of such leanings has relied on textual analysis to identify stance indicative terminology groseclose milyo gentzkow shapiro while automated supervised classification offers a viable approach it necessitates the availability of manually labeled datasets groseclose milyo gentzkow shapiro mohammad et al alternatively political orientation can be inferred by analyzing content propagation patterns on social media platforms empirical evidence suggests that users are predisposed to share content originating from sources that align with their own viewpoints an et al morgan et al ribeiro et al wong et al leveraging this behavioral observation the present research characterizes influencers by analyzing the stances of the users who disseminate their content the process of ascertaining user positions known as stance detection involves identifying an individual s alignment with a specific topic entity or claim mohammad et al for instance regarding the topic of abortion in the united states user stances typically bifurcate into pro choice and pro life positions this paper proposes the application of unsupervised stance detection to automatically assign stance labels to a large cohort of twitter users based on specific topics darwish et al this tagging mechanism identifies clusters of vocal users by analyzing the accounts they retweet thereby facilitating the broader characterization of media and influencer bias

to identify puns existing unsupervised approaches typically assign a score to each word within a sentence and select the word with the lowest score however such models rely exclusively on semantic features to detect heterographic puns thereby neglecting the rich information inherent in pronunciation doogan et al attempted to address this by integrating word embeddings with phonetic data through the concatenation of pronunciation strings yet this method suffers from limited expressive capacity furthermore the authors note that their system performs suboptimally on short sentences due to the lack of contextual information in standard word embeddings in a different approach zou and lu jointly detect and locate puns from a sequence labeling perspective by employing a novel tagging schema similarly diao et al expanded word embeddings using wordnet to resolve the polysemy associated with homographic puns subsequently utilizing a neural attention mechanism to extract collocations for detection despite these advancements all aforementioned methods remain constrained by their reliance on limited contextual information beyond pun recognition yu et al proposed a method for generating homographic puns without requiring labeled training data he et al further refined homographic pun generation based on the local global surprisal principle which posits that the pun word and its alternative maintain strong associations with the immediate and distant contexts respectively regarding representation learning standard word embeddings map words to vectors such that semantically similar terms are proximal in the embedding space however most models utilize only textual information omitting the significant data contained in pronunciation as noted by zhu et al pronunciation is a critical component of language prior research has demonstrated the utility of phonetic information in speech recognition bengio heigold spell correction toutanova moore and speech synthesis miller a by projecting phonetic data into the embedding space words with similar sounds can be positioned in close proximity bengio heigold additionally kamper et al leveraged word pair information to enhance acoustic word embeddings while zhu et al demonstrated that combining pronunciation with written text significantly improves the performance of word embeddings

thus we encourage researchers to evaluate with constructed observational studies or semisynthetic datasets although measuring latent confounders from text increases the difficulty of creating realistic datasets that can be used for empirical evaluation of causal methods constructed observational studies constructed observational studies collect data from both randomized and nonrandomized experiments with similar participants and settings evaluations of this kind include job training programs in economics advertisement marketing campaigns and education for instance shadish et al randomly assign participants to a randomized treatment math or vocabulary training and nonrandomized treatment participants choose their own training they compare causal effect estimates from the randomized study with observational estimates that condition on confounders from participant surveys e g sex age marital status like of mathematics extroversion etc open problems to extend constructed observational studies to text data one could build upon shadish et al and additionally a ask participants to write freeform essays of their past educational and childhood experiences and or b obtain participants’ public social media posts then causal estimates that condition on these textual representation of confounders could be compared to both those with surveys and the randomized settings alternatively one could find observational studies with both real covariates and text and randomize treatment conditional on the propensity score model constructed from the covariates but not the text and estimate causal effect given only text not the covariates then any estimated nonzero treatment effect is only bias semisynthetic datasets semisynthetic datasets use real covariates and synthetically generate treatment and outcome as in the atlantic causal inference competition several applications in this review use real metadata or latent aspects of text to simulate treatment and outcome johansson et al simulate treatment and outcome from two centroids in topic model space from newswire text veitch et al use indicators of an article’s “buzzy” keywords roberts et al use “quantitative methodology” categories of articles that were handcoded by other researchers open problems semisynthetic datasets that use real covariates of text seem to be a better evaluation strategy than purely synthetic datasets

a single tweet representation h is computed as the sum of the resulting contextualized vector representations p i a i h i where a i is the selfattention score in timestep i the tweet representation h is subsequently passed to the output layer using a sigmoid activation function ulmfit the universal language model finetuning ulmfit is a method for efficient transfer learning the key intuition is to train a text encoder on a language modelling task i e predicting the next token in a sequence where data is abundant then finetune it on a target task where data is more limited during finetuning ulmfit uses gradual layer unfreezing to avoid catastrophic forgetting we experiment with using awdlstm as the base text encoder pretrained on the wikitext data set and we finetune it on our own parody classification task for this purpose after the awslstm layers we add a fullyconnected layer with a relu activation function followed by an output layer with a sigmoid activation function before each of these two additional layers we perform batch normalization bert and roberta bidirectional encoder representations from transformers bert is a language model based on transformer networks pretrained on large corpora the model makes use of multiple multihead attention layers to learn bidirectional embeddings for input tokens it is trained for masked language modelling where a fraction of the input tokens in a given sequence are masked and the task is to predict a masked word given its context bert uses wordpieces which are passed through an embedding layer and get summed together with positional and segment embeddings the former introduce positional information to the attention layers while the latter contain information about the location of a segment similar to ulmfit we finetune the bertbase model for predicting parody tweets by adding an output dense layer for binary classification and feeding it with the ‘classification’ token we further experiment with roberta which is an extenstion of bert trained on more data and different hyperparameters roberta has been showed to improve performance in various benchmarks compared to the original bert

for each assignment this study reports the assignment identifier the number of student attempts and the assignment size measured in lines of code loc students were permitted multiple attempts per assignment consequently the automated assessment system processed and archived a total of lines of verilog code amounting to megabytes of data while these average loc figures may appear modest compared to other programming assignments this is consistent with the terse notation characteristic of hardware description languages hdls regarding submission trends while the general pattern aligned with prior observations a notable decline in the tenth assignment was unexpected for the analysis the final submission from each student was selected these submissions consisted of single text files containing verilog module descriptions three distinct versions of each submission were generated to represent varying levels of code normalization original the unmodified student submission cleaned all comments were removed and whitespace was normalized to ensure a single space between tokens with newlines retained only at the end of statements and blocks tokenized based on the cleaned version but with all identifiers and numeric literals replaced by generic constants to generate the cleaned and tokenized versions the scanner component of an open source verilog frontend was adapted this component originally implemented using flex and c was extended with additional c code to handle comment removal whitespace normalization and the substitution of identifiers and literals the analysis was conducted on a per line basis as this represents a natural unit of division in verilog code it is noted that verilog is less sensitive to statement ordering than procedural languages such as java an initial prototype utilizing unix sort and comm tools was developed however this approach proved computationally prohibitive consequently the analysis was reimplemented in python reducing runtime to seconds and enabling the execution of numerous analytical variations the primary objective of this study was to quantify the extent to which shared code influenced the assessments submitted by students

while research question rq maintains a broad scope rqs and are specifically directed toward preparation for industry due to space constraints findings pertaining to preparation for academic careers are omitted from the main text with only key results summarized in sections and to comprehensively capture the spectrum of faculty perspectives regarding the objectives of a computer science cs degree a qualitative interview methodology was deemed most appropriate a quantitative approach such as a multiple choice questionnaire would have constrained responses to predetermined options potentially introducing bias by limiting the data to the researchers preconceived notions conversely the qualitative approach afforded participants the autonomy to articulate their views fully and in their own words to ensure that the research questions were adequately addressed while preserving participant freedom of expression a semi structured interview format was employed this method utilizes a core set of questions supplemented by follow up inquiries prompted by the participants responses for data analysis a phenomenographic approach was selected to identify the full range of experiences and beliefs among participants rather than seeking a singular objective truth all interviews were audio recorded and subsequently transcribed for analysis the interview protocol was developed through an iterative process involving an initial draft and a pilot study conducted with four senior phd students based on the pilot results the questions were refined to ensure they elicited responses that directly addressed the research objectives representative questions included what does it mean to you for someone to be a computer scientist and what do you perceive as the role of undergraduate cs education in preparing students for industry the complete interview protocol is available publicly online participant recruitment utilized convenience sampling wherein personalized invitations were sent to professional contacts of the final two authors while recruitment from external institutions proved challenging the final sample included faculty from three distinct institutional types a research intensive university the authors home institution a large primarily undergraduate institution and a small private liberal arts college the distribution of participants across these institutions along with the size of their respective cs programs is detailed in the accompanying data all interviews were conducted by the first author with the second or third author taking notes during the initial ten sessions

this initiative facilitates family engagement by creating opportunities for discussions regarding school curricula enabling family members to contribute their expertise and allowing students to reinforce their understanding through peer instruction by leveraging libraries as central hubs for the circulation and gameplay of board games the project aims to expand the temporal and spatial boundaries within which computational thinking can be cultivated and supported however the efficacy of this approach is contingent upon the circulation patterns of these materials and the extent to which family involvement can be sustained beyond the institutional settings of the library and school consequently the primary objective is to design school based computer science instruction that strategically integrates school library resources specific focus is placed on the library s capacity to serve as a focal point for the development and maintenance of student interest as well as on the mobility of computational learning objects—specifically computer science cs board games—to ensure they are accessible to students in home environments in collaboration with educators and librarians an eight week instructional unit was developed utilizing an unplugged to plugged pedagogical sequence this unit incorporates cs board games alongside their corresponding scratch based digital counterparts the curriculum follows a structured progression introduction and gameplay weeks – students learn and engage with the physical cs board games digital adaptation weeks – students interact with scratch instantiations of the games performing edits and modifications design and dissemination weeks – students design custom levels for the board games presentation week students share their designs with instructors and peers instructional delivery was coordinated to include six preparatory lessons led by classroom teachers each lasting to minutes for example the initial lesson introduced the board game and its fundamental rules subsequently during scheduled library sessions librarians reviewed daily activities building upon the concepts introduced in the classroom the remaining library time approximately minutes was dedicated to paired gameplay during which librators circulated to provide targeted assistance and address inquiries in later phases classroom teachers delivered brief presentations on scratch demonstrating the controls of the digital game versions following these introductions students engaged with the digital iterations of the board games during their library sessions

complementary properties of convolutional neural networks cnns and svms to synthesize their respective advantages to construct the input for the cnn feature extractor a dimensional vector was generated for each word by concatenating two distinct components word embeddings a publicly available wordvec dictionary trained on a million word google news corpus using the continuous bag of words cbow architecture provided dimensional vectors for out of vocabulary terms random vectors were utilized part of speech pos tags six fundamental pos categories noun verb adjective adverb preposition and conjunction were encoded as a dimensional binary vector utilizing the stanford tagger for annotation the final input sequence for the cnn was formed by concatenating three segments left padding two dummy tokens represented by zero vectors were prepended to each text to accommodate convolutional kernels with a maximum size of three text sequence the dimensional vectors corresponding to the words were concatenated in their original order right padding at least two dummy zero vector tokens were appended to each sentence to ensure sufficient space for convolution to maintain uniform dimensionality across the corpus all texts were further right padded with additional dummy tokens as necessary the experimental corpus consisted exclusively of short single sentence texts with a maximum length of words the cnn architecture comprised seven layers input layer neurons convolutional layer kernel size of with feature maps utilizing the hyperbolic tangent tanh activation function max pooling layer pooling size of convolutional layer kernel size of with feature maps also employing the tanh activation function max pooling layer pooling size of fully connected layer neurons serving as the source for extracted features regularization was applied via dropout on this penultimate layer alongside l norm constraints on the weight vectors output layer a softmax layer with two neurons corresponding to the binary sentiment polarity labels positive or negative

the provided text illustrates a fundamental challenge in aspect based sentiment analysis absa the identification of sentiment bearing phrases associated with specific aspects within a sentence for instance in a given context the term better may convey a positive sentiment toward coffee while overpriced indicates a negative sentiment regarding sandwiches the primary objective of an absa classifier is to learn these intricate mappings between aspects and their corresponding sentiment indicators this work posits that accurate sentiment prediction for a specific aspect is significantly enhanced by incorporating knowledge of other aspects present within the same sentence sentiments are often interdependent particularly in sentences containing conjunctions such as and but however or though which can cause aspects to share or contrast in sentiment consider the example food is usually very good though i wonder about freshness of raw vegetables here the aspect raw vegetables lacks an explicit sentiment marker however the positive sentiment attributed to food via good combined with the adversative conjunction though logically implies a negative sentiment for the vegetables consequently aspects arranged sequentially exhibit high correlation and complex interplay to model these dynamics we propose a two stage framework first we independently generate aspect aware sentence representations for all identified aspects utilizing gated recurrent units grus and an attention mechanism subsequently we employ memory networks to iteratively align the representation of the target aspect with those of other aspects thereby refining the target s representation this optimized representation is then passed to a softmax classifier for final sentiment determination empirical evaluations demonstrate that our proposed method outperforms current state of the art approaches by an average of across two distinct domains restaurant reviews and laptop evaluations as the volume of textual data from wikis microblogs and social media platforms continues to grow sentiment analysis has become increasingly critical this field necessitates the resolution of several related natural language processing nlp challenges including aspect extraction absa a pivotal subtask of sentiment analysis focuses on classifying the sentiment polarity of individual aspects within a sentence recent advancements in deep neural network research have yielded novel frameworks that have achieved notable performance improvements in this domain

our method does not require direct supervision from the probing tasks nor do we introduce additional parameters to the probing process our experiments on bert show that syntactic trees recovered from bert using our method are significantly better than linguisticallyuninformed baselines we further feed the empirically induced dependency structures into a downstream sentiment classification task and find its improvement compatible with or even superior to a humandesigned dependency schema introduction recent prevalent pretrained language models such as elmo bert and xlnet achieve stateoftheart performance for a diverse array of downstream nlp tasks an interesting area of research is to investigate the interpretability of these pretrained models i e the linguistic properties they capture most recent approaches are built upon the idea of probing classifiers a probe is a simple neural network with a small additional set of parameters that uses the feature representations generated by a pretrained model e g hidden state activations attention weights and is trained to perform a supervised task e g dependency labeling the performance of a probe is used to measure the quality of the generated representations with the assumption that the measured quality is mostly attributable to the pretrained language model one downside of such approach as pointed out in is that a probe introduces a new set of additional parameters which makes the results difficult to interpret is it the pretrained model that captures the linguistic information or is it the probe that learns the downstream task itself and thus encodes the information in its additional parameter space in this paper we propose a parameterfree probing technique called perturbed masking to analyze and interpret pretrained models the main idea is to introduce the perturbed masking technique into the masked language modeling mlm objective to measure the impact a word x j has on predicting another word x i sec and then induce the global linguistic properties e g dependency trees from this interword information

transductive support vector machines tsvm joachims represent a widely adopted semisupervised approach that extends standard support vector machines by minimizing the number of unlabeled data points located near the decision margin similarly the semisupervised variational autoencoder semivae kingma et al leverages deep generative models to effectively utilize unlabeled data this architecture serves as a core component of the proposed semiorc model furthermore the semisupervised sequential variational autoencoder ssvae xu et al employs a modified long short term memory lstm network as its decoder and currently stands as the state of the art semisupervised model for text classification however a common limitation among these baselines is their lack of interpretability as black box semisupervised learning models they are unable to highlight keywords that are informative to prediction outcomes additionally we include an ablation baseline orc which constitutes the supervised variant of semiorc unlike the proposed method orc excludes unlabeled data from the document representation modeling process regarding evaluation we adhere to standard metrics for multilabel classification specifically hamming loss subset accuracy and the micro f score hamming loss schapire and singer quantifies the average hamming distance between true and predicted label sets accuracy is defined as the subset accuracy measuring the proportion of instances where the predicted label set exactly matches the true label set the micro f score manning et al provides a weighted average of precision and recall derived from the aggregate counts of true positives false negatives and false positives the experimental setup involves implementing the semiorc model using tensorflow on a workstation equipped with an nvidia geforce gtx ti gpu model training is optimized via the adam optimizer kingma and ba and regularized using dropout srivastava et al gal and ghahramani hyperparameters are configured as follows the number of projection matrices h is set to and the word embedding dimension d is set to the learning rate and the weight parameter alpha are empirically tuned to and respectively while the dropout rate is varied between and for the logistic regression and tsvm baselines document representations are learned using docvec le and mikolov with risk label prediction performed via classifiers built using the scikit learn library pedregosa et al for the semivae and ssvae baselines the encoders decoders and classifiers are all constructed using lstm networks finally to ensure statistical robustness all methods are evaluated using fold cross validation with each experiment repeated times

are easy to spot using the frequency in the two spaces and do not neatly fit the definition of usage change note that our method does not require extensive filtering of words – we only filter words based on their frequency in the corpus we use a large value of k in practice because large neighbor sets are more stable than small ones leading to improved stability for our algorithm as well limitations similar to previous methods our method assumes high quality embeddings and for english experiments we also filter stopwords according to the predefined list from nltk while this value may seem arbitrary we tested several values in that range which yielded very similar results however the appropriate range may change when used with smaller corpora or substantially different vocabulary sizes we consider k to be the only hyperparameter of our method and note that it is rather easy to set hence also a relatively large corpus indeed in many cases we can expect large quantities of data to be available to the user especially when considering the fact that the data needed is raw text rather than labeled text using a limited amount of data results in lower quality embeddings but also with smaller vocabulary size which might affect our method for highquality embeddings with small vocabulary sizes we believe that changing k accordingly should suffice naturally results will likely degrade as embeddings quality deteriorate it is also important to note that like previous approaches our method does not attempt to provide any guarantees that the detected words have indeed undergone usage change it is only intended to propose and highlight candidates for such words these candidates are meant to later be verified by a user who needs to interpret the results in light of their hypothesis and familiarity with the domain

mazidi and tarau flor and riordan use dependency srl and ner templates but do not handle modalities and negation in a robust manner moreover there is plenty of availability of core linguistic resources like verbnet and propbank which provide further unique ways to look at sentences and ask questions differently besides the generally wellestablished dependency and srl parses synqg is a rulebased framework which generates questions by identifying potential short answers in the nodes of crucial dependency relations the modifying arguments of each predicate in the form of semantic roles named entities and other generic entities the states of verbnet’s thematic roles in the form of semantic predicates and propbank roleset specific natural language descriptions each of the five heuristics works independently generating a combined set of questionanswer pairs which are eventually backtranslated we describe each of these five sources dependency heuristics dependency trees are syntactic tree structures wherein syntactic units in the form of words are connected via directed links the finite verb is considered as the structural root of the tree and all other syntactic units are either directly nsubj dobj etc or indirectly xcomp iobj etc dependent on this finite verb we present rules over such dependency trees annotated according to the universal dependencies ud format to extract dependency structures we use the parser of gardner et al we make use of propbank s predicateargument structure srl for clausal extraction of the verb headed by a select few dependency nodes which can serve as answers these rules treat the clause as a combination of a subject an object the head verb and other noncore arguments the clause is further refined with modals auxiliaries and negations if found around the verb finally we make use of a set of predefined handwritten templates a few of which are described in table in each of the templates we convert what to who whom when or where depending on the named entity of the potential answer and do to does or did according to the tense and number of the subject to ensure subjectverb agreement

this study presents a framework designed to enhance diversity equity and inclusion dei objectives for both employers and local communities by analyzing the alignment between educational curricula and occupational requirements leveraging neural representations of curricular and job related documents the framework quantifies education occupation alignment across three primary dimensions the extent of shared specialized knowledge and skills the prevalence of instructional design features fostering general social and cognitive competencies and the overall semantic similarity between the respective corpora the proposed methodology aims to assist researchers in addressing critical education and economic policy questions while empowering stakeholders to make more informed decisions regarding recruitment curriculum development and academic pathway selection while this work outlines the high level conceptual architecture through illustrative examples it intentionally reserves technical specifics for future elaboration by the authors and the broader research community given the heightened significance of education occupation alignment in the post pandemic era and the underutilization of available curricular and job content data we advocate for increased interdisciplinary collaboration to foster healthier future dynamics in this domain this research was conducted under the auspices of the ibm science for social good initiative furthermore the study addresses challenges within augmentative and alternative communication aac systems which are designed to facilitate conversation for individuals with complex communication needs but currently suffer from low adoption and retention rates a review of prior text recommendation systems indicates their limited success in mitigating these issues to address this gap we propose the application of dialogue act classification to aac conversations dialogue act classification involves analyzing conversation transcripts to identify the specific intent of each turn e g classifying how are you as an open ended question with labeling schemas adapted to the specific context we evaluated a state of the art model on a limited aac dataset comparing performance between models trained on aac specific data versus those trained on non aac corpora the model trained on aac data demonstrated superior performance accuracy compared to the model trained on non aac data accuracy these findings underscore the critical necessity of incorporating representative datasets in future experiments consequently we emphasize the need for the collection of additional labeled aac datasets and outline key areas for future research

abstract this study presents state of the art results for multilingual all words word sense disambiguation wsd achieved through a training regimen utilizing exclusively english data introduction recent scholarship has increasingly focused on integrating prior knowledge into neural networks for natural language processing nlp tasks this integration is typically realized either through pretraining on self supervised objectives such as language modeling peters et al devlin et al or by incorporating information derived from knowledge bases peters et al logan et al in the domain of word sense disambiguation wsd —the task of mapping a word within a specific context to its most appropriate meaning from a finite set of candidates navigli —the historical divide between supervised learning and knowledge based approaches has been addressed by various initiatives these efforts aim to learn effective vector representations loureiro and jorge scarlini et al within the same latent space as contextualized embeddings as well as by leveraging definitional knowledge within supervised sequence learning architectures luo et al kumar et al huang et al however lexical knowledge bases lkbs such as wordnet miller and babelnet navigli and ponzetto offer a substantial repository of structured relational knowledge e g hypernymy meronymy and similarity this resource is frequently underutilized due to the complexities associated with integrating such structured data into neural architectures while knowledge based wsd algorithms agirre and soroa moro et al can exploit this information to rival supervised approaches utilizing pre contextualized embeddings maru et al their performance generally remains inferior huang et al vial et al building upon the extended wsd integrating sense embeddings ewise framework kumar et al a neural system that incorporates prior knowledge via synset embeddings we introduce enhanced wsd integrating synset embeddings and relations ewiser ewiser represents a hybrid approach that combines knowledge based and supervised methodologies by explicitly integrating relational information from the wordnet lkb the primary contributions of this work include the introduction of a novel structured logits mechanism which facilitates the exploitation of concept relatedness as defined by the edges within the lkb

artificial intelligence ai is increasingly transforming healthcare by enabling cost effective diagnostic alternatives and enhancing clinical decision making for instance applications such as total body photography utilize advanced image recognition technology to analyze dermal lesions captured via mobile devices by comparing patient imagery against extensive databases containing millions of malignant cases such systems can reduce the reliance on costly in person dermatological consultations beyond dermatology ai facilitates the simulation of biological processes to generate predictive models for diagnosis this capability is underpinned by big data analytics which integrates vast datasets from wearable devices with historical medical records a notable example of this data consolidation occurred in when ibm acquired several entities including truven health analytics which held hundreds of millions of medical records for billion in the realm of natural language processing nlp ai systems are being deployed to manage scientific literature electronic health records and patient narratives while challenges remain in fully comprehending the nuances of human language cognitive computing platforms like ibm watson for oncology have demonstrated significant utility watson accesses diverse data sources including medical encyclopedias scientific databases and knowledge ontologies to provide accurate responses to clinically formulated queries since the system has been utilized at the memorial sloan kettering cancer center to support treatment decisions for lung cancer patients with its knowledge base continuously updated to reflect new disease data furthermore initiatives such as ibm medical sieve aim to develop next generation cognitive assistants equipped with multimodal analytics and clinical reasoning capabilities for radiology and cardiology collaborative efforts continue to expand the scope of ai in medicine google deepmind health in partnership with university college london hospital is developing algorithms to automate the differentiation between healthy and malignant tissues in the head and neck region additionally mobile platforms like babylon health facilitate remote consultations with licensed physicians while organizations such as turbine ai leverage ai to formulate personalized oncology treatment protocols collectively these advancements illustrate the growing integration of artificial intelligence into modern medical practice

abstract furthermore we demonstrate that the proposed calibration methodology can function as an uncertainty aware entity specific decoding mechanism this approach enhances the performance of the underlying model without incurring additional training costs or necessitating supplementary data empirical results indicate that our method surpasses existing calibration techniques across named entity recognition ner part of speech pos tagging and question answering qa tasks moreover the integration of this decoding step yields performance improvements across multiple tasks and benchmark datasets including in out of domain test scenarios thereby simultaneously advancing both model calibration and predictive accuracy introduction contemporary natural language processing nlp systems based on machine learning frequently generate confidence scores alongside their output predictions these scores serve as quantitative measures of predictor confidence a well calibrated confidence score constitutes a probability measure that exhibits a strong correlation with the actual likelihood of a prediction s correctness consequently nlp systems equipped with calibrated confidence metrics can identify instances where predictions are likely erroneous thereby signaling a lack of reliability this capability is a prerequisite for the responsible deployment of nlp systems in safety critical domains such as healthcare and finance while the calibration of predictors is a well established problem in machine learning guo et al platt widely adopted methods in this field are predominantly formulated for binary or multiclass classification problems naeini et al nguyen and o connor however the structured output schemes inherent to nlp tasks—such as information extraction ie sang and de meulder and extractive question answering qa rajpurkar et al —often involve output spaces that are excessively large for standard multiclass calibration frameworks formally this study examines nlp models that provide conditional probabilities p theta y x for a structured output y given an input x depending on the specific task the output y may represent a label sequence as in pos tagging or ner a span prediction as in extractive qa or a relation prediction as in relation extraction while p theta y x is intended to serve as a metric for the model s confidence in its prediction it frequently constitutes a poor estimate of true confidence in sequence labeling tasks the output space is often vast consequently the probability p theta y x for any specific output instance y tends to be negligible rendering it an unreliable indicator of model certainty

the hale model is closer to the traditional model of suspense as being about uncertainty in contrast the ely model is more in line with the second view that uncertainty matters less than consequentially different outcomes in nlp suspense is studied most directly in natural language generation with systems such as dramatis and suspenser two planningbased story generators that use the theory of gerrig and bernardo that suspense is created when a protagonist faces obstacles that reduce successful outcomes our approach in contrast models suspense using general language models finetuned on stories without planning and domain knowledge the advantage is that the model can be trained on large volumes of available narrative text without requiring expensive annotations making it more generalisable other work emphasises the role of characters and their development in story understanding or summarisation a further important element of narrative structure is plot i e the sequence of events in which characters interact neural models have explicitly modelled events or the results of actions on the other hand some neural generation models just use a hierarchical model on top of a language model our architecture follows this approach models of suspense definitions in order to formalise measures of suspense we assume that a story consists of a sequence of sentences these sentences are processed one by one and the sentence at the current timepoint t is represented by an embedding e t see section for how embeddings are computed each embedding is associated with a probability p e t continuations of the story are represented by a set of possible next sentences whose embeddings are denoted by e i t

evaluation of the text based ideal point model this study evaluates the text based ideal point tbip model using senate speeches benchmarking its performance against established methods for scaling political texts slapin and proksch lauderdale and herzog a the tbip demonstrates superior efficacy yielding ideal points that most closely align with those derived from roll call voting records furthermore an analysis of tweets authored by u s senators confirms that the model accurately recovers vote based ideal points in this medium notably the deviations observed between text derived and vote based ideal points in both speeches and tweets offer qualitatively significant insights finally the model is applied to tweets from the democratic presidential candidates a dataset lacking corresponding voting records in this context the tbip successfully maps the candidates along an interpretable spectrum ranging from progressive to moderate the text based ideal point model we introduce the text based ideal point tbip model a probabilistic framework designed to infer political ideology from textual data the development of this model builds upon two foundational probabilistic approaches bayesian ideal point estimation and poisson factorization topic models background bayesian ideal points ideal points serve as quantitative measures of a lawmaker s political preferences derived from their roll call voting behavior poole and rosenthal jackman clinton et al consider a cohort of lawmakers casting binary yea or nay votes on a common set of legislation let v ij denote the binary vote of lawmaker i on bill j the bayesian ideal point model posits the existence of scalar latent variables for each lawmaker x i and scalar latent variables for each bill alpha j beta j the model assumes that voting behavior follows a factor structure defined as begin aligned x i sim mathcal n alpha j beta j sim mathcal n v ij sim text bernoulli sigma alpha j x i beta j end aligned tag where sigma t frac e t represents the logistic function in this framework the latent variable x i is defined as the lawmaker s ideal point while beta j represents the bill s polarity when x i and beta j share the same sign lawmaker i is more likely to support bill j conversely opposing signs indicate a higher probability of opposition the per bill intercept term alpha j is termed popularity this parameter accounts for the inherent consensus or controversy of a bill capturing scenarios where all lawmakers are likely to vote uniformly for or against a measure irrespective of their ideological positioning

parkman delineates the scope of research regarding the impostor phenomenon ip noting its documentation across a diverse array of professional sectors including k– education healthcare accountancy finance law marketing and higher education historically the preponderance of ip research has been conducted within clinical and university settings consequently the majority of studies have drawn participants from individuals undergoing clinical therapy or enrolled as university students substantial inquiry has also been directed toward the fields of gender psychology health professions and medicine and academia notably however a quantitative study specifically focusing on computer science students remains absent from the current literature it is widely accepted that the experience of ip exerts a detrimental influence on individual well being with empirical evidence linking it to elevated levels of depression and anxiety cokley et al initiated an investigation into the correlation between perceived discrimination and mental health in a subsequent study the authors examined a cohort of educational psychology students from a southwestern united states university who self identified as members of minority groups this research assessed participants perceptions of discrimination levels of depression and anxiety and the presence of impostor feelings the findings across these studies were consistent offering deeper insight into the impact of ip on student populations specifically the research indicated that while african american students reported the highest levels of perceived discrimination asian american students exhibited higher levels of the impostor phenomenon compared to their african american and latino a american counterparts an analysis of predictive relationships revealed that contrary to initial expectations impostor feelings served as a more significant predictor of mental health outcomes than perceived discrimination or minority status stress for african american students furthermore for asian american students impostor feelings were predictive of increased depression and anxiety these adverse outcomes are not exclusive to minority populations similar negative effects have been observed in other communities for instance in the conclusions of a pilot study regarding medical students villwock et al reported that nearly one quarter of male students and almost half of female students experienced the impostor phenomenon which was found to be significantly associated with burnout indices

in the initial stages of an engineering career professionals are frequently tasked with establishing testing protocols for components such as antennas while technicians typically execute the tests and collect data the engineer is responsible for analyzing the results and recommending design modifications should performance metrics deviate from targets at this juncture assigned analytical and design tasks are generally well defined the expectation for creative insight typically arises only after several years of professional experience new engineers must recognize that budget and schedule are the primary constraints within the commercial engineering sector success in this environment requires ingenuity in developing innovative cost effective and timely solutions this endeavor is facilitated by significant advancements in analytical tools over the past few decades including superior computer software and enhanced machining precision however reliance on software necessitates a critical approach to accuracy while familiarity with specific tools increases confidence in their output engineers must remain vigilant as software can occasionally yield incorrect results consequently validating the reasonableness of computational outputs is an essential practice contemporary engineering practice involves extensive computer utilization estimated at to percent of working time this encompasses not only technical analysis but also communication via email and the preparation of necessary documentation strong written and oral communication skills are therefore vital to professional advancement engineers are required to produce various reports including quarterly status updates engineering accomplishment summaries and final design documents documentation requirements vary significantly by sector in defense related projects where rigorous documentation is mandatory up to ten percent of project costs may be allocated to this function with formats often specified by the client conversely commercial projects typically require minimal documentation limited primarily to product data sheets and maintenance or installation guides with design details often omitted nevertheless the commercial sector places a high premium on writing skills through the process of proposal development when a client solicits proposals to address specific requirements the responding organization must invest resources to articulate a strategic approach and associated costs making effective proposal writing a critical competency

the previous research on sentiment used adjectives to identify the polarity of sentences some researchers used adverbs and adjectives together to build a lexicon some used adjectives adverbs and verbs for our persian sentiment lexicon we used adjectives adverbs verbs and nouns because all these words and phrases are useful to determine the polarity of the sentence a lexicon can be developed in different ways such as manually or using existing lexicons such as sentiwordnet or general inquirer the words and phrases used in our lexicon were taken from different resources such as movie review websites weblogs and facebook there were four different categories of sources namely websites related to movies news mobile phones and computers we manually assigned polarity between – and to each word and phrase the degree of intensity was indicated for example the persian words for happy cheerful and delighted have different positive values in order to assign polarity manually to some words and phrases we used the textblob python package used to assign polarity to words phrases and sentences in english for this we translated persian words into english we also manually assigned a part of speech pos tag to each word or phrase the lexicon contains adjectives adverbs nouns and verbs in order to evaluate the performance of our lexicon we used two classification algorithms we used our lexicon to assign polarity to the features extracted from the dataset the preprocessing step consisted of four parts tokenisation normalisation stopword removal and stemming normalization was used to remove noise from the text stemming was used to remove inflection of infected forms the lexicon only provides the base form of the words the purpose of the feature selection was to remove unnecessary features which improved the performance and efficiency of the classification the features we used were based on word polarity pos tag and presence and frequency of sentiment words presence and frequency of sentiment words the sentiment words identify the overall polarity for sentiment classification

this paper demonstrates an application of a mlm in cs education research because of the naturally occurring nesting in education settings mlms are needed when samples are drawn from multiple courses or institutions as the cs education field continues to grow and conduct larger studies it will be necessary for researchers to become familiar with and be able to implement mlms of central importance to this paper students’ individual perceptions of instructional practices were not associated with retention a student who perceives the classroom as using more peer cooperation or as being more teacherdirected is no more or less likely to take additional cs courses than a student who perceives that same classroom environment as less so a second central finding of this paper is that the aggregate rating of cl in a course section was associated with a lower retention rate it is possible that this unexpected relationship is due to a greater emphasis on using cl in introductory courses which contain higher numbers of nonmajor students who are unlikely to take cs beyond that level reviewing the course section means for cl revealed not that cs courses were consistently higher in using cl but that cs course sections tended to be more similar in their aggregate ratings of cl ms from to than upperlevel courses ms from to at this time it is unknown how this variability in the extent to which cl is used in upperlevel courses might impact students’ experiences and satisfaction with their course but future research should explore this topic the measures of perceived instructional practices included in this study were targeted at general instructional practices and did not include csspecific practices that are prominent in the literature future research should examine the impact of more specific instructional practices on retention such as active learning pair programming gamebased learning contextbased instruction and the use of multimedia a final point of importance is that the general instructional practices examined here were not predictive of retention in cs but concept mastery was underscoring the importance of using highquality evidence based teaching title screenplay summarization via latent narrative structure authors pinelopi papalampidi frank keller lea frermann and mirella lapata affiliations institute for language cognition and computation school of informatics university of edinburgh school of computing and information systems university of melbourne abstract conventional extractive summarization models are predominantly trained on news corpora which are characterized by brevity and an inverted pyramid structure that prioritizes critical information at the document s onset consequently these models exhibit a positional bias often relying on heuristics that favor initial sentences such approaches prove insufficient for long form narratives which possess complex architectures and distribute information incrementally this study proposes the explicit integration of underlying narrative structures into both unsupervised and supervised extractive summarization frameworks we formalize narrative structure through the identification of key narrative events or turning points treating them as latent variables to optimize the extraction of scene sequences from screenplays empirical evaluation on the csi corpus of television screenplays augmented with scene level summarization annotations demonstrates that latent turning points correlate significantly with pivotal episode elements furthermore the proposed method yields superior summarization performance compared to general extractive algorithms resulting in summaries that are both more comprehensive and diverse introduction the field of automatic summarization has experienced a resurgence in recent years driven by the efficacy of modern neural network architectures and the availability of large scale datasets comprising hundreds of thousands of document summary pairs to date the majority of research efforts have focused on news articles which are typically concise and adhere to a formulaic structure that places the most essential and novel information at the beginning of the text however this focus limits the applicability of current models to other genres particularly long form narratives where information is presented piecemeal rather than upfront to address this limitation this paper introduces a methodology that incorporates the intrinsic structural properties of narratives into the summarization process by modeling narrative structure as a sequence of latent turning points we aim to capture the essential progression of a story we validate this approach using the csi corpus of tv screenplays a dataset we have enhanced with scene level summarization labels our results indicate that identifying these latent turning points not only aligns with the critical aspects of the narrative but also significantly enhances the quality of the generated summaries overcoming the positional biases inherent in models trained solely on news data note the specific case details regarding victim mike kimble and the subsequent plot points provided in the source text appear to be illustrative examples of the narrative content within the csi corpus rather than part of the formal academic argument as such they have been excluded from this formal rewrite to maintain the generic high level style of the abstract and introduction

this approach effectively eliminates surface level language specific variations that often impede traditional n gram models retaining instead persistent and semantically significant linguistic features with clear interpretability we posit that this constitutes an optimal method for integrating syntactic information into machine learning frameworks it is important to note that while syntactic n grams sn grams are derived through distinct mechanisms they remain fundamentally n grams and are applicable to any task traditionally served by standard n grams however the adoption of sn grams entails specific computational costs primarily the generation of these features requires syntactic parsing although numerous parsers exist for a wide range of languages the parsing process introduces latency furthermore while parsers are readily available for well studied languages such as english and spanish coverage remains limited for others a pertinent avenue for future research involves evaluating the efficacy of shallow parsing while significantly faster than full parsing it remains to be determined whether it yields sn grams of sufficient quality our hypothesis suggests that shallow parsing may be adequate for many applications additionally future work should investigate whether sn grams facilitate more robust cross lingual result comparisons although translation inevitably alters certain syntactic relations many are preserved consequently by mitigating the influence of language specific surface structures sn grams offer enhanced comparability across languages in this study we apply sn grams to the authorship attribution task employing three standard classifiers to benchmark their performance against traditional n grams experimental results indicate superior performance when utilizing sn grams beyond empirical gains we argue that sn grams provide genuine linguistic interpretability regarding authorial style as they directly reflect underlying syntactic relations to illustrate the application of sn grams consider the phrases eat with wooden spoon and eat with metallic spoon both dependency and constituency representations of syntactic relations can be utilized these are equivalent provided the head of each constituent is identified in dependency based approaches sn grams are extracted by traversing the paths indicated by dependency arrows in constituency based approaches head nodes are first promoted to occupy bifurcation points sn grams are then constructed by initiating from dependent constituents and incorporating the heads from these bifurcations

instructors of advanced computing courses should recognize that students may have acquired relevant prior knowledge through coursework at community colleges familiarity with specific curricula and transfer protocols from local and state institutions would enable educators to more effectively contextualize new material within students existing academic frameworks such pedagogical adjustments can enhance instructor accessibility for upward transfer students furthermore it is essential to actively dismantle misconceptions regarding community college and transfer students this demographic is highly diverse empirical evidence indicates that upward transfer students frequently demonstrate academic performance comparable to or exceeding that of their peers consequently advanced computing cohorts likely include a significant number of high achieving transfer students whose success challenges negative stereotypes regarding their preparation and potential explicitly acknowledging these realities can help mitigate bias and foster more inclusive learning environments research findings indicate that upward transfer students are disproportionately likely to experience financial hardships necessitating leaves of absence this trend is consistent with data showing that these students rely more heavily on loans whereas their non transfer counterparts more frequently utilize scholarships grants and other non debt financial aid to address these inequities institutions should develop scholarship programs specifically targeted at upward transfer students in computing in designing such initiatives it is critical to recognize that these students often require support extending beyond tuition costs given their tendency to be older and to have dependents additionally upward transfer students report a higher incidence of financing their education through part time or full time employment while empirical studies on the impact of student employment yield mixed results existing literature generally suggests that off campus employment may correlate with adverse academic outcomes whereas on campus employment can enhance student engagement furthermore internships—though not always classified as traditional paid employment—are consistently linked to positive academic and career trajectories in light of these findings it is imperative that institutions provide upward transfer students with access to high impact employment opportunities
