motivated by prior research and preliminary empirical findings this study proposes the utilization of word vector norms as a metric for quantifying sentence difficulty the methodology involves training a lightweight word embedding model on the training corpus to derive an embedding matrix denoted as e wv consequently a source sentence x x dots x i dots x i is transformed into its distributed representations mathbf x dots mathbf x i dots mathbf x i via e wv the norm based sentence difficulty is subsequently calculated as the aggregate of the norms of these vector representations text difficulty x sum i i mathbf x i quad empirical observations indicate that longer sentences as well as those containing rare or semantically significant terms typically exhibit higher difficulty scores within the context of curriculum learning cl the proposed norm based criterion offers three primary advantages computational efficiency the approach requires minimal computational resources and time as it relies on the training of a simple word embedding model linguistic fidelity it effectively captures linguistically motivated features including word frequency and sentence length model adaptability it efficiently incorporates model dependent features such as the dynamic significance of words during the learning process norm based model competence in addition to establishing an optimal difficulty metric the strategic ordering of the curriculum is critical as categorized by zhang et al b cl strategies are generally divided into deterministic and probabilistic approaches evidence suggests that probabilistic strategies yield superior performance in neural machine translation nmt largely due to the stochasticity introduced during mini batch training without loss of generality this study evaluates the proposed norm based difficulty metric within a standard probabilistic cl framework competence based cl platanios et al within this framework model competence is defined as a function of the training step t outputting a value c t within the interval c t min left frac t sqrt lambda t c right quad here c represents the initial competence at the onset of training while lambda serves as a hyperparameter governing the duration of the curriculum

to ensure replicability the evaluation sets derived from marvin and linzen are restricted to third person singular versus plural distinctions across all target languages rather than encompassing the full range of present tense inflections experimental setup corpora adhering to the methodology of gulordava et al recent wikipedia dumps were acquired for each target language the raw data were processed by removing wikipedia markup via wikiextractor and subsequently tokenized and segmented into sentences using treetagger sentences containing more than unknown tokens were excluded from the dataset given that the evaluation is conducted at the sentence level the preprocessed sentences were shuffled prior to partitioning into training development and test sets to minimize the presence of cross sentential dependencies in the training corpus for each language the resulting corpus comprises approximately million tokens for training with million tokens allocated each for development and testing language specific vocabularies were constructed by selecting the most frequent tokens from the combined training and development sets consistent with standard practice out of vocabulary tokens in all splits were replaced with the token training and evaluation experiments were conducted using both recurrent language models lms and transformer based bidirectional encoders monolingual lstm lms were trained for each language utilizing the optimal hyperparameters established by van schijndel et al additionally a multilingual lstm lm was trained on a concatenated dataset comprising the training corpora of all languages the validation and test sets as well as the vocabularies were similarly concatenated the multilingual model employed the same hyperparameters as the monolingual counterparts during training the corpora were randomly shuffled at the beginning of each epoch prior to batching consequently individual training batches consisted of sentences from multiple languages with high probability to determine model accuracy the total probability of each sentence within the challenge set was computed performance was then assessed by verifying within each minimal pair whether the grammatical sentence received a higher probability score than its ungrammatical counterpart

we constructed a binary classification dataset derived from wikipedia articles wherein a single pronoun is presented alongside two potential antecedent names requiring annotators to identify the correct referent the existing gap dataset webster et al was deemed unsuitable for direct application as it restricts examples to cases where the two candidate antecedents share the same gender in contrast our objective is to examine annotator decision making processes specifically when gender information is available consequently we developed the maybe ambiguous pronoun map dataset while adhering to the methodological framework of webster et al the map dataset does not impose gender matching constraints on the candidate names a primary challenge in ablating gender information lies in the ubiquity of social and syntactic cues social gender associations e g the term nurse often implying female are pervasive and cannot be entirely eliminated without altering the text s natural context similarly syntactic cues cannot be removed in a non circular manner for instance reflexive pronouns e g herself may strongly imply a specific antecedent thereby influencing subsequent coreference judgments even though natural language data occasionally contains exceptions due to gender fluidity or misgendering to address these challenges we compiled a high coverage inventory of english terms signaling lexical gender including terms of address e g mr mrs and semantically gendered nouns e g mother this list was generated by aggregating multiple online resources primarily those designed for english language learners followed by manual curation the compilation methodology and the final lexicon are published alongside the map dataset and its datasheet to systematically obscure specific gender related features we implemented the following substitution protocols neg pro replacement of third person pronouns with gender neutral variants e g they xe ze neg name replacement of proper names with randomized alternatives consisting of a single initial and a surname neg sem substitution of semantically gendered nouns with gender indefinite equivalents neg addr removal of terms of address an illustrative example of these substitutions is provided in figure we conducted two distinct experimental series to evaluate the impact of these features a forward selection ablation which begins with all gender cues removed and reintroduces them sequentially and a backward selection ablation which removes each cue individually from the complete dataset

furthermore an additional gated recurrent unit gru is utilized to monitor emotional states throughout the dialogue while dialoguernn is designed to model inter speaker relationships and is applicable to multiparty datasets the proposed dialoguegcn model demonstrates superior performance compared to both the state of the art and all baseline models across all evaluated datasets these improvements are statistically significant as confirmed by paired t tests on the iemocap dataset dialoguegcn establishes a new state of the art average f score of and an accuracy of these results represent an approximate improvement over dialoguernn and a margin of at least over all other baseline models similarly on the avec dataset dialoguegcn outperforms the current state of the art across all four emotion dimensions valence arousal expectancy and power to elucidate these performance disparities it is essential to examine the architectural distinctions among the models both dialoguegcn and dialoguernn aim to model speaker level context albeit through different mechanisms whereas the remaining baseline models encode only sequential context lacking speaker level representation this absence constitutes a critical limitation in the baseline approaches given the pivotal role of speaker level context in conversational emotion recognition the performance gap between dialoguernn and dialoguegcn is attributed to their differing methods of encoding this context dialoguernn employs a gru network to model individual speaker states however recurrent encoders often struggle with long term information propagation this limitation is particularly pronounced in the iemocap and avec datasets which contain numerous conversations exceeding utterances with average lengths of and utterances respectively in contrast dialoguegcn mitigates this issue by utilizing neighborhood based convolution to model speaker level context thereby enhancing its efficacy on long sequences regarding the meld dataset which comprises multiparty conversations emotion recognition proves considerably more challenging than in the dyadic iemocap and avec datasets utterances in meld are notably shorter and frequently lack explicit emotion specific expressions rendering emotion modeling highly dependent on context furthermore with an average conversation length of utterances and many interactions involving more than five participants the majority of speakers contribute only a limited number of utterances per conversation this sparsity complicates the modeling of inter speaker and self dependencies consequently the performance differential between the baseline models and dialoguegcn on the meld dataset is less pronounced than the disparities observed on the iemocap and avec datasets

to address the problem sets participants were required to author q code fragments implementing the specified functionality adhering to the format established in the quantum katas and standard programming assignments solutions were submitted via a dedicated web platform where they underwent automated verification against a predefined test suite participants received immediate feedback on their submissions enabling the iterative refinement and resubmission of incorrect solutions final assessment was determined exclusively by the count of successfully resolved tasks notably only one student achieved a perfect score securing nd place among participants in alignment with established pedagogical practices in computer science the course replaced the traditional final examination with a capstone project this multifaceted practical assignment served as a culminating academic experience reflecting the course s vision of a hands on introduction to quantum computing for this project students collaborated in teams to define a problem develop a solution implement it using q document their methodology in a written report and deliver a ten minute in class presentation this structure was designed to cultivate diverse software development competencies—including research collaboration technical writing and public speaking—while simultaneously advancing quantum software engineering skills students self organized into teams of three with some forming pairs and selected a project topic for a four week development cycle projects could involve an in depth exploration of course material or an independent study of quantum computing topics not covered in the curriculum the assessment comprised three graded components with the primary requirement being the submission of functional q code consistent with the course s practical emphasis the code was required to illustrate or explore the chosen topic representing an effort equivalent to two to three standard programming assignments crucially the implementation had to be capable of solving at least some problem instances using the quantum development kit qdk simulator this constraint necessitated that students analyze their selected problems to identify small yet non trivial instances capable of demonstrating the efficacy of their implementations thereby adding significant depth to the assignment

recent literature has explored various architectural strategies to enhance sequence generation tasks particularly in the domains of natural language generation and code synthesis to integrate syntactic and lexical representations wan et al employed a tree to sequence model eriguchi et al similarly addressing the structural complexity of sql queries xu et al b modeled queries as directed graphs and utilized a graph to sequence framework to encode global structural information a critical challenge in these tasks is the handling of out of vocabulary oov terms which has been effectively mitigated through copying mechanisms this approach allows models to reuse input segments rather than relying solely on a fixed target vocabulary notable implementations include the hybrid pointer generator network proposed by see et al which integrates pointer networks vinyals et al into standard sequence to sequence seqseq architectures for abstractive summarization furthermore gu et al introduced copynet which incorporates a selective copying mechanism into the seqseq framework while ling et al applied similar techniques to copy strings directly from source code given that the target task represents the inverse of the natural language to code nl to code process methodologies developed for nl to code generation serve as relevant references for instance dong and lapata distinguished logical form nodes based on the presence of child nodes subsequent works by yin and neubig rabinovich et al and xu et al a advanced this by incorporating abstract syntax tree ast node types to guide code generation additionally cai et al leveraged concepts from automata theory specifically utilizing backus naur form bnf to model sql grammar constraints thereby improving the accuracy of generated sql queries

recent literature on domain adaptation has explored several rectification strategies notably the employment of shared private encoder architectures these models are designed to simultaneously capture domain invariant and domain specific features li et al bousmalis et al a kim et al b chang et al often leveraging adversarial and orthogonality loss functions liu et al li et al while the present work does not utilize private encoders we posit that our model effectively captures domain specificity through a sentence specific concept graph furthermore the proposed approach possesses sufficient flexibility to be adapted to shared private encoder frameworks regarding the integration of external knowledge prior research has investigated its utility in both inductive and transductive settings banerjee deng et al specifically a limited number of studies have applied external knowledge such as wikipedia as auxiliary information for domain adaptation using co clustering wang et al and semi supervised learning ssl xiang et al ssl has also been examined within the twitter domain alam et al although our work shares a similar motivation with these studies fundamental distinctions exist primarily we learn graph embeddings at the concept level rather than across complete instances additionally we do not perform classification on individual concept nodes rendering standard ssl methodologies inapplicable to our specific setup in the realm of graph based domain adaptation the emergence of graph neural networks gnns has established graph based methods as a prominent trend in data preprocessing and experimental constraints in the context of the lyrics dataset infilling models offer significant utility particularly given the domain s established reliance on external aids such as rhyming dictionaries to ensure experimental consistency across all models training data was filtered to exclude examples exceeding the maximum sequence length of tokens a constraint dictated by the model with the longest capacity lmall this filtering process resulted in no data loss for the stories dataset a negligible reduction for lyrics and a substantial reduction for the abstracts dataset masking function design we propose a masking function designed to operate on entire documents by selectively obscuring text spans across multiple granularities words n grams sentences paragraphs and full documents models trained via infilling language modeling ilm utilizing this function enable users to explicitly define the granularity of text to be generated at specific locations this mechanism provides intuitive control over output length preventing the generation of extensive text segments e g multiple paragraphs when a user anticipates a single token or word the masking procedure operates as follows hierarchical construction a tree structure is constructed for each training example reflecting the natural hierarchy of documents paragraphs sentences and words probabilistic masking a preorder tree traversal is performed wherein each subtree is masked with a probability of provided that none of its ancestor nodes have already been masked if the root node the entire document is masked the task becomes equivalent to standard language modeling if a leaf node a word is selected for masking the system applies one of two strategies with equal probability mask the individual word mask an n gram of random length ranging from to min n where n is the number of remaining words in the sentence note as gpt utilizes subword tokenization sennrich et al a single word may comprise multiple tokens the masking probability was selected to achieve a marginal token mask rate of approximately across the evaluated datasets aligning with the configuration established by devlin et al furthermore special tokens corresponding to each granularity e g have been added to the model s vocabulary this allows users to explicitly specify the desired output granularity this functionality is demonstrated in our interactive demo available at

unlike prior approaches which modify model architectures to solve the problem this method uses dynamically computed corpuslevel statistics to determine which conversational participants to collect data from diversityinformed data collection produces significantly more diverse data than baseline data collection methods and better results on two downstream tasks emotion classification and dialogue generation this method is generalizable and can be used with other corpuslevel metrics introduction it is welldocumented that neural dialogue models struggle with generating engaging relevant responses and often produce banal responses such as “yeah ” while this may be an appropriate response to a chitchat conversation to keep a human participant engaged diversity of responses is important diverse models vary the language used and the content referenced and the generated utterances differ from the most typical conversation responses some proportion of the time a model which only generates “yeah ” “no ” and “i don’t know” is not diverse and is not be engaging to converse with past work has improved model diversity with innovation on model architectures and decoding strategies we build upon this work to propose a novel method to collect and determine more diverse data to train these models with our method can be used in conjunction with existing generationspecific model innovations some prior work on data collection processes has prioritized diversity for instance rashkin et al prompts crowdworkers to choose an underused emotion class to generate dialogue this work encourages coverage of emotion classes but does not consider the likelihood that some crowdworkers are better at producing certain types of data than others this paper introduces diversityinformed data collection didc a new strategy for creating a dataset of conversational utterances via selecting which participants’ data to include in the collection the strategy progressively builds up a more diverse subcorpus from an existing larger collection the main idea is to grow the subcorpus by adding conversations sequentially and to assess the contribution of a new participant’s utterances to the diversity of the entire subcorpus this strategy is also applicable to onthefly collection of new datasets via crowdworking or similar methods

by contrast other unsupervised methods like siamesecbow or bert use adjacent sentences as part of the training signal length constraint our discrete searching approach is able to impose the output length as a hard constraint allowing the model to generate summaries of any given length suppose the desired output length is s then our length scorer is we use the terminology unsupervised summarization following zhou and rush while we train the language models on the desired target language we do not need parallel sourcetarget pairs i e sentences together with their groundtruth summaries f len y s if y s −∞ otherwise in other words a candidate summary y is infeasible if it does not satisfy the length constraint in practice we implement this hard constraint by searching among feasible solutions only search space most sentence generation models choose a word from the vocabulary at each time step such as autoregressive generation that predicts the next word and editbased generation with deletion or insertion operations in these cases the search space is v s given a vocabulary v and a summary length s however reference summaries are highly extractive in the headline generation dataset for example of the words in the reference summary also appear in the source sentence this yields a ceiling of r ouge f points for a purely extractive method which is higher than the current stateoftheart supervised abstractive result of points we are thus motivated to propose our wordextraction approach that extracts a subsequence of the input as the summary additionally we arrange the words in the same order as the input motivated by the monotonicity assumption in summarization

in addition we recognize that this study only includes consenting participants for the assignment and exam scores but we minimize this threat by comparing the mean performance of nonconsenting students with those consenting in cs to make sure these values are representative of the whole class coding is not the only way to orient undergraduate students to the discipline of computer science at a university and in fact it might deter intimidate students from taking the classes distract and frustrate students with language syntax and make students without the prior coding experience feel inferior to those with preparatory privilege without providing much advantage in a subsequent c programming class our research findings align with other research studies indicating that the mere exposure to prior programming is not a strong predictor of success but yet the most wellknown approach to introducing undergraduate students to computer science is with coding our past research has shown that story programming is an alternative for a cs class and this research study shows that students who receive this introduction perform as well as or better than those that are taught a traditional pythoncoding approach actually students from the story programming haskell approach who are not in the international bridge program consistently outperform students from the traditional coding approaches in cs however students in cs without any coding tend to do a little worse even though there is no statistical difference in these mean values for grades assignments and exams this suggests that the approach used and amount of coding in cs does not impact students’ grades in cs as much as making below a b in cs does this is the motivation for using a more abstract approach focused on algorithmic concepts such as the story programming approach as a more inclusive alternative to the traditional codefirst approach based on the results from this study we plan to offer the story programming with haskell again to get another comparison group and we will likely not offer another story programming section without coding while this approach without coding might be a good option for students outside the major or not continuing to the subsequent c programming class it does not seem to prepare the students as well as the classes with some exposure to coding

methodology and experimental setup in response to methodological concerns regarding the attribution of learning capabilities to diagnostic classifiers rather than the underlying representations liu et al a hewitt and liang this study employs a parameter free deterministic method for converting contextualized representations into static embeddings this approach ensures that any observed properties in the resulting static representations are intrinsic to the data and not artifacts introduced by the conversion process for the evaluation of static embedding performance wordvec mikolov et al and glove pennington et al are utilized as baseline models given their established prominence in the field regarding contextualized models bert is selected as the primary architecture due to its widespread adoption in downstream applications to ensure robustness and generalizability identical analyses are conducted across four additional contextualized architectures gpt radford et al xlnet yang et al roberta liu et al b and distilbert sanh et al in total the study encompasses nine distinct sets of pretrained weights a comprehensive enumeration of all models weights and naming conventions is provided in appendix c and table the primary results reported herein correspond to the bert base uncased configuration supplementary results for bert large uncased are presented in figure additional quantitative assessments of representation quality are detailed in tables – and figures – representation quality evaluation details to assess the representational quality of the derived static embeddings performance is evaluated across four standard word similarity and relatedness datasets rg rubenstein and goodenough ws agirre et al simlex hill et al and simverb gerz et al further details regarding these datasets are available in section a collectively these datasets comprise examples and define a vocabulary v of unique words each example consists of a word pair w w accompanied by a gold standard annotation of semantic similarity or relatedness derived from human judgments the evaluation metric for a given word embedding is determined by the relative correctness of its ranking against these gold standard annotations note justification for the selection of these evaluation metrics is discussed in section a

capture long distance dependencies these findings substantiate the hypothesis that the utilization of phrase representation sequences enhances the model s capacity to model long range relational structures background and related work this section provides a review of prior research integrating phrases into recurrent sequence to sequence models followed by a concise overview of the transformer architecture which serves as the foundation for the present study integration of phrases in rnn based neural machine translation the majority of existing literature focuses on leveraging phrases from statistical machine translation smt to mitigate coverage limitations inherent in neural machine translation nmt tu et al dahlmann et al observed that while nmt generally achieves superior overall translation quality smt demonstrates greater efficacy in translating rare words and benefits significantly from phrasal translation units to address this they introduced a hybrid search algorithm for attention based nmt extending the standard nmt beam search with phrase translations derived from smt similarly wang et al a posited that whereas nmt typically generates fluent yet occasionally inadequate translations smt produces adequate translations that may lack fluency to resolve coverage and unknown word issues they proposed incorporating smt recommendations into each decoding step of the nmt process expanding on this wang et al b emphasized the critical role of phrases in machine translation given the challenge of integrating phrases into nmt systems that operate on a token by token basis they proposed a method to translate phrases by integrating target phrases from an smt system via a phrase memory this memory supplied by the smt model dynamically selects relevant phrases based on the partial translation generated by the nmt decoder at each step the transformer translation model the current research is grounded in the transformer translation model vaswani et al illustrated in figure this architecture significantly outperforms previous recurrent sequence to sequence approaches and facilitates efficient parallel computation the transformer comprises an encoder and a decoder each consisting of a stack of six identical layers in addition to the embedding and positional embedding matrices present in both components the decoder incorporates a softmax classifier layer to generate translated tokens the weights of this classifier are typically tied to the target embedding matrix both the encoder and decoder layers utilize the multi head attention mechanism to process input sequences

with the rise of the internet and later the development of video call technology life has changed significantly especially in professional settings where many meetings now take place online instead of in traditional offices or large conference halls in this essay i will discuss both the potential benefits and some drawbacks of this innovation and ultimately determine whether it is truly cost effective throughout modern history organizing national and international conferences has required considerable financial resources to bring together experts from around the globe today however online communication platforms allow companies and organizations to meet with renowned and influential individuals more frequently and at a lower cost additionally employees can save the time they would have spent traveling between locations by attending meetings virtually these meetings can also be recorded for documentation and future reference for instance this technology proved to be essential during the covid pandemic without it the negative impact on businesses would likely have been far greater on the flip side there are important concerns to consider technical issues with internet connectivity can still arise leading to unavoidable cancellations furthermore interacting with colleagues solely through screens can have a negative effect on workplace social dynamics the lack of face to face interaction reduces opportunities to build strong and meaningful relationships which may in turn weaken teamwork and overall cohesion within an organization in conclusion after weighing the advantages and disadvantages of this modern innovation i firmly believe that the significant benefits outweigh the few drawbacks mentioned above nevertheless it is important to remain aware of these potential issues and take steps to address them to minimize their impact in the workplace

although the absolute number of women pursuing computer science cs degrees has risen educational institutions continue to face significant challenges in achieving genuine gender parity this study presents a granular quantitative analysis of gender distribution throughout the cs curriculum at a large public research university consistent with national trends the institution has experienced substantial growth in cs enrollment over the past decade however this expansion has not translated into proportional representation for women while the broader undergraduate population demonstrates near gender equilibrium—with women comprising of students in the – academic year compared to men—the cs major remains markedly imbalanced with women accounting for only of undergraduates in the field despite incremental improvements a substantial disparity persists the issue extends beyond initial enrollment to retention and career progression research indicates that women are twice as likely as men to consider abandoning a cs major furthermore even among those who persist a significant portion does not transition into industry or academic roles within computing scholars attribute this attrition to several key factors first the prevailing culture of cs is often perceived as asocial and disconnected from broader societal contexts leading many students particularly women and underrepresented minorities to feel a lack of belonging second confidence gaps play a critical role studies suggest that women majoring in cs often report lower self efficacy regarding technical skills than men who are not majoring in the field consequently many women with significant potential depart the discipline due to diminished self confidence while understanding the drivers of attrition is essential it is equally imperative to investigate the factors that enable women and underrepresented minorities to persist and succeed in computing programs

the key design decision here is designing such heuristics that map continuous importance scores to discrete rationales any strategy for this will likely involve trading conciseness shorter rationales against performance greater predictive accuracy for explainability we can present users with the snippet s that pred used to make a prediction as an explanation from ext and we can be certain that the only tokens that contributed to the prediction made by pred are those included in the this text in addition to transparency this framework may afford efficiency gains in settings in which humans are tasked with classifying documents in this case we can use ext to present only the short relevant snippets indeed we use exactly this approach as one means of evaluation in section fresh implementations the highlevel framework described above requires making several design choices to operationalize we propose and evaluate a set of such choices in this work detailed below specifically we must specify a feature importance scoring mechanism for supp section and a strategy for inducing discrete targets from these continuous scores in addition we need to specify a trained or heuristic extractor architecture ext in this work all instances of pred exploit bertbased representations for fair comparison we have modified all baselines to similarly capitalize on bertbased representations feature scoring methods all models considered in this work are based on bidirectional encoder representations from transformer bert encoders and its variants namely roberta and scibert see appendix b for more details for sake of brevity we simply refer to all of these as bert from here on we define supp as a bert encoder that consumes either a single input in the case of standard classification or two inputs e g in the case of question answering tasks separated by the standard token while we emphasize that the proposed framework can accommodate arbitrary input feature scoring mechanisms we consider only a few obvious variants here leaving additional exploration for future work specifically we evaluate attention scores and input gradients

our approach chooses which population of participants to collect data from for a given round this population changes dynamically depending on calculated participant’s diversity scores when utilizing a humancreated preexisting corpus we assume responses of the dataset are wellformed and of acceptable quality with this assumption we can maximize diversity scores without worrying that quality will be sacrificed for this diversity however when using this approach to collect data onthefly additional quality controls may be necessary to ensure diverse data does not come at the cost of quality we assess two experimental conditions simulated data collection and corpuswide oracle upperbound simulated data collection is set up to mimic crowdsourcing data collection processes leveraging a large precollected corpus while corpuswide oracle upperbound gathers an maximally diverse subcorpus of utterances corpus for all experiments we utilize the precollected empatheticdialogues corpus we experiment with this corpus because it has crowdworker ids associated with each utterance which allows us to experiment with varying the participant population future work should conduct further experimentation to examine this approach’s adaptability to other chitchat and goaloriented datasets the corpus has a large number of utterances over conversations each conversation is centered around a situation such as getting a promotion at work and is associated with one of emotions such as anger excitement or guilt each conversation takes place between two crowdworkers and is an average of turns there are unique crowdworkers in this dataset each completing an average of utterances each across an average of conversations our task is to select sub c of size from the larger empatheticdialogues corpus c we choose as it is a sufficient number of utterances to train downstream models but still a small proportion of the original dataset allowing examination of differences between subcorpora implementation utilizes cornell convokit simulated data collection we simulate realtime crowdsourcing using a large precollected corpus c this allows for running multiple trials each time selecting sub c and examining significance of different diversity metrics and participant selection conditions we simulate collecting data onthefly using an artificiallyconstructed environment formally described in algorithm which completes multiple rounds of data collection until the progressively built subcorpus size sub c is the desired size

this study extends existing research by examining the fine grained artifacts resulting from various modeling decisions with the objective of enhancing the broader understanding of machine generated text methodology this section outlines the experimental framework and configuration generative model configuration the experiments utilize grover zellers et al as the primary text generation engine three distinct generation configurations are investigated model scale generative models typically offer predefined scales corresponding to layer widths and parameter counts for grover the available configurations include base large and mega sampling strategy the decoding process is governed by specific sampling functions this study evaluates variants of top k sampling fan et al and top p nucleus sampling holtzman et al alongside their associated hyperparameters conditioning this parameter refers to the length of the initial context provided to the model denoted as ell specifically the first ell tokens are appended to the title sequence to initiate generation while the parameter space is vast this study deliberately focuses on settings that represent generalizable or subtle variations such fine grained modifications are hypothesized to present greater detection challenges than coarse changes for instance distinguishing between specific grover parameterizations is a more rigorous task than differentiating grover from gpt the former subsumes the latter implying that success in the former necessitates the capability to solve the latter classifier models a classifier is trained to discriminate between the aforementioned generative configurations the task is formulated as a multiclass classification problem wherein each unique model configuration constitutes a distinct class the input consists of token sequences processed through either parameterized or non parameterized encoders the outputs of which are fed into a softmax classification layer this work benchmarks the efficacy of various encoding inductive biases including recurrent convolutional and self attention based architectures this comparative analysis serves as a diagnostic probe into the problem domain by observing the performance of diverse encoder architectures we aim to derive deeper insights into the intrinsic nature of these tasks and datasets

the expanding universe of resources available to cs students potentially increases the complexity of solving the information problems that arise during the course which could also increase the challenge of succeeding that is learning the concepts and skills needed for more advanced courses in the course students who engage in external source hunting might think they are “doing homework” but this activity is not the kind of engagement with skills concepts and cognitive processes that instructors hope for when they develop homework assignments the variety of information sources–and a willingness on the part of students to consult them in ways ranging from haphazard for example william’s google searching to purposeful for example juliet’s searching through lecture slides –suggest that some students might need additional scaffolding so that they can develop better ways to regulate their consulting of information sources and develop their metacognitive skills from our initial evidence of william’s digressive searching and subsequent frustration when he was not able to quickly find the sample code he was searching for in codelab we are interested in knowing how many other students in the course appear to struggle with their ability to interpret their own information needs or find appropriate sources of information whether human static or algorithmic for any such struggling students online searching might seem like one of the only accessible option if this is the case is it possible to develop interventions to teach students how to use this information or design scaffolded assignments to help such students move beyond this kind of behavior finally we observed that some students appeared more willing or eager than others to engage in information search as opposed to thinking through a solution we wondered if some students might believe that their own abilities to find relevant information were stronger than their programming skills or perhaps students were choosing to avoid expending the mental energy required to work through a solution by searching for a “quick fix” this latter explanation has grounding in cognitive science humans form mental models for their experiences and observations and one of the characteristics of mental models is that they “tend to minimize expenditure of mental energy” we would need to extend our interview protocol to explicitly explore these potential cases

furthermore an analysis reveals that only of texts enclosed within code delimiters are genuinely code related whereas serve to highlight natural language segments the remaining instances refer to non code entities such as software names and version identifiers although markdown tags may facilitate entity segmentation section we underscore the critical necessity of utilizing a human annotated corpus for the training and evaluation of natural language processing tools within the software domain annotation agreement the corpus was annotated by four undergraduate students majoring in computer science we employed the web based annotation tool brat stenetorp et al and provided annotators with links to the original stackoverflow posts in each iteration every annotator processed question and answer threads of which were double annotated an adjudicator subsequently resolved discrepancies through discussion with the annotators who also cross verified the single annotated questions within each batch the inter annotator agreement prior to adjudication was as measured by span level cohen s kappa cohen additional github data to further assess the domain adaptability of our approach we annotated readme files and issue reports from repositories randomly sampled from the github dump gousios and spinellis covering the period from october to december all code blocks were excluded from the collected issue reports and readme files the resulting github named entity recognition ner dataset comprises sentences and entities across categories labeled by two in house annotators the inter annotator agreement for this dataset is measured by span level cohen s kappa stackoverflow github tokenization we developed a specialized tokenizer sotokenizer designed specifically for the social computer programming domain posts from stackoverflow and github exhibit characteristics common to web based text including abbreviations emoticons urls ungrammatical sentence structures and spelling errors

previous research has investigated the utility of keystroke dynamics for student identification and performance prediction initial studies established typing profiles based on programming assignments to assess identification accuracy across different contexts such as programming examinations and natural language essay writing these findings indicate that identification accuracy diminishes when the text modality shifts between profile creation and identification specifically profiles derived from programming keystrokes yield lower accuracy when applied to natural language essays consequently the contextual environment appears to significantly influence the predictive validity of keystroke based models thomas et al explored the correlation between keystroke timings and programming performance by categorizing digraphs based on character composition their analysis revealed moderate correlations between specific digraph categories and exam scores the categories demonstrating the highest predictive power included numeric digraphs comprising two digits digraphs involving navigation characters e g arrow keys and edge digraphs comprising characters from distinct categories leinonen et al partially replicated and extended this work by examining the extent to which prior programming experience could be inferred from typing patterns consistent with thomas et al they identified numeric and edge digraphs as the most predictive indicators furthermore their results suggest that both academic performance and prior experience can be partially inferred from typing behavior specifically high performing students and those with greater prior experience demonstrated increased typing speed for programming specific digraphs such as i and sequences containing special characters e g in a separate study conducted at a mid sized public university in the united states python keystroke data were collected over the first eight weeks of an introductory computer science cs course using a custom web based integrated development environment ide during the ninth week students transitioned to a mainstream python ide lacking keystroke logging capabilities the study cohort comprised students distributed across three sections two sections were instructed by a single faculty member while the third was taught by a different instructor this report presents findings derived from five programming assignments each consisting of two distinct projects totaling ten programming tasks although each pair of projects was assigned and due simultaneously the tasks within each pair were unrelated

in this work we bring awareness to the risk of generating inconsistent explanations similarly hendricks et al identify the risk of mentioning attributes from a strong class prior without any evidence being present in the input generating adversarial examples generating adversarial examples is an active research area in natural language processing however most works build on the requirement that the adversarial input should be a small perturbation of an original input or should be preserving the semantics of the original input our setup does not have this requirement and any pair of taskrealistic inputs that causes the model to produce inconsistent explanations suffices most importantly to our knowledge no previous adversarial attack for sequencetosequence models generates full target sequences for instance cheng et al require the presence of predefined tokens anywhere in the target sequence they only test with up to required tokens and their success rate dramatically drops from for token to for tokens for the task of summarization similarly zhao et al proposed an adversarial framework for adding and removing tokens in the target sequence for the task of machine translation our scenario would require as many tokens as the desired adversarial explanation and we also additionally need them to be in a given order thus tackling a much challenging task finally minervini and riedel attempted to find inputs where a model trained on snli violates a set of logical constraints however their method needs to enumerate and evaluate a potentially very large set of perturbations of the inputs besides the computational overhead it also may easily generating ungrammatical inputs moreover their scenario does not address the question of automatically producing undesired inconsistent sequences summary and outlook we drew attention that models generating natural language explanations are prone to producing inconsistent explanations this concern is general and can have a large practical impact for example users would likely not accept a selfdriving car if its explanation module is prone to state that “ the car accelerates because there are people crossing the intersection ”

comparison after matching the amount of exposure that humans and pretrained transformers receive to the quirks of the data set knowledge of syntax it would most likely be difficult to convince humans to ignore syntax when understanding a sentence as bert does the generalization leaderboard what is the way forward my goal is not to argue that there is no value to the leaderboard approach where a single number or a small set of numbers can be used to quickly compare models despite the drawbacks of this approach—in particular its tendency to obscure the finegrained strengths and weaknesses of particular models as i discuss below—hill climbing on a metric can enable a productive division of labor between groups that develop strong benchmarks groups that propose new models and inference methods and groups that have the engineering skills and computational resources necessary to train those models on the number of gpus they require to thrive instead my argument is that the current division of labor is unproductive at the risk of belaboring the mountaineering metaphor one might say that groups with access to engineering and computing resources are climbing the paid hill while other groups which document the same models’ unreliable generalization behavior—or retrain them on smaller data sets to produce the learning curves that are often missing from engineering papers—are climbing the interpretability track hill producing papers that are more and more sophisticated and wellrespected but do not influence the trajectory of mainstream model development this section describes some design decisions that can lead to better alignment between the two sets of research groups many of these points are not new—in fact some of these properties were standard in evaluation paradigms or years ago—but are worth revisiting given recent evaluation trends standard moderately sized pretraining corpora to complement current evaluation approaches we should develop standard metrics that promote sample efficiency at a minimum we should standardize the pretraining corpus across all models as some conll shared tasks do multiple leaderboards can be created that will measure performance on increasingly small subsets of this pretraining corpus size—including ones that are smaller than million words

interpolated α with pretrained embeddings variants respectively these results support the notion that paired neural lms can be used effectively to screen for possible dementia at earlier stages of cognitive impairment the contributions of our work can be summarized as follows first our results demonstrate that the relationship between lm perplexity and lexical frequency is consistent with the phenomenology of dat and its deleterious effects on patients’ vocabulary we show that the “two perplexities” approach is successful at distinguishing between cases and controls in the dementiabank corpus because of its ability to capture specifically linguistic manifestations of the disease second we observe that interpolating between dementia and control lms mitigates the tendency of dementiabased lms to be “surprised” by transcripts indicating severe dementia which is detrimental to performance when the difference between these lms is used as a basis for classification in addition we find a similar smoothing effect when using pretrained word embeddings in place of a randomly instantiated word embedding layer finally we develop a modification of fritsch et al’s “two perplexity” approach that is consistent with these observations replacing the dementia model with an interpolated variant and introducing pretrained word embeddings at the embedding layer both modifications exhibit significant improvements in performance with best results obtained by using them in tandem though not strictly comparable on account of differences in segmentation of the corpus amongst others we note the performance obtained also exceeds that reported with models trained on text alone in prior research code to reproduce the results of our experiments is available on github while using transcript text directly is appealing in its simplicity others have reported substantial improvements in performance when pos tags and paralinguistic features are incorporated suggesting fruitful directions for future research furthermore prior work on using acoustic features shows that they can contribute to discriminative models however dementia bank audio is challenging for acoustic analysis due to poor quality and background noise

inference pass section existing methodologies for integrating pre trained mlms into sequence to sequence architectures such as those for automatic speech recognition asr or neural machine translation the neural architecture was trained on a large scale corpus of general web texts derived from the commoncrawl dataset to ensure data quality by mitigating noise and retaining structured training examples the following filtering procedures were applied language filtering only sentences identified as being written in the target language were retained length constraint sentences exceeding words were excluded grammatical integrity sentences were required to contain at least one function word from a predefined list of approximately ten high frequency terms specific to each language this step ensures the retention of well formed grammatical sentences while discarding noisy fragments document continuity from the remaining sentences only those appearing consecutively within a single document were preserved following these filtering steps a training corpus comprising million words was constructed for each language the vocabulary consists of the most frequent words less frequent tokens are replaced with an token the probability of which is set to zero during the generation phase the model architecture comprises both an encoder and a decoder each consisting of two gated recurrent unit gru layers with a hidden state dimension of word embeddings are set to a size of in accordance with press and wolf the embeddings for the encoder decoder and output layers are shared model parameters were optimized using stochastic gradient descent sgd with an initial learning rate of this learning rate was reduced by a factor of four whenever the loss failed to improve on a held out validation set training was conducted with a batch size of incorporating gradient clipping the implementation was developed using pytorch paszke et al with significant reliance on the opennmt framework klein et al for the application of topical constraints an entropy threshold of was utilized the n gram component employs a standard kneser ney smoothed trigram model implemented via kenlm heafield while the non negative matrix factorization nmf model was factorized to dimensions both the n gram and nmf models were trained on a separate unfiltered corpus of billion words derived from web texts subject only to language identification finally for syllable length constraints the parameters were set to mu and sigma

this study presents a dual analysis aimed at enhancing the understanding of student engagement and the methodological rigor of attitude assessment in computing education first an examination of student reflection data was conducted to identify major themes and subthemes aligned with the music model of online course design the analysis confirms the presence of all five core components—empowerment usefulness success interest and caring—within the course design framework findings indicate that structural elements including module organization content scope project and assessment design and the integration of instructional resources are critical determinants in fostering an engaging online learning environment while this design model demonstrates adaptability for various skill based or project based online modules its efficacy relies on intentional planning and strict alignment with specific learning outcomes second this paper details the validation of a modified instrument for assessing computer science attitudes building upon dorn and tew s computing attitude survey the instrument was expanded to include items addressing gender dynamics and student perceptions regarding the utility of computing these modifications respond to the widening gender disparity in computer science degree attainment the revised survey explores attitudes toward women in the field and evaluates the perceived relevance of computer science to students personal and professional trajectories consequently a revalidation process was undertaken to compare the psychometric properties of the revised instrument against the original this work addresses a broader challenge identified by sahami regarding the need for computing education research to establish itself as a more accepted and rigorous academic discipline sahami emphasizes the necessity of consistent objective benchmarks to facilitate cross study comparability and track field wide progress by validating this modified instrument this study aims to contribute a foundational tool for measuring student attitudes thereby supporting the development of such benchmarks the research underscores the significance of factors such as self efficacy confidence interest and perceived usefulness noting that deficits in self efficacy often correlate with academic struggle furthermore the study highlights how gender differences and prior successful experiences in computing significantly influence student attitudes and success rates contextualized by the historical decline in female computer science graduates soft cardinality has been shown to be a very strong textoverlapping baseline for the task of measuring semantic textual similarity sts obtaining rd place in semeval at sem shared task beside the plain textoverlapping approach we tested within soft cardinality two distributional wordsimilarity functions derived from the ukwack corpus unfortunately we combined these measures with other features using regression obtaining positions th nd and rd among the participants systems in the official ranking already after the release of the gold standard annotations of the test data we observed that using only the similarity measures without combining them with other features would have obtained positions th th and th moreover an arithmetic average of these similarity measures would have been th this paper describes both the systems as they were submitted and the similarity measures that would have obtained those better results the task of textual semantic similarity sts consists in providing a similarity function on pairs of texts that correlates with human judgments such a function has many practical applications in nlp tasks e g summarization question answering textual entailment paraphrasing machine translation evaluation among others which makes this task particularly important numerous efforts have been devoted to this task and major evaluation campaigns have been held at semeval and in sem the experimental setup of sts in consisted of three data sets roughly divided in for training and for testing which contained text pairs manually annotated as a gold standard furthermore two data sets were provided for surprise testing the measure of performance was the average of the correlations per data set weighted by the number of pairs in each data set the best performing systems were ukp takelab and soft cardinality ukp and takelab systems used a large number of resources such as dictionaries a distributional thesaurus monolingual corpora wikipedia wordnet distributional similarity measures kb similarity pos tagger machine learning and others unlike those systems the soft cardinality approach used mainly text overlapping and conventional text preprocessing such as removing of stop words stemming and idf term weighting this shows that the additional gain in performance from using external resources is small and that the soft cardinality approach is a very challenging baseline for the sts task soft cardinality has been previously shown to be also a good baseline for other applications such as information retrieval entity matching paraphrase detection and recognizing textual entailment

silver data and the best annotation strategies neural architectures that can be used for data filtering before expert validation postediting introduction owing to the upsurge in the use of social media platforms over the past decade hate speech hs has become a pervasive issue by spreading quickly and widely meanwhile it is difficult to track and control its diffusion since nuances in cultures and languages make it difficult to provide a clearcut distinction between hate and dangerous speeches the standard approaches to prevent online hate spreading include the suspension of user accounts or deletion of hate comments from the social media platforms smps paving the way for the accusation of censorship and overblocking alternatively to weigh the right to freedom of speech shadowbanning has been put into use where the content account is not deleted but hidden from smp search results still we believe that we must overstep reactive identifyanddelete strategies to responsively intervene in the conversations in this line of action some nongovermental organizations ngos train operators to intervene in online hateful conversations by writing counternarratives a counternarrative cn is a nonaggressive response that offers feedback through factbound arguments and is considered as the most effective approach to withstand hate messages to be effective a cn should follow guidelines similar to those provided in the ‘get the trolls out’ project in order to avoid escalating the hatred in the discussion still manual intervention against hate speech is not scalable therefore datadriven nlg approaches are beginning to be investigated to assist ngo operators in writing cns as a necessary first step diverse cn collection strategies have been proposed each of which has its advantages and shortcomings in this study we aim to investigate methods to obtain high quality cns while reducing efforts from experts we first compare data collection strategies depending on the two main requirements that cn datasets must meet i data quantity and ii data quality

han considers the hierarchical structure of documents document – sentences – words and includes an attention mechanism that finds the most important words and sentences in a document while taking the context into consideration there are two levels of attention wordlevel attention identifies the important words in a sentence and sentence level attention identifies the important sentences in a document the overall architecture of han is shown in figure we train a han model on contextualized corpus with the generated pseudolabels the predicted labels are used in seed expansion and disambiguation seed expansion and disambiguation seed expansion given contextualized documents and their predicted class labels we propose to rank contextualized words and add the top few words into the seed word sets the core element of this process is the ranking function an ideal seed word s of label l is an unusual word that appears only in the documents belonging to label l with significant frequency hence for a given class c j and a word w we measure its ranking score based on the following three aspects • labelindicative since our pseudolabel generation follows the presence of seed words in the document ideally the posterior probability of a document belonging to the class c j after observing the presence of word w i e p c j w should be very close to therefore we use p c j w as our labelindicative measure li c j w p c j w f c j w where f c j refers to the total number of documents that are predicted as class c j and among them f c j w documents contain the word w all these counts are based on the prediction results on the input unlabeled documents • frequent ideally a seed word s of label l appears in the documents belonging to label l with significant frequency to measure the frequency score we first compute the average frequency of seed word s in all the documents belonging to label l

this study illustrates the application of multilevel modeling mlm within the domain of computer science cs education research given the inherent hierarchical structure of educational environments mlm is essential for analyses involving samples drawn from multiple courses or institutions as the field of cs education expands and undertakes increasingly large scale investigations proficiency in implementing mlm will become a requisite competency for researchers a primary finding of this research indicates that students individual perceptions of instructional practices are not significantly associated with retention rates specifically a student s perception of a classroom environment as more or less oriented toward peer cooperation or teacher direction does not predict their likelihood of enrolling in subsequent cs courses conversely a second central finding reveals that the aggregate rating of collaborative learning cl within a course section is associated with lower retention rates this counterintuitive relationship may be attributed to the heightened emphasis on cl in introductory courses which typically enroll a larger proportion of non major students who are less likely to pursue further cs coursework an examination of course level means for cl indicates that while introductory cs courses cs do not consistently exhibit higher cl usage than upper level courses cs sections demonstrate greater homogeneity in their aggregate cl ratings means ranging from to compared to the wider variability observed in upper level courses means ranging from to the implications of this variability in cl implementation within upper level courses for student experience and satisfaction remain unclear however this warrants further investigation in future research furthermore the measures of perceived instructional practices employed in this study focused on general pedagogical approaches and did not encompass cs specific practices prominent in existing literature future inquiries should therefore examine the impact of specific instructional strategies on retention including active learning pair programming game based learning context based instruction and the integration of multimedia finally while the general instructional practices examined herein were not predictive of retention in cs concept mastery was a significant predictor this underscores the critical importance of employing high quality evidence based teaching practices that enhance the probability of students mastering cs concepts and skills consistent with prior expectations the strongest predictor of continued enrollment in cs courses was the student s status as a cs major or their consideration of declaring a cs major

at the moment we use only a limited set of relations but we plan to extend it to include some domainspecific semantic relations and to start using more elements of the conceptual graph formalism for instance nary relations and contexts for the purposes of information retrieval or text mining it is important to be able to compare two phrases or texts represented with conceptual graphs in particular in information retrieval one of the texts is the document and the other one is the user s query each phrase or text may be represented as a set of conceptual graphs for instance a long phrase or a whole text consisting of many phrases are represented as a set of conceptual graphs in general terms our algorithm for the comparison of two conceptual graph representations of two texts consists of two main parts first find the intersection of the two set of graphs second measure the similarity between the two set of graphs as the relative size of each one of their intersection graphs in general we can find more than one subgraph as the intersection of the initial graphs but the measurement of similarity is applied to each one of them separately and only the highest value is kept in the first step we build the intersection of the two original conceptual graphs this intersection consists of all concept nodes that appear in both original conceptual graphs and all relation nodes that appear in both graphs and relate the same concept nodes for the arc between two common concepts to be included in the intersection it should have the same label and orientation in the two original graphs in the second step we measure the similarity between the graphs based on their intersection graph the similarity measure is a value between and where indicates that there is no similarity between the two texts and indicates that the two texts are semantically equivalent because of the bipartite concepts and relations nature of the conceptual graph representations the similarity measure is defined as a combination of two types of similarity the conceptual similarity and the relational similarity the conceptual similarity measures how similar the concepts and actions mentioned in both texts are like topical comparison the relational similarity measures the degree of similarity of the information about these concepts concept interrelations communicated in the two texts

comparison with state of the art models finally our proposed strategies are benchmarked against the most effective hierarchical text classification htc models documented in prior literature kowsari et al sinha et al the experimental results demonstrate that our methods achieve superior performance regarding both classification accuracy and model parameter efficiency furthermore the training duration for each model is approximately one hour over epochs notably the integration of the proposed auxiliary task does not introduce any significant computational overhead related work research in flat text classification has predominantly centered on the development of novel neural architectures conneau et al zhang et al alternative methodologies employ transfer learning to leverage unlabeled data for instance mccann et al utilized the encoder component of a neural machine translation model to provide contextual embeddings for various natural language processing tasks while howard and ruder pretrained a language model on a general domain monolingual corpus before fine tuning it for specific text classification objectives in the domain of htc methodologies are generally categorized into local or global strategies silla and freitas local strategies exploit information specific to individual layers of the taxonomy whereas global strategies address the classification task using a unified model across all classes and hierarchical levels neural network based approaches have demonstrated robust performance in both paradigms kowsari et al sinha et al additionally recent studies have explored transfer learning to model dependencies between parent and child categories banerjee et al and deep reinforcement learning to incorporate hierarchical information during the inference phase mao et al the integration of external information into neural models has shown considerable promise across various tasks including flat text classification previous work has notably enhanced classification performance at a moderate computational cost by incorporating categorical metadata of target classes kim et al and word level linguistic features margatina et al moreover liu et al demonstrated that multitask learning can outperform several state of the art classification baselines to the best of our knowledge these specific strategies have not yet been explicitly applied to the htc domain

for example in figure if code “ acute laryngitis without mention of obstruction ” is assigned to a patient it is unlikely to assign the code “ acute laryngitis with obstruction ” to the patient at the same time if automatic icd coding models ignore such a characteristic they are prone to giving inconsistent predictions thus a challenging problem is how to model the code hierarchy and use it to capture the mutual exclusion of codes code cooccurrence since some diseases are concurrent or have a causal relationship with each other their codes usually cooccur in the clinical text such as “ hypertension ” and “ heart disease ” in this paper we call such characteristic code cooccurrence which can capture the correlations of codes the code cooccurrence can be utilized to correctly predict some codes which are difficult to predict by only using the clinical text itself for example in figure the code of “ acute respiratory failure ” can be easily inferred via capturing apparent clues i e the green bold words from the text although there are also a few clues to infer the code of “ acidosis ” they are very obscure let alone predict the code of “ acidosis ” by only using these obscure clues fortunately there is a strong association between these two diseases one of the main causes of “ acidosis ” is “ acute respiratory failure ” this prior knowledge can be captured via the fact that the codes of the two diseases usually cooccur in clinical texts by considering the correlation the automatic icd coding model can better exploit obscure clues to predict the code of “ acidosis ” therefore another problem is how to leverage code cooccurrence for icd coding in this paper we propose a novel method termed as hyper bolic and co graph re presentation method hypercore to address above problems since the treelikeness properties of the hyperbolic space make it more suitable for representing symbolic data with hierarchical structures than the euclidean space we propose a hyperbolic representation learning method to learn the code hierarchy meanwhile the graph has been proved effective in modeling data correlation and the graph convolutional network gcn enables to efficiently learn node representation

evaluation of constructed sentences when evaluating models on naturally occurring sentences high performance may stem from statistical regularities within the data rather than a genuine mastery of syntax for instance in a context such as the players mask the ball a model might correctly predict the verb form by exploiting the heuristic that animate nouns typically function as subjects whereas inanimate nouns do not as noted by gulordava et al such reliance allows a model to achieve correct predictions for incorrect reasons to determine whether our models were leveraging this specific statistical heuristic we constructed a sentence test set designed to render such heuristics ineffective this dataset was generated using a probabilistic context free grammar pcfg in which all words of a given part of speech are equally likely to appear in any position each sentence adheres to a subject verb object structure with noun phrases optionally modified by adjectives and or prepositional phrases see appendix f as illustrated in example the fern near the sad teachers hates the singer the dependency lstm is particularly susceptible to word co occurrence heuristics due to its architectural limitation it lacks the capacity for a parent node to account for the sequential position of its children this deficiency becomes critical when determining subject verb agreement as the model cannot robustly distinguish between a verb s subject and its direct object consistent with this hypothesis the dependency model performed at chance levels see figure a this result suggests that the model s high accuracy on natural data is partially attributable to lexical heuristics rather than syntactic processing in contrast the other models maintained strong performance indicating a lower susceptibility to reliance on word co occurrence patterns experiment finetuning in experiment tree based models significantly outperformed the bilstm in the presence of attractors this disparity may arise because most natural language sentences are structurally simple failing to generate sufficient signal to demonstrate the importance of tree structure to a low bias learner such as a bilstm recent research has demonstrated the efficacy of syntactically motivated finetuning in enhancing the robustness of neural models min et al consequently this experiment investigates whether incorporating a limited number of training examples that preclude nonsyntactic heuristics would improve the generalization capabilities of our models

this paper presents a bring your own device byod examination framework utilizing lockdown browser technology to transform student laptops into secure restricted workstations with limited system and internet access by integrating this technology with a learning management system lms and a cloud based programming environment the proposed solution facilitates the assessment of both conceptual understanding and practical programming skills within an interactive yet controlled setting drawing upon the implementation of this framework in a large scale undergraduate programming course the study emphasizes that the efficacy of such systems relies as heavily on robust policies and support mechanisms as on the underlying technology traditionally university assessment has relied on written examinations characterized by physical separation time constraints and direct invigilation while this model offers administrative simplicity and a highly controlled environment that mitigates academic dishonesty it is increasingly misaligned with the pedagogical objectives of modern computer science curricula specifically introductory programming courses emphasize iterative processes such as compilation debugging and documentation consultation—activities that are incompatible with pen and paper testing consequently traditional formats often necessitate the simplification of assessment questions and encourage students to train specifically for the exam format rather than developing genuine programming proficiency although project based components can partially address these limitations final examinations remain a prevalent method for evaluating individual learning outcomes ideally programming assessments should replicate the authentic development environment in which students learn including access to their preferred integrated development environments ides while dedicated computer laboratories with restricted user privileges and no internet connectivity have proven effective and cost efficient the byod approach offers a scalable alternative that maintains security while leveraging students personal devices
