abstract this paper presents a scalable fault localization framework designed for dynamic programming languages specifically targeting the debugging challenges encountered by novice programmers the proposed methodology integrates static dynamic and contextual features within a machine learning guided architecture to generalize across a broad spectrum of common errors the efficacy of this approach was evaluated using a comprehensive dataset comprising over diverse real world user interactions collected over a four year period from pythontutor com a widely utilized educational resource for both formal and informal learners empirical results demonstrate that the system is scalable generalizable and highly accurate specifically the top three fault localization suggestions generated by our approach would have assisted of historical novice users a significant improvement over the assistance rate provided by the default interpreter furthermore a controlled human study revealed a statistically significant preference for our approach over the baseline p with participants noting its particular utility in resolving bugs requiring multiple code edits introduction a fundamental component of programming proficiency is the ability to accurately localize the root cause of execution failures novice developers who often lack familiarity with advanced debugging tools or the expertise to interpret complex compiler error messages frequently struggle to identify and rectify the source of errors this challenge is particularly acute in dynamically typed languages such as python which have become increasingly prevalent in computer science education the demand for effective debugging support is substantial for instance the pythontutor online visualization tool serves over users monthly with nearly half of the program crashes encountered requiring multiple attempts to resolve a primary difficulty in fault localization within this context is the existence of multiple syntactically valid corrections for a single error some of which may be more pedagogically valuable than others consider a scenario where a program attempts arithmetic operations involving a user provided number and the current year resulting in a type exception due to the addition of an integer and a string while one valid correction involves removing a type conversion to force string concatenation a more appropriate fix—aligning with the developer s likely intent—is to apply a type cast to the input variable both solutions are well typed however the latter is more conducive to novice learning consequently an effective debugging aid must transcend mere error detection to provide context aware hints that implicate locations consistent with developer intent thereby facilitating both immediate error resolution and long term skill acquisition

the biaffine scorer generates a probability matrix encompassing all potential dependency arcs which can be conceptualized as an edge weighted directed graph this representation offers more comprehensive explicit syntactic information compared to a single best best parse tree the syntax aware approach despite recent advancements in dependency parsing dozat and manning existing parsers continue to struggle with achieving high accuracy on out of domain or irregular data to address this limitation this work leverages the rich syntactic information inherent in edge weighted graphs to mitigate the impact of parsing errors specifically we first employ graph convolutional networks gcns to encode these edge weighted graphs these encoded representations are subsequently integrated into various processing levels of the orl framework complementing the implicit hidden states of the parser finally we utilize novel multi task learning mtl frameworks to further alleviate the problem of error propagation dependency graph convolutional networks dep gcn in this section we propose dependency graph convolutional networks dep gcn to enhance the encoding of syntactic information derived from edge weighted graphs compared to explicit best parse trees edge weighted graphs convey richer structural information by representing all latent syntactic structures thereby avoiding the error propagation associated with selecting a single parse furthermore unlike the implicit hidden states of parser encoders zhang et al a xia et al a an edge weighted graph—formally represented as an attention matrix—explicitly captures the modification strength between word pairs the standard gcn architecture is designed for the direct modeling of graph structured data kipf and welling while a single gcn layer restricts information flow to immediate neighbors multi layer gcns facilitate global information propagation provided connected paths exist formally the output representation h i l for node i at layer l is computed as follows h i l f left sum j n a ij w l h j l b l right where a denotes the adjacency matrix of a graph with n nodes w l and b l represent the learnable model parameters f is the activation function and h i is the initial input vector as illustrated in figure e the dep gcn module serves to bridge the parser model and the orl model

this study applies an integrated syntactic graph isg feature extraction methodology to the domain of automatic authorship attribution by leveraging a graph based representation this approach unifies multiple levels of linguistic description within a single structural framework specifically textual patterns are derived from shortest path traversals across these integrated syntactic graphs to facilitate author identification empirical evaluation demonstrates that on average this method surpasses current state of the art approaches yielding consistently high performance across diverse corpora—a reliability often lacking in existing methodologies these findings confirm the efficacy of the proposed textual patterns for authorship attribution tasks the contemporary landscape is characterized by an exponential proliferation of written texts including books journals and technical reports this surge necessitates the development of robust text processing and mining tools with advanced feature extraction techniques emerging as a critical requirement for various text analysis applications enhanced textual features directly correlate with improved performance in tasks such as document classification and information retrieval conventional approaches typically represent documents as simple feature vectors within the vector space model vsm utilizing words or n grams in this paradigm document similarity is estimated via cosine similarity between corresponding vectors consequently traditional similarity metrics rely predominantly on lexical overlap effectively disregarding the semantic and syntactic nuances inherent in the text when employing a bag of words representation in contrast graph structures offer an intuitive and natural mechanism for data representation in natural language processing nlp comprising nodes vertices and edges arcs which may be directed or undirected and associated with labels or weights graphs are widely utilized in computer science to model real world entities within nlp graph based representations are increasingly employed during preprocessing for tasks such as textual entailment and word sense disambiguation accordingly this paper presents a generalized method for extracting text patterns through shortest path traversal over integrated syntactic graphs

the following section delineates the historical evolution of computer science and examines the scope of its potential upon completion of this overview the reader will be equipped to articulate the historical trajectory that established computer science as a distinct discipline define the fundamental parameters of computer science and evaluate the capabilities of the field alongside its ethical and functional limitations computer science cs is the systematic study of computing encompassing all phenomena associated with computational systems including the internet grounded in the principles of engineering and mathematics the discipline centers on the analysis of algorithms an algorithm is defined as a precise sequence of instructions that facilitates computation governing the components utilized by computers to process information through the study and application of these algorithms computer science generates applications and solutions that exert a profound influence across all sectors of society notable examples include the software architectures enabling e commerce digital communication media streaming and various other technological processes although computing devices are ubiquitous in the contemporary era their prevalence is a relatively recent development for individuals whose lives are deeply integrated with technology the field may appear ahistorical characterized by a relentless focus on rapid innovation and improvement that often overlooks historical reflection nevertheless the foundational concepts of computer science established between fifty and one hundred years ago continue to dictate the boundaries of modern computational possibility the earliest computing instruments differed significantly from modern electronic computers these initial devices were physical calculation tools such as the abacus which emerged independently in various societies thousands of years ago the abacus facilitated basic arithmetic operations including tallying counting and addition while abaci remain in limited use today—serving educational purposes for children scoring mechanisms in games and assistive tools for individuals with visual impairments—they have largely been superseded by the development of advanced number systems specifically the adoption of the arabic numeral system which incorporates zero and place value enabled computational complexities unattainable with abaci concurrent with these developments the concept of the algorithm was formalized algorithms utilize specific inputs and a finite series of steps to execute arithmetic operations—such as addition subtraction multiplication and division—thereby producing the outputs essential to modern computing

the fuzzy clustering procedure utilized the previously described feature set for each concept and across the six designated groups the clustering output consisted of membership functions quantifying the degree to which a specific concept belongs to a class defined by a particular emotion label these values ranging from to represent the estimated probability that a given token expresses the associated emotion the analysis employed a modified fuzzy c means fcm algorithm standard fcm accepts a dataset of n points within a p dimensional feature space and generates c centroids where c is a predefined parameter alongside a set of membership functions these functions denote the degree of membership for each point within each cluster interpreted as the probability of cluster affiliation consequently membership values are constrained to the interval and must sum to unity for each data point to determine the optimal cluster distribution and centroid placement the algorithm minimizes a specific objective function the fuzziness of the resulting clusters is regulated by a power parameter p which was set to in this study the optimization process is iterative beginning with random membership values the algorithm alternately updates the centroids and membership values until the change in the objective function falls below a convergence threshold of to enhance cluster compactness and ensure that highly similar elements are grouped together an additional term was incorporated into the standard objective function this modification biases the algorithm to increase the membership value of a data point in the cluster associated with the nearest centroid thereby improving the cohesion of similar points upon completion of the fuzzy clustering the correspondence between the c generated classes and the six target emotion labels was established this mapping was achieved by first converting the fuzzy results into hard clustering assignments wherein each data point was assigned to the cluster yielding the maximum membership value subsequently the emotion label for each hard cluster was determined via majority voting

in the most complex tasks a goal must be decomposed into subgoals that in turn require complex plans each in this case a plan combination is needed which appears to be one important source of difficulties in writing programs we considered this richness in types of plans and goals in defining our marking scheme which hence includes all goals and subgoals but assigns them different weights according to the complexity of the corresponding plans goals that rely on very basic knowledge are unitarily weighted whereas the combination of plans is doubly weighted during this process we realized that eliciting goals and plans required a deep comprehension of the structure of the assigned task especially when the assignments are challenging and require the combination of strategies even though we have no explicit measure for this we have the impression that defining a marking scheme based on goals and plans requires more effort and time than a holistic approach but not much more and anyways comparable to other analytic approaches we analyze an exercise named “uppercase letters” where students are required to write a program that extracts uppercase letters from input words and concatenates them the program must use the given method string extractuppercase string w which extracts all uppercase letters from a string w and returns the string obtained by concatenating them an example of given input and expected output is provided see a solution of the exercise is given in by analyzing the task in a topdown manner we can list the main goals some of these goals can be further decomposed hence we get the list of goals and subgoals shown in some goals like a or can be achieved with simple plans that rely on basics knowledge of syntax and semantics other goals require an iterative plan both goals and require a counting loop plan these plans are highlighted in the former loop is over lines the latter is over words and is in fact a conditional counting loop plan since words must be counted only if a condition is met in both cases the plan consists in initializing a counter variable linecount at line and wordcount at line respectively and possibly conditionally incrementing the counter at each iteration line and respectively

an analysis of the cooking and diy corpora reveals a prevalence of complex verbal forms such as entgraten deburr in the diy domain and abbinden thicken a sauce in culinary contexts conversely the hunting domain is characterized by significant terminological ambiguity featuring expressions unfamiliar to laypersons for instance the term licht light is employed specifically to denote the eyes of game given these variations it is posited that surface form features are unlikely to yield predictive utility furthermore frequency based features may prove ineffective due to the high degree of semantic ambiguity inherent in these domains regarding levels of technicality certain terms exhibit low ambiguity and restricted usage for example the culinary term blanchieren blanch frequently co occurs with salzwasser salted water within domain specific contexts notably similar domain specific contexts appear in the general language corpus despite initial expectations to the contrary as the general language corpus is derived from web crawling it inevitably incorporates a subset of domain specific texts consequently for highly technical and unambiguous terms the contexts within the general and domain specific corpora demonstrate maximal similarity in contrast it is hypothesized that contextual variation will be more pronounced for basic terms while non technical terms are unlikely to appear in domain specific sentences within the general corpus the situation differs for ambiguous terminology where sense distributions vary across corpora for the hunting term licht both general and domain specific meanings are present within the domain corpus in contrast for the culinary term zauberstab wand or hand blender the senses appear largely disjunctive across the respective corpora illustrative examples of this phenomenon are provided in table based on these observations we propose adopting the framework established by amjadian et al as a foundation for detecting degrees of technicality as both general and domain specific word embeddings are expected to encode termhood attributes additionally we hypothesize that comparing these word vectors will provide valuable information for a prediction system as a baseline a decision tree classifier dt is employed utilizing three standard features commonly associated with term familiarity prediction corpus size normalized frequency word length and character n grams

used it to conduct practical programming exams in java with around students at each sitting on top of a feature to compile and run java code the platform also allowed other types of questions to be asked in an exam such as multiplechoice ones or parson’s puzzles internet access was restricted via a firewall it was reported that the exam was “done supervised in a lecture hall or computer lab” thus it is unclear whether exams were conducted solely on a byod basis or whether lab computers were used too the motivation for designing a byod solution came directly from “digital world” our institution’s course on computational thinking and programming using python in the following we present an overview of the course how the students are assessed and the limitations of its previous exam formats that we set out to overcome digital world is a compulsory introductory programming course taken by every firstyear undergraduate at the singapore university of technology and design regardless of the subject they later choose to major in the course uses elements of the flipped classroom paradigm requiring students to complete a set of short readings and a corresponding quiz on our lms before the first class of each week during class hours students are given a weekly problem set containing programming problems that instructors may use as the basis of their lessons and homework problems that are left to the students to tackle on their own or with their peers solutions to these problems are submitted to vocareum which automatically grades students’ solutions according to a variety of test cases as well as a submission platform vocareum is a webbased ide that students can use to write run and debug python programs during the term students are assessed at multiple junctures via quizzes homework and group projects nonetheless a significant portion of their final grade is derived from their performance in a midterm and final exam
